Naszym obowi脛聟zkiem jest znale脜潞脛聡 rozwi脛聟zania, kt脙鲁re s脛聟 i skuteczne, i proporcjonalne. Takie, kt脙鲁re uderzaj脛聟 w sprawc脙鲁w, a nie w osoby niewinne.

脗聽

3. European Ocean Pact (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 L'ordine del giorno reca la discussione sulla dichiarazione della Commissione sul Patto europeo per gli oceani (2025/2744(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Costas Kadis, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, I am extremely pleased to be here today to present the new European Ocean Pact to this House, only one week after President von der Leyen presented it at the United Nations Ocean Conference in Nice.脗聽I want to sincerely thank the European Parliament for its invaluable contributions to this new pact: the ideas shared throughout our exchanges, your position papers and the very fruitful plenary discussion in April have been taken on board in their greatest majority. With our new Ocean Pact, we now have a common European vision of ocean governance combined with concrete policy actions.

Let me briefly present some of the key deliverables of the Ocean Pact, built around six fundamental pillars. To protect and restore the health of our ocean and to uphold its function as a major carbon sink, the Commission will, among others, support Member States to designate and effectively manage marine protected areas and to ensure the implementation of existing ambitious environmental legislation. The Commission will also support coastal communities to build new business models that benefit both nature and people, including through European blue carbon reserves.

To boost the competitiveness of the EU's blue economy, the Commission will present an industrial maritime strategy and an EU ports strategy, and it will continue to support the rollout of offshore wind and ocean energy technologies. A blue generational renewal strategy will foster access to young professionals in marine research, ocean tech and sustainable fisheries and aquaculture. As promised, sustainable fisheries and aquaculture will be at the core of the Ocean Pact. The comprehensive evaluation of the Common Fisheries Policy and its possible revision, as well as Vision 2040 for fisheries and aquaculture 芒聙聯 which will put particular emphasis on small-scale fishers 芒聙聯 will be important milestones.

To better support our coastal and island communities and outermost regions, we will present a dedicated strategy for the development and resilience of EU coastal communities, a new strategy for EU islands and an updated strategy for outermost regions.

Through the Ocean Pact, the EU announced the launch of an EU ocean observation initiative, which will be underpinned by a European ocean research and innovation strategy. We will also operationalise the digital twin of the ocean by 2030.

To enhance maritime security and defence, we will establish a coordinated strategy to remove unexploded munitions from European waters. We will also support investing in a pioneering European airborne drone fleet.

To strengthen EU ocean diplomacy and international ocean governance, among others, we will push for the swift ratification and entry into force of the BBNJ Agreement, as well as step up our fight against illegal, unreported and unregulated fishing through the mandatory implementation of the digital IUU catch certification scheme as of January脗聽2026.

The Ocean Pact should be implementable in order to be meaningful: this is why we have developed a strong governance mechanism. We will propose an ocean act by 2027, but we will ensure that existing targets linked to the ocean are identifiable under one single roof.脗聽This should facilitate their coherent and effective implementation, while at the same time decreasing administrative burdens.

Honourable Members, the United Nations Ocean Conference in Nice created a clear momentum for ocean action. We have used this momentum by presenting our Ocean Pact that demonstrates how the European Union is an Ocean Union. Now, implementation will be key. We need everybody's support, commitment and enthusiasm to keep this precious momentum and to build on it by turning a vision into concrete action. So I thank you and I now look forward to our debate.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Isabelle Le Callennec, au nom du groupe PPE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, la 3e脗聽conf脙漏rence des Nations unies pour les oc脙漏ans a 脙漏t脙漏 l'occasion pour la Commission de pr脙漏senter son pacte: un pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an qui a 脙漏t脙漏 脙漏labor脙漏 脙聽 partir des contributions de l'ensemble des parties prenantes. Votre capacit脙漏 脙聽 synth脙漏tiser des attentes parfois contradictoires, souvent compl脙漏mentaires, a 脙漏t脙漏 largement salu脙漏e.

Nombre des propositions du PPE ont 脙漏t脙漏 retenues dans la version finale. Je pense 脙聽 votre volont脙漏 de d脙漏velopper l'脙漏conomie bleue, 脙聽 la place faite aux p脙陋cheurs pour valoriser la ressource halieutique tout en pr脙漏servant la biodiversit脙漏, 脙聽 l'urgence de mettre en place une diplomatie des oc脙漏ans. Je note aussi votre d脙漏termination 脙聽 promouvoir notre s脙漏curit脙漏 alimentaire, 脙聽 lutter contre la p脙陋che ill脙漏gale, 脙聽 moderniser la flotte de p脙陋che, 脙聽 mieux g脙漏rer les conflits d'usage. Sur les aires marines prot脙漏g脙漏es, sachez que nous vous soutenons dans votre approche pragmatique au cas par cas, sur la base d'avis scientifiques et 脙聽 l'脙漏chelle des territoires marins.

L'Union europ脙漏enne a donc pris les commandes et a d脙漏sormais une feuille de route. Reste 脙聽 lui donner les moyens n脙漏cessaires 脙聽 sa mise en 脜聯uvre, financiers et r脙漏glementaires, 脙聽 horizon脗聽2027. Avec une premi脙篓re 脙漏tape d脙漏terminante, la r脙漏vision de la directive sur la planification de l'espace maritime.

Sachez, Monsieur le Commissaire, que les d脙漏put脙漏s du PPE sauront se mobiliser pour que ce pacte ne soit pas une bouteille jet脙漏e 脙聽 la mer, mais bien une promesse faite aux g脙漏n脙漏rations futures d'un d脙漏veloppement durable de nos oc脙漏ans.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christophe Clergeau, au nom du groupe S&D. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, nous 脙漏tions ensemble, il y a quelques jours, 脙聽 la conf脙漏rence des Nations unies sur l'oc脙漏an et nous avons pu mesurer toute la formidable mobilisation, l'脙漏lan citoyen, port脙漏s notamment par les ONG bleues que je voudrais saluer. La pr脙漏sentation du pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an par la pr脙漏sidente脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen et par vous-m脙陋me a 脙漏t脙漏 aussi un moment fort et je voudrais vous en remercier, parce qu'il fixe un cadre ambitieux pour l'avenir.

Maintenant, il y a urgence 脙聽 agir et je voudrais ici relayer la demande des ONG, qui est aussi la n脙麓tre, d'avoir un plan d'action脗聽2025-2030 pour traduire concr脙篓tement votre ambition. Ensuite, il y a urgence 脙聽 changer la gouvernance des oc脙漏ans pour r脙漏ussir l脙聽 o脙鹿 nous avons 脙漏chou脙漏, notamment pour le retour au bon 脙漏tat 脙漏cologique des oc脙漏ans.

Nous avons trois demandes pour le pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an, qui doit en 脙陋tre l'outil: la premi脙篓re, que le pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an arrive d脙篓s脗聽2026 et non en脗聽2027, sinon nous risquons de ne pas l'adopter au cours de cette l脙漏gislature; la deuxi脙篓me, que la r脙漏vision de la directive-cadre pour le milieu marin soit int脙漏gr脙漏e au pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an, sinon il n'y a pas de coh脙漏rence; et la troisi脙篓me, que la Commission puisse mettre 脙聽 l'脙漏tude tous les sujets l脙漏gislatifs qui m脙漏riteraient d'脙陋tre int脙漏gr脙漏s dans le pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an pour permettre d'atteindre les objectifs fix脙漏s par ce pacte. Ce chantier l脙漏gislatif, Monsieur le Commissaire, nous sommes mobilis脙漏s pour le mener 脙聽 vos c脙麓t脙漏s et cela d脙篓s maintenant.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Silvia Sardone, a nome del gruppo PfE. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, siamo stanchi di chi vuole criminalizzare i pescatori e i settori economici legati al mare. Non vorremmo che le nuove regole sui mari volute dall'Unione europea fossero ancora una volta guidate dall'ideologia, dalla proliferazione di norme e divieti e da tentativi di colpire la pesca tradizionale.

Occorre considerare che sono gi脙聽 stati compiuti notevoli sforzi per conformarsi alle normative europee. La transizione ecologica non pu脙虏 essere davvero sostenibile se non tiene conto del mare e delle persone che da esso dipendono.

Vogliamo quindi che la pesca europea venga tutelata, introducendo clausole specchio in tutti gli accordi commerciali per garantire che le importazioni rispettino gli stessi standard di qualit脙聽, ambientali e sociali, imposti ai pescatori europei. Non possiamo infatti accettare regole ecologiche che penalizzano solo gli operatori europei. Chiediamo inoltre che i nostri pescatori possano operare senza dover affrontare la concorrenza sleale delle imbarcazioni di paesi terzi, spesso impegnate in pratiche di pesca non sostenibili.

Vogliamo difendere la sovranit脙聽 nazionale anche sui mari, insieme alle tradizioni e alle esigenze locali, senza subire modelli centralizzati imposti dall'Unione europea, che portano a nuove regolamentazioni e ulteriori oneri per le attivit脙聽 economiche legate al mare. Insomma, difendiamo i nostri mari e difendiamo anche chi lavora nelle nostre acque.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen, namens de ECR-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, hoe kunnen we onze oceanen gezond houden en aangetaste mariene ecosystemen zoals in de Oostzee weer herstellen? Een brede, integrale aanpak is daarbij nodig, waarbij alle gebruikers van de zee hun verantwoordelijkheid nemen. Daar draait het in de kern om in het oceaanpact, en ik vind dat een aansprekende benadering.

Ik noem echter wel een aantal aandachtspunten. Allereerst de oproep: zet niet in op nog meer beperkingen voor de visserij en sluiting van gebieden. Laten we perspectief bieden voor de visserij door duurzame methoden te bevorderen: zuinigere motoren en selectiever vistuig bijvoorbeeld. Daar moet de Europese Unie dan ook voldoende budget voor vrijmaken.

In de tweede plaats zou ik willen oproepen tot meer maatwerk. In sommige gebieden moet je voorzichtig zijn met bodemberoering bijvoorbeeld, maar in andere gebieden is dat veel minder een probleem. Ik ben blij dat de Commissie dat ook onderkent, maar waarom wordt er in de bijlage dan nog wel over uitfasering van bodemberoering in alle beschermde gebieden gesproken? Dat moet haast wel een vergissing zijn.

Tot slot zou ik willen oproepen tot een breder onderzoek naar de effecten van windmolenparken op het ecosysteem. Dat is wat mij betreft een onderbelicht thema in het oceaanpact. Bij de noodzakelijke integrale benadering moet Europa hier naar durven kijken.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽St脙漏phanie Yon-Courtin, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, nous revenons de la conf脙漏rence des Nations unies sur l'oc脙漏an 脙聽 Nice, o脙鹿 l'Europe a montr脙漏 la voie et assum脙漏 son r脙麓le de premier espace maritime mondial, mais ce n'脙漏tait que le point de d脙漏part et nous devons maintenant passer du pacte 脙聽 l'acte.

Pour cela, nous avons d'abord besoin d'une m脙漏thode pour transformer la strat脙漏gie globale en actions locales. Le pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an doit se d脙漏cliner dans nos r脙漏gions littorales en concertation avec les professionnels de la mer, les p脙陋cheurs, les scientifiques, tous les acteurs en premi脙篓re ligne de l'oc脙漏an, ces acteurs de nos territoires trop souvent exclus des grands-messes internationales.

Le pacte doit 脙漏videmment nous impliquer, nous aussi, les parlementaires europ脙漏ens. De l'acte oc脙漏an 脙聽 l'omnibus bleu, en passant par la politique des p脙陋ches ou la planification de l'espace maritime, nous seront indispensables pour b脙垄tir un pacte l脙漏gislatif puissant et coh脙漏rent qui adopte une vraie vision g脙漏n脙漏rale, transversale, holistique, inclusive. Nous demandons donc 脙聽 la Commission de pr脙漏senter un plan d'action et un calendrier de mise en 脜聯uvre pour concr脙漏tiser les engagements.

Enfin, pour 脙陋tre cr脙漏dibles, nous devons aussi mettre les moyens 脙聽 la hauteur des ambitions. Sans financement, le pacte se limitera 脙聽 une simple d脙漏claration d'intention. C'est pourquoi le pacte doit 脙陋tre soutenu par un fonds oc脙漏an et des objectifs d脙漏di脙漏s dans le prochain budget pluriannuel.

Avec le pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an, l'Europe se donne les moyens de devenir une v脙漏ritable union oc脙漏anique et nous serons au rendez-vous pour en faire une r脙漏alit脙漏, en particulier pour d脙漏fendre nos p脙陋cheurs et les gens de la mer. Alors, Monsieur le Commissaire, nous comptons sur vous et vous pouvez compter sur nous.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Isabella L脙露vin, f脙露r Verts/ALE gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Det 脙陇r l脙陇tt att ta havet f脙露r givet. Vi har fiskat, kastat plast och dumpat gifter och badat och semestrat och havet har bara gett oss allt: producerat fisk och syre, dolt skr脙陇pet, absorberat v脙陇rme och koldioxid. Men snart orkar det inte l脙陇ngre, det 脙陇r p脙楼 v脙陇g att kollapsa och det 脙陇r vi som b脙陇r ansvaret.

D脙陇rf脙露r v脙陇lkomnar jag EU:s nya havspakt och den Ocean Act som Milj脙露partiet har drivit p脙楼 f脙露r. Den kan bli en v脙陇ndpunkt, men bara om vi g脙露r den till mer 脙陇n fina ord. Vi beh脙露ver en samordnad och bindande havspolitik med en havslag som faktiskt har t脙陇nder.

Vi vill ge haven, inte minst 脙聳stersj脙露n, en chans att 脙楼terh脙陇mta sig och d脙楼 kr脙陇vs en ny ordning, en plan med en tydlig tidslinje: p脙楼 kort sikt, rej脙陇lt s脙陇nkta fiskekvoter och p脙楼 l脙楼ng sikt skarpa 脙楼tg脙陇rder l脙陇ngs hela kedjan fr脙楼n land till hav.

Framf脙露r allt m脙楼ste vi bryta med det gamla. Att olika aktiviteter till havs hanteras var f脙露r sig. Vi beh脙露ver ett helhetsgrepp och modet att f脙露rbjuda destruktiva metoder som bottentr脙楼lning.

Det handlar inte bara om fiskbest脙楼nd, det handlar om livskraftiga ekosystem, om hav som kan andas, om v脙楼r egen framtid. Vi har ett val att agera eller att passivt se p脙楼 n脙陇r haven d脙露r och n脙陇r 脙聳stersj脙露n f脙露rst脙露rs. Jag vet vilket val vi m脙楼ste g脙露ra.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Emma Fourreau, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, 脗芦La haute mer ne sera plus le Far West脗禄, a affirm脙漏 Emmanuel脗聽Macron pendant la conf脙漏rence des Nations unies sur l'oc脙漏an, mais il s'est bien gard脙漏 de nommer les cow-boys: les industries du p脙漏trole, du gaz ou de la p脙陋che intensive.

脗芦La haute mer ne sera plus le Far West.脗禄 Pourtant, quand Macron annonce atteindre 4脗聽% d'aires marines sans chalutage de fond, il ment. Cette pratique est d脙漏j脙聽 interdite ou impossible dans les zones en question. Pourtant, alors qu'il annon脙搂ait l'entr脙漏e en vigueur prochaine du trait脙漏 sur la biodiversit脙漏 en haute mer, le seuil de pays signataires n'est finalement pas atteint et tout reste 脙聽 faire.

La conf脙漏rence des Nations unies sur l'oc脙漏an aura 脙漏t脙漏 un sommet mondial de plus pour le 脗芦greenwashing脗禄. Dans la d脙漏claration finale, aucune mention des 脙漏nergies fossiles, aucune mention de la p脙陋che industrielle. Le pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an de la Commission suit la m脙陋me logique pour ne surtout f脙垄cher personne.

脗芦La haute mer ne sera plus le Far West.脗禄 Pourtant, lorsqu'un petit bateau qui transportait de l'aide humanitaire pour Gaza a 脙漏t脙漏 attaqu脙漏 en toute ill脙漏galit脙漏 par Isra脙芦l dans les eaux internationales, Macron n'a pas eu un seul mot. Pourtant, lorsque, dans cet acte de piraterie, dix Europ脙漏ens ont 脙漏t脙漏 kidnapp脙漏s, dont notre coll脙篓gue Rima脗聽Hassan, aucun dirigeant de l'Union europ脙漏enne n'a eu le moindre mot. Ils d脙漏fendaient le droit international. Pendant ce temps, complices et impuissants, vous le regardez couler en mer, comme sur terre.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 Colleghi, non siamo allo stadio. Quindi vi pregherei di essere rispettosi nei confronti di chi interviene. Quando 脙篓 il vostro turno, poi, avrete la possibilit脙聽 di esprimere il vostro pensiero. Evitiamo il tifo da stadio che non 脙篓 proprio di quest'Aula. Grazie.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegbert Frank Droese, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, verehrte Kollegen, meine lieben Landsleute, liebe Leipziger! Zun脙陇chst einmal m脙露chte ich heute, an diesem 17.脗聽Juni, meinen mutigen Landsleuten gedenken, die sich 1953 dem Stalinismus, der sozialistischen Unterdr脙录ckung entgegenstellten. Viele von denen wurden ermordet oder zeitlebens verfolgt oder gebrochen. Es ist daher eine Schande, dass heute in Europa der barbarische Sozialismus wieder auf dem Vormarsch ist.

In der Regel gehen gro脙聼e Versprechen in der Europ脙陇ischen Union mit gro脙聼em Geld einher, und der Europ脙陇ische Ozeanpakt bildet hier keine Ausnahme. Allt脙陇gliche Dinge wie verantwortungsvoller Umweltschutz werden eher unterfinanziert. Um F脙露rdermittel zu sichern, braucht man ein wirklich gro脙聼es, wahnsinniges Vorhaben, das den gesamten Globus umfasst. Wir rechtfertigen diese Entwicklung mit dem Anspruch, global leader zu sein. Doch angesichts unserer stagnierenden Startup-Kultur und Schw脙陇chen in Zukunftstechnologien sind andere L脙陇nder zunehmend weniger geneigt, unserem Beispiel zu folgen. So haben die Vereinigten Staaten unter der Trump-Regierung Offshore-Windprojekte gestoppt und setzen stattdessen auf eine intensive F脙露rderung der fossilen Energiegewinnung脗聽芒聙聯 Stichwort脗聽Drill, baby, drill.脗聽Bravo!

Klimapolitik geht jedoch nicht nur zulasten der Wirtschaft, sondern lenkt auch wichtige Ressourcen von Naturschutzprojekten ab. So behindern beispielsweise Windparks den Naturschutz und binden Gelder, die stattdessen kleinen, umweltschonenden Fischereien oder der besseren 脙聹berwachung von Meeresschutzgebieten zugutekommen k脙露nnten. Diese systemverzerrende Agenda wird mit einem sogenannten Klimanotstand gerechtfertigt, den allein die EU als existenzielle Bedrohung betrachtet. Man bedenkt, dass die Kommission offenbar mehr Angst vor CO2 hat als vor dem Atomkrieg mit Russland. Wir haben es hier mit diesem Ocean-Pakt offensichtlich mit Klimawahnsinn zu tun.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carmen Crespo D脙颅az (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario y equipo: primero, enhorabuena. Enhorabuena por ser capaces de llevar un Pacto europeo tan importante y con tantos aspectos a todo el panorama internacional. Yo creo que es un 脙漏xito sin precedentes que hoy la Comisi脙鲁n Europea plantee un Pacto de estas caracter脙颅sticas. Empezando por ah脙颅, creo que todo es positivo.

Es verdad que lo que hemos tratado tambi脙漏n es que la pesca tenga ah脙颅 un papel fundamental 芒聙聰porque lo ha tenido siempre, se lo ha ganado a lo largo de la historia芒聙聰 y, adem脙隆s, que en el Pacto sea fundamental la apuesta por la alimentaci脙鲁n de los europeos a trav脙漏s de la econom脙颅a azul y, en este caso, a trav脙漏s de la pesca.

Creo que, adem脙隆s, diversificar, cohabitar, ordenar el espacio marino siempre afecta positivamente a todos, y creo que apostar por llevar a cabo estudios del impacto econ脙鲁mico 芒聙聰por que sea una pr脙隆ctica habitual llevar a cabo estudios del impacto econ脙鲁mico de todas las decisiones que tomemos芒聙聰 tambi脙漏n es una buena decisi脙鲁n.

Adicionalmente, desde luego, hay que apostar por la financiaci脙鲁n a trav脙漏s del FEMPA, por la modernizaci脙鲁n de la flota y por situaciones equilibradas que se basen en los informes cient脙颅ficos. Cuando se cierran 87脗聽zonas, se est脙隆n cerrando zonas para nuestros pescadores, y esto tiene que estar muy fundamentado cient脙颅ficamente; si no, tenemos que hacerlo de forma equilibrada, al igual que en el Mediterr脙隆neo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andr脙漏 Rodrigues (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, a Confer脙陋ncia das Na脙搂脙碌es Unidas para os Oceanos foi, uma vez mais, clara.

A degrada脙搂脙拢o marinha est脙隆 a acelerar e, sem a脙搂脙拢o imediata, o futuro da vida marinha e, por consequ脙陋ncia, de todos n脙鲁s estar脙隆 comprometido.

Neste contexto, sa脙潞do a proposta da Comiss脙拢o Europeia sobre o Pacto Europeu dos Oceanos. Sublinho, de modo particular, o reconhecimento do papel fundamental dos pescadores na gest脙拢o dos nossos mares, da Pol脙颅tica Comum de Pescas, da Inova脙搂脙拢o Azul, bem como a salvaguarda das regi脙碌es ultraperif脙漏ricas e das frotas artesanais, sem esquecer a dimens脙拢o internacional da pol脙颅tica dos mares da Uni脙拢o Europeia.

Portugal, os A脙搂ores e a Madeira, t脙陋m aqui uma oportunidade hist脙鲁rica para liderar. Mas, sejamos francos, sem uma alavanca financeira poderosa, da qual um fundo pr脙鲁prio e abrangente e dedicado da Uni脙拢o Europeia tem de emergir, o Pacto arrisca-se a ser apenas um manifesto de boas inten脙搂脙碌es.

A nossa ambi脙搂脙拢o precisa, pois, de ser financiada. A reconvers脙拢o do setor das pescas deve ser mais apoiada e as palavras precisam mesmo de se traduzir em mais a脙搂脙拢o e em mais investimento.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, este Pacto tem aspetos positivos, mas tamb脙漏m tem aspetos muito negativos. Nomeadamente, tenta defender a biodiversidade, mas ao mesmo tempo aprova projetos que danificam habitats, expulsam pescadores e privatizam o mar, que 脙漏 do dom脙颅nio p脙潞blico. Estou a falar das e脙鲁licas no mar, que s脙拢o gravosas, quer para quem pesca, quer para as popula脙搂脙碌es mar脙颅timas, quer para os pr脙鲁prios pa脙颅ses.

Por outro lado, as aquaculturas que t脙陋m sido beneficiadas pela Comiss脙拢o no meu pa脙颅s, Portugal, n脙拢o t脙陋m sido um sucesso. Pelo contr脙隆rio, porque muita gente recebe o dinheiro e deixa cair as aquaculturas que s脙拢o, neste momento, uma fonte de polui脙搂脙拢o marinha.

Portanto, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, acho que este Pacto tem de ser revisto tendo em vista melhorar a vida dos cidad脙拢os europeus. Porque de hoje para amanh脙拢, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, n脙鲁s n脙拢o vamos poder comer caldeiradas de p脙隆s de e脙鲁licas. N脙鲁s temos de comer caldeiradas de peixe; e se n脙拢o h脙隆 pescadores, n脙拢o h脙隆 peixe, se n脙拢o h脙隆 pesca, n脙拢o h脙隆 peixe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nora Junco Garc脙颅a (NI). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, se脙卤or脙颅as, celebramos el paso que ha supuesto la firma del Pacto Europeo por el Oc脙漏ano como resultado de la Cumbre de las Naciones Unidas celebrada en Niza. Es, sin duda, un avance pol脙颅tico relevante, una declaraci脙鲁n de intenciones que reconoce la urgencia de actuar por la salud de nuestros mares, pero no se puede hablar de sostenibilidad mientras se imponen restricciones sin base cient脙颅fica, se reparten fondos con opacidad y se ignora la voz de quienes llevan generaciones cuidando del mar.

No podemos pedir m脙隆s sacrificios a nuestros pescadores mientras flotas ilegales extraeuropeas faenan impunemente a las puertas de Europa. El Pacto habla de conservaci脙鲁n, pero ignora la sostenibilidad econ脙鲁mica de nuestras comunidades costeras y carece de herramientas reales de financiaci脙鲁n e innovaci脙鲁n. La econom脙颅a azul solo ser脙隆 sostenible si tambi脙漏n es justa, transparente y respetuosa con nuestras tradiciones.

Mientras tanto, estas flotas de terceros pa脙颅ses siguen operando fuera de control y al margen de este Pacto. Si queremos que el Pacto tenga un impacto real, estas flotas tendr脙颅an que estar sujetas a las mismas normas y exigencias que las nuestras.

S脙颅 a un pacto por los oc脙漏anos, s脙颅 a una protecci脙鲁n del medio marino, pero, sobre todo, s脙颅 a nuestros pescadores, que son los verdaderos guardianes de nuestros mares.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Vasconcelos (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, colleagues, Europe controls the largest maritime area on Earth, yet we treat the ocean as a blind spot rather than our competitive advantage.

The Ocean Pact is an important first step to deliver change, and we must ensure that change means targeted action anchored in strategic investments and measurable results. The Ocean Pact correctly identifies six essential priorities: ocean health, blue economy, resilient coastal communities, technological innovation, maritime security and global leadership.

But priorities without execution are merely aspirations, and our continent's prosperity depends on the ocean as both a climate stabiliser and a backbone of global trade.脗聽Markets need clarity. Innovators need capital and citizens need proof that the EU can deliver results.

The pact's focus on implementation before more regulation is a step in the right direction, but we must see more clarity soon. What is the timeline for the blue carbon reserve? How will we mobilise private investment to complement EU funds? And EUR脗聽1 billion in EU funds will be allocated to ocean-related projects. Will the EU see the Ocean Pact as an opportunity to revise our funding policy and address the challenges our continent faces?

If we want progress, we must empower initiative and unlock innovation, because innovation is key to deliver on recovering oceans' health, economic competitiveness and security.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Nordqvist (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President,脗聽Commissioner, the Ocean Pact is and should be about international ocean governance and protection. And what better way to evaluate what the Pact can deliver than looking at the UN Conference on Oceans last week: a moratorium on deep-sea mining announced by President Costa, with growing numbers of countries supporting, and a helpful push once more to finally secure an ambitious plastic treaty, with 95 countries calling for a comprehensive binding treaty covering the full life cycle of plastics. This call includes a proposal to phase out harmful chemicals, and represents a critical move away from the downstream clean-up to actually an upstream control.

But what we also saw at UNOC, in the same way as in the proposed Ocean Pact, is a lot of declaratory quick fixes to measures or targets that should have been implemented years ago. Something as simple as forbidding one of the most obvious negative activities in our ocean 芒聙聯 bottom trawling 芒聙聯 yes, even on this, we don't see any meaningful action in marine target areas where bottom trawling is actually existing.

So, what we need now is actually shared accountability on the issues of ocean protection, and much less declarations.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paulo Do Nascimento Cabral (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, parab脙漏ns pelo que conseguiu.

O Pacto dos Oceanos marcar脙隆 uma nova era e reassume a lideran脙搂a da Uni脙拢o Europeia na prote脙搂脙拢o do oceano, numa abordagem de oceano 脙潞nico.

Os oceanos s脙拢o rela脙搂脙碌es transatl脙垄nticas. Unem-nos a outros continentes, povos e culturas. E, em tempos de tens脙拢o geopol脙颅tica, o foco no oceano que nos 脙漏 comum 脙漏 essencial para ultrapassar as diferen脙搂as, pelo que defendo uma nova estrat脙漏gia para o Atl脙垄ntico.

O oceano 脙漏 tamb脙漏m fonte de alimento e este Pacto coloca os pescadores no seu centro: pela sustentabilidade dos recursos, rendimentos justos, renova脙搂脙拢o das frotas, defini脙搂脙拢o plurianual das quotas e a considera脙搂脙拢o dos tr脙陋s pilares da sustentabilidade ambiental, social e econ脙鲁mica. Tendo em conta que ainda importamos 70 % do pescado consumido na Uni脙拢o Europeia, temos de combater a concorr脙陋ncia desleal de produtos de fora da nossa Uni脙拢o.

脙聣 fundamental para a nossa seguran脙搂a alimentar e para as nossas comunidades costeiras e pesca artesanal, t脙拢o caracter脙颅sticas da nossa cultura e das nossas costas.

Importa sublinhar tamb脙漏m a quest脙拢o das 脙隆reas marinhas protegidas que, ao contr脙隆rio do que alguns pretendem fazer passar, podem ser aliadas dos pescadores, aumentando a biomassa e os rendimentos. Contudo, na transi脙搂脙拢o, estes devem ser compensados por eventuais perdas.

Portugal 脙漏 um bom exemplo da lideran脙搂a europeia, tendo em conta que os A脙搂ores, a Madeira e agora o Banco de Gorringe a n脙颅vel nacional, estando a cinco anos na meta, j脙隆 alcan脙搂aram exatamente 20 % do que era pretendido. Isto deve ser reconhecido.脗聽Por isso, nas regi脙碌es ultraperif脙漏ricas 脙漏 tamb脙漏m crucial restabelecer o POSEI Pescas.

O oceano 脙漏 tamb脙漏m seguran脙搂a e defesa, pois temos de proteger as infraestruturas submarinas, desde logo os cabos submarinos que nos ligam a v脙隆rios continentes.

O oceano 脙漏 ci脙陋ncia, investiga脙搂脙拢o e observa脙搂脙拢o; 脙漏 fundamental coordenar e integrar a investiga脙搂脙拢o sobre o mar e o mar profundo, o que ser脙隆 feito no nosso Observat脙鲁rio Europeu do Mar Profundo na ilha do Faial, nos A脙搂ores.

Para terminar, apoio o Pacto apresentado pela Confer脙陋ncia das Na脙搂脙碌es Unidas sobre os Oceanos e sa脙潞do a proposta da Comiss脙拢o Europeia de investir mil milh脙碌es de EUR no setor...

(a Presidente retira a palavra ao orador)

(O orador aceita responder a uma pergunta 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Deputado Paulo Nascimento Cabral, este an脙潞ncio do Pacto dos Oceanos arrisca-se a acrescentar pouco ao que j脙隆 existe, para al脙漏m da habitual propaganda que est脙隆 associada a estes instrumentos.

Portanto, queria perguntar-lhe 芒聙聯芒聙聯 e at脙漏 partindo da situa脙搂脙拢o espec脙颅fica de Portugal 芒聙聯 que medidas em concreto podem resultar deste Pacto dos Oceanos? Relativamente 脙聽 necessidade de valoriza脙搂脙拢o da pesca de pequena escala costeira e artesanal, 脙聽 renova脙搂脙拢o das frotas e 脙聽 promo脙搂脙拢o da atividade da constru脙搂脙拢o e da repara脙搂脙拢o naval, que medidas concretas podem resultar deste Pacto, especificamente direcionadas para a realidade de Portugal?脗聽E, j脙隆 agora, com que verbas? Porque tamb脙漏m aqui faltam as verbas que, infelizmente, no Or脙搂amento da Uni脙拢o Europeia s脙拢o desviadas para o militarismo e para a guerra.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paulo Do Nascimento Cabral (PPE), Resposta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhor Deputado Jo脙拢o Oliveira, de facto, estive presente na Confer脙陋ncia das Na脙搂脙碌es Unidas em Nice, e foi anunciado pela pr脙鲁pria presidente da Comiss脙拢o Europeia um pacote de mil milh脙碌es de EUR para a Economia Azul.脗聽O foco na sua interven脙搂脙拢o foi centrado exatamente na pequena pesca 芒聙聯芒聙聯 pesca artesanal e pesca costeira 芒聙聯芒聙聯, que nos compete preservar, por ser uma heran脙搂a cultural e t脙拢o caracter脙颅stica das nossas costas.

Permita-me tamb脙漏m dar nota de que, de facto, o nosso pa脙颅s, Portugal, e em particular as regi脙碌es aut脙鲁nomas dos A脙搂ores e da Madeira t脙陋m sido essenciais na defesa dos pescadores, mas tamb脙漏m na cria脙搂脙拢o das 脙隆reas marinhas protegidas, que 脙漏 fundamental preservarmos hoje para termos recursos amanh脙拢. Muito obrigado pela sua quest脙拢o, Senhor Deputado.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonio Decaro (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, proteggere e ripristinare la salute degli oceani, rafforzare la competitivit脙聽 della脗聽blue economy, sostenere le comunit脙聽 costiere e insulari nella tutela delle coste: queste sono alcune delle priorit脙聽 del Patto europeo per gli oceani che sono state presentate a Nizza.

Incoraggeremo gli Stati membri a individuare e gestire aree marine protette, non a scapito dell'economia del mare, ma realizzando insieme una nuova economia del mare virtuosa e sostenibile. L'Europa pu脙虏 e deve dettare l'agenda sulle politiche ambientali delle acque, ma per farlo dobbiamo innanzitutto crederci noi.

Io vengo da una citt脙聽 del sud Italia, dove il mare 脙篓 vita, 脙篓 lavoro, 脙篓 cultura, 脙篓 identit脙聽, 脙篓 frontiera. Ma la mia citt脙聽 come anche le altre citt脙聽 costiere in tutta Europa non possono essere lasciate da sole davanti alle sfide che dobbiamo affrontare: dall'inquinamento alla protezione degli ecosistemi, alla fruizione pubblica, fino alla riorganizzazione delle economie marittime.

La commissione ENVI 脙篓 pronta a lavorare con grande determinazione su questo tema, certi che salvare il nostro mare significhi salvare la storia e il futuro dell'Europa che oggi viviamo e conosciamo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andr脙漏 Roug脙漏 (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, Monsieur le Commissaire, Nice vient d'accueillir la conf脙漏rence des Nations unies sur les oc脙漏ans. S'il faut saluer le r脙麓le de la France, grande puissance oc脙漏anique, d脙漏plorons cependant un manque de discernement sur les convoitises que suscitent les zones 脙漏conomiques exclusives (ZEE) fran脙搂aises.

Ces risques qui p脙篓sent sur nos ZEE, les deuxi脙篓mes plus vastes au monde, sont autant av脙漏r脙漏s dans les 脙庐les 脙漏parses que dans le canal du Mozambique, au large de Mayotte, mais aussi 脙聽 Clipperton ou dans le Pacifique sud. Toutes ces terres fran脙搂aises sont convoit脙漏es, menac脙漏es, intimid脙漏es par de grandes puissances 脙漏trang脙篓res, avec le risque de devoir peut-脙陋tre c脙漏der un jour une partie de leurs ZEE et de leurs ressources dans les fonds marins et sous-marins.

Sur cette question qui touche 脙聽 l'avenir de la France, l'ONU ne dit pas grand-chose, renfor脙搂ant l'inclination de la communaut脙漏 internationale 脙聽 bloquer l'exploitation mini脙篓re des fonds. En compl脙漏ment des Nations unies, l'Europe de la r脙漏gulation s'applique aussi d脙漏sormais aux fonds oc脙漏aniques. Le pacte pour l'Oc脙漏an porte la marque de ce joug supranational dont la France devra se d脙漏faire pour redevenir une puissance de premier rang capable d'exploiter respectueusement, mais librement, ses fonds marins...

(La Pr脙漏sidente retire la parole 脙聽 l芒聙聶orateur)

(L'orateur accepte une question carton bleu)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Christophe Clergeau (S&D), question 脗芦carton bleu脗禄. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le d脙漏put脙漏, vous parlez du risque de pillage des ressources. Vous parlez des zones qui rel脙篓vent de la France, mais vous ne parlez pas des grands fonds marins internationaux. Et la principale menace aujourd'hui, c'est la d脙漏cision prise par les 脙聣tats-Unis d'exploiter les fonds marins, de lancer l'exploitation mini脙篓re en eaux profondes au d脙漏triment de toutes les r脙篓gles internationales.

J'aimerais vous entendre sur cette question pour savoir si vous condamnez cette d脙漏cision am脙漏ricaine et si vous souhaitez le respect des accords et de la l脙漏gislation internationale en la mati脙篓re pour prot脙漏ger les oc脙漏ans.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andr脙漏 Roug脙漏 (PfE), r脙漏ponse carton bleu. 芒聙聯 Mon cher coll脙篓gue, comme vous le constatez, je suis d脙漏put脙漏 fran脙搂ais et ma premi脙篓re pr脙漏occupation concerne les eaux territoriales fran脙搂aises, qui sont l'objet de convoitises non pas des 脙聣tats-Unis, mais de la Chine et de bien d'autres grandes puissances.

Donc, ne soyons pas na脙炉fs, ces grandes puissances ont en t脙陋te d'exploiter les fonds marins et sous-marins afin de r脙漏cup脙漏rer des m脙漏taux rares, des nodules polym脙漏talliques, qui seront au XXI谩碌聣脗聽si脙篓cle ce que le p脙漏trole aura 脙漏t脙漏 au XX谩碌聣. Ne soyons pas na脙炉fs et regardons les choses avec lucidit脙漏, voyons clairement que les grandes puissances, les 脙聣tats-Unis, la Chine et la Russie, toutes les grandes puissances ont en t脙陋te l'exploitation des terres rares des fonds marins et sous-marins.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 C'脙篓 un'altra blue card, ma possiamo accettarne soltanto una. Quindi chiedo scusa all'onorevole Gerbrandy, ma non possiamo accettare la sua richiesta.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michal Wiezik (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, I would like to speak about deep-sea mining. In deep-sea mining, a precautionary pause is a conscious and responsible position over an issue which potentially involves impacts on a million-year timescale.

We don't know, at this point, what we could lose by greedily looking at resources located in areas which belong to no one (and hence are common mankind heritage), what consequences our decisions to extract those resources might have, or what processes we could disrupt. For this ecosystem it took millions of years to evolve, yet they can be destroyed in a blink of an eye. For certain forms of deep-sea mining, the negative effect would be forever.

I therefore applaud the Commission calling for a precautionary pause until sufficient scientific evidence is available to confirm that deep-sea mining poses no substantive threat to marine ecosystems. From this place, as a Member of the European Parliament and as a scientist, I also call on the Council to confirm the approach of a precautionary pause.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽M脙漏lissa Camara (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, Monsieur le Commissaire, sans oc脙漏ans en bonne sant脙漏, l'humanit脙漏 est en p脙漏ril. L'annonce par la Commission europ脙漏enne d'un pacte pour les oc脙漏ans est un signal positif. Mais il y a urgence. Oui, les oc脙漏ans suffoquent: plastique, pollution, surp脙陋che, disparition de la p脙陋che artisanale, l'heure n'est plus aux promesses mais 脙聽 l'action concr脙篓te. Aujourd'hui, ce pacte est une d脙漏claration d'intention sans calendrier clair ni implication des premiers concern脙漏s, notamment les p脙陋cheurs artisanaux. Comme pour le pacte vert, il n'aura de sens que s'il d脙漏bouche sur des lois contraignantes, ambitieuses et justes. L'urgence climatique et l'effondrement de la biodiversit脙漏 l'exigent.

Il est inacceptable aujourd'hui que 86脗聽% des aires marines prot脙漏g脙漏es en Europe subissent encore des activit脙漏s industrielles. Prot脙漏ger signifie interdire la surexploitation et non poser une 脙漏tiquette verte sur des zones ouvertes 脙聽 l'extraction ou 脙聽 la p脙陋che destructrices. Alors que la droite et l'extr脙陋me droite d脙漏tricotent les acquis du pacte vert, mena脙搂ant ainsi notre avenir collectif, il faut faire preuve de courage politique et de coh脙漏rence. Nous jugerons donc ce pacte sur pi脙篓ce et nous nous mobiliserons pour qu'il serve la biodiversit脙漏, les territoires et les p脙陋cheurs artisanaux, pas les int脙漏r脙陋ts industriels. L'Europe doit prouver qu'elle est capable d'agir pour les oc脙漏ans et donc pour notre avenir et celui de nos enfants.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Martins (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, h脙隆 duas formas de irresponsabilidade: a dos negacionistas clim脙隆ticos 芒聙聯芒聙聯 que aqui s脙拢o representados pela extrema-direita 芒聙聯芒聙聯, mas tamb脙漏m a das boas inten脙搂脙碌es, sem concretiza脙搂脙拢o, que a direita veio hoje aqui saudar.

O Pacto dos Oceanos sofre desta irresponsabilidade por inconsequ脙陋ncia. Na altura de mudar alguma coisa, no que 脙漏 mais urgente, n脙拢o h脙隆 metas concretas e vinculativas, nem mesmo investimento suficiente. S脙鲁 boas inten脙搂脙碌es.

O oceano 脙漏 a nossa 脙潞ltima barreira de prote脙搂脙拢o clim脙隆tica e o risco 脙漏 tremendo. Transformar as pescas, apoiar os pescadores, acabar com o arrasto industrial e travar o f脙鲁ssil e a praga do pl脙隆stico nos oceanos tem de ser vinculativo, a par do aumento das 脙隆reas protegidas e da proibi脙搂脙拢o da minera脙搂脙拢o em mar profundo.

No caminho para a COP 30, um Pacto Europeu que foge a metas concretas 脙漏 o sinal errado.

Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, espero que ainda o possamos corrigir.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脜陆eljana Zovko (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, povjereni脛聧e Kadis,脗聽europski pakt o oceanima prepoznao je na脜隆e dugogodi脜隆nje zahtjeve da se 脛聧uje glas onih koji su stolje脛聡ima 脜戮ivjeli u skladu s morem i od mora, ba脜隆 poput hrvatskih ribara.

S gotovo 70 tisu脛聡a kilometara obale i 40 posto stanovni脜隆tva koje 脜戮ivi unutar 50 kilometara od mora, pomorska pitanja su strate脜隆ka pitanja za Europsku uniju. Plava ekonomija zapo脜隆ljava pet milijuna ljudi i godi脜隆nje doprinosi vi脜隆e od 250 milijardi na脜隆im gospodarstvima. Pozdravljam 脜隆to europski pakt o oceanima po prvi put izrijekom isti脛聧e podr脜隆ku malom obalnom ribarstvu kao prioritet. U samom Sredozemlju, hrvatski ribari drugi su po te脜戮ini iskrcaja, 脜隆to nam govori koliko je ovaj pakt bitan za Republiku Hrvatsku.

Generacijska obnova, dostupno financiranje i privla脛聧nost ovog zvanja moraju biti okosnica budu脛聡e uredbe o oceanima. Oceanska diplomacija na kojoj radim kao izvjestiteljica bit 脛聡e alat za ja脛聧u za脜隆titu na脜隆ih interesa. Dok su se na脜隆i mali ribari gu脜隆ili u regulativama riba iz tre脛聡ih zemalja je zavr脜隆avala na europskim stolovima. Sna脜戮nom oceanskom diplomacijom ribarima 脛聡emo osigurati ravnopravne uvjete s tre脛聡im zemljama i za脜隆tititi ocean kao izvor 脜戮ivota i suverenosti hrane.

Zdravi oceani i sna脜戮no lokalno i tradicionalno ribarstvo temelj su na脜隆eg opstanka, sigurnosti a i na脛聧ina 脜戮ivljenja.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sofie Eriksson (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, thank you. I am here with a plea from the Baltic Sea, one of the most overfished seas in the world. Herring stocks have declined by over 80脗聽% since the 1990s. Almost no sprat goes to human consumption, but to pigs, chickens and salmon. Cod stocks have already collapsed. Industrial fishing is emptying our sea, mixed species trawling is halting recovery and underreporting is undermining the credibility of our fisheries data.

The Ocean Pact is a welcome step. We do need binding legislation 芒聙聯 it is crucial for our small-scale fisheries, for our recreational fisheries, for our local coastal communities. You simply cannot fish in a dead sea. We have to move from words to action now. Ban the industrial trawling in the Baltic Sea. Set recovery quotas based on science, not short-term profit. I plead to you, my colleagues, and to the Commission. The Baltic Sea cannot wait any longer!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽France Jamet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, ce pacte pour les oc脙漏ans s'attaque directement 脙聽 notre fili脙篓re de p脙陋che europ脙漏enne que la Commission a d脙漏cid脙漏 de sacrifier aux int脙漏r脙陋ts du libre-脙漏change et de la mondialisation. Premi脙篓rement, elle tente de s'arroger un pouvoir sur toutes les eaux territoriales des 脙聣tats membres. Deuxi脙篓mement, aucune mesure concr脙篓te n'est propos脙漏e contre la p脙陋che ill脙漏gale ni contre la privatisation de nos espaces maritimes par les 脙漏oliennes. Troisi脙篓mement, elle s'appuie enfin sur des ONG qui se livrent 脙聽 une v脙漏ritable campagne de d脙漏sinformation 脙聽 l'脙漏gard de nos p脙陋cheurs artisans qui ne sont pas le probl脙篓me mais le d脙漏but de la solution.

Enfin, la d脙漏fense et la pr脙漏servation de nos oc脙漏ans passent par la coop脙漏ration entre nations libres et souveraines. 脙聙 Nice, lors de la conf脙漏rence des Nations unies sur l'oc脙漏an, un ambassadeur oc脙漏anien nous a interpell脙漏, vous a interpell脙漏 ainsi Monsieur Kadis: 脗芦Vous nous dites sauvez les oc脙漏ans, nous vous disons respectez-nous et sauvez notre mode de vie et notre identit脙漏.脗禄

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, het enorme belang van de oceanen voor zowel de mens als de natuur is intussen wel duidelijk, dankzij de wetenschap, en nu ook voor het grote publiek dankzij de prachtige film Ocean van David Attenborough.

Maar ondanks dat waanzinnig grote belang gaat er toch heel erg veel mis. Neem de Oostzee, waar we dode zones hebben, waar door enorme hoeveelheden pesticiden, Russische chemicali脙芦n 芒聙聰 noem maar op 芒聙聰 hele gebieden zijn waar helemaal niks meer leeft. Sleepnetvisserij, die de bodem sloopt 芒聙聰 de kraamkamers van de zee. In Nederland is zelfs in 70脗聽% van de beschermde gebieden nog sleepnetvisserij toegestaan.脗聽Ik ben blij dat de Europese Commissie de diepzeemijnbouw op pauze wil zetten. Daar ben ik een groot voorstander van en ik hoop dat we dat internationaal door kunnen zetten.

En tot slot: we moeten aan de slag met z'n allen. De natuur heeft ons een heel mooi cadeau gegeven, namelijk het herstelvermogen. Nergens is het herstelvermogen zo groot als in de oceanen, dus als we handelen, zien we ogenblikkelijk effect.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Everding (The Left). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Wieder einmal f脙陇rbte sich eine Bucht auf den F脙陇r脙露er-Inseln in den letzten Tagen blutrot. Mindestens 245脗聽Grindwale wurden auf brutalste Art und Weise abgeschlachtet, darunter 25脗聽tr脙陇chtige Weibchen und 13脗聽Jungtiere脗聽芒聙聯 und das, wo Wale eine zentrale Rolle im 脙露kologischen Gleichgewicht unserer Meere spielen. Im Ocean Pact fehlt hingegen jede Erw脙陇hnung des Tierschutzes. Es fehlen auch konkrete Ma脙聼nahmen f脙录r die Meeresschutzgebiete sowie jegliche Bestrebungen, Offshore-脙聳l- und -Gasf脙露rderung angesichts der Klimakrise st脙陇rker einzustellen.

Ein begr脙录脙聼enswerter Ansatz ist jedoch, den Auswirkungen von Unterwasserl脙陇rm auf die biologische Vielfalt der Meere Priorit脙陇t einzur脙陇umen, denn die L脙陇rmbelastung unter Wasser muss dringend angegangen werden. Diese Lichtblicke k脙露nnen jedoch nicht dar脙录ber hinwegt脙陇uschen, dass der Pakt in seiner Gesamtheit eher entt脙陇uschend ist. Keine neuen, rechtlich verbindlichen Ziele, kein konkreter Zeitplan f脙录r den Schutz von 30脗聽% unserer Meeresfl脙陇chen bis 2030, kein Verbot sch脙陇dlicher Aktivit脙陇ten in Schutzgebieten. Stattdessen vage Versprechungen und mit dem Ocean Act ein Meeresgesetz in zwei Jahren. Insgesamt zu wenig, zu unambitioniert, zu sp脙陇t und zu unkonkret.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francisco Jos脙漏 Mill脙隆n Mon (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario脗聽Kadis, yo celebro mucho que la pesca se encuentre en el centro del Pacto Europeo por el Oc脙漏ano, enmarcado en la econom脙颅a azul, y tambi脙漏n celebro que una de sus prioridades sea impulsar la competitividad de esa econom脙颅a azul. La pesca y la acuicultura son sectores estrat脙漏gicos para nuestra seguridad alimentaria y necesitamos, por tanto, que sean competitivos. Tambi脙漏n debe serlo la industria transformadora y conservera, tan importante en muchas regiones como la m脙颅a, Galicia, y en muchas comunidades costeras.

Me centrar脙漏 en algunos puntos concretos. En primer lugar, celebro que se dedique un cap脙颅tulo a la diplomacia internacional. Necesitamos una diplomacia activa en los foros internacionales y en las organizaciones regionales de ordenaci脙鲁n pesquera (OROP) para alcanzar las condiciones de competencia equitativas tan ansiadas por la pesca europea. En segundo lugar, la financiaci脙鲁n europea es esencial para la pesca y acuicultura, y confieso que me habr脙颅a gustado una menci脙鲁n espec脙颅fica al FEMPA posterior a 2027 en el Pacto. Necesitamos, en tercer lugar, un equilibrio entre la protecci脙鲁n de los oc脙漏anos y la competitividad de los pescadores. Por eso, celebro que no se demonice ning脙潞n arte pesquera y que la gesti脙鲁n en las zonas marinas protegidas se decida caso por caso. En este sentido, creo, comisario, que debemos explorar las llamadas 脗芦otras medidas de conservaci脙鲁n eficaces basadas en 脙隆reas脗禄.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thomas Bajada (S&D). 芒聙聯 Sinjura President, g脛搂alina Maltin, il-ba脛搂ar mhux biss xi sfond g脛搂ar-ritratti, huwa darna, huwa 脛搂ajjitna, jag脛搂tina l-arja li nie脛搂du n-nifs minnha, jitmag脛搂na u jsawwar l-identit脙聽 tag脛搂na.

Imma llum il-komunitajiet kostali huma impattati mill-fatt li huma mbeg脛搂din u maqtug脛搂in, minn tni脛隆脛隆is, mil-livell tal-ba脛搂ar li qed jog脛搂la, minn maltempati aktar qawwija u minn 脛搂ut li qed jisparixxi, u g脛搂alhekk li l-Patt tal-O脛聥ean g脛搂andu jipprote脛隆i n-natura kif ukoll lil niesu. Il-komunitajiet kostali u s-sajjieda tieg脛搂u g脛搂andhom ikunu parti mis-soluzzjoni. Mhux biss ix-xenarju.

Dear Commissioner, we are waiting and still waiting for action to tackle pollution from source to sea, to safeguard our ocean. For those who depend on it, let this pact be a promise of a comprehensive Ocean Act. Not a patchwork of intentions, but a bold and binding blueprint to protect our ocean, to stop overexploitation and to deliver for nature, for fishers, for our islands and coasts, and for generations to come.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), mistoqsija karta blu. 芒聙聯 Grazzi 脛搂afna Sinjur kollega, int b脛搂ala wild ta' g脜录ira, imwieled imdawwar bil-ba脛搂ar. Nixtieq nistaqsi: x'nistg脛搂u nag脛搂mlu aktar, lil hinn mill-proposti attwali?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thomas Bajada (S&D), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Thank you for the attempt to speak in my language. I think we need to look at the ocean as one. It is one ocean: what happens from one side of the ocean will affect the other. It will affect communities. It will affect people. It will affect our livelihood.

Therefore, we need to look at it not only as a European Union; we need to look beyond. We need to work with different countries, with different regions in order to see that we all together protect the ocean that is there for our own livelihood, and for generations to come, but also to bring communities together and to protect our livelihood.

And therefore we need action. Commissioner, I beg you on pollution and seeing that we can prevent pollution from its source.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Yvan Verougstraete (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, la plan脙篓te m脙篓re est en danger. C'est une r脙漏alit脙漏. Les oc脙漏ans sont essentiels au bon fonctionnement de la plan脙篓te. Ils fournissent la moiti脙漏 de l'oxyg脙篓ne que nous respirons. Ils absorbent un quart du CO2 de l'atmosph脙篓re. Trois milliards de personnes dans le monde en d脙漏pendent. Et pourtant, ils suffoquent. Ou plut脙麓t, nous les faisons suffoquer.

Il est donc urgent, vital m脙陋me, de changer de cap. Le Pacte europ脙漏en pour les oc脙漏ans doit 脙陋tre une r脙漏ponse politique 脙聽 la hauteur du d脙漏fi climatique, 脙漏conomique et g脙漏opolitique qui se joue en mer. Il doit 脙陋tre aussi la base de nos relations multilat脙漏rales. Prot脙漏ger les 脙漏cosyst脙篓mes et la biodiversit脙漏, d脙漏velopper les 脙漏nergies renouvelables marines durables est notre responsabilit脙漏 commune et nous ne pouvons pas accepter que certains y renoncent ou en tirent avantage, fussent-ils am脙漏ricains ou autres. Renoncer, c'est nous agresser.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Luke Ming Flanagan (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madam President, the best way to protect our oceans is to support small-scale fishers over super-trawlers, and the best way to protect local fishing communities is to support small-scale fishers over super-trawlers. Is it happening? Not at the moment, no.

In Ireland, 87.5 % of all fishers operate out of boats of 12 metres and less, yet they have access to less than 1 % of herring stocks and 4 % of mackerel stocks. When these fishers tried to organise into POs, they were delayed by a regime in Ireland that would prefer if they didn't exist. Now they have been recognised as POs, it is made as difficult as possible for them to access funding. This needs to change if we are serious about an ocean pact and protecting our oceans.

Ireland doesn't need permission to do this, however. Article 17 allows for it and encourages it. The EMFAF regulation doesn't preclude funding for POs. So, people might be tempted to blame Europe for the problems of small-scale fisheries in Ireland. That's not the fact. The fact is, it's our own fault and that needs to change.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sander Smit (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, niet alleen de oceaan bevindt zich op een kruispunt: dat geldt ook voor onze visserij. Het oceaanpact moet visserij en aquacultuur erkennen als hoekstenen van de blauwe economie. Deze sectoren zijn van essentieel belang voor voedselzekerheid, voedselsoevereiniteit en de veerkracht van de Europese Unie.

We moeten ervoor zorgen dat visserij niet verder wordt verdrongen door offshorewindenergie. Eerlijke maritieme ruimtelijke ordening is cruciaal om toegang tot traditionele visgronden te behouden. Voldoende financiering voor innovatie en modernisering van schepen is essentieel voor energie芒聙聭effici脙芦nte, emissiearme vaartuigen en de duurzame overgang naar AI芒聙聭ondersteund en selectief vistuig op een technologieneutrale wijze. Dat wil ik de collega芒聙聶s meegeven. Ook voor moderne schepen van gezinsbedrijven van meer dan 24脗聽meter.

Goede woorden alleen volstaan niet. We hebben concrete actie nodig, zodat onze vissers overleven in onze kustregio芒聙聶s, waarvan ze het kloppend sociaal en maatschappelijk hart vormen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Gonz脙隆lez Casares (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, la semana pasada, miles de lisboetas saboreaban deliciosas sardinas en las fiestas de san Antonio en Alfama. Y en los pr脙鲁ximos d脙颅as, en las villas y ciudades de Galicia, haremos lo mismo. 脗驴Por qu脙漏 en Galicia o en Portugal comemos sardinas en junio? Porque sabemos que es la mejor temporada para aprovechar sus saludables nutrientes; es decir, tenemos una cultura del mar, sabemos lo que vale un oc脙漏ano 芒聙聰el Atl脙隆ntico芒聙聰 y queremos que esta simbiosis entre nuestra cultura, sociedad y econom脙颅a se mantenga.

Bien, se脙卤or comisario, por el Pacto Europeo por el Oc脙漏ano. Bien por el 脙漏xito en Niza. Desde nuestro Grupo, saludamos la iniciativa, pero nos preguntamos si este es el pacto que Europa necesita. Tengo dudas, porque la ret脙鲁rica hacia el sector pesquero sigue siendo negativa, porque los compromisos con las comunidades costeras siguen siendo gen脙漏ricos.

Es necesario defender los oc脙漏anos, protegerlos; velar por su aprovechamiento sostenible es imprescindible, en equilibrio con las actividades preexistentes, pero estaremos vigilantes porque este Pacto nace con una debilidad estructural. Una comunicaci脙鲁n sin presupuesto se puede quedar en un mensaje vac脙颅o.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Billy Kelleher (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner Kadis, I would like to thank you for visiting Ireland recently, and meeting with stakeholders and particularly fishers in the west of Ireland and Castletownbere. I appreciate it and I know they appreciate the engagement as well.

At the outset, the lawless exploitation and abuse of our oceans has to stop, and I hope that the European Ocean Pact is a policy that will bring about that change in mindset. From my perspective, when we talk about sustainable fishing, we have to address a very fundamental issue, Commissioner, and that is the fact that super-trawlers across the planet are industrial-scale fishing and exploiting stocks beyond sustainable capacities.

So I believe that, if we are to have a review of the common fisheries policies, we have to look at the fact that we are overfishing in many areas. And that has predominantly been done by super-trawlers, as opposed to the smaller trawlers and coastal community fishers that are taking sustainable stocks from our seas.

So any re-evaluation and review of the common fisheries policy must look at sustainable coastal communities, supporting our fishers, but certainly addressing the issue of the massive exploitation of fishing stocks across the globe and in European waters by super-trawlers.

脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Salvatore De Meo (PPE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, per troppo tempo abbiamo focalizzato la nostra attenzione alla terraferma o tutt'al pi脙鹿 alle fasce costiere, senza renderci conto di un orizzonte pi脙鹿 ampio verso il quale oggi stiamo guardando con la consapevolezza che i nostri mari sono un patrimonio comune, vitale per l'equilibrio climatico, ma anche per la nostra sicurezza alimentare ed energetica, indispensabile per rafforzare la nostra autonomia strategica.

In Europa, l'economia blu d脙聽 lavoro a circa quattro milioni e mezzo di persone e genera quasi 650 miliardi di euro di valore aggiunto. Tuttavia, gli oceani sono sotto pressione non solo a causa dell'inquinamento e dei cambiamenti climatici, ma anche per l'assenza di un quadro regolatorio certo che ne possa proteggere e valorizzare le loro potenzialit脙聽.

Il Patto per gli oceani rappresenta una risposta politica ambiziosa e necessaria per coinvolgere istituzioni, imprese, comunit脙聽 costiere e cittadini. In uno scenario globale sempre pi脙鹿 complesso e delicato, il Patto per gli oceani non 脙篓 solo una visione, ma una roadmap concreta per un futuro pi脙鹿 blu, pi脙鹿 sicuro e pi脙鹿 sostenibile, con cui contribuire ad avere un'Europa pi脙鹿 forte, resiliente e autonoma.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Lupo (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, porto qui la voce dei pescatori che ho incontrato in queste settimane, che condividono che il Patto per gli oceani rappresenta un'opportunit脙聽 per rafforzare l'azione europea a tutela degli ecosistemi marini e per una economia blu sostenibile e socialmente giusta.

Sono d'accordo con le proposte del Commissario Kadis, ma ritengo essenziale che la transizione blu tenga conto delle diverse realt脙聽 territoriali e delle sfide che vivono molte comunit脙聽 costiere, in particolare nel Mediterraneo.

Signor Commissario, condivido con Lei che 脙篓 necessaria una nuova strategia per le isole. Penso alle grandi isole del Mediterraneo, come la Sicilia e la Sardegna, e penso alle nostre tante isole minori, contesti che contribuiscono in modo decisivo al settore pesca, che necessitano oggi di strumenti adeguati per affrontare un momento di grave crisi.

脙聢 quindi fondamentale rafforzare il FEAMP per la pesca per garantire condizioni migliori di lavoro ai pescatori. Continuiamo a lavorare insieme per una politica della pesca europea pi脙鹿 equa, pi脙鹿 attenta al lavoro dei pescatori e dell'intera filiera, pi脙鹿 attenta alle comunit脙聽 costiere e alle isole.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽C脙漏sar Luena (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, el Pacto Europeo por el Oc脙漏ano es un paso positivo, pero, mire, le quiero recordar que el Acuerdo relativo a la conservaci脙鲁n y el uso sostenible de la diversidad biol脙鲁gica marina de las zonas situadas fuera de la jurisdicci脙鲁n nacional no lo hemos firmado, y otros Estados miembros tampoco.

Le pido que la Ley del Oc脙漏ano tenga objetivos vinculantes y un calendario claro, porque tenemos que frenar la pesca de arrastre y la sobreexplotaci脙鲁n; si no, ser脙隆n bonitas palabras, pero no hechos. El Tratado Mundial sobre los Pl脙隆sticos: lo estamos esperando.

Hay que mantener y reforzar la moratoria sobre la miner脙颅a en los fondos marinos, porque solo 37脗聽pa脙颅ses la respaldan oficialmente.

Y, por 脙潞ltimo, le pido que aumentemos los recursos para el conocimiento cient脙颅fico. F脙颅jese: solo el 2,7脗聽% de los oc脙漏anos mundiales est脙隆 protegido, muy lejos del 30脗聽% que tenemos para las 脙隆reas terrestres.

As脙颅 que, s脙颅, est脙隆 bien, es un paso positivo, hemos avanzado, pero nos queda mucho para alcanzar las metas 脙鲁ptimas.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Idoia Mendia (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, no valen las promesas vagas, porque es el porvenir de las generaciones futuras de nuestros hijos e hijas lo que tenemos en juego. El Pacto es un paso en la buena direcci脙鲁n para afrontar las amenazas, pero tiene que contener medidas m脙隆s concretas para los pescadores. Es importante que el Pacto ponga en el centro a los pescadores de peque脙卤a escala, abordando sus retos reales, que no son otros que el relevo generacional, la capacidad pesquera y la sobrepesca ejercida por terceros pa脙颅ses.

Se trata de proteger, fomentar y dotar de medios suficientes a los pescadores, para mejorar la competitividad del sector, apostando por la actividad pesquera, aumentando la producci脙鲁n sostenible de productos pesqueros, a la vez que se protegen los oc脙漏anos y se apoya el desarrollo econ脙鲁mico de nuestras comunidades costeras y su forma tradicional de vida. Para los socialistas es una oportunidad, pero solo si lo dotamos de medidas concretas y con financiaci脙鲁n suficiente.

Este tema no deber脙颅a tener ideolog脙颅as porque es una cuesti脙鲁n de supervivencia: es el porvenir de Europa lo que est脙隆 en juego.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Procedura "catch-the-eye"

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ingeborg Ter Laak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, onze oceaan is de blauwe long van onze planeet. Oceanen geven ons meer dan 50脗聽% van onze zuurstof en nemen een kwart van onze CO2 op. Deze oceanen dragen bij aan biodiversiteit, voedsel- en energiezekerheid 脙漏n het klimaat.

Het oceaanpact moet meer zijn dan mooie woorden. De zee geeft ons zo veel, het is alleen maar terecht dat we daar verantwoordelijk mee omgaan. Minder vervuiling, minder illegale activiteiten en meer ruimte voor innovatie voor een goed gebruik van deze oceaan. Geen overregulering die lokale ondernemers klem zet, maar ook geen vrijbrief om te vervuilen. Daarom zijn internationale afspraken over hoe we omgaan met de industrialisatie van de oceaan van cruciaal belang. Denk aan windparken, datacenters, maar ook diepzeemijnbouw.

Europa kan en moet hierin het voortouw nemen, met duidelijke regels, fatsoenlijke wetenschappelijke onderbouwing en internationale samenwerking. Want een gezonde oceaan is van levensbelang.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam President, over 400 poor condition vessels sailing with a heavy oil load, day after day. This is called the Russian 'shadow fleet'. It is a fleet that poses two types of massive security threats. But now it should be crystal clear for all of us that this fleet is funding Putin's expansionist policy and war crimes in Europe. But now, as we discuss the European Ocean Pact, we should not forget the huge environmental threat that this bloody fleet poses constantly for our oceans.

The European Ocean Pact is built around pillars such as protecting ocean health and enhancing maritime security and defence 芒聙聯 good goals. I think the biggest deed we could now do for our oceans would be doing everything we can to get these Russian 'shadow fleet' vessels out of our seas.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio Kadis, o Pacto dos Oceanos 脙漏 mais uma estrat脙漏gia da UE que pouco acrescenta ao que j脙隆 existe para l脙隆 da habitual propaganda neste tipo de instrumentos.

Sem surpresas, o oceano continuar脙隆 a ser um dep脙鲁sito do lixo da competitividade, dos grandes interesses econ脙鲁micos, da liberaliza脙搂脙拢o dos mercados, com o destacado e perverso exemplo do mercado do carbono.

A preserva脙搂脙拢o dos oceanos exige a considera脙搂脙拢o dos seus recursos na rela脙搂脙拢o equilibrada entre a preserva脙搂脙拢o ambiental e a satisfa脙搂脙拢o de necessidades sociais, retirando o lucro da equa脙搂脙拢o. Exige o respeito pelo caminho soberano do desenvolvimento que melhor sirva cada povo. Exige verbas que hoje n脙拢o existem para alavancar esse caminho de desenvolvimento, porque as op脙搂脙碌es da UE se orientam para a guerra e as bombas e n脙拢o para servir os povos. Exige, no caso de Portugal, a valoriza脙搂脙拢o de setores tradicionais, al脙漏m do desenvolvimento de setores emergentes.

Valorizar a pesca de pequena escala costeira e artesanal, renovar as frotas, dignificar as profiss脙碌es, aumentar os rendimentos da pesca na cadeia de valor, defender e promover a constru脙搂脙拢o e repara脙搂脙拢o naval, retomar o controlo p脙潞blico dos portos 芒聙聯芒聙聯 nada disto 脙漏 considerado neste Pacto, que n脙拢o defende nem os oceanos nem os interesses dos povos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Costas Kadis, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, I would like to thank you all for this constructive debate.

Our collective acknowledgement of the Ocean Pact's importance demonstrates a shared commitment to preserving the health of our oceans, for our coastal communities, and for our citizens at large. I have taken good note of your various comments and questions, and I would like to briefly reply to some of the key issues raised today.

Many of you have inquired about the Ocean Act. We will deliver the Ocean Act as soon as possible. We have to follow the required procedures. And of course, we will broadly consult all relevant stakeholders, including primarily this House, the European Parliament.

The Ocean Act will be a legislative instrument which will build on the Maritime Spatial Planning Directive, a directive which, by its nature, coordinates different ocean related policies with the aim to revise and strengthen it, broaden its scope and to make it a more effective tool for implementing ocean芒聙聭related policies and clear targets stemming from ambitious existing sectoral EU legislation and in line with our better regulation principles. This will also be aimed at decreasing administrative burden and making things more flexible.

As regards the evaluation of the CFP Regulation and further measures to support the EU fisheries sector, we are currently looking at the current CFP Regulation in all its aspects, how it is functioning, and the evaluation will provide a good basis to make informed decisions on how we address any weaknesses of the Common Fisheries Policy Regulation that will be pointed out, and to develop our long芒聙聭term vision for a resilient, competitive and sustainable fisheries and aquaculture sector with a 2040 perspective.

On the issue of marine protected areas. I can only recall that the Ocean Pact follows the precautionary principle and takes a source to sea, a science芒聙聭based and an ecosystem芒聙聭based approach. The Commission will support Member States to accelerate their efforts to meet the EU biodiversity 2030 target of legally protecting 30脗聽% and strictly protecting 10脗聽% of their seas.

And I was very pleased to see several Member States, presidents or prime ministers in Nice to announce ambitious decisions in this direction during the United Nations Ocean Conference. So the Commission will work on the effective implementation and enforcement of existing EU legislation, notably the Birds and Habitats Directives, the Marine Strategy Framework Directive and the Nature Restoration Regulation.

As regards bottom trawling, we are not in favour of the one芒聙聭size芒聙聭fits芒聙聭all approach. We need to assess each marine protected area based on sound scientific evidence, taking into consideration its objectives. Efficient management measures should be in place based on protected area芒聙聭specific management plans. This by definition means a case芒聙聭by芒聙聭case analysis.

At the same time, we need to invest in innovation to make our fishing gears more selective and less harmful to the marine environment. We need to be able to find alternatives that allow us the best use of our resources, without losing sight of social and economic costs of our fishers.

On the funding necessary to reach the objectives of the pact, President von der Leyen was very clear in her speech in Nice. However, it is evident that public funding will not be enough. Therefore, strong synergies between the different EU financing instruments are needed to support ocean innovation and to help getting new technologies and solutions to market and to scale them up. We need to optimise the use of the EU budget to crowd in more private investment, EU financing from structural and cohesion funds as well as Horizon Invest EU, Blue Invest Platform, and the EIB loans can contribute to the achievements of the European Ocean Pact objectives.

Honourable Members of the European Parliament, the Ocean Pact is not only a roadmap. It is a call to action and responsibility, and the Commission looks forward to continuing the close cooperation with this House to deliver concrete results.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 La discussione 脙篓 chiusa.

La seduta 脙篓 sospesa e riprender脙聽 tra alcuni minuti con l'allocuzione di Sua Maest脙聽 Re Abdullah II, sovrano del Regno Hashemita di Giordania.

Grazie a tutte e a tutti.

Dichiarazioni scritte (articolo 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alicia Homs Ginel (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 Acogemos positivamente que la Comisi脙鲁n Europea haya adoptado el Pacto Europeo de los Oc脙漏anos, una iniciativa que debe dar coherencia y ambici脙鲁n a la acci脙鲁n europea en esta materia, tanto por parte de la Comisi脙鲁n como por la de los Estados miembros, y ayudar a la prosperidad de las comunidades costeras, la competitividad de los sectores ligados a la econom脙颅a azul 芒聙聰con especial atenci脙鲁n a la pesca y la acuicultura sostenibles芒聙聰 y la protecci脙鲁n de nuestros mares y oc脙漏anos.

Desde nuestro Grupo, apoyamos especialmente el anuncio de la futura Ley del Oc脙漏ano, una iniciativa reclamada por el S&D y que debe ir mucho m脙隆s all脙隆 de la mera revisi脙鲁n de la Directiva por la que se establece un marco para la ordenaci脙鲁n del espacio mar脙颅timo, contando con objetivos vinculantes y calendarios claros.

Son muchas las oportunidades que brindan nuestros mares: energ脙漏ticas, de transporte, bioeconom脙颅a芒聙娄 Pero es imprescindible que el desarrollo sostenible de estas se haga sin menoscabo de las actividades preexistentes, en di脙隆logo y cooperaci脙鲁n con las comunidades y sectores afectados.

Con todo, vemos debilidades en el Pacto presentado: la ret脙鲁rica hacia el sector pesquero sigue siendo negativa; los compromisos con las comunidades costeras, gen脙漏ricos; la financiaci脙鲁n, inconcreta. Y sin un presupuesto a la altura, el Pacto corre el riesgo de quedarse en un ejercicio m脙隆s de palabras vac脙颅as. Estaremos vigilantes.

IN THE CHAIR: ROBERTA METSOLA
President

脗聽

4. Resumption of the sitting
Video of the speeches
脗聽脗聽

(The sitting resumed at 12:06)

脗聽

5. Formal sitting 芒聙聯 Address by His Majesty King Abdullah II, King of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Good afternoon to you all, dear colleagues. It is an honour to welcome His Majesty King Abdullah II of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan back to the European Parliament. Your Majesty, this is not the first time that you address this House, and each time your inspiring words have left a lasting impact on all who listened. We are grateful that every time that it feels that the world is at an inflection point, leaders like Your Majesty are able to come here and outline a way forward for our common humanity.

Ten years ago, in the midst of the horror inflicted by Daesh, I was here in the Chamber when you said: 'Our world force faces an assault by terrorists with ruthless ambition. The motive is not faith, it is power; power pursued by ripping countries and communities apart in sectarian conflicts, and inflicting suffering across the world.' Your words echoed through this House and beyond, and the urgency of our shared responsibility against the threat of extremism has only since grown.

Five years ago, as the globe faced further crises, you were here to remind us all that a life spent in the service of others is a life fully lived, but only if we meet the expectations of the people we represent. Words that mattered then to us all and that ring true still today.

Your Majesty, on behalf of this House, I want to commend you and your great country on its commitment to stability and peace in the Middle East. We appreciate Jordan's critical efforts in reducing regional tensions; in pushing for a ceasefire in Gaza; in supporting the West Bank; in all you have been you have done for the sick; in the need for the return of hostages; for facilitating so much urgently needed humanitarian aid to those who so desperately need it; and for your unwavering support for Palestinian and Syrian refugees and a two-state solution as a path to lasting peace.

The G7 has reiterated this week that Iran should not have a nuclear bomb. And with what we have witnessed between Israel and Iran in recent days and its potential spillover effects, Jordan's role has become even more vital. The challenges call for stronger ties and a renewed relationship between Europe and the Middle East as we push for broad de-escalation.

So, Your Majesty, Jordan is not only a great friend to this Parliament, but an important partner for the European Union. We welcome the EU-Jordan Comprehensive and Strategic Partnership that was signed earlier this year. It represents an important political milestone in our bilateral relations, and reflects our shared commitment towards regional stability and economic cooperation.

In this Parliament, we look forward to further building on it, on values, on market access, on trade. Because while our people may come from different countries, as you see before you, they may carry different histories and hold different perspectives, ultimately they all want the same things: they want peace, they want to feel safe, they want a fair chance to succeed 芒聙聯 the same aspirations that our partnership aims to secure.

So, Your Majesty, it is my great pleasure to inform you that the floor is now yours.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽 Abdullah II, King of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan. 芒聙聯 In the name of God, the Compassionate, the Merciful; Madam President, honourable Members, your Excellencies, thank you all. It is an honour, as always, to be here to address the European Parliament.

My friends, five years ago, I stood at this podium and spoke of the urgent need to find political solutions to conflicts, to restore confidence in global justice and help people 芒聙聯 especially young people 芒聙聯 find hope and opportunity.

Since then, multiple political, technological and economic upheavals have tested our international community. A global pandemic, new security threats, unprecedented technological acceleration, rampant hyper-charged misinformation, and a raging war in Ukraine and a cruel one on Gaza. And now the attacks on Iran, which threaten a dangerous escalation of tensions in my region and beyond.

We are living through wave after wave of disruption, without pause.脗聽It is no wonder our world feels untethered 芒聙聯 like it has lost its moral gravity. Rules are unravelling; truth shifts by the hour, hatred and division thrive, and moderation and universal values are losing ground to ideological extremes.

In the chaos, we risk forgetting who we are, and what we stand for. But it is precisely at these junctures of history that we must recommit to our values.脗聽Because when the world loses its moral bearings, we lose our shared sense of right and wrong 芒聙聯 of what is just, and what is cruel. And when that happens, conflict is never far behind.

History teaches us that wars are rarely just about territory. They are battles over worldviews 芒聙聯 over which ideas and ideals will shape our future.

Europe understands that.脗聽After World War II, it made a choice: to rebuild not just its cities, but its founding pillars. The people of Europe were determined to leave the past in the past, and build a new era of peace. They chose human dignity over domination, values over vengeance, law over might, cooperation over conflict.

In the wake of war, Europe realised that real security does not lie in the strength of armies, but in the strength of shared values, and that peace imposed by force or fear would never last.

Equally, you chose to recognise that what you had in common was far greater than what set you apart.

Today, so must our global community.脗聽We know that disputes and differences are a human reality, that the challenges we face will only get more complex, and that disruptions are the hallmark of our age. But it's how we handle them, and the values we anchor ourselves to that count.

Throughout Arab and European history, respect, responsibility, goodwill, and good faith have guided collaborations that led to our mutual good. And they can guide our response to today's challenges as well.

Speaking from this podium, and many others in the last two decades, I have sought to shed light on the values we share 芒聙聯 many rooted in our different faiths: Islam, Christianity, and Judaism.脗聽The values of compassion, justice, and equality. The moral teachings passed to us over generations that call on us to love our neighbours, to protect children and the innocent, to aid the poor and injured, to care for God's earth, and more.

In Jordan, our firm belief in these common values, grounded in our history and heritage, drives our national principles of tolerance and mutual respect. We are proud to be home to the Baptism Site of Jesus Christ, peace be upon him, at Bethany Beyond the Jordan.

Our Muslim country is home to a historic Christian community, and all our citizens share in building our nation.脗聽The same call to goodwill lies behind the Hashemite Custodianship of Muslim and Christian Holy Sites in Jerusalem, whose historical and multi-religious identity we have vowed to protect from attack.

Indeed, our commitment echoes a promise to the people of Jerusalem made many centuries ago 芒聙聯 the Pact of Omar ordered Muslims to respect and protect the city's churches and not to harm a priest, nor kill a child, woman or elder.

A thousand years later, the Geneva Conventions made these tenets universal.脗聽Recent events have called them into question. But we must ensure that these values and principles stand the test of time and turmoil.

My friends, when I was last in this hall, I shared my conviction that 'there is always a better version of us around every corner.'脗聽Eighty years ago, you turned that corner, you chose a better Europe, and, for the longest time, the choices you made helped shape a more stable, principled world.

Today, that world is in moral decline. A shameful version of our humanity is unfolding before our eyes in real time and our global values are unravelling at a shocking pace, with devastating consequences.脗聽Nowhere is that clearer than in Gaza, a place where the world has stood at countless corners, each an opportunity to become something better, and time after time, it failed to do so.

Think back to 2023 芒聙聯 the first Israeli attacks and raids on a hospital in Gaza sparked global shock and outrage. Since then, the World Health Organisation has documented nearly 700 attacks on Gaza芒聙聶s healthcare facilities.脗聽How is it that what was considered an atrocity just 20 months ago, is now so commonplace it barely registers?脗聽What version of our humanity allows the unthinkable to become routine? Permits weaponising famine against children? Normalises the targeting of health workers, journalists, and civilians seeking refuge in camps?

20 months. That should alarm us all. But not surprise us. Because when our global community fails to bridge the gap between principle and action, when values are not practised, they become performative, abstract, and expendable.

We are at another defining crossroad in our history, one that demands a choice 芒聙聯 power or principle, the rule of law or the rule of force, decline or renewal. Because that is what芒聙聶s at stake, for everyone.

This is not just about Gaza. And it is not just another political moment. It is a struggle over who we are as a global community and who we will become.脗聽This year is likely to be a time of pivotal decisions for our entire world. Europe's leadership will be vital in choosing the right course. And you can count on Jordan as your staunch partner.

There are two essential areas for action. First, is supporting development, because a thriving Middle East creates opportunities that benefit us all. But as we have seen time and again, that reality cuts both ways. When hope is diminished, the consequences ripple across borders.

Second, is strong, coordinated action to ensure global security.脗聽Our mutual security won't be assured until our global community acts, not only to end the three-year war in Ukraine, but also the world's longest and most destructive flashpoint: the eight-decade-long Palestinian-Israeli conflict.

Because, my friends, Palestinians, like all people, deserve the rights to freedom, sovereignty, and, yes, statehood.脗聽What is happening in Gaza today defies international law, moral standards and our common values. And we are witnessing transgression after transgression in the West Bank, with the situation worsening by the day.

If our global community fails to act decisively, we become complicit in rewriting what it means to be human. Because if Israeli bulldozers continue to illegally demolish Palestinian homes, olive groves, and infrastructure, so too will they flatten the guardrails that define moral conduct. And now with Israel's expansion of its offensive to include Iran, there is no telling where the borders of this battleground will end. That, my friends, is a threat to people everywhere.

Ultimately, this conflict must end. And the only viable solution is one grounded in a just peace, international law, and mutual recognition.

My friends, the path to our better selves cannot be paved by technological advancements, scientific breakthroughs, or political triumphs alone. It is forged by the choices we make, each day, as individuals and as leaders.脗聽The path to peace has been walked before. It can be again, if we have the courage to choose it, and the will to walk it together.

Thank you.

(The House rose and accorded the speaker a standing ovation)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(The sitting was suspended at 12:26)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 La prossima votazione concerne le modifiche del regolamento del Parlamento concernenti la dichiarazione dei contributi (articolo 8 dell'allegato I del regolamento) (cfr. punto 7.7 del processo verbale).
