脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sven Simon, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam President, colleagues, with this report, the Constitutional Affairs Committee is proposing to amend Article脗聽8(1) of Annex脗聽I to our Rules of Procedure, according to which rapporteurs shall list the entities or persons from whom they received input on matters pertaining to the subject of the file in an annex to their report or opinion.

In practice, it has become apparent that the wording 'entities and persons' is unclear, and there has been uncertainty as to which input must be included in the annex. The amendment proposed today addresses this issue.

First, it limits your obligation as rapporteur to listing only those contributions that you have actually included in your report or opinion.

Second, it narrows the scope to interest representatives registered in the Transparency Register and authorities of third countries.

Unfortunately, colleagues, this is not the only provision with unclear wording in our Rules of Procedure, and when obligations are too vague or open to interpretation, there is always a risk that colleagues may find themselves subject to public criticism, even in the absence of any wrongdoing. That is why further amendments will be needed and we will come up with further proposals.

Today's amendment will remove the ambiguous wording, while still addressing the intention of the provision, so I ask for your approval.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 Vi ricordo che le modifiche del regolamento di procedura possono essere approvate solo a maggioranza dei deputati che compongono il Parlamento.

脗聽脗聽Charles Goerens, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, point n'est besoin de faire l'inventaire de toutes les situations de d脙漏tresse que rencontrent les personnes fuyant les conflits, rong脙漏es par la mis脙篓re et la pr脙漏carit脙漏, elles se comptent par millions. Toutes ces victimes, qui vivent essentiellement en Afrique, ont quatre choses en commun.

Premi脙篓rement, leurs souffrances sont souvent le r脙漏sultat de d脙漏veloppements pour lesquels elles ne sont pas responsables elles-m脙陋mes. Deuxi脙篓mement, d脙漏j脙聽 en manque cruel de moyens de subsistance, elles se voient depuis le d脙漏but de cette ann脙漏e encore priv脙漏es de 60脗聽milliards de dollars d'aide publique au d脙漏veloppement, suite au retrait de USAID. Troisi脙篓mement, elles portent le regard sur l'Union europ脙漏enne, qui reste pour la plupart d'entre elles le premier et, il faut en convenir, souvent le dernier rempart contre l'indiff脙漏rence, contre la rage destructrice de tout 脙漏lan de solidarit脙漏. Aussi une orgie utilitariste se met-elle en place en Europe m脙陋me, voulant soustraire 脙聽 notre solidarit脙漏 les pays qui n'ont ni lithium, ni p脙漏trole, ni gaz, ni coltan, et j'en passe. Quatri脙篓mement, aucune de ces personnes dont je viens de parler, vivant dans les pays en d脙漏veloppement, ne va voter pour nous, ni pour le PPE, ni pour le S&D, ni pour Renew, ni pour les Verts, ni pour la gauche, ni pour ECR. Ce n'est pas une raison pour rester indiff脙漏rents 脙聽 leur sort.

Ce que propose le pr脙漏sent rapport, c'est que l'Union europ脙漏enne soit 脙聽 la hauteur, c'est de rester fid脙篓le 脙聽 ses principes et engagements pris en mati脙篓re de solidarit脙漏 internationale et notamment en mati脙篓re de financement de l'aide publique au d脙漏veloppement. Ce que propose le pr脙漏sent rapport, c'est de rester en phase avec l'esprit des architectes de notre politique de d脙漏veloppement, des architectes comme Willy脗聽Brandt, Simone脗聽Veil, Emma脗聽Bonino, Bernard脗聽Kouchner, les Commissaires脗聽Piebalgs, Louis脗聽Michel et Urpilainen ou avec des ministres du d脙漏veloppement comme Gert脗聽M脙录ller, pour n'en citer que quelques-uns.

Plus nous sommes unis, plus l'Union europ脙漏enne pourra convaincre la communaut脙漏 internationale 脙聽 S脙漏ville de s'impliquer dans la solidarit脙漏 active avec nos partenaires du Sud. Je vous invite 脙聽 voter massivement pour le pr脙漏sent rapport.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 Ho ricevuto una richiesta di dichiarazione per fatto personale da parte dell'onorevole Mar脙漏chal, ai sensi dell'articolo 180 del regolamento, in riferimento a un'osservazione fatta ieri in apertura della tornata.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marion Mar脙漏chal (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, selon l'article脗聽180 du r脙篓glement int脙漏rieur, je demande une intervention pour un fait personnel.

Mme脗聽Benedetta脗聽Scuderi m'a hier personnellement mise en cause et insult脙漏e pour avoir d脙漏nonc脙漏 la pr脙漏sence de l'association Femyso 脙聽 l'脙漏v脙漏nement脗聽EYE. J'assume totalement m'脙陋tre oppos脙漏e 脙聽 la pr脙漏sence, au sein du Parlement europ脙漏en, de cette organisation li脙漏e aux islamistes de la confr脙漏rie des Fr脙篓res musulmans.

Le Femyso est une structure cr脙漏脙漏e avec le soutien de l'Union des organisations islamiques en Europe, bras historique des Fr脙篓res musulmans sur le continent. De nombreux chercheurs sp脙漏cialis脙漏s, tout comme le r脙漏cent rapport du minist脙篓re fran脙搂ais de l'int脙漏rieur publi脙漏 en mai脗聽2025, le confirment: le Femyso est un acteur central de la galaxie fr脙漏riste qui encadre, forme et endoctrine des jeunes musulmans en Europe. Le Femyso a organis脙漏 des 脙漏v脙漏nements avec Tariq脗聽Ramadan, petit-fils du fondateur de la confr脙漏rie, des partenariats avec le mouvement islamiste Milli G脙露r脙录s ou avec l'association des jeunes musulmans de France, autre structure des Fr脙篓res musulmans. Il a aussi apport脙漏 son soutien public au Collectif contre l'islamophobie en France, interdit en France, puis r脙漏fugi脙漏 脙聽 Bruxelles. C'est encore le Femyso qui fut 脙聽 l'initiative d'une campagne de communication pro-hijab financ脙漏e par l'Union europ脙漏enne.

Alors pourquoi Mme脗聽Scuderi m'attaque-t-elle? Parce qu'elle est une alli脙漏e du Femyso. Le脗聽30脗聽septembre, elle a particip脙漏 脙聽 un de leurs 脙漏v脙漏nements. Mme脗聽Scuderi ne d脙漏fend pas les valeurs de ce Parlement, elle d脙漏fend ses propres compromissions. Elle m'accuse de mettre en danger des personnes, mais le seul danger pour nos jeunes c'est celui de l'islamisme qui soumet les femmes, met nos lois d脙漏mocratiques sous pression, pousse 脙聽 tuer des innocents par le djihad. La vraie menace, ce ne sont pas les mots, ce ne sont pas les rapports ou les banderoles. La vraie menace, c'est le silence face 脙聽 l'islamisme.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 Il processo verbale e i testi approvati della seduta di ieri sono disponibili. Se non ci sono osservazioni, il processo verbale 脙篓 approvato.

脗聽

10. Implementation report on the Recovery and Resilience Facility (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 L'ordine del giorno reca la relazione di Victor Negrescu e Siegfried Mure脜聼an, a nome della commissione per i bilanci e della commissione per i problemi economici e monetari, sulla relazione sull'attuazione del dispositivo per la ripresa e la resilienza (2024/2085(INI) (A10-0098/2025).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Victor Negrescu, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, the Recovery and Resilience Facility is a historic European achievement. Never before has the European Union mobilised more than EUR脗聽700脗聽billion in such a short time. It is a symbol of solidarity, but also a test of our capacity to deliver results where they are needed the most.

By the end of 2024, unfortunately, only 28脗聽% of milestones and targets had been fully implemented. Over EUR脗聽300脗聽billion still need to be disbursed. Unless we act, critical investments risk being left incomplete. We need to speed up implementation, reduce bureaucracy and help beneficiaries. That is why, as rapporteur, I am pushing for pragmatic and concrete solutions.

Firstly, we call in the report for an 18-month extension for mature projects. Secondly, we demand an easy-to-use transfer mechanism so that unfinished projects can continue under other EU instruments such as the Cohesion Fund, InvestEU or a future Competitiveness Fund. Thirdly, we call for clear, objective and fair evaluation criteria and more flexibility for the revision process of the national plans.

Fourth, the report reinforces the role of local and regional authorities who are closest to the citizens and often bear the responsibility for delivering these projects. Fifth, we insisted on the involvement of social partners and civil society who must help shape reforms that affect people's daily lives. Sixth, we reiterate our call for a 10脗聽% allocation for education and a 2脗聽% allocation for the cultural and creative sectors. Finally, we emphasise the need to keep the pillar of social rights and the social criteria a priority.

These concerns are backed by the European Court of Auditors, which highlighted in its special report the need for more transparency, better planning and stronger ownership at national and local level. All of those points are referred to with solutions in our report.

I am proud that the European Commission had already taken on board several of these ideas in its recent communication: NextGenerationEU 芒聙聯 the road to 2026. The funny fact is that, initially, the Commission said no when we underlined those specific calls. But we need, today, to do more than partial progress. We need the full package, because it is not enough to spend 芒聙聯 we must ensure that every euro is used correctly, transparently and where it matters most.

This is about public trust and it is our responsibility to safeguard it. A hospital cannot function with only the walls built, a digital training cannot happen without connectivity, a reform is useless if no one can implement it. Tomorrow, through our vote, this Parliament will send, I believe, a clear message: that we stand by the citizens, that we fight for the finalisation of essential projects, that we want every cent to count, and that Europe can and will deliver if it dares to adjust when reality demands it.

I would like, of course, to thank my co-rapporteur and the shadows for the good work. When Parliament is united, we are stronger. Thank you for your support.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegfried Mure脜聼an, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, indeed, at the very beginning of the pandemic generated by the coronavirus, the European Union acted swiftly, united and we launched the biggest package of economic support ever created at European level to help those affected by the pandemic (citizens, companies, regions, Member States) to cope with the economic and social consequences of the pandemic. This had a very important impact, then, also upon the private sector, which kept on investing, knowing that financial support from Europe would be coming.

We showed then that Europe is united, that we express solidarity and that we support those in need. It was Europe at its best. Our main objective, then, in the short term was to help those affected. But our main objective in the long term was to strengthen our economy, to strengthen also the private sectors, to reform them, to make them more digital, more energy efficient, to improve their resilience, to make sure that whenever a next crisis hits us, we will be better prepared. And for that, we knew that investments are important, but that reforms are needed as well.

This is why this is a package of investments and reforms. Whenever money is involved, we tend to speak only about money, but we should not forget this is a package of investments and reforms, and its benefits can only be maximised if authorities at local, regional but particularly at national level reform and improve the state of their economy and also of their public sector.

Now, there are 14 more months for the implementation and the conclusion of this instrument.脗聽This is why Victor Negrescu and myself are putting forward an implementation reform, which should be factual, objective and European. It should help us draw the lessons. What went well? What could have been done better? One conclusion is that this is a new instrument. It functions differently than all other EU funds. This is why it took time for Member States to get used to it. It took time for the European Commission to get used to it. And we want for this instrument to be successfully concluded. It's a one-off instrument. This was decided in the beginning. This was never up for debate. It shall end. For the time being, the rules say that everything ends 31脗聽August 2026.

We say that those projects which are mature, which are eligible, which were approved by the Commission, which are in the implementing phase 芒聙聯 we should make sure that they can properly conclude. This is why we say no handing in of new projects after the 31脗聽August 2026, but give some more time, and the proposal of the脗聽Parliament, adopted by a large majority at the committee level, is to give projects 18 months more time to conclude successfully. We also ask Member States to prioritise mature projects in the upcoming months, to focus only on those projects which make sense, which are mature, and which can be properly implemented.

Of course, when we speak about lessons to be learned, there are some things which could have been done better. Firstly, the involvement of local regional authorities 芒聙聯 national governments did not involve them properly. The more you involve the national and regional level, the faster you implement and the more relevant the projects are close to the citizen. This is the first shortcoming.

The second shortcoming is lack of cross-border projects. Governments, as they submitted their national recovery plans, each of them thought only nationally. But we as a European Parliament see this as a shortcoming, as a mistake. We would have wanted more cross-border projects and more European added value. Large, ambitious infrastructure projects, for example, which help Europe as a whole.

And of course, transparency. We need to know how money is spent. We need to know who the final beneficiaries are. And there we also need to be strong.

This is what we're putting forward for debate. I would also like to thank my co-rapporteur Victor Negrescu, the shadows and everyone who has worked on this report. It's an objective, factual, useful, I think, assessment of an important tool, and it draws some useful lessons for the future, as well as for the budget of the European Union and for any other future project linked to EU funds.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Raffaele Fitto, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Chairs, honourable Members, I would like to start by thanking this House for our ongoing collaboration and support. The Facility has been a game changer in Europe's response to the impacts of the pandemic. The RRF has had a great stabilising effect on European economies. It also helped drive the implementation of important reforms.

That being said, implementation is delayed. Overall, 51脗聽% of the RRF's total committed funds still need to be disbursed and 68脗聽% of milestones and targets still need to be assessed as fulfilled, even though Member States have already reported that, of those, 20脗聽% are completed.

In this regard, the RRF implementation report is an important input to the discussion. Two weeks ago, the Commission adopted a communication on NextGenerationEU 芒聙聯 the road to 2026. The communication reviews progress, recalls the legal deadlines and proposes ways to speed up implementation. The Commission takes note of the call in the implementation report for an 18-month extension of the deadline for mature projects under the RRF.

The RRF was created as a temporary supporting instrument, and the RRF regulation is explicit about completing all milestones and targets by the end of August脗聽2026, and making the last payments and the respective borrowing by the end of December脗聽2026. These deadlines cannot be changed without amending the RRF Regulation, the EURI Regulation and the Own Resources Decision. As you know, the latter requires unanimity in Council and ratification by a number of national parliaments.

The Commission prioritises legal certainty while providing options to accelerate implementation and to ensure completion by the end of the Facility. The Commission is urging Member States to review their plans as soon as possible, to only retain measures that can be implemented by the end of August脗聽2026. Member States can explore several options, including scaling-up successful measures, funding critical projects that have been awarded the STEP Seal, transferring funds to InvestEU or to EU programmes for satellite communication. Member States can also split RRF projects to ensure that elements no longer achievable by August脗聽2026 are financed by national or, if eligible, other EU funds with a longer timeline, such as the Cohesion Policy.

RRF funds could also be used to deliver additional important benefits for common EU priorities, including security and defence. A possible option would be to make voluntary contributions to the future European defence industry programme.

Another important point is simplification, which is necessary to reduce the administrative burden. Of course, the plans will have to continue to meet the assessment criteria and the requirements linked to the protection of the EU's financial interests. If the plans are simplified and revised quickly, we are confident that the RRF will reach its objectives within the set deadlines. The implementation report also raises concerns as regards transparency, monitoring and control within the framework of the RRF.

The Commission is firmly committed to high levels of accountability. Last脗聽April, 24 Member States updated the data on their 100 largest final recipients, and the Commission is following up with the remaining Member States. Following the request from the Chair of the CONT Committee in the context of the 2023 discharge, the Commission asked all Member States to provide a list of the 100 largest natural persons or entities receiving funds under the RRF per Member State, including contractors and subcontractors.

To conclude, I look forward to the debate and hearing your views.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carla Tavares, relatora do parecer da Comiss脙拢o CONT. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, n脙拢o podemos estar neste debate sem destacar uma vez mais o efeito estabilizador que o Mecanismo de Recupera脙搂脙拢o e Resili脙陋ncia teve para os Estados芒聙聭Membros num momento de grande incerteza.

Atenuou as consequ脙陋ncias econ脙鲁micas e sociais negativas e apoiou os governos, promovendo a recupera脙搂脙拢o econ脙鲁mica, social e a competitividade, refor脙搂ando a resili脙陋ncia e inova脙搂脙拢o e apoiando as transi脙搂脙碌es ecol脙鲁gica e digital com a preocupa脙搂脙拢o de n脙拢o deixar ningu脙漏m para tr脙隆s.

Este mecanismo preveniu tamb脙漏m uma maior fragmenta脙搂脙拢o do mercado interno e um agravamento da diverg脙陋ncia macroecon脙鲁mica na promo脙搂脙拢o da coes脙拢o social e territorial. No entanto, nem tudo foi perfeito e h脙隆 espa脙搂o para melhorar.

A aus脙陋ncia de uma auditoria adequada ao MRR e a falta de transpar脙陋ncia sobre os 100 maiores benefici脙隆rios finais, introduzida a pedido deste Parlamento, 脙漏 sintom脙隆tica disso. Importa que as listas dos maiores benefici脙隆rios finais de cada Estado芒聙聭Membro sejam regularmente atualizadas e tornadas p脙潞blicas. Precisamos de conhecer os benefici脙隆rios efetivos a quem chegam, efetivamente, os apoios.

Por fim, importa assinalar as defici脙陋ncias nos mecanismos nacionais de comunica脙搂脙拢o e controlo, devido, em parte, 脙聽 complexidade dos procedimentos internos de auditoria e controlo, o que gera incerteza nos Estados芒聙聭Membros e uma sobrecarga de procedimentos administrativos n脙拢o desej脙隆vel que acentua as dificuldades de execu脙搂脙拢o.

Precisamos, pois, de garantias sobre o funcionamento adequado dos sistemas de controlo dos Estados芒聙聭Membros; precisamos de verificar a conformidade dos projetos de investimento financiados pelo MRR com as normas nacionais e da Uni脙拢o Europeia.

Por fim, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, permita-me que lance tamb脙漏m aqui um desafio: precisamos de encontrar todos os caminhos poss脙颅veis para prolongar a execu脙搂脙拢o do MRR para os projetos que j脙隆 foram iniciados e est脙拢o em curso. N脙拢o podemos desistir; n脙拢o 脙漏 tarde ainda para continuarmos neste caminho.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie-Pierre Vedrenne, rapporteure pour avis de la commission EMPL. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Vice-pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, le choc que nous avons travers脙漏 avec la pand脙漏mie aurait pu briser notre coh脙漏sion. Mais nous avons construit un instrument de solidarit脙漏 in脙漏dit, un plan de relance massif financ脙漏 par un emprunt commun. Cet effort commun a permis d'injecter plus de 650 milliards脗聽d'euros.

Toutefois, les rapporteurs l'ont soulign脙漏, des obstacles persistent. Ce m脙漏canisme de solidarit脙漏 ne peut rester une simple parenth脙篓se. Il doit devenir le fondement d'une Europe plus juste 芒聙聯 en assurant un acc脙篓s 脙漏gal aux investissements que vous soyez agriculteur en Pologne, artisan en Espagne ou infirmi脙篓re en France 芒聙聯, plus verte 芒聙聯 en finan脙搂ant la r脙漏novation des b脙垄timents, l'脙漏nergie nucl脙漏aire ou encore la mobilit脙漏 durable 芒聙聯, plus comp脙漏titive en soutenant les PME pour qu'elles innovent et qu'elles cr脙漏ent des emplois.

Pour cela, nous devons simplifier les formalit脙漏s pour que n'importe quelle petite entreprise ou association puisse pr脙漏senter une demande sans lourdeurs administratives. Nous devons aussi suivre pr脙漏cis脙漏ment les impacts de ces dispositifs. Car l'Europe qui prot脙篓ge, c'est celle qui se mesure dans la vie quotidienne des Europ脙漏ens. L'Europe qui investit, c'est celle qui se voit dans des salles de classe, dans des services de sant脙漏 renforc脙漏s ou encore dans des entreprises locales qui innovent. C'est ainsi que nous sommes attendus.

脗聽脗聽Jonas Sj脙露stedt, f脙露redragande av ENVI-utskottets yttrande. 芒聙聯 Fru talman! F脙露r att klara den gr脙露na omst脙陇llningen kr脙陇vs mycket stora investeringar, annars blir det inte m脙露jligt att ers脙陇tta de fossila br脙陇nslena. H脙陇r skulle RRF kunna spela en avg脙露rande roll, men det 脙陇r uppenbart att fonden inte fullt ut har uppfyllt f脙露rv脙陇ntningarna p脙楼 gr脙露na investeringar.

Revisionsr脙陇tten har gjort en s脙陇rskild granskning som visar att flera av de investeringar som utger sig f脙露r att vara klimatinvesteringar eller gr脙露na, i sj脙陇lva verket inte 脙陇r det, fullt ut. De var redan planerade.脗聽Fonden fungerar mer som ett budgetst脙露d 脙陇n som en katalysator f脙露r nya investeringar.

RRF lider ocks脙楼 av bristande sp脙楼rbarhet. Det 脙陇r sv脙楼rt, ibland om脙露jligt, att kontrollera hur pengarna anv脙陇nds. 脙聳ver 300 fall av missbrukade medel utreds av EU:s 脙楼klagarmyndighet Eppo. Till det kommer ett stort antal fall som utreds av Olaf. Det 脙陇r pengar som sannolikt inte har anv脙陇nts p脙楼 ett korrekt s脙陇tt och gynnat den gr脙露na omst脙陇llningen. 脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Lupo, relatore per parere della commissione TRAN. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, il NextGenerationEU ha certamente salvato in questi anni l'economia europea e milioni di posti di lavoro dalla crisi indotta dalla pandemia COVID. Vorrei ringraziare in questa sede quanti hanno lavorato per questo importante obiettivo, tra questi anche il Commissario all'Economia dell'epoca, Paolo Gentiloni.

L'attuazione dell'RRF, tuttavia, ha mostrato dei limiti, in particolare, ad esempio, nelle regioni periferiche e soprattutto nelle isole, proprio per quanto riguarda il settore dei trasporti, ma in generale in Europa per quanto riguarda i collegamenti transfrontalieri.

Aspettiamo ancora in Sicilia il progetto di finanziamento per la realizzazione del raddoppio della linea ferroviaria Castelbuono-Patti, cos脙卢 come in Sardegna si attende ancora il progetto e si attendono ancora le risorse per la realizzazione della linea ferroviaria Abbasanta-Nuoro. Nuoro 脙篓 l'unico capoluogo d'Italia a non avere una linea ferroviaria degna di questo nome.

Quindi 脙篓 necessario dare nuovo slancio agli investimenti del NextGenerationEU, prevedendo la proroga di diciotto mesi. A nostro avviso 脙篓 una proroga necessaria per i progetti maturi, soprattutto per i trasporti transfrontalieri, per l'ambiente, per la realizzazione di infrastrutture necessarie per ridurre le diseguaglianze e migliorare anche le condizioni della cittadinanza relative alla sanit脙聽 e all'istruzione e la competitivit脙聽 dell'Unione europea, che deve essere certamente migliorata creando anche nuove opportunit脙聽 di lavoro, soprattutto per i pi脙鹿 giovani, soprattutto dove purtroppo il lavoro manca.

脗聽脗聽Markus Ferber, im Namen der PPE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Exekutiv-Vizepr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Der Bericht 脙录ber die Umsetzung der Aufbau芒聙聭脗聽und Resilienzfazilit脙陇t zeichnet ein sehr ern脙录chterndes Bild. Eine Vielzahl von Meilensteinen bleiben unvollst脙陇ndig, Verz脙露gerungen h脙陇ufen sich, die versprochene Transparenz bleibt aus, und der wirtschaftliche Mehrwert ist unklar. Die Sonderberichte des Europ脙陇ischen Rechnungshofs haben ein ums andere Mal eklatante Konstruktionsfehler dieser angeblich 芒聙聻leistungsbasierten" Finanzarchitektur aufgezeigt: unklare Definition von Leistungskriterien, mangelnde Nachvollziehbarkeit der Ausgaben und ein erh脙露htes Risiko der Doppelfinanzierung durch 脙聹berschneidungen mit anderen Programmen der Europ脙陇ischen Union. Statt klarer Abrechnung tats脙陇chlicher Ausgaben gibt es ein undurchsichtiges Auszahlungssystem, das nur schwer nachvollziehbar ist. Dadurch fehlen verl脙陇ssliche Kontrollen, und es bleibt unklar, ob die Mittel auch wirklich dort ankommen, wo sie ben脙露tigt worden w脙陇ren.

Die eingeschr脙陇nkte Transparenz weckt berechtigte Zweifel an der Effizienz und am verantwortungsvollen Umgang mit den Geldern unserer Steuerzahler. Angesichts dieser M脙陇ngel kann der Aufbaufonds weder als Blaupause f脙录r k脙录nftige EU芒聙聭Programme dienen, noch macht eine Verl脙陇ngerung Sinn. Hinzu kommt das Damoklesschwert der nach wie vor ungekl脙陇rten Refinanzierung. Die einst versprochenen neuen Eigenmittel脗聽芒聙聯 der Rat ist nicht einmal pr脙陇sent hier脗聽芒聙聯 sind vier Jahre danach noch nicht im Ergebnis vorhanden. Die R脙录ckzahlung der Schulden wird die zuk脙录nftigen Haushalte der Europ脙陇ischen Union belasten und die Handlungsf脙陇higkeit der Europ脙陇ischen Union einschr脙陇nken. Das sollte eigentlich auch dem Letzten zeigen, dass neue Schulden keine L脙露sung der Probleme in Europa sind.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jean-Marc Germain, au nom du groupe S&D. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, au plus profond de la crise sans pr脙漏c脙漏dent, nous, les Europ脙漏ens, avons su nous rassembler pour trouver ensemble le chemin de la rel脙篓ve. Le grand plan d'investissement, financ脙漏 pour la premi脙篓re fois par l'emprunt europ脙漏en, dont nous parlons aujourd'hui en fut l'un des instruments majeurs. Cet 脙漏lan nous a permis de surmonter la pand脙漏mie, de rendre concret l'id脙漏al de solidarit脙漏 europ脙漏enne, de prot脙漏ger nos 脙漏conomies et nos emplois, d'acc脙漏l脙漏rer la transition 脙漏cologique et num脙漏rique. Alors ne tirons pas de mauvaises le脙搂ons de cette exp脙漏rience.

Oui 脙聽 de nouveaux emprunts europ脙漏ens pour financer le mur d'investissements publics n脙漏cessaires 脙聽 une transition 脙漏conomique et num脙漏rique qui soit juste. Mais non, non, 1脗聽000脗聽fois non 脙聽 cette funeste id脙漏e de conditionner le paiement des fonds europ脙漏ens 脙聽 l'accomplissement pr脙漏alable de r脙漏formes脗聽芒聙聯 脗芦money for value, payments against reforms脗禄.

Mais enfin, l'Europe n'est pas l脙聽 pour faire de l'argent, mais pour faire le bien des gens! L'Europe n'est pas l脙聽 pour contraindre les nations, mais pour les transcender. Ce chantage aux r脙漏formes fut une erreur pour la facilit脙漏 pour la reprise. Ce serait une faute de reproduire et de g脙漏n脙漏raliser cette approche dans le prochain budget pluriannuel europ脙漏en. Et vous devez le savoir, Monsieur le Commissaire, c'est une ligne rouge pour nous, les socialistes et d脙漏mocrates europ脙漏ens. Voil脙聽 qui est dit.

脗聽脗聽Enik脜聭 Gy脜聭ri, a PfE k脙漏pvisel脜聭csoport nev脙漏ben. 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! Tudjuk, hogy mir脜聭l besz脙漏l脙录nk, hiszen a baloldali br脙录sszeli elit a mai napig jogtalanul tartja vissza a magyar embereket megillet脜聭 helyre脙隆ll脙颅t脙隆si p脙漏nzeket, szimpl脙隆n az脙漏rt, mert mi, magyarok a hagyom脙隆nyos 脙漏rt脙漏kek ment脙漏n, m脙隆smilyen haz脙隆ban 脙漏s m脙隆smilyen Eur脙鲁p脙隆ban gondolkodunk.脗聽Nem engedhetj脙录k meg, hogy a j脙露v脜聭ben a Von der Leyen-kabinet 脙露nk脙漏nye hat脙隆rozza meg az uni脙鲁s forr脙隆sokhoz val脙鲁 hozz脙隆f脙漏r脙漏st.

M脙隆sodszor, a jelent脙漏s 脙潞j, eur脙鲁pai szint脜卤 ad脙鲁k bevezet脙漏s脙漏t s脙录rgeti, hogy az Uni脙鲁 vissza tudja fizetni az RRF keret脙漏ben felvett k脙露z脙露s hitelt. Mi, patri脙鲁t脙隆k erre hat脙隆rozott nemet mondunk. Egy脙漏rtelm脜卤en ki脙隆llunk amellett, hogy Br脙录sszel hib脙隆s politik脙隆j脙隆nak k脙露vetkezm脙漏nyeit ne lehessen az eur脙鲁pai ad脙鲁fizet脜聭kre h脙隆r脙颅tani. Tal脙隆n nem kellett volna felel脜聭tlen脙录l d脙露ntenie a baloldalnak 脙漏s elad脙鲁s脙颅tani a j脙露v脜聭 nemzed脙漏k脙漏t.

Harmadszor, ez a h脙隆z ism脙漏t semmibe venn脙漏 az uni脙鲁s szerz脜聭d脙漏seket, 脙漏s hat脙隆sk脙露r脙漏n t脙潞lmutat脙鲁an akarja n脙露velni a befoly脙隆s脙隆t. Ha 脙聳n脙露k脙露n, a baloldali k脙漏pvisel脜聭k脙露n m脙潞lna, az Eur脙鲁pai Parlament m脙隆r a tag脙隆llami helyre脙隆ll脙颅t脙隆si tervek kialak脙颅t脙隆s脙隆ba is belesz脙鲁lhatna. Ez szerept脙漏veszt脙漏s.

Arra k脙漏rem teh脙隆t 脙聳n脙露ket, hogy tarts脙隆k tiszteletben a j脙隆t脙漏kszab脙隆lyokat, a Bizotts脙隆got pedig arra, hogy hagyjon fel a politikai zsarol脙隆ssal, fizesse ki a tag脙隆llamokat megillet脜聭 p脙漏nzeket. 脗聽脗聽Denis Nesci, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Vicepresidente esecutivo Fitto, la relazione ci aggiorna sull'andamento dell'RRF. I cantieri sono partiti, ma la curva di spesa segnala un rallentamento che rischia di aprire un vuoto negli investimenti. Occorre quindi agire subito con misure concrete che sostengano i progetti senza appesantire i bilanci pubblici.

Il testo denuncia ritardi, sovrapposizioni e colli di bottiglia amministrativi, chiede audit pi脙鹿 incisivi e piattaforme digitali per tracciare in tempo reale ogni euro speso. Ricordo inoltre che una parte consistente dei prestiti resta inutilizzata, mentre il debito gi脙聽 contratto graver脙聽 sempre di pi脙鹿 sul bilancio dell'Unione. Finch脙漏 queste risorse non entreranno effettivamente in circolo, un nuovo debito non 脙篓 giustificato.

In chiave costruttiva, per脙虏, l'Italia ha dimostrato e continua a dimostrare che accelerare 脙篓 possibile. Il governo Meloni ha snellito le procedure, rafforzato il monitoraggio e orientato la spesa su progetti immediatamente realizzabili, offrendo un modello di gestione rigorosa.

Inoltre, la Corte dei conti europea certifica l'impatto trascurabile sulla transizione verde. Prima di imporre nuovi criteri ideologici, pretendiamo risultati concreti, dati aperti sui beneficiari, indicatori di esito chiari e un controllo parlamentare costante.

Infine, le Regioni e i Comuni devono essere pienamente coinvolti. Solo loro possono tradurre i finanziamenti europei in opere concrete e benefici tangibili.

La relazione indica una via pragmatica a controlli pi脙鹿 forti, trasparenti e totale responsabilit脙聽 condivisa, che merita di essere seguita per trasformare gli investimenti in risultati duraturi per tutta l'Unione. Facciamo in modo che le scelte di oggi diventino libert脙聽 economica e speranze concrete per le generazioni di domani.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脛陆udov脙颅t 脙聯dor, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President,脗聽Commissioner, colleagues, the Recovery and Resilience Facility is an important testing case for the European Union for at least two reasons.

The first one is that there is a unique combination of reforms and investments to finance the green and digital transitions, and it might be a useful tool to prioritise our economic policies. The second reason is that joint borrowing to finance European public goods is also an innovative example worth considering also in other cases.

So, what have the lessons been so far? The results are a little bit mixed, but my assessment is mostly positive. For the future, I would like to point out the following shortcomings which should be addressed before launching a similar instrument in the future.

The first one is that, in my view, more focus is needed on European value added in the investment. The second one is that we need ex-ante agreement on the financing, because otherwise it can crowd out a lot of different expenditures in the MFF. An outcome-based evaluation: it is not spending the money that is important, but spending it wisely. Better administrative capacities in order not to crowd out other European funds at national level. And more granular definition of reforms and higher transparency.

I hope that with these lessons, we can build a more resilient and better Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

VORSITZ: SABINE VERHEYEN

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rada Laykova, on behalf of the ESN Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues and dear European taxpayers, the Recovery and Resilience Fund is nothing more than a covert budget bailout of some Member States on the basis of massive EU-level debt under the NextGenerationEU programme.

This absurd financing model is devoid of any cost analysis and the allocation of the funds is non-transparent and inconsistent. We have complex procedures to obtain the funds that only serve to obscure this reality, and in some countries national budget items have been largely replaced by their RRF.

But what is even more scandalous is that the RRF is used as a political tool to impose EU plans to save the world's climate. In my country, Bulgaria, shutting down essential coal-fired power plants was a condition to receive the funds from their RRF.

Yet the most serious concern here is the transformation of the EU into a debt and fiscal union. Under the pretext of a crisis, the rules that prohibit common debt are bypassed. As a result, starting in 2028, interest payments alone will consume 15-20脗聽% of the EU's annual budget. If more debt is added, for example, due to the so-called Russian pandemic, this burden will only grow, especially since there is no cap in place.

The EU is not pursuing repayment plans and is not using leftover RRF funds to reduce the debt. Instead, there is a new EU credit card invented, more borrowing or worse, new EU-level taxes just to service the interest. This is not only unsustainable, but it is legally questionable.

The current path benefits financial institutions and shifts the costs to EU citizens and future generations. This is not resilient recovery; this is a debt trap for Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Karlo Ressler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, po脜隆tovani potpredsjedni脛聧e Komisije, kolegice i kolege,脗聽uz sve svoje nedostatke i izazove Instrument za oporavak i otpornost pokazao se kao povijesni odgovor i na stvarni test europske solidarnosti i na jednu od najve脛聡ih kriza na脜隆eg vremena.

Sprije脛聧ena je dublja gospodarska fragmentacija me脛聭u dr脜戮avama 脛聧lanicama, a zajedni脛聧ki instrument omogu脛聡io je investicije bez presedana, uz konkretne reforme koje ja脛聧aju otpornost i konkurentnost na脜隆ega gospodarstva. Istodobno, dodatna vrijednost i pravi u脛聧inci ovog instrumenta tek 脛聡e se u potpunosti vidjeti kroz protek vremena. Mnogi projekti jo脜隆 su u provedbi, a dio njih riskira ostati nedovr脜隆en. Klju脛聧no pitanje vi脜隆e nije samo koliko 脛聡emo sredstva uspjeti iskoristiti, nego i kako. Koliko 脛聡e svaki euro biti upotrjebljen pametno, svrhovito i s dugoro脛聧nim u脛聧inkom za na脜隆e dru脜隆tvo i gospodarstvo.

Zato je razumno razmotriti i ciljano 18-mjese脛聧no produljenje roka za provedbu onih projekata koji su ve脛聡 zapo脛聧eti, ali koji zbog objektivnih okolnosti ne脛聡e mo脛聡i biti dovr脜隆eni u zadanim rokovima. Takvim pristupom sprje脛聧avamo da sredstva ostanu脗聽neiskori脜隆tena ili, jo脜隆 gore, da budu iskori脜隆tena na brzinu, a ne u脛聧inkovito. Hrvatska je, i po glavi stanovnika, ali i po kori脜隆tenju me脛聭u najuspje脜隆nijima. To mislim da nije slu脛聧ajnost, nego ovaj mehanizam u svakom slu脛聧aju daje zamah i u Hrvatskoj, ali isto tako i na nacionalnoj razini i zbog toga iz njega moramo izvu脛聡i dobre pouke.

(Govornik je pristao odgovoriti na pitanje postavljeno podizanjem plave kartice.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Deputado Ressler, um dos principais problemas relativamente ao MRR 脙漏 o prazo. O prazo para o fim do MRR n脙拢o vai permitir que uma boa parte dos fundos seja utilizada e que uma parte significativa dos projetos que estavam a ser considerados possa ser executada.

O relat脙鲁rio apresenta uma proposta de mais 18 meses para os projetos com alguma maturidade. Parece-nos uma proposta t脙颅mida, pelo que apresent脙隆mos uma proposta de extens脙拢o do prazo at脙漏 2028.

O seu grupo pol脙颅tico est脙隆 de acordo com essa extens脙拢o do prazo ou prefere perder os fundos do MRR?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Karlo Ressler (PPE), odgovor na pitanje postavljeno podizanjem plave kartice. 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, ideju koju sadr脜戮i ovo izvje脜隆脛聡e o ciljanom produ脜戮enju za 18 mjeseci za one projekte koji su zreli, koji su objektivno u mogu脛聡nosti da budu provedeni na vrijeme treba podr脜戮ati.

Mislim da europska sredstva ne mogu biti svrha sama po sebi i da na taj na脛聧in mo脜戮emo sprije脛聧iti, kao 脜隆to sam i rekao, da s jedne strane ta sredstva ne ostanu neiskori脜隆tena, ali i s druge strane, ono 脜隆to mo脜戮e biti jo脜隆 i gore, da budu, a 脜隆to se trenutno, na脜戮alost i doga脛聭a, da budu iskori脜隆tena na brzinu, a neu脛聧inkovito.

Zbog toga podr脜戮avam ovo 18 mjese脛聧no produ脜戮enje.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jon脙隆s Fern脙隆ndez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, es un placer discutir en estos momentos la evaluaci脙鲁n y la implementaci脙鲁n del Mecanismo de Recuperaci脙鲁n y Resiliencia que, como se ha dicho previamente, ha sido una pieza clave para superar conjuntamente, todos los europeos unidos, la dura crisis de la COVID-19.

Quiz脙隆 pueda a脙卤adir dos apuntes sobre lo que se ha dicho: es importante que gastemos bien, es importante acelerar la implementaci脙鲁n, como ha dicho el comisario, pero necesitamos encontrar una soluci脙鲁n para el pr脙鲁ximo verano. Entiendo que es dif脙颅cil revisar la decisi脙鲁n de recursos propios a la que ha hecho referencia el comisario, pero en junio de 2026 necesitamos poder seguir financiando proyectos que ya est脙隆n en marcha y yo creo que hay un acuerdo amplio en este Parlamento para solicitar esa ampliaci脙鲁n de dieciocho meses, lo que facilitar脙颅a una reforma legislativa. Hay en esta C脙隆mara una mayor脙颅a para esa reforma legislativa.

Por 脙潞ltimo, hoy escribe la presidenta del BCE en el Financial Times a favor de mantener un activo libre de riesgos en Europa. Necesitamos MRR para el futuro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Julien Sanchez (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, en脗聽2020, la Commission europ脙漏enne d脙漏cidait de grands emprunts de 800脗聽milliards appel脙漏s NextGenerationEU, soi-disant pour relancer l'脙漏conomie, on a vu le r脙漏sultat. La plus grande part de ces emprunts a servi aux fonds pour la facilit脙漏 pour la reprise et la r脙漏silience (FRR), dont votre rapport pr脙漏tend tirer aujourd'hui un bilan positif. Je ne sais pas si c'est un mensonge d脙漏lib脙漏r脙漏, de l'aveuglement ou un peu des deux; permettez-moi donc de rappeler les faits, qui viennent contrecarrer votre propagande grossi脙篓re.

D'abord, nous d脙漏plorons qu'aucun contr脙麓le parlementaire, r脙漏el et digne de ce nom, n'existe sur ces fonds d'un montant historique de pr脙篓s de 800脗聽milliards qui ont 脙漏t脙漏 improvis脙漏s avec un grand manque de professionnalisme dans leur structure et leur gestion financi脙篓re脗聽芒聙聯 c'est de notre point de vue extr脙陋mement grave. Mais ce n'est pas 脙漏tonnant, car, d脙篓s le d脙漏but, la FRR a contourn脙漏 la proc脙漏dure budg脙漏taire classique. Son financement repose sur une dette commune contract脙漏e dans l'urgence, sans d脙漏bat d脙漏mocratique, qui p脙篓sera sur les contribuables europ脙漏ens jusqu'en脗聽2058, 脙聽 hauteur de 15 脙聽 20脗聽% du budget europ脙漏en chaque ann脙漏e, vous amenant aujourd'hui脗聽芒聙聯 parce que vous r脙漏fl脙漏chissez aux recettes apr脙篓s avoir d脙漏pens脙漏脗聽芒聙聯 脙聽 envisager de nouvelles ressources propres, imp脙麓t europ脙漏en, taxe nouvelle ou hausse des contributions des 脙聣tats que nous refusons cat脙漏goriquement.

Quant aux r脙漏sultats, je le disais, ils sont globalement nuls: au脗聽3脗聽juin脗聽2025, seuls 31脗聽% des jalons ont 脙漏t脙漏 atteints, mais 316脗聽milliards d'euros ont d脙漏j脙聽 脙漏t脙漏 vers脙漏s sans garantie sur leur utilisation correcte. Le Parquet europ脙漏en a d脙漏j脙聽 ouvert 307脗聽enqu脙陋tes pour fraude, repr脙漏sentant 2,8脗聽milliards d'euros de pr脙漏judice potentiel. Face 脙聽 cette r脙漏alit脙漏, notre groupe Patriotes pour l'Europe a d脙漏pos脙漏 80脗聽% des amendements pr脙漏sent脙漏s en pl脙漏ni脙篓re sur ce dossier. Nous invitons chacun 脙聽 faire attention 脙聽 ces votes publics sur nos amendements, car l'histoire jugera vos comportements. Il s'agit ici de contr脙麓le budg脙漏taire et fermer les yeux n'est pas la solution. Nous demandons la transparence totale sur l'utilisation des fonds.

(L'orateur accepte une question carton bleu)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Dear colleague, I was just wondering, as you said that there was no debate about this before, and then you said that now there is no oversight afterwards. In light of the fact that we speak exactly about these topics, about these funds in this European Parliament, the only directly-elected institution of the European Union, before and, now, also after, and you have the freedom to outline all your criticism 芒聙聯 if that is not a democratic debate, in your opinion what is a democratic debate?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Julien Sanchez (PfE), r脙漏ponse carton bleu. 芒聙聯 C'est une mascarade, tout simplement, parce que le seul contr脙麓le que nous avons, c'est de demander aux 脙聣tats quels sont les 100 principaux b脙漏n脙漏ficiaires finaux. Mais moi je ne veux pas seulement les 100 principaux b脙漏n脙漏ficiaires finaux par 脙聣tat, je veux tous les b脙漏n脙漏ficiaires par 脙聣tat et je veux que ce soit public. Je veux qu'on voie tout cela; aujourd'hui on nous le cache.

Et quand je pose des questions 脙聽 la Cour des comptes de Lettonie, par exemple, o脙鹿 je me suis rendu, des questions tr脙篓s pr脙漏cises sur l'utilisation de ces fonds et qu'on me dit: 脗芦Je n'ai pas de r脙漏ponse 脙聽 vous donner, je n'ai pas de r脙漏ponse 脙聽 vous donner脗禄, je constate et je conclus qu'absolument rien n'est contr脙麓l脙漏. Je ne veux pas de 脙搂a pour l'avenir de l'Europe, je veux des fonds contr脙麓l脙漏s.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ruggero Razza (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Vicepresidente esecutivo della Commissione, io penso che, a tratti, questo dibattito non abbia voluto o saputo tenere in considerazione alcune delle novit脙聽 che sono emerse nelle ultime settimane.

脙聢 giusto ed 脙篓 molto corretto che il Parlamento si chieda come andare avanti su tanti progetti che i piani hanno realizzato nei diversi Stati e che potrebbero, per mille ragioni, non trovare la loro completa esecuzione entro il termine ad oggi previsto.

Ma a questa domanda la Commissione europea, proprio nelle ultime settimane, ha fornito due risposte assolutamente convincenti che penso debbano essere ricordate nel dibattito parlamentare.

Perch脙漏, per un verso la modifica della strategia di medio termine della politica di coesione e per altro verso, soprattutto lo dico con profondo rispetto anche al dibattito che ho ascoltato da parte di chi forse non vuole che il programma abbia successo, e per questo lo vuole posticipare e posticipare nel tempo quanto alla sua scadenza.

Ma la comunicazione della Commissione europea del 4 giugno scorso ha gi脙聽 indicato degli strumenti che consentono sia il completamento delle iniziative che sono in via di esecuzione, sia, soprattutto attraverso la costituzione di fondi specifici presso gli istituti di credito, la possibilit脙聽 di investire sugli stessi obiettivi e andare anche oltre. Quindi io penso che mentre la Commissione indicava la luna qualcuno qui continua a osservare il dito.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Andresen (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Der (unverst脙陇ndliche Worte) Wiederaufbaufonds ist in einer wirtschaftlich sehr komplizierten Phase, die Europ脙陇ische Union stabilisiert zu haben. Und ja, wie einige Kollegen es auch schon deutlich gemacht haben, es sind auch viele konkrete Infrastrukturprojekte dadurch angeschoben worden, die auch dazu beitragen, unsere Wirtschaft gr脙录n zu transformieren und nachhaltig aufzustellen. Aber gerade wenn man will, dass es eine Fortsetzung eines Wiederaufbaufonds gibt, muss man sich auch mit den problematischen Aspekten auseinandersetzen, und da muss ich ganz klar sagen, dass die Umsetzung zum Teil zu lange dauert, dass die Qualit脙陇t der Projekte in Teilen sehr gering ist, und auch, dass das Geld zu oft in nationale Haushalte versickert.

In einem Monat wird der neue Vorschlag zum mehrj脙陇hrigen Finanzrahmen pr脙陇sentiert, und da sollten wir darauf achten, dass eine neue Investitionskapazit脙陇t mit klaren Zielen zu Investitionen in gr脙录ne und innovative Infrastruktur ausgestattet ist, dass es um europ脙陇ischen Mehrwert und nicht um nationale Wahlgeschenke geht und dass es eine breite Beteiligung gibt, nicht nur vom Parlament, sondern auch von der Zivilgesellschaft.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jussi Saramo (The Left). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, Eurooppa on j脙陇脙陇m脙陇ss脙陇 Kiinasta j脙陇lkeen vihre脙陇n siirtym脙陇n, tulevaisuuden teknologioissa. Yhdysvalloista olemme j脙陇脙陇neet j脙陇lkeen digipalveluissa. Kiristyv脙陇ss脙陇 maailmantilanteessa strategiset riippuvuudet ovat vaarallisia. Emme saa olla riippuvaisia autoritaarisista johtajista Kiinassa, Yhdysvalloissa tai 脙露ljyvaltioissa.

RRF on auttanut Eurooppaa siirtym脙陇ss脙陇 tulevaisuuteen, mutta tuloksista emme valitettavasti tied脙陇 paljoa. Voimme vain olettaa. Voimme valitettavasti my脙露s olettaa, ett脙陇 osa rahoista on k脙陇ytetty v脙陇脙陇rin. Mutta varsinainen ongelma on, jos EU:n ty脙露kaluna on ainoastaan uuden rahan ty脙露nt脙陇minen talouteen. Sit脙陇kin voidaan v脙陇lill脙陇 tarvita, mutta toisin kuin Yhdysvalloilla ja Kiinalla, k脙陇temme ovat muilta osin sidotut.脗聽Yhdysvaltain IRA:ssa rahaa ker脙陇ttiin my脙露s takaisin yrityksi脙陇 verottamalla. Se on inflaation ja julkisen talouden kannalta v脙陇ltt脙陇m脙陇t脙露nt脙陇. Velan sijaan tarvitsemme mekin omia varoja, esimerkiksi digiveroista. Meid脙陇n on my脙露s suljettava veroparatiisit, my脙露s EU:n sis脙陇ll脙陇.

EU:n rajoitteet voidaan ylitt脙陇脙陇 vain kunnianhimolla ja yhteisty脙露ll脙陇. Se edellytt脙陇脙陇 peruutuspeiliin tuijottavien ohittamista. Meill脙陇 ei yksinkertaisesti ole varaa haihatella vihre脙陇n siirtym脙陇n perumista ja paluusta Ven脙陇j脙陇-riippuvuuteen, kuten 脙陇脙陇rioikeisto t脙陇脙陇ll脙陇 haluaa. Kun kuivuus, kuumuus, tulvat ja myrskyt tekev脙陇t el脙陇misest脙陇 ja viljelyst脙陇 siet脙陇m脙陇t脙露nt脙陇 osissa Eurooppaa, on meill脙陇 velvollisuus 芒聙聯 my脙露s tulevia sukupolvia kohtaan 芒聙聯 toimia.

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Ich wollte nur noch erl脙陇utern: Wir haben einen sehr engen Zeitplan heute, und deshalb k脙露nnen wir leider nur einmal pro Mitglied in einer Debatte eine blaue Karte zulassen脗聽芒聙聯 nur zur Information f脙录r die Kollegen, die eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der blauen Karte stellen wollen. 脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexander Jungbluth (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Was hat die Finanzierung von Photovoltaikanlagen f脙录r Immobilienbesitzer im S脙录den Europas mit Corona zu tun? Richtig, gar nichts, aber trotzdem wurden solche Ma脙聼nahmen durch die im Volksmund Coronafonds genannte ARF finanziert. H脙陇user wurden zu 100脗聽% steuerfinanziert klimagerecht saniert, 10脗聽% gab es f脙录r die Banken obendrauf. Finanzpolitischer Wahnsinn, den deutsche Steuerzahler mit 133脗聽Milliarden脗聽EUR finanzieren mussten. Bis zu 750脗聽Milliarden脗聽EUR darf die EU insgesamt an Schulden aufnehmen, f脙录r die alle Mitgliedstaaten haften. Corona diente offensichtlich lediglich als Deckmantel zur Etablierung von gemeinsamen Schulden.

Die EU schafft stets Pr脙陇zedenzf脙陇lle, die eigentlich eine einmalige Ausnahme sein sollten, doch daraus wird dann im Regelfall ein dauerhafter Mechanismus. Nicht nur im Draghi-Bericht wurde bereits gefordert, dauerhaft gemeinsame Schulden aufzunehmen, was im vorliegenden Bericht ebenfalls positiv Erw脙陇hnung findet. Dass das zulasten der finanzpolitischen Stabilit脙陇t der Mitgliedstaaten und vor allem zuk脙录nftiger Generationen geht, interessiert die Verantwortlichen nicht. Die EU-Kommission hat sich offenbar intern l脙陇ngst darauf geeinigt, dass zu einer Transformation, zu einer sogenannten gr脙录nen Wirtschaft, nur steuerfinanziertes Wachstum beitragen kann.

Das EU-Parlament will daf脙录r nun die Grundlagen schaffen. Dass es eine Milchmenschenrechnung ist, die s脙陇mtliche 脙露konomischen Grunds脙陇tze ignoriert, ist f脙录r jeden offensichtlich. Die Zinsen f脙录r diesen Fonds sind in den n脙陇chsten Jahren so exorbitant hoch, dass sie 15 bis 20脗聽% der aktuellen Haushaltsmittel binden werden. Um das auszugleichen, werden nat脙录rlich wieder Eigenmittel, also EU-Steuern, gefordert. Verantwortliches Haushalten sieht ganz anders aus.

Und genau in diese Kerbe schl脙陇gt auch der Bericht. Es wird darin behauptet, dass die Vergemeinschaftung von Schulden grunds脙陇tzlich richtig sei. Auch wird wieder gefordert, EU-Steuern zu erheben. Das alles machen wir nicht mit, deshalb lehnen wir den Bericht ab.

(Der Redner ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Radan Kanev (PPE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 So it's the second time I hear from the ESN Group that the RRF has somehow sharply increased the European debt burden. But I look at facts and figures, and I see that during and after Covid, our debt has increased much more slowly than the Chinese and the US debt and is now below the Chinese and far, far below the US debt.

So doesn't it seem, at the end of the day, that the RRF was a rather efficient financial instrument?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexander Jungbluth (ESN), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Ja, sehen Sie, wir lehnen grunds脙陇tzlich Schulden seitens der EU ab. Und Sie k脙露nnen es nicht losgel脙露st betrachten, sondern Sie m脙录ssen nat脙录rlich als EU einmal die Schulden, die die EU macht, und der jeweiligen Mitgliedstaaten betrachten. Insofern ist es unsererseits ein fundamentales Anliegen, dass schuldenfinanzierte Programme innerhalb der EU nicht stattfinden sollen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thomas Geisel (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Exekutiv-Vizepr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Drei Viertel der Laufzeit der Aufbau- und Resilienzfazilit脙陇t sind mittlerweile vorbei, und da ist es in der Tat Zeit, einmal Bilanz zu ziehen脗聽芒聙聯 und die f脙陇llt ziemlich erb脙陇rmlich aus. Bislang sind gerade einmal knapp die H脙陇lfte der zur Verf脙录gung gestellten Mittel von den Mitgliedstaaten abgerufen, und es gibt begr脙录ndete Zweifel, ob sie bestimmungsgem脙陇脙聼 verwendet wurden. Jetzt soll die Flexibilit脙陇t weiter erh脙露ht werden.

Ich finde, wir sollten da nicht mitmachen. Lassen Sie uns nicht vergessen: Die Fazilit脙陇t war von Anfang an ein ziemlicher Alleingang der Kommission. Die Coronakrise war nur der wohlfeile Anlass; in Wahrheit ging es darum, mehr oder weniger am Parlament vorbei, mit Hunderten von Milliarden vergemeinschafteter Schulden die Politik der Mitgliedstaaten im Sinne der Kommission zu synchronisieren, gestern im Sinne des Green Deal, heute f脙录r die Aufr脙录stung Europas. Dass sich nicht alle Mitgliedstaaten kaufen lassen, ist beruhigend. Und wir als Parlamentarier sollten den Kakao nicht trinken, durch den uns Frau von der Leyen ziehen will.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dirk Gotink (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, gaat u met mij mee terug naar het jaar脗聽2020, een jaar waarin een strijd werd gestreden over het aangaan van gezamenlijke leningen, ofwel eurobonds. Sommige lidstaten, zoals Nederland, maakten zich daar zorgen over. We hadden zorgen over het opbouwen van een heel grote schuldenberg, maar ook over de vraag of de hervormingen daadwerkelijk resultaten zouden opleveren. We zijn nu vijf jaar verder en we kunnen de rekening opmaken.

Eerlijk gezegd ziet het er niet goed uit. De Europese Rekenkamer was vernietigend. Die kwam tot de conclusie dat onduidelijk is wat belastingbetalers terugkrijgen voor hun geld. We weten niet waar het uiteindelijk terechtkomt, wat de resultaten zijn of wat de toegevoegde waarde is voor Europa. Wat de Europese burgers er wel voor terugkrijgen, is een heel grote schuldenberg. Nederland, bijvoorbeeld, kan maximaal 5脗聽miljard脗聽euro ontvangen uit het fonds. Tot 2058 gaat Nederland meer dan 35脗聽miljard脗聽euro betalen. Deze schuldenberg zal dus nog lang op de Europese en nationale begrotingen drukken.

Het eerlijke verhaal is dat dit ook ten koste zal moeten gaan van Europese uitgaven en dat dit niet met nieuwe Europese inkomsten kan worden opgevangen. Het coronaherstelfonds脗聽en de gebrekkige uitvoering ervan schaden het vertrouwen in Europa, want dit is niet hoe je met belastinggeld van burgers omgaat. Wat betreft eurobonds zeg ik dan ook: eens, maar nooit weer.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Costas Mavrides (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, RRF has been an unprecedented instrument aiming to help the recovery and resilience in the EU after the Covid pandemic.

As a group, we have always stood for the full use of the opportunities it offers for the benefit of our societies. In this specific report, we call for an 18-month extension for the implementation of mature projects, with the possibility for the finalisation of ongoing projects also through other funding schemes. Protecting these projects is a matter of economic and social responsibility, and flexibility and consistency are required from the Commission.

Also, due to our group's leading role, the need to tackle the housing crisis is included, urging the Member States to accelerate investments in affordable housing for all using RRF funds. We acknowledge, though, the positive impact of the RRF on society and the economy, while we are concerned about the gap in public investments.

We call for the continuation of EU support for public investment without diverting resources from existing programmes and, moreover, to create a common, permanent long-term investment instrument.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

Nam脙颅sto budov脙隆n脙颅 nov脙陆ch slo脜戮it脙陆ch mechanism脜炉 bychom m脛聸li pos脙颅lit to, co se dlouhodob脛聸 osv脛聸d脛聧ilo, tj. spolupr脙隆ci, d脜炉v脛聸ru a respekt k m脙颅stn脙颅m podm脙颅nk脙隆m. 脗聽脗聽Gordan Bosanac (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, Fond za oporavak i otpornost va脜戮an je mehanizam kojim je Europska unija odgovorila na izazov COVID epidemije.

Dobra vijest je da se donijela odluka o zadu脜戮ivanju kako bi se novac ulo脜戮io u oporavak i izbjegla nova ekonomska kriza. Nau脛聧ena je lekcija iz pro脜隆losti kada su se forsirale mjere 脜隆tednje umjesto mjera zadu脜戮ivanja i ulaganja.

No, danas moramo postaviti pitanje kako i u 脜隆to se ulagalo. Odgovor na to pitanje daje Europski revizorski sud 脛聧iji su nalazi oko tro脜隆enja RRF-a zabrinjavaju脛聡i. Tro脜隆i se bez jasnih indikatora o uspje脜隆nosti ulaganja, bez jasno脛聡e tko je krajnji primatelj. 脜聽to to konkretno zna脛聧i? U mojoj zemlji je pet milijuna eura vlada dala investiciji za izgradnju Hilton hotela na jednom otoku. Pet miliona eura za Hilton hotel. Investicija koja bi super lagano na脜隆la podr脜隆ku od bilo koje komercijalne banke. Ali ne, uzeo se javni novac. Nije li taj novac mo脜戮da trebao oti脛聡i u stanovanje? Ovo je tipi脛聧an primjer lo脜隆eg tro脜隆enja RRF-a, koji sigurno ne pridonosi ni kompetitivnosti ni konkurentnosti, ni oporavku ni otpornosti.

Prestanimo tro脜隆iti javni novac kao pijani milijarderi i umjesto deregulacija, fokusirajmo se na pametno i odgovorno tro脜隆enje javnog novca.

脗聽脗聽Sun脛聧ana Glavak (PPE), pitanje koje je podizanjem plave kartice postavio. 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, kolega Bosanac, prvo, va脜戮no je pogledati 脜隆to je rekao Revizorski sud, drugo, va脜戮no je ne blatiti svoju zemlju krivim informacijama i tre脛聡e, da nije bilo ovih sredstava, 脜隆to mislite kako bi izgledala Hrvatska nakon razornih potresa, kako bi izgledao grad Zagreb da ga je obnavljala, primjerice va脜隆a stranka?

脗聽脗聽Milan Mazurek (ESN). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, povedzme si pravdu. Oklamali a podviedli ste ob脛聧anov v 脛聧lensk脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆toch Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie. Tv脙隆rite sa ako spasitelia, ktor脙颅 prin脙隆脜隆aj脙潞 me脜隆ec pln脙陆 pe脜聢az脙颅, ktor脙陆 rozd脙隆vaj脙潞 脛聧lensk脙陆m 脜隆t脙隆tom, a tie by v脙隆s n脙隆sledne mali posl脙潞cha脜楼 v脙陆menou za to, 脜戮e vy im prinesiete finan脛聧n脙漏 prostriedky. Toto v脜隆etko je ale jedno ve脛戮k脙漏 a podl脙漏 klamstvo, preto脜戮e vy ste 脜戮iadne finan脛聧n脙漏 prostriedky nezarobili. Tento parlament nezarobil ani jeden jedin脙陆 cent. Vy 脜戮iadne hodnoty a zdroje nevytv脙隆rajte. Ka脜戮d脙漏 jedno euro, ktor脙漏 t脙陆mto 脛聧lensk脙陆m 脜隆t脙隆tom z vydiera脛聧sk脙陆mi podmienkami d脙隆vate, s脙潞 peniaze, ktor脙漏 musia da脜聢ov脙颅 poplatn脙颅ci v Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nii zaplati脜楼 na zv脙陆脜隆en脙陆ch 脙潞rokoch a zv脙陆脜隆en脙陆ch poplatkoch do tejto Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie. Preto脜戮e ka脜戮d脙漏 jedno z t脙陆chto eur je na dlh. Vytvorili ste peniaze, ktor脙漏 sp脙麓sobili infl脙隆ciu, predra脜戮ili 脛戮u脛聫om 脜戮ivot a v脜隆etko toto musia zaplati脜楼 v脜隆etci ob脛聧ania 脛聧lensk脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆tov. A ke脛聫 si v niektor脙陆ch 脛聧lensk脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆toch dovolia neposl脙潞cha脜楼 ako v Ma脛聫arsku alebo u n脙隆s na Slovensku, tak za脛聧nete prikazova脜楼, ko脛戮ko pe脜聢az脙颅 mus脙颅 脙颅s脜楼 do r脙麓znych sexu脙隆lnych 脛聧i n脙隆bo脜戮ensk脙陆ch men脜隆脙颅n. Ko脛戮ko pe脜聢az脙颅 mus脙颅 脙颅s脜楼 do takzvanej zelenej transform脙隆cie. Mus脙颅te budova脜楼 sol脙隆rne panely. Mus脙颅te budova脜楼 vetern脙漏 elektr脙隆rne. Ak to nechcete, neposl脙潞chate, Eur脙鲁pska 脙潞nia pr脙颅de, potrest脙隆 v脙隆s a vezme v脙隆m va脜隆e finan脛聧n脙漏 zdroje, ktor脙漏 ale ob脛聧ania aj v Slovenskej republike musia predsa zaplati脜楼 a vy im ich chcete blokova脜楼. Va脜隆e granty a va脜隆e p脙麓脜戮i脛聧ky nie s脙潞 ni脛聧 in脙漏, len n脙隆stroj na totalitn脙潞 kontrolu a vydieranie jednotliv脙陆ch 脛聧lensk脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆tov. A 脛戮udia to vidia. 脛陆udia sa preb脙潞dzaj脙潞. Preto je tu poslancov, ktor脙颅 tak脙漏to zvr脙隆ten脙漏 mechanizmy podporuj脙潞 脛聧oraz menej. T脙隆to Eur脙鲁pska 脙潞nia, ktor脙隆 sa postupne krok za krokom rok za rokom men脙颅 a transformuje na nedemokratick脙陆 totalitn脙陆 projekt, si chce uzurpova脜楼 v脜隆etku moc, aby mala kontrolu e脜隆te aj nad rozpo脛聧tami jednotliv脙陆ch 脛聧lensk脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆tov. Ale za t脙陆m stoj脙颅me my tu a teraz, za Eur脙鲁pou suver脙漏nnych a hrd脙陆ch n脙隆rodn脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆tov.

脗聽脗聽Danu脜隆e Nerudov脙隆 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, the Union reacted swiftly and effectively to the pandemic 芒聙聯 we stood united, supported businesses and protected jobs. In a moment of deep crisis, we showed that the EU is not a bureaucratic machine, it is a political community capable of real action.

The recovery fund, of course, was not perfect. There were delays in implementation and the use of cross-border projects and ongoing issues with transparency. And despite the scale of the effort, only 13脗聽countries requested loans, leaving billions unused. So the question becomes urgent: what do we do with the unused funds and the bonds already issued? I believe we should invest in our society, especially in defence. I welcome the Commission's recent proposals, because without security there can be no long-term growth on this continent.

But we cannot ignore the elephant in the room. We don't have enough revenue to pay our debts. It is like asking a bank for a loan with no plan to pay back. How would any bank react? In two脗聽years, we face EUR脗聽30脗聽billion in repayments annually. That is nearly one fifth of the EU budget. There is only one sustainable solution: new own resources for the EU. Member States must act, the clocks are ticking. Let's give the Union the tools it needs for a stable and secure future.

脗聽脗聽Pierre Pimpie (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, la facilit脙漏 pour la reprise et la r脙漏silience repr脙漏sente plusieurs centaines de milliards d'euros. Je rappelle qu'il s'agissait de surmonter la crise sanitaire. Cette facilit脙漏, cens脙漏e rester provisoire, nourrit des critiques sur son efficacit脙漏, sa transparence et son contr脙麓le. La Cour des comptes et l'OLAF ont relev脙漏 des manquements.

J'observe cependant que certains voudraient maintenir cette facilit脙漏 sans le moindre retour d脙漏mocratique sur la dette contract脙漏e ou sur le bilan r脙漏el des projets financ脙漏s. La man脜聯uvre est toujours la m脙陋me: profiter des crises pour transf脙漏rer des comp脙漏tences 脙聽 l'Union europ脙漏enne, puis rendre ces transferts irr脙漏versibles. Qui va prendre en charge la dette de l'Union? Sont-ce des 脙聣tats faillis comme la France? Le cynisme consiste 脙聽 rendre les 脙聣tats faibles et d脙漏pendants, dans l'incapacit脙漏 de contr脙麓ler leur dette, de fa脙搂on 脙聽 ce qu'ils ne puissent plus sortir de l'orbite de l'Union. Les peuples que l'Union aveugle芒聙娄

(La Pr脙漏sidente retire la parole 脙聽 l芒聙聶orateur)

脗聽脗聽Alex Agius Saliba (S&D). 芒聙聯 Sinjura President, Kummissarju, meta l-Ewropa sabet ru脛搂ha f'po脜录izzjoni inkrepattiva meta faqqg脛搂et il-pandemija, f'din il-kamra g脛搂a脜录ilna li nag脛搂mlu l-investimenti me脛搂tie脛隆a li kien hemm b脜录onn. 脗聽脗聽Tomasz Buczek (NI). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Instrument na rzecz Odbudowy i Zwi脛聶kszania Odporno脜聸ci jest aktualnie najwi脛聶kszym narz脛聶dziem szanta脜录u politycznego i nacisku politycznego, kt脙鲁rym Komisja Europejska pr脙鲁buje wp脜聜yn脛聟脛聡 na procesy demokratyczne w pa脜聞stwach cz脜聜onkowskich: faworyzowa脛聡 pos脜聜uszne sobie rz脛聟dy, a g脜聜odzi脛聡 finansowo te, kt脙鲁re chc脛聟 by脛聡 od polityki Brukseli niezale脜录ne. Brak zad脜聜u脜录ania z KPO jest lepszy z punktu widzenia interesu pa脜聞stwa ni脜录 uzale脜录nienie si脛聶 od znacznych pieni脛聶dzy przeznaczonych na cele korzystne g脜聜脙鲁wnie dla brukselskich elit i dla stoj脛聟cego za nimi zielonego lobby. 脗聽脗聽Dick Erixon (ECR). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Det som 脙陇r bra med den h脙陇r genomf脙露randerapporten 脙陇r att den noterar revisionsr脙陇ttens varningar om att bidragsekonomi medf脙露r stora administrativa b脙露rdor.

脗聽脗聽Gheorghe Falc脛聝 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, dragi colegi, salut acest raport important 脠聶i 脙庐i felicit pe colegii raportori pentru profesionalism 脠聶i echilibru. A fost un efort comun care arat脛聝 c脛聝 putem construi 脙庐mpreun脛聝 脙庐n Europa sprijinul dezvolt脛聝rii 脙庐n momente de criz脛聝. Mecanismul de redresare 脠聶i rezilien脠聸脛聝 a fost 脠聶i se dore脠聶te a fi o poveste de succes, dar tocmai pentru a proteja succesul avem nevoie de realism.

Ca om care am lucrat 19 ani 脙庐n administra脠聸ia local脛聝, 脠聶tiu ce 脙庐nseamn脛聝 implementarea de fonduri europene. De aceea sus脠聸in ferm propunerea de a prelungi cu 18 luni perioada de implementare a proiectelor mature. 脠聵i da, avem nevoie din partea Comisiei de mai mult脛聝 claritate, obiectivitate 脠聶i transparen脠聸脛聝.

脙聨n final, salut posibilitatea de a crea un sistem de prioritizare post-2026 bazat pe performan脠聸脛聝, care s脛聝 permit脛聝 finalizarea proiectelor prin alte instrumente. Avem nevoie, cu contribu脠聸ia acestui Parlament, de o strategie solid脛聝 pentru investi脠聸iile publice dup脛聝 2026, inclusiv 脙庐n ap脛聝rare, dar f脛聝r脛聝 a fi sacrificate resursele destinate s脛聝n脛聝t脛聝脠聸ii, educa脠聸iei 脠聶i coeziunii.

脗聽脗聽Idoia Mendia (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, doy las gracias a los ponentes por su trabajo y por tener en cuenta la opini脙鲁n de la Comisi脙鲁n de Empleo y Asuntos Sociales.

Nos encontramos en un momento crucial para el futuro de Europa y es imprescindible hacer balance, pero tambi脙漏n proyectar hacia el futuro. El Mecanismo de Recuperaci脙鲁n y Resiliencia ha sido nuestra respuesta europea ante una crisis in脙漏dita, basada en los valores europeos de justicia, igualdad y cohesi脙鲁n territorial.

Por tanto, ha funcionado: ha evitado la fragmentaci脙鲁n de nuestro mercado interior y ha contribuido a reducir las divergencias econ脙鲁micas y sociales entre los Estados. Gracias a reformas inclusivas 芒聙聰como las de Espa脙卤a芒聙聰 se ha reducido la pobreza y se ha creado empleo de calidad en sectores estrat脙漏gicos y emergentes como nunca antes.

Ha ayudado a mitigar el impacto social de la pandemia, protegiendo empleos y empresas y mejorando la situaci脙鲁n de los m脙隆s vulnerables, incluidos los j脙鲁venes y las personas de zonas rurales. Por tanto, otra Europa es posible: ha ense脙卤ado a todos que la inversi脙鲁n p脙潞blica no es un gasto, sino una inversi脙鲁n competitiva por el futuro de Europa. Los mecanismos de recuperaci脙鲁n y resiliencia son la prueba de que, cuando actuamos unidos, somos mucho m脙隆s fuertes.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ang脙漏line Furet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, l'Europe nous avait promis 750脗聽milliards d'euros pour sortir de la crise de la COVID, mais la v脙漏rit脙漏, c'est que c'est une gigantesque arnaque. Bruxelles a emprunt脙漏 cet argent aupr脙篓s de banques priv脙漏es, en notre nom, et cela va nous co脙禄ter 1脗聽400脗聽milliards avec les int脙漏r脙陋ts. R脙漏sultat, chaque Fran脙搂ais devra rembourser chaque euro gaspill脙漏, et pourquoi? Des panneaux solaires chinois? Des voitures 脙漏lectriques hors de prix? Des formations inutiles?

Aujourd'hui, ce plan est d脙漏j脙聽 un 脙漏chec total. L'脙漏conomie europ脙漏enne ne repart pas, les prix continuent d'exploser, les entreprises ferment et les Fran脙搂ais s'appauvrissent. La relance promise n'a jamais eu lieu. Pourtant, Bruxelles veut r脙漏cidiver avec un nouveau plan pour financer une arm脙漏e europ脙漏enne, le programme de soutien 脙聽 la d脙漏fense europ脙漏enne. Exactement la m脙陋me m脙漏thode, faire payer aux Fran脙搂ais une dette d脙漏cid脙漏e ailleurs. Pendant ce temps, notre propre arm脙漏e ne peut m脙陋me plus payer ses factures: il manque 8脗聽milliards d'euros. Nous avons tout donn脙漏 脙聽 l'Ukraine. Et maintenant, alors que le monde s'enflamme, notre pays est sans d脙漏fense.

Demain, Bruxelles ne se contentera plus d'emprunter en notre nom. Elle ira directement chercher l'argent dans nos poches avec de nouvelles taxes europ脙漏ennes. Elle appelle cela des ressources propres. Je refuse de voir notre libert脙漏 brad脙漏e par des bureaucrates irresponsables. 脙聡a suffit! Je suis ici pour d脙漏fendre la France, notre libert脙漏 et votre argent. Il est temps d'arr脙陋ter ce scandale, maintenant.

脗聽脗聽Giovanni Crosetto (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, inizio con il ringraziare i nostri relatori per aver inserito quelle che sono le nostre sensibilit脙聽 nella relazione sull'attuazione del dispositivo per la ripresa e la resilienza, cos脙卢 come ringrazio il Vicepresidente esecutivo Fitto per il suo grande lavoro di organizzazione, gestione e soprattutto programmazione di questo fondamentale capitolo di spesa.

Abbiamo di fronte a noi sfide troppo importanti per non mettere in discussione quell'approccio ideologico che troppe volte ha frenato l'Europa e le sue potenzialit脙聽, come la grande sfida della competitivit脙聽, che se non verr脙聽 inserita concretamente tra le priorit脙聽, non ci consentir脙聽 mai di chiudere il gap con le altre grandi potenze.

Politiche come il dispositivo per la ripresa e la resilienza ci hanno dimostrato che l'Europa, se lo vuole, pu脙虏 creare quegli strumenti di investimento necessari ad affrontare e superare le crisi. Sta a noi creare le condizioni politiche per replicare e sicuramente migliorare quegli strumenti necessari a far fronte alle grandi sfide dei nostri giorni.

脗聽脗聽Nils U脜隆akovs (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, when the COVID crisis struck Europe, the response was often shaky, leading to decisions that proved controversial or simply wrong. But out of that chaos emerged something truly new that seemed to be even revolutionary: that was the Recovery and Resilience Facility.

Europe abandoned its playbook on austerity; it chose to borrow money together to help its Members to recover. It was a huge shift in approach. But red tape, lack of administrative capacities, peculiarities of domestic management or politics in various Member States and other reasons have resulted in implementation below 50脗聽% or around 50脗聽% in many Member States like Latvia.

We have lots of questions about the effectiveness of this 50脗聽% being spent. We have the overall feeling that the experiment flagship response programme is gradually failing. The failure of the RRF would mean for us the failure of our new approach 芒聙聯 that means support instead of austerity measures 芒聙聯 and that cannot be accepted. EU needs RRF type of instruments beyond the MMF to deal with future crises.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marlena Mal脛聟g (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Sprawozdania Europejskiego Trybuna脜聜u Obrachunkowego dotycz脛聟ce RRF nie odkrywaj脛聟 nic nowego, potwierdzaj脛聟 to, co wielu z nas podejrzewa脜聜o. Instrument jest nieefektywny, 脜潞le zarz脛聟dzany i kosztowny, wdra脜录any opieszale. Kamienie milowe s脛聟 nier脙鲁wne, a bezstronno脜聸脛聡 Komisji budzi w脛聟tpliwo脜聸ci. Mo脜录na zaryzykowa脛聡 stwierdzenie, 脜录e cz脛聶脜聸脛聡 脜聸rodk脙鲁w zosta脜聜a wydana nieefektywnie.

Pewne jest, 脜录e ca脜聜o脜聸脛聡 kosztowa脛聡 b脛聶dzie ponad 200 mld euro, z czego sp脜聜ata poch脜聜onie nawet 20% przysz脜聜ego bud脜录etu. W praktyce oznacza to mniej 脜聸rodk脙鲁w na rolnictwo i polityk脛聶 sp脙鲁jno脜聸ci. Nowy model bud脜录etu to pr脙鲁ba ukrycia przez Komisj脛聶 tego, jakie koszty konstrukcji ponosz脛聟 obywatele, rolnicy i regiony.

RRF zamiast odbudowa脛聡 Europ脛聶, pos脜聜u脜录y脜聜 umacnianiu w脜聜adzy Komisji, kt脙鲁ra z dotacji uczyni脜聜a narz脛聶dzie presji dla niepokornych rz脛聟d脙鲁w, czego przyk脜聜adem jest Polska. Je脜聸li uzna脛聡, 脜录e idea instrumentu by脜聜a dobra, to operacjonalizacja ju脜录 nie. Ten eksperyment si脛聶 nie powi脙鲁d脜聜, pozosta脜聜y tylko rachunki i pytanie. Z tym pytaniem Pa脜聞stwa pozostawiam na sali: kto zap脜聜aci za b脜聜脛聶dy Komisji?

脗聽脗聽Evelyn Regner (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! In Zeiten gr脙露脙聼ter Unsicherheit hat Europa Mut gezeigt. Mit dem Wiederaufbau- und Resilienzfonds, also dem RRF, haben wir auf die Pandemie und auf die Energiekrise nicht nur reagiert, wir haben mit Milliarden investiert. Zusammenhalt und Zukunft wurden in Zahlen gegossen, in ein Investitionsinstrument. Wo M脙陇rkte wieder mal versagten, hat Europa gehandelt. Wo Menschen ihre Jobs verloren, hat der RRF geholfen. Wo Staaten Unterst脙录tzung gebraucht haben, hat der RRF investiert, in Schulen, in Spit脙陇ler, Bahnen, Windr脙陇der und in vieles mehr.

Er hat nicht nur Arbeitspl脙陇tze gesichert, er hat Zukunft geschaffen, soziale Gerechtigkeit, wirtschaftliche Stabilit脙陇t, 脙露kologische Erneuerung脗聽芒聙聯 das alles in einem Investitionsinstrument. Ein Erfolg, der nicht auslaufen, sondern weiterentwickelt werden soll, nat脙录rlich verbessert, nat脙录rlich gut kontrolliert. Denn was in der Krise tr脙陇gt, kann auch in Zukunft st脙陇rken.

Ein Vorbild f脙录r eine echte Investitionsunion脗聽芒聙聯 darum geht es. F脙录r ein soziales, widerstandsf脙陇higes Europa. Mehr Europa wagen hei脙聼t, mehr in Zukunft investieren, und daher ein Dankesch脙露n an die beiden Berichterstatter.

脗聽脗聽Marion Mar脙漏chal (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, nous examinons ce jour l'application de la facilit脙漏 pour la reprise et la r脙漏silience. D'un montant de pr脙篓s de 675脗聽milliards d'euros, cet instrument financier est une pi脙篓ce ma脙庐tresse du plan de relance europ脙漏en adopt脙漏 en脗聽2020.

En ayant institu脙漏 une dette commune europ脙漏enne, Bruxelles a ouvert la voie 脙聽 la cr脙漏ation de nouvelles ressources propres et 脙聽 de nouvelles taxes europ脙漏ennes. Au-del脙聽 donc de la question du remboursement, ce plan est surtout une 脙漏tape suppl脙漏mentaire vers une Europe centralis脙漏e et une Europe supranationale dont les peuples ne veulent pas. Nous devons imp脙漏rativement rejeter cette tendance permanente 脙聽 profiter des crises pour transf脙漏rer des comp脙漏tences 脙聽 l'Union europ脙漏enne dans le dos des peuples, en particulier quand on conna脙庐t la complexit脙漏 de la bureaucratie bruxelloise qui rend l'acc脙篓s 脙聽 ces budgets emprunt脙漏s particuli脙篓rement complexe pour nos entreprises, en particulier quand on conna脙庐t la fa脙搂on dont la Commission utilise le versement de ces fonds comme levier de chantage envers les nations r脙漏calcitrantes 脙聽 sa politique.

Nous attendons de l'Europe non pas une dette suppl脙漏mentaire, mais vision et protection.

脗聽脗聽Adnan Dibrani (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! EU:s 脙楼terh脙陇mtningsfond efter Coronapandemin var ett historiskt svar p脙楼 en historisk kris. EU:s snabba agerande med gemensamma l脙楼n gjorde att vi klarade krisen b脙陇ttre 脙陇n vad vi annars hade gjort. Men det var ocks脙楼 ett undantag. 脗聽脗聽C脙漏sar Luena (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, quiero expresar dos ideas: en primer lugar, los fondos deben continuar y este Mecanismo debe continuar. Es de lo mejor que hicimos los que est脙隆bamos aqu脙颅 la legislatura pasada y debe continuar.

Y ahora, en estos 50 segundos que me quedan, voy a hacer una aproximaci脙鲁n a un tema muy concreto: el tema del medio ambiente y de la ejecuci脙鲁n de los fondos en la parte de medio ambiente y de biodiversidad, porque son el 37脗聽%, m脙隆s o menos, y se han ejecutado bien, lo que pasa es que, a la hora de contabilizarlo, se realiza un seguimiento que solo est脙隆 relacionado con las medidas del clima, pero se dejan fuera las cuestiones de medio ambiente y de biodiversidad. 脗驴Qu脙漏 ocurre, comisario? Que estamos en una crisis de alcance planetario: no solamente el calentamiento, sino tambi脙漏n la degradaci脙鲁n de los ecosistemas y la p脙漏rdida de biodiversidad.

Por tanto, creo que deben abordar estas deficiencias de dise脙卤o, de implementaci脙鲁n, para garantizar la eficiencia, la buena gobernanza, el control de futuros instrumentos financieros, y le repito, no solo para las cuestiones de clima, sino tambi脙漏n para las cuestiones medioambientales y para las cuestiones de biodiversidad. Esta es mi petici脙鲁n concreta para unos fondos que espero que puedan continuar, como dec脙颅a la compa脙卤era Sandra G脙鲁mez.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Damian Boeselager (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, thank you for the flexibility, and thank you to the rapporteurs Mr脗聽Mure脜聼an and Mr脗聽Negrescu for the continued good cooperation.

I want to start with addressing a misunderstanding. When the Recovery and Resilience Fund was set up, we saw a situation where within the euro area, interest rates for treasuries were increasing. The financial markets were not sure if the euro area would, in its current form, be able to shoulder this crisis with countries having different fiscal space in their different fields. So it was a daring and courageous huge success to put the RRF forward already with its inception by stabilising the markets.

I think there are a couple of really miraculous innovations within it, and I think we should really acknowledge them. The first one is that we had this unprecedented solidarity by taking up debt, and probably pushed GDP up to 1.4脗聽%. Second, we combined reforms with investment, which is in itself a huge success and should be carried over into the future. Third, we tried to really bind outcomes and targets into the measures to make sure that citizens can really feel this.

I think the learning from all of this is the RRF is a success, and we should make sure it continues.

To je put kojim se Hrvatska bori protiv masovnog turizma, za za脜隆titu okoli脜隆a, za za脜隆titu 脜戮ivota lokalnog stanovni脜隆tva i u vrijeme korone trebalo je pomo脛聡i najugro脜戮enijima, a to je gospodarstvo.

Ja razumijem da kolega Bosanac to ne razumije, da je upravo potrebno ulagati u gospodarstvo i da je to klju脛聧, a hrvatski primjer pokazuje da Mehanizam za oporavak i otpornost donosi stvarne rezultate kad postoji strate脜隆ko vodstvo i posve脛聡enost reformama.

Svi zapo脛聧eti projekti reforme moraju se nastaviti i zato sna脜戮no podr脜戮avam produ脜戮etak projektne provedbe.

Aceste fonduri sunt necesare 脠聶i trebuie s脛聝 adapt脛聝m, pentru c脛聝 au ap脛聝rut condi脠聸ii noi, deci nu cred c脛聝 statele membre nu au vrut s脛聝 cheltuiasc脛聝 ace脠聶ti bani. S-au schimbat condi脠聸iile 脠聶i cred c脛聝 este pertinent s脛聝 existe flexibilitate, s脛聝 existe aceast脛聝 prelungire 脠聶i, evident, un control al banilor, pentru c脛聝 脠聶i eu sunt 脙庐n Comisia de control bugetar 脠聶i am cerut s脛聝 脠聶tim acei utilizatori finali, acea list脛聝 cu firmele care cheltuiesc 脠聶i obiectivele. 脠聵i da, s-au schimbat condi脠聸iile 脠聶i poate c脛聝 脙庐n unele state membre anumite proiecte nu 脙庐脠聶i mai au acum utilitatea 脠聶i trebuie s脛聝 se g脙垄ndeasc脛聝 la alte proiecte. Deci, convinge脠聸i Consiliul s脛聝 se prelungeasc脛聝 termenul.

脗聽脗聽Diana Iovanovici 脜聻o脜聼oac脛聝 (NI). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, vorbi脠聸i de stabilizarea economiei europene din cauza plandemiei. De fapt, nu a脠聸i f脛聝cut altceva dec脙垄t s脛聝 da脠聸i bani unor 脠聸脛聝ri, iar celelalte 脠聸脛聝ri, prin cet脛聝脠聸enii no脠聶tri, s脛聝 pl脛聝tim 脙庐mprumuturile dumneavoastr脛聝.

Ar trebui s脛聝 vorbi脠聸i cu cei doi raportori care sunt rom脙垄ni 脠聶i care fac parte din partidele care au distrus Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i care au f脛聝cut ca 脙庐n acest moment Rom脙垄nia s脛聝 se 脙庐mprumute cu dob脙垄nzi de 8,9脗聽%, cea mai mic脛聝 dob脙垄nd脛聝. 脗聽脗聽H脙漏lder Sousa Silva (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, caros colegas, estamos a debater a avalia脙搂脙拢o da execu脙搂脙拢o do Mecanismo de Recupera脙搂脙拢o e Resili脙陋ncia num momento determinante, dada a proximidade da conclus脙拢o deste instrumento.

O estado de implementa脙搂脙拢o deste programa est脙隆 claramente abaixo do esperado e do desej脙隆vel, n脙拢o chegando sequer a 50 % das taxas de execu脙搂脙拢o em alguns Estados芒聙聭Membros. Neste sentido, 脙漏 fundamental garantir que o esfor脙搂o financeiro que est脙隆 a ser feito 芒聙聯芒聙聯 est脙隆 a ser feito por todos n脙鲁s 芒聙聯芒聙聯, no MRR se materializa脗聽verdadeiramente numa transforma脙搂脙拢o da Europa.

N脙拢o vamos desistir do MRR. Para isso, temos de garantir que os planos de recupera脙搂脙拢o e resili脙陋ncia nacionais mant脙陋m ou ampliam a ambi脙搂脙拢o inicial.
