脗聽脗聽Raffaele Fitto, Executive Vice-President of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, first of all, I would like to thank the two rapporteurs, Negrescu and Mure脜聼an, for their work. Thank you for all interventions, of which I have taken good note. I appreciate your acknowledgement that the RRF played a vital role in stabilising our economies.

With just over one year of implementation remaining, it is clear that certain aspects require improvement. I also hear your call for greater transparency regarding the final recipients of RRF funds, as well as for more effective engagement with stakeholders and regional and local authorities.

The European Parliament has been instrumental in shaping the RRF. Your input provided valuable feedback to the Commission on this unique instrument. We share the objective of realising its full potential by maximising implementation. The Commission continues working with Member States to maximise implementation and the impact.

As already announced in the communication on the mid-term review of a modernised cohesion policy, the Commission urges Member States to identify investment under the RRF that might not be completed by August 2026, and that could be considered for funding from the Cohesion Fund, provided that such investment is not subjected to double funding.

I look forward to our ongoing close cooperation to ensure that the facility continues to meet its objectives with the highest level of accountability and transparency.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Victor Negrescu, Raportor. 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, stima脠聸i colegi, Mecanismul de redresare 脠聶i rezilien脠聸脛聝 a fost un pas uria脠聶 pentru Europa. A 脙庐nsemnat investi脠聸ii f脛聝r脛聝 precedent, reforme importante 脠聶i un r脛聝spuns rapid la o criz脛聝 istoric脛聝.

Dar, la trei ani de la lansare, toate statele membre se confrunt脛聝 cu provoc脛聝ri majore 脙庐n implementare. La nivel european, gradul de execu脠聸ie este mult sub a脠聶tept脛聝ri. Prea pu脠聸ine jaloane 脠聶i 脠聸inte au fost finalizate cu succes. Sute de miliarde de euro 脙庐nc脛聝 urmeaz脛聝 s脛聝 fie pl脛聝tite, iar Curtea de Conturi European脛聝 avertizeaz脛聝 c脛聝 exist脛聝 probleme serioase de transparen脠聸脛聝 脠聶i implementare.

Inclusiv 脠聸ara mea, Rom脙垄nia, este 脙庐ntr-o situa脠聸ie similar脛聝 脠聶i datorit脛聝 unui plan na脠聸ional conceput gre脠聶it. Acum se vede lipsa consult脛聝rii reale a administra脠聸iilor locale, a implic脛聝rii partenerilor sociali 脠聶i termenele nerealiste.

De aceea, grupul social-democrat din Parlamentul European a cerut ca aceste consult脛聝ri s脛聝 fie atent monitorizate, iar planurile s脛聝 fie mai u脠聶or de modificat, pentru c脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 ac脠聸ion脛聝m acum ca s脛聝 nu pierdem proiecte esen脠聸iale precum spitale, 脠聶coli, proiecte de infrastructur脛聝, proiecte 脙庐n zona de energie sau digitalizare.

Reiterez apelul nostru: prelungirea cu 18 luni a proiectelor mature, transferul proiectelor nefinalizate c脛聝tre alte surse de finan脠聸are, simplificarea accesului la finan脠聸are pentru autorit脛聝脠聸i, implicarea real脛聝 a partenerilor sociali. Aceste solu脠聸ii sunt 脙庐n raportul Parlamentului European 脠聶i au fost preluate par脠聸ial 脙庐n comunicarea Comisiei Europene, de脠聶i ini脠聸ial acestea fuseser脛聝 respinse. Dar nu e suficient, e nevoie de o decizie complet脛聝.

Acest raport nu este doar un document tehnic, este un mesaj politic puternic 脠聶i o lupt脛聝 pentru ca 脠聸脛聝rile noastre s脛聝 nu piard脛聝 investi脠聸ii vitale. Este o garan脠聸ie c脛聝 banii europeni sunt cheltui脠聸i corect, transparent 脠聶i acolo unde conteaz脛聝 cel mai mult.

Acum este momentul ca Europa s脛聝 demonstreze c脛聝 nu las脛聝 脙庐n urm脛聝 proiectele esen脠聸iale, c脛聝 sprijin脛聝 comunit脛聝脠聸ile locale 脠聶i c脛聝 脙庐n脠聸elege miza uria脠聶脛聝 pe care o reprezint脛聝 investi脠聸iile. C脙垄nd exist脛聝 voin脠聸脛聝 politic脛聝, 脙庐ntotdeauna exist脛聝 脠聶i solu脠聸ii.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegfried Mure脜聼an, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, thank you very much for this long and fruitful debate, and thank you for the broad support for the report, which Victor Negrescu and myself have drafted and are putting in front of you.

We are, of course, noticing different opinions, the overall support, and let me say that in our report we wanted to present a factual, objective, non-ideological and European report 芒聙聯 a report which properly assesses the Recovery and Resilience Facility with all its merits, but which also points out its shortcomings and draws some lessons to be learned for the future.

When speaking about merits, we are all seeing that hospitals were built with support from the Recovery and Resilience Facility, that transport infrastructure was made more energy efficient, that the education system was modernised, was digitalised, that public administration was also modernised, and that overall economies became more competitive, more resilient due to investments, but also due to the reforms which were implemented and which are important.

So we all see the merits, we all see the achievements, but of course some things we could do better in the future. One of them is the way in which we link reforms and investments. We believe that such a link needs to be precise 芒聙聯 we do not want beneficiaries of certain funds to be punished because the national government wouldn't do a pension or taxation reform. So if ever, in the future, investments and reforms are linked, the reform needs to be precise.

Then, of course, the role of the regions needs to be better taken into account, and the role of the European Parliament. Whenever European money is being spent, the European Parliament needs to be involved properly because this is the only way to ensure democratic accountability and legitimacy, then transparency, accountability and stakeholder involvement 芒聙聯 here, Commissioner, we count on you. We count on the European Commission playing an active role to make sure that we know the final beneficiaries, that governments will publish the final beneficiaries. People have the right to know who received the money. And of course, a strong role for the European Public Prosecutor and the European Anti-Fraud Office.

We thank you all for the constructive work. We thank you for the support which the report has received at the level of committee, and we are looking forward to the vote tomorrow.

脗聽脗聽Dan-脜聻tefan Motreanu (PPE), 脙庐n scris. 芒聙聯 脙聨nc脛聝 de anul trecut, am semnalat cu 脙庐ngrijorare 脙庐nt脙垄rzierile semnificative 脙庐n implementarea Planurilor na脠聸ionale de redresare 脠聶i rezilien脠聸脛聝 脠聶i am solicitat Comisiei Europene s脛聝 analizeze posibilitatea prelungirii termenului-limit脛聝 de aplicare dincolo de anul 2026.

脙聨n lipsa unei astfel de prelungiri, numeroase state membre 芒聙聰 inclusiv Rom脙垄nia 芒聙聰 risc脛聝 s脛聝 piard脛聝 oportunitatea de a implementa reforme esen脠聸iale 脠聶i investi脠聸ii structurale majore, cu impact direct asupra competitivit脛聝脠聸ii, rezilien脠聸ei 脠聶i coeziunii.

脙聨n consecin脠聸脛聝, sus脠聸in pe deplin concluziile acestui raport, care eviden脠聸iaz脛聝 riscul major al pierderii unei p脛聝r脠聸i semnificative din fondurile disponibile, av脙垄nd 脙庐n vedere c脛聝 aproximativ 70脗聽% dintre jaloanele 脠聶i 脠聸intele asumate nu au fost 脙庐nc脛聝 脙庐ndeplinite de c脛聝tre statele membre.

Sus脠聸in ferm propunerea din raport de a extinde cu 18 luni termenul de implementare 脠聶i lansez pe aceast脛聝 cale un apel c脛聝tre Consiliu s脛聝 accepte aceast脛聝 solu脠聸ie echilibrat脛聝, care ar permite asigurarea continuit脛聝脠聸ii reformelor 脠聶i investi脠聸iilor at脙垄t de necesare 脙庐ntr-un context geopolitic 脠聶i economic dificil.

脗聽脗聽Sirpa Pietik脙陇inen (PPE), kirjallinen. 芒聙聯 EU:n elpymis- ja palautumistukiv脙陇line luotiin poikkeuksellisissa olosuhteissa poikkeukselliseksi ty脙露kaluksi. Todellisuus toteutuksen osalta j脙陇i kuitenkin uupumaan tavoitteesta, ja mahdollisuus aidosti kunnianhimoiseen politiikkaty脙露kaluun menetettiin j脙陇senmaiden yritt脙陇ess脙陇 saada omia hankkeitaan kaupatuksi meikaten niit脙陇 EU:n k脙陇rkiteemojen kanssa yhteensopiviksi.脗聽Ep脙陇onnistumista merkitt脙陇v脙陇n vaikuttavuuden luomisessa ennusti jo rahoitukselle asetettu reilun kolmanneksen kest脙陇vyystavoite - kun sen olisi pit脙陇nyt olla l脙陇hemm脙陇s 80 % vastatakseen sosiaalisiin ja ekologisiin tarpeisiin tieteen viitoittamalla tiell脙陇.

Hankkeiden er脙陇p脙陇iv脙陇 l脙陇hestyy syksyll脙陇 2026, ja vasta puolet rahoituksesta on l脙陇htenyt liikkeelle. T脙陇m脙陇 kuvastaa selke脙陇脙陇 haastetta joko rahoituksen k脙陇ytett脙陇vyydess脙陇 tai kohdentumisessa, sill脙陇 investoinnit kaipaavat rahaa entist脙陇 kipe脙陇mmin Euroopassa.

Vaikka elpymisv脙陇lineen k脙陇ytt脙露脙露notossa on ollut huomattavia haasteita, olemme oppineet kaksi asiaa: etenkin avustusmuotoiselle rahoitukselle voidaan asettaa muitakin kuin taloudellisia reunaehtoja. Jatkossa 芒聙聺ei merkitt脙陇v脙陇脙陇 haittaa芒聙聺 -periaatteen tulisikin olla osa jokaista EU:n ja julkista rahoitusohjelmaa.脗聽Lis脙陇ksi Eurooppaan tulisi perustaa yksi IMF:脙陇脙陇 vastaava elin, Euroopan valuuttarahasto, jonka alle koottaisiin erilaiset rahastot ja pelastusv脙陇lineet niin j脙陇senmaille kuin pankeille. Kriisitilanteessa nopeus ja asiantuntijuus ovat valttia avun saamiseksi tehokkaasti kansalaisille ja yrityksille.

11. The Commission芒聙聶s 2024 Rule of Law report (debate)

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber den Bericht von Ana Catarina Mendes im Namen des Ausschusses f脙录r b脙录rgerliche Freiheiten, Justiz und Inneres 脙录ber den Bericht der Kommission 脙录ber die Rechtsstaatlichkeit 2024 (2024/2078(INI)) (A10-0100/2025).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Catarina Mendes, Relatora. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, caros e caras colegas, a Uni脙拢o Europeia 脙漏, acima de tudo, um projeto de paz, de liberdade e de respeito pela dignidade humana.

S脙拢o valores que durante d脙漏cadas nos pareciam garantidos, mas que hoje est脙拢o sob ataque e sob amea脙搂a. Vivemos tempos marcados por incertezas, guerras 脙聽s portas da Europa, desinforma脙搂脙拢o em massa potenciada pelas redes sociais, crises sociais e econ脙鲁micas. 脙聣 precisamente nestes momentos de fragilidade que a democracia se torna ainda mais vulner脙隆vel, e a extrema-direita e os seus inimigos sabem isso.

Vemos em v脙隆rios pa脙颅ses europeus for脙搂as antidemocr脙隆ticas a conquistar o espa脙搂o pol脙颅tico atrav脙漏s do medo, da mentira e do 脙鲁dio. Minam as institui脙搂脙碌es por dentro, colocam em causa a separa脙搂脙拢o de poderes, deslegitimam os meios de comunica脙搂脙拢o livres e independentes, atacam os direitos das mulheres, das minorias e dos migrantes. Este n脙拢o 脙漏 apenas um desvio ideol脙鲁gico, 脙漏 uma tentativa clara de destruir o Estado de direito.

Senhoras e Senhores Deputados, o Estado de direito n脙拢o 脙漏 uma op脙搂脙拢o pol脙颅tica; 脙漏 mesmo a base da nossa conviv脙陋ncia democr脙隆tica. 脙聣 aquilo que impede o arb脙颅trio, que protege os mais vulner脙隆veis e que garante que ningu脙漏m est脙隆 acima da lei 芒聙聯芒聙聯 nem governos, nem partidos.

A Uni脙拢o Europeia tem de ser firme. N脙拢o podemos financiar quem ataca os valores fundamentais que todos subscrevemos. Precisamos de mecanismos vinculativos e eficazes, de condicionar o acesso aos fundos europeus ao respeito pleno pelo Estado de direito, de refor脙搂ar o papel do Tribunal de Justi脙搂a da Uni脙拢o Europeia e de apoiar de forma concreta a sociedade civil, que resiste nos pa脙颅ses onde a democracia est脙隆 a ser sufocada.

Hoje, defender a democracia 脙漏 mais do que votar; 脙漏 agir, 脙漏 falar, 脙漏 lutar e 脙漏 resistir. N脙拢o podemos calar-nos perante a normaliza脙搂脙拢o da intoler脙垄ncia, do racismo, da censura ou do discurso do 脙鲁dio. A democracia n脙拢o 脙漏 eterna, e o sil脙陋ncio dos democratas 脙漏 o maior aliado dos extremistas.

O Relat脙鲁rio de 2024 da Comiss脙拢o Europeia sobre o Estado de direito 脙漏 um alerta claro e urgente: a democracia e os valores fundamentais da Uni脙拢o Europeia est脙拢o sob amea脙搂a, n脙拢o apenas fora, mas tamb脙漏m dentro das nossas fronteiras. 脙聣 por isso mesmo que o relat脙鲁rio, sublinhando avan脙搂os, tamb脙漏m revela retrocessos perigosos. Mais de metade das recomenda脙搂脙碌es feitas em 2023 foram ignoradas ou apenas superficialmente abordadas. Isto mostra que os mecanismos atuais s脙拢o insuficientes para travar o decl脙颅nio democr脙隆tico.

Senhoras e Senhores Deputados, amanh脙拢 votaremos este relat脙鲁rio. Permitam-me ser clara: 脙漏 de direitos fundamentais que estamos a falar no Relat脙鲁rio sobre o Estado de direito para este ano. Quando jornalistas s脙拢o alvos do software espi脙拢o em It脙隆lia, quando os direitos LGBTQI+ s脙拢o atacados na Hungria, quando a liberdade reprodutiva 脙漏 amea脙搂ada por um novo presidente nacionalista na Pol脙鲁nia, o Parlamento Europeu tem de enviar uma mensagem clara.

Aos que tentam reescrever a hist脙鲁ria respondemos com mem脙鲁ria. Aos que atacam as conquistas destes anos respondemos com mais democracia. Aos que manipulam a informa脙搂脙拢o respondemos com a verdade dos factos. Aos que restringem os direitos das mulheres respondemos com mais igualdade. Aos que querem destruir a democracia respondemos com energia, para defender uma sociedade de progresso e justi脙搂a social.

A democracia vive da separa脙搂脙拢o de poderes, da liberdade de imprensa, do acesso 脙聽 justi脙搂a e do respeito pelas liberdades fundamentais. Sem isso, o Estado de direito transforma-se numa coisa vazia e abre-se o espa脙搂o ao autoritarismo, mesmo dentro dos regimes democr脙隆ticos.

O Parlamento tem hoje nas suas m脙拢os a responsabilidade de continuar a bater-se pela democracia nos nossos Estados芒聙聭Membros, com mais cuidado, com maior vigil脙垄ncia, com muito mais coragem e com muito mais a脙搂脙拢o. 脙聣 para este combate que o relat脙鲁rio vos convoca.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael McGrath, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear members, dear rapporteur, Ms Mendes, it is a pleasure for me to be here today for this debate on your own-initiative report on the Commission's 2024 rule of law report.

The EU has increasingly recognised that a proactive approach to promote and defend the rule of law is at the heart of its success. The rule of law cycle, with the rule of law report at its centre, has bolstered mutual trust among Member States and shaped a better-shared understanding of how to cultivate an environment in which the rule of law can thrive.

The report is a core part of an evolving rule of law toolbox. The report serves as a basis for regular debates, both here in Parliament and in national parliaments, as well as in the General Affairs Council. It has also sparked internal discussions at national level in Member States about the rule of law, and about measures that can be taken to strengthen it. Together with the recommendations the Commission has issued since 2022, the report is a keystone for continuous dialogue with 芒聙聯 and indeed among 芒聙聯 the Member States.

I would like to thank Parliament for acknowledging the rule of law report as an important preventive tool for monitoring the state of the rule of law and promoting it both in individual Member States and across the EU. The report includes the Commission's assessment based on a well-established and transparent methodology and a variety of sources. It is the result of a close cooperation with national authorities and consultations of a wide range of stakeholders, including civil society organisations, and it covers four key areas: national justice systems, anti-corruption frameworks, media pluralism and media freedom, and other institutional issues related to checks and balances.

Over the years, the Commission has kept deepening its assessment. For the preparation of the 2024 report, we had more than 650 meetings across all 27 Member States and enlargement countries, with around 900 national authorities, ministries, courts, independent bodies, civil society organisations and other stakeholders.

The report has also kept evolving over the years, responding to new developments and indeed challenges. For example, the '24 report started to systematically look at public service media, the use of emergency measures and the implementation of judgements of the European Court of Human Rights.

And this year, for the first time, and to deliver on the commitment in the political guidelines of President von der Leyen, we will give a particular emphasis to the single market dimension to address rule of law issues affecting companies, especially our SMEs, across borders. This has been a long-standing request from this House. The 2025 report will also look forward to the link between the rule of law and the correct and efficient use of EU funds.

We have also made the framework for civil society a key component of our reports, covering issues such as registration requirements, access to funding, or concrete threats against civil society organisations. The input from civil society has been crucial for deepening our country-specific knowledge. Civil society organisations, as well as judges' associations, journalists or police unions have helped and enabled us to better ensure the protection of fundamental rights and the protection of the rule of law on the ground.

In direct response to requests from Parliament and civil society, we've continued to increase the transparency and the inclusiveness of our joint work, for instance, for 2025, the Commission published additional information about the country visits schedule.

In follow-up to last year's report, we've stepped up the number of national rule of law dialogues organised jointly with the Fundamental Rights Agency and directly involving national authorities and civil society in Member States. We intend to gradually extend these dialogues to all Member States and since last year, as colleagues will know, the report also covers four enlargement countries: Albania, Montenegro, North Macedonia and Serbia. These four candidate countries are the most advanced in their respective accession process, or when it comes to the level of preparedness concerning rule of law matters.

In line with the Commission's political guidelines, we will also include other accession countries in the rule of law report as and when they are ready. The report has become a true driver of positive reforms.

Finally, the recommendations addressed to each Member State aim at assisting and supporting Member States in their reform efforts and to identify where improvements or follow-up to recent changes or reforms may be needed. Member States make important efforts to follow up on these recommendations. This is, however, happening at various speeds and levels of completeness, with some important concerns remaining in certain Member States.

Following the adoption of the report, the Commission has intensified its focus on actively tracking the implementation of the recommendations over time and based on the report, the Commission has engaged in direct outreach throughout the year to discuss with Member States at political and technical level how progress and recommendations can be achieved, providing technical expertise where needed.

Honourable Members, in the EU, dialogue always comes first, but it must lead to results and this is why we match dialogue with decisive actions. And I can say a few words about that in my closing remarks, but for now, I look forward to hearing the contributions of the honourable Members.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

IN THE CHAIR: CHRISTEL SCHALDEMOSE
Vice-President

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Isabel Wiseler-Lima, rapporteure pour avis de la commission AFET. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, notre union est bas脙漏e sur les valeurs fondamentales. Elle est bas脙漏e sur les droits de l'homme, sur l'脙漏tat de droit. Ce qui nous caract脙漏rise avec l'脙漏tat de droit, c'est que nous avons cette absence de l'arbitraire. L'脙漏tat de droit est le fondement de notre d脙漏mocratie. Nous avons aujourd'hui des probl脙篓mes au sein de notre Union. Nous ne devons pas le cacher. Nous devons l'affronter et faire de notre mieux pour y rem脙漏dier.

L'importance de ce rapport, et notre commissaire vient de l'脙漏voquer, elle est absolument centrale. Ce rapport a 脙漏t脙漏 demand脙漏 par le Parlement 脙聽 maintes reprises et nous avons aussi demand脙漏 脙聽 ce qu'il y ait non seulement un 脙漏tat des lieux, mais qu'on dise aussi aux pays ce qu'ils doivent faire. Et cela a 脙漏t脙漏 fait. Le rapport a 脙漏t脙漏, au long des ann脙漏es, am脙漏lior脙漏. Il peut encore l'脙陋tre, mais il est d脙漏j脙聽 un outil fort dont nous disposons.

Nous devons aussi faire absolument la diff脙漏rence entre les probl脙篓mes syst脙漏miques dans certains pays et les probl脙篓mes ponctuels. Il faut que ce soit clair dans le rapport. Je n'accepte pas que des pays comme la Hongrie, par exemple, mettent en avant des critiques contre d'autres pays qui n'ont absolument pas de probl脙篓mes syst脙漏miques. Il faut absolument que la diff脙漏rence soit faite.

脨聵 脨陆脨碌 脨陆脨掳 脨驴脨戮脩聛脨禄脨碌脨麓脨陆脨戮 脨录脩聫脩聛脩聜脨戮, 脩聙脨掳脨路脨卤脨赂脩聙脨掳 脩聛脨碌, 脩聜脩聙脩聫脨卤脨虏脨掳 脨麓脨掳 脩聙脨掳脨路脩聢脨赂脩聙脨赂脨录 脨赂 脨戮脩聜脩聡脨碌脩聜脨陆脨戮脩聛脩聜脩聜脨掳 脨陆脨掳 脨驴脩聝脨卤脨禄脨赂脩聡脨陆脨赂脩聜脨碌 脨戮脩聙脨鲁脨掳脨陆脨赂. 脨聮 脩聙脨碌脨麓脨赂脩聠脨掳 脨麓脩聤脩聙脨露脨掳脨虏脨赂 脨陆脨掳脨卤脨禄脩聨脨麓脨掳脨虏脨掳脨录脨碌 脨潞脨掳脨潞 脨麓脩聤脩聙脨露脨掳脨虏脨陆脨赂 脨赂脨陆脩聛脩聜脨赂脩聜脩聝脩聠脨赂脨赂 脨赂 脩聙脨碌脨鲁脩聝脨禄脨掳脩聜脨戮脩聙脨赂 脩聛脨碌 脨虏脩聤脨路脨驴脨戮脨禄脨路脨虏脨掳脩聜 脨戮脩聜 脨驴脩聙脨掳脨虏脨戮脨录脨戮脩聣脨赂脩聫 脨虏脨碌脩聡脨碌 脨驴脩聙脨赂 脨赂脨路脩聜脨碌脨潞脩聤脨禄 脨录脨掳脨陆脨麓脨掳脩聜. 脨垄脨戮脨虏脨掳 脩聛脨掳 脩聛脨赂脩聛脩聜脨碌脨录脨陆脨赂 脨驴脩聙脨戮脨卤脨禄脨碌脨录脨赂 脨陆脨掳 脨碌脨虏脩聙脨戮脨驴脨碌脨鹿脩聛脨潞脨掳脩聜脨掳 脨麓脨碌脨录脨戮脨潞脩聙脨掳脩聠脨赂脩聫 脨赂 脩聡脨掳脩聛 脨驴脨戮-脩聛脨潞脨戮脩聙脨戮 脩聜脩聙脩聫脨卤脨虏脨掳 脨麓脨掳 脩聛脨碌 脨掳脨陆脨鲁脨掳脨露脨赂脩聙脨掳脨录脨碌 脩聛 脩聜脨戮脨路脨赂 脨虏脩聤脨驴脩聙脨戮脩聛. 脨垄脨戮脨鹿 脨陆脨碌 脩聜脩聤脩聙脨驴脨赂 脨戮脩聜脨禄脨掳脨鲁脨掳脨陆脨碌.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Wawrykiewicz, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, colleagues, I am really happy, as one of the shadow rapporteurs, that we are finally presenting this report to Parliament. Thank you, Ana Mendes, for your cooperation, and all of the other colleagues that were working on this important report, because respect for the rule of law can never be taken for granted. It is not an empty, well-sounding phrase 芒聙聯 it is the very foundation of our European Union. It is a frame without which the Union and any of its policies cannot exist. It is the backbone for our independence, for our freedom and democracy. That is why continuous and credible monitoring of the rule of law is essential to detect cracks in the system early and react swiftly and firmly when democracy is under threat.

Five years ago, the European Commission launched its first annual rule of law review cycle, and since then, I believe that we, as the European Union, significantly strengthened the EU's capacity to respond to challenges and support necessary reform. Our report, Parliament's report, supports and complements the Commission's assessment. In this year's report, we focus on expanding our toolbox, notably by calling for the establishment of a permanent group of independent experts, a 'wise persons group'. Their role would be to monitor developments in Member States, issue regular assessment and sound early warning to the Commission when needed.

For the first time, this report also addresses the vital link between the single market and the rule of law. It rightly highlights that corruption and judicial inefficiency erode investor confidence, hamper cross-border cooperation and threaten the integrity of the internal market. In case of systemic rule of law breaches, we should explore mechanisms to protect the internal market from the consequences of such violations 芒聙聯 violations which undermine trust, mutual recognition and the EU legal order.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Birgit Sippel, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Eine Analyse zur Lage der Rechtsstaatlichkeit in allen Mitgliedstaaten einmal im Jahr脗聽芒聙聯 das ist ein wichtiges Instrument. Aber zugleich zeigen die Ergebnisse des Berichts von 2024 脙录berdeutlich: Wir brauchen mehr als einen Bericht. Die EU芒聙聭Institutionen脗聽芒聙聯 allen voran die Kommission als H脙录terin der Vertr脙陇ge脗聽芒聙聯 m脙录ssen endlich alle ihnen zur Verf脙录gung stehenden Mittel nutzen, um Demokratie, Rechtsstaatlichkeit und Grundrechte zu verteidigen. Wir brauchen mehr und sehr viel schnellere Vertragsverletzungsverfahren. Die Auszahlung von Fonds muss deutlich st脙陇rker an die Einhaltung unserer Werte gekn脙录pft sein, 脙录ber den Konditionalit脙陇tsmechanismus und den MFF, und die horizontale Gleichberechtigungsrichtlinie darf nicht zur脙录ckgezogen werden.

Beispiel Ungarn, allein in den letzten Monaten: Das Regime Orb脙隆n hat Gesetze durchs Parlament gedr脙录ckt, die die Versammlungsfreiheit weiter einschr脙陇nken, und missbraucht dazu auch Gesichtserkennungstechnologien脗聽芒聙聯 Zielscheibe LGBTIQ+芒聙聭Personen. Die Verfassung wurde mal eben ge脙陇ndert, um angeblichen Kinderschutz 脙录ber andere Grundrechte zu stellen, und Transgenderpersonen wurde faktisch ihre Identit脙陇t verwehrt. Ein neues Transparenzgesetz droht die verbleibende ungarische Zivilgesellschaft komplett auszuschalten.

Viel zu oft habe ich im Plenum schon diese eine Frage stellen m脙录ssen: Was muss eigentlich noch passieren? Deshalb noch einmal: F脙录r mich ist klar脗聽芒聙聯 der Bericht ist eine wichtige Grundlage, aber wir brauchen mehr konkrete Aktivit脙陇ten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba, en nombre del Grupo PfE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, informe sobre el Estado de Derecho y, acerca del Gobierno m脙隆s corrupto de Europa, que es el socialista de S脙隆nchez, ni una sola menci脙鲁n. Populares y socialistas tapan, respectivamente, sus verg脙录enzas del Gobierno popular en Polonia y el Gobierno socialista de S脙隆nchez. Y todos tapan la corrupci脙鲁n de Bruselas. Ni caso Pfizer, ni Huawei, ni Qatar, ni Marruecos, ni lobbies verdes.

Vuelven a utilizar el Estado de Derecho como mecanismo para atacar aquellos Gobiernos que no controlan. Orb脙隆n, muy mal, porque quiere que en Hungr脙颅a los h脙潞ngaros sepan si donaciones extranjeras van a parar a entidades que socavan su identidad. Pero con S脙隆nchez no pasa nada: aunque amnist脙颅a a los golpistas, protege a los corruptos, mantiene a su fiscal general aunque est脙隆 procesado y, por supuesto, persigue a periodistas en el Congreso y utiliza a las instituciones contra la oposici脙鲁n patriota.

Algo huele a podrido en Bruselas. No se puede hablar de democracia y pedir m脙隆s control en redes, m脙隆s dinero para los verificadores y m脙隆s persecuci脙鲁n para los que disienten. Su democracia es un peligro para la gente corriente.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandro Ciriani, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, lo Stato di diritto 脙篓 un pilastro dell'Unione europea e nessuno lo mette in discussione. Garantire sistemi giudiziari indipendenti, il rispetto delle libert脙聽 fondamentali e corretti equilibri istituzionali deve essere un interesse comune. Su questo non pu脙虏 esserci ambiguit脙聽.

Tuttavia, non possiamo ignorare ci脙虏 che accade in quest'Aula. Uno strumento nato con finalit脙聽 di monitoraggio tecnico e dialogo costruttivo si 脙篓 trasformato progressivamente in un mezzo di pressione politica e ideologica. Stiamo passando dalla rule of law alla rule of left. Anzich脙漏 limitarsi a fotografare la realt脙聽 nei diversi ordinamenti, vengono proposti giudizi ideologici, definiti standard unilaterali e formulate raccomandazioni che poco hanno a che fare con la tutela dello Stato di diritto e molto con l'ambizione di orientare le scelte politiche degli Stati membri.

Non 脙篓 accettabile che lo Stato di diritto venga usato per condizionare governi democraticamente eletti solo perch脙漏 non si allinea a una visione politica arbitrariamente ritenuta superiore. Non 脙篓 accettabile che si tenti di imporre modelli sociali, culturali e normativi in settori chiaramente di competenza nazionale. Questo approccio mina la legittimit脙聽 dello stesso monitoraggio europeo, alimenta sfiducia e allontana i cittadini dalle istituzioni.

Difendere lo Stato di diritto significa anche difendere la libert脙聽 dei popoli europei di decidere secondo le proprie tradizioni, sensibilit脙聽 e valori. Perch脙漏, forse 脙篓 utile ricordarlo, lo Stato di diritto non pu脙虏 essere utilizzato per imporre dall'alto ci脙虏 che i cittadini respingono nelle urne.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Moritz K脙露rner, im Namen der Renew-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen, Herr Kommissar! Liebe Ana, vielen Dank f脙录r deinen Bericht, der sehr, sehr wichtig ist, den wir gemeinsam bearbeitet haben, zur Rechtsstaatlichkeit. Denn oft ist Rechtsstaatlichkeit so ein gro脙聼er Begriff, aber er ist tats脙陇chlich ganz zentral f脙录r die Europ脙陇ische Union, denn wenn wir nicht gemeinsame Regeln haben, an die sich alle halten, und unabh脙陇ngige Gerichte, die diese Regeln auslegen, dann k脙露nnen wir uns die ganze europ脙陇ische Zusammenarbeit ja auch sparen. Das m脙录ssen wir immer wieder deutlich machen, wenn wir 脙录ber Rechtsstaatlichkeit reden.

Nat脙录rlich sind wir eine Werteunion, das hei脙聼t, wir wollen nicht nur Rechtsstaatlichkeit einhalten, sondern auch demokratische Verfahren. Das versuchen wir hier zu sch脙录tzen, 脙录brigens bei jedem Mitgliedstaat. Der Rechtsstaatsbericht ist nicht nur auf einige wenige gerichtet, sondern er erfasst die Rechtsstaatlichkeit in der ganzen Europ脙陇ischen Union. Wir m脙录ssen viel konsequenter hier vorgehen, und da haben wir auch neue Vorschl脙陇ge gemacht, zum Beispiel st脙陇rker auch die Binnenmarktperspektive mit einzubeziehen: Wenn die Arbeit von Unternehmen eingeschr脙陇nkt wird, wenn dort die Rechtsstaatlichkeit fehlt, dann hat das auch einen ganz konkreten wirtschaftlichen Schaden f脙录r die Europ脙陇ische Union. Es ist wichtig, dass wir das aufgreifen und dass die Europ脙陇ische Kommission diese st脙陇rkere脗聽single market dimension auch mit aufnehmen will.

Aber wir m脙录ssen auch dar脙录ber sprechen, dass wir noch konsequenter vorgehen mit den Ergebnissen, die wir im Rechtsstaatsmechanismus haben, vielleicht auch uns als Parlament noch einmal fragen, ob wir schon fr脙录her mit unserer Berichterstattung dazu loslegen, denn der n脙陇chste Bericht der Kommission, den erwarten wir ja schon bald. Das hei脙聼t, wir m脙录ssen dort auch schneller sein, und es muss endlich Konsequenzen geben, denn es reicht nicht, nur 脙录ber Rechtsstaatlichkeit zu reden; wir m脙录ssen sie auch ganz konkret in der Europ脙陇ischen Union verteidigen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Freund, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, Mr脗聽Commissioner, soon you are going to launch the sixth report on the rule of law in the European Union. What we're seeing is that the rule of law is under attack. Actually, some of the countries in the world where the rule of law, democracy and press freedom are declining the fastest in international rankings are EU Member States.

But just publishing a chronicle every year of this decline cannot be enough. We need to act when the rule of law is under attack. We just went to Slovakia, for example, with the rule of law monitoring group of this Parliament. What we see is they abolished the special prosecutor on corruption, they abolished the National Crime Agency, they changed the laws so that the friends and political allies of the Prime Minister can walk free and so that the investigations are stopped. The amount of indictments on corruption has gone down 95脗聽% since the abolishment of the special prosecutor. When are you going to act?

I negotiated the rule of law conditionality so that we have a pre-emptive tool so that we don't have to wait until democracy is destroyed, like in Hungary. We need to act on these cases if we're serious about defending the rule of law.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gaetano Pedulla', a nome del gruppo The Left. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario, dal 2022 la relazione annuale della commissione LIBE sullo Stato di diritto include raccomandazioni chiare per i paesi in cui arretrano i valori fondanti della stessa Unione europea: libert脙聽, diritti civili, rispetto della persona.

Eppure, in questi anni non si 脙篓 visto alcun miglioramento. Anzi, in diversi Stati i governi respingono le conclusioni di questo Parlamento 芒聙聭 abbiamo sentito poco fa cosa ha detto l'onorevole Ciriani 芒聙聭 e questi governi aumentano le loro violazioni. In questo senso, il caso dell'Italia 脙篓 tra i pi脙鹿 emblematici per l'assalto all'indipendenza della magistratura, l'abolizione dell'abuso d'ufficio, la concentrazione, il controllo politico dei media, i giornalisti spiati, le leggi bavaglio, la carcerazione dei migranti, la compressione dei diritti democratici, come avviene con il recente vergognoso decreto sicurezza.

Dunque, le raccomandazioni non bastano. 脙聢 ora di passare a un'applicazione pi脙鹿 incisiva delle norme, degli strumenti politici e giuridici per rispondere alle violazioni, cominciando con sanzionare sul serio gli autoritarismi, i ducetti e le ducette che vanno tanto di moda in questo momento in Europa.

脗聽脗聽Dolors Montserrat (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, vengo a decirles lo que este informe sobre el Estado de Derecho calla: Espa脙卤a est脙隆 secuestrada por un aut脙鲁crata. Pedro S脙隆nchez es el primer y 脙潞ltimo responsable de una organizaci脙鲁n criminal corrupta, se脙卤alada por la Guardia Civil por ama脙卤ar contratos p脙潞blicos y repartirse comisiones millonarias.

Llevamos siete脗聽a脙卤os denunciando en esta C脙隆mara cada atropello a la justicia y al Estado de Derecho. Es una operaci脙鲁n de Estado dise脙卤ada y ejecutada por Pedro S脙隆nchez para someter las instituciones y aniquilar todos los contrapoderes.

Comenz脙鲁 en 2018, cuando compr脙鲁 su investidura, indultando a delincuentes, eliminando el delito de sedici脙鲁n del C脙鲁digo Penal y rebajando el de corrupci脙鲁n, y amnistiando a un golpista a cambio de apoyo parlamentario. Y ha continuado con censura a la prensa y con el asalto a la televisi脙鲁n p脙潞blica, con acusaciones a jueces que investigan la corrupci脙鲁n de su familia, de su partido y de su Gobierno. Tambi脙漏n bloqueando al control del Parlamento y arrastrando al fiscal general del Estado hasta un procesamiento judicial por revelar secretos para destruir a rivales pol脙颅ticos.

Se脙卤or comisario, esto es el Gobierno de S脙隆nchez, que va contra la democracia. S脙隆nchez est脙隆 arrastrando a Espa脙卤a hacia un r脙漏gimen chavista mientras la Comisi脙鲁n Europea mira hacia otro lado. El informe sobre el Estado de Derecho no puede ser una colecci脙鲁n de buenas intenciones: debe ser un documento valiente que llame delito al delito y corrupto al corrupto, que se脙卤ale con nombres y apellidos a los enemigos que pisotean el Estado de Derecho de Europa para perpetuarse en el poder.

Se脙卤or comisario, act脙潞e antes de que nos roben la democracia en la cara.

脗聽脗聽Marco Tarquinio (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, grazie alla relatrice Mendes, attacchi contro le libert脙聽 fondamentali e Stato di diritto minacciano numerosi paesi del mondo, inclusi 芒聙聭 lo sottolineo anch'io con amarezza 芒聙聭脗聽alcuni Stati membri.

Ai leader dei paesi candidati dobbiamo saper dire con forza: non tradite le attese dei vostri popoli, portate avanti le riforme per compiere il percorso europeo, ancoratele a saldi valori umani. 脗聽脗聽Fabrice Leggeri (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, la Commission europ脙漏enne, soutenue par la gauche, instrumentalise son rapport annuel sur la situation de l'脙漏tat de droit dans les 脙聣tats membres pour imposer son id脙漏ologie wokiste et faire pression sur les gouvernements de droite. 脗聽脗聽Mary Khan (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Ja, wor脙录ber wir heute hier reden, ist wirklich unfassbar, denn dieser Bericht fordert allen Ernstes, auch Abtreibung als Menschenrecht in der EU-Grundrechtecharta zu verankern. Und diese Kommission wird das stillschweigend mittragen, weil es nun mal auch Teil eines politischen Deals zur Wiederwahl von Ursula von der Leyen ist. Die Gr脙录nen haben es verlangt, und Br脙录ssel soll liefern: So l脙陇uft Machtpolitik in der Europ脙陇ischen Union.

Und dieser Bericht nennt sich allen Ernstes Rechtsstaatlichkeitsbericht脗聽芒聙聯 in Wahrheit ist er ein ideologisches Kampfmittel. Wer bei Migration, Medien oder Familienpolitik nicht spurt, wird mit EU-Geldern unter Druck gesetzt. Man nennt das in diesem Bericht auch ganz freundlich Konditionalit脙陇t, es ist aber in Wahrheit ein politischer Angriff auf L脙陇nder wie Ungarn und Polen. Und w脙陇hrend ein ganzes Heer steuerfinanzierter NGOs als Zivilgesellschaft hofiert wird, werden Meinungsabweichler mit Begriffen wie Desinformation und Hassreden zum Schweigen gebracht.

Ich kann Ihnen gerne mal sagen, wie wir Rechtsstaatlichkeit definieren脗聽芒聙聯 n脙陇mlich als Meinungsfreiheit, Souver脙陇nit脙陇t und echte Demokratie; und deshalb lehnen wir diesen Bericht ab.

T脙颅m urazil nejen slovenskou vl脙隆du, ale Slovensko jako celek. Kdo m脙颅sto nestrann脙漏 kontroly vyu脜戮ije kontroln脙颅 misi k sebepropagaci a ur脙隆脜戮k脙隆m 脛聧lensk脙漏ho st脙隆tu, sni脜戮uje d脜炉stojnost Evropsk脙漏ho parlamentu. Apeluji proto na to, aby nap脜聶脙颅脜隆t脛聸 kontroln脙颅 mise vedli lid脙漏 zku脜隆en脙颅 v pr脙隆vu 脛聧i auditu a bez politick脙陆ch vazeb v脜炉脛聧i kontrolovan脙漏 zemi a kte脜聶脙颅 se um脙颅 chovat.

脗聽脗聽Javier Zarzalejos (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, el art脙颅culo脗聽2 del Tratado de la Uni脙鲁n Europea es muy claro, no deja lugar a dudas sobre el car脙隆cter esencial del Estado de Derecho como piedra angular de la Uni脙鲁n. Sin Estado de Derecho no hay Uni脙鲁n Europea y este no es un concepto abstracto ni indefinido y, por eso, este Parlamento ha querido fortalecer los instrumentos de control, de seguimiento y de vigilancia del Estado de Derecho.

El informe se脙卤ala con claridad que estos desaf脙颅os persisten: ataques a la independencia judicial y acoso a los jueces, concentraci脙鲁n de poder sin contrapesos efectivos, falta de protecci脙鲁n efectiva del periodismo, del pluralismo informativo, ataques a la integridad de los procesos democr脙隆ticos, retrocesos en la lucha contra la corrupci脙鲁n. Y s脙颅, es verdad que no todos los casos son iguales, pero tampoco debemos mirar solo a los sospechosos habituales.

Estados que hasta ahora han gozado de est脙隆ndares institucionales s脙鲁lidos presentan problemas muy preocupantes.脗聽Y s脙颅, me estoy refiriendo a Espa脙卤a, con una Ley de amnist脙颅a cuya constitucionalidad sigue siendo cuestionada y cuya legitimidad est脙隆 m脙隆s cuestionada a脙潞n, cuando se ve el prop脙鲁sito de formar una mayor脙颅a de gobierno a trav脙漏s de la impunidad, o la reforma del Consejo General del Poder Judicial, que la Comisi脙鲁n ha instado y que todav脙颅a no se ha producido. O esta situaci脙鲁n ins脙鲁lita en la que el Fiscal General del Estado ha sido formalmente procesado por un juez del Tribunal Supremo y contin脙潞a en su cargo. O la campa脙卤a orquestada por el Gobierno para intimidar a periodistas y para calumniar a jueces que investigan casos de corrupci脙鲁n.

Lo que exigimos a unos debemos exig脙颅rselo a todos y, por tanto, al mismo tiempo que fortalecemos el informe anual, tambi脙漏n queremos exigir a la Comisi脙鲁n que utilice este instrumento...

(la presidenta retira la palabra al orador)

脗聽脗聽Chlo脙漏 Ridel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, alors que nous discutons aujourd'hui du rapport de l'Union europ脙漏enne sur l'脙漏tat de droit, je voudrais vous parler d'un ph脙漏nom脙篓ne qui menace l'脙漏tat de droit et la d脙漏mocratie: la corruption. La corruption ressemble 脙聽 un iceberg. Une toute petite partie seulement est visible et une toute petite partie des fautifs sont condamn脙漏s.

Cette semaine encore, en France, nous avons appris que Rachida Dati, actuellement ministre, avait per脙搂u 300脗聽000 euros de l'entreprise GDF Suez alors qu'elle si脙漏geait ici m脙陋me comme d脙漏put脙漏 au Parlement europ脙漏en et que, dans le m脙陋me temps, elle d脙漏fendait les int脙漏r脙陋ts du secteur gazier et qu'elle freinait les investissements dans les 脙漏nergies renouvelables en toute impunit脙漏.

Si la corruption perdure, c'est parce que nous n'avons pas assez de r脙篓gles pour pr脙漏venir les conflits d'int脙漏r脙陋ts, pour faire la transparence sur les collusions avec les lobbys, sur le financement des partis politiques ou encore pour renforcer les moyens de la justice. C'est pour cela que nous n脙漏gocions actuellement une directive europ脙漏enne contre la corruption qui va mettre des r脙篓gles.

Mais cette directive, les 脙聣tats font tout pour en r脙漏duire l'ambition. C'est l'Italie de Giorgia Meloni qui bloque l'adoption de cette directive en refusant par exemple que l'abus de fonction y soit puni comme un crime. L'abus de fonctions, je le rappelle, c'est un 脙漏lu qui attribue un march脙漏 public 脙聽 une entreprise amie sans proc脙漏dure transparente. Voil脙聽 ce que d脙漏fend l'extr脙陋me droite europ脙漏enne aujourd'hui autour de Giorgia Meloni. Voil脙聽 ce qui fait qu'en 2025, nous devons toujours nous battre contre l'impunit脙漏 des d脙漏linquants en col blanc...

(La Pr脙漏sidente retire la parole 脙聽 l'oratrice)

脗聽脗聽Patryk Jaki (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! Przede wszystkim chcia脜聜em zapyta脛聡, jak wy sobie radzicie z tym rozdwojeniem ja脜潞ni? W artykule 39 m脙鲁wicie tak: politycy nie mog脛聟 ingerowa脛聡 w dzia脜聜alno脜聸脛聡 medi脙鲁w, a w Polsce nie tylko si脜聜脛聟 przej脛聶li media, ale jeszcze media publiczne finansowa脜聜y dzia脜聜alno脜聸脛聡 kandydata waszej partii i nawet si脛聶 do tego przyzna脜聜y.

Az脙漏rt, kedves k脙漏pvisel脜聭t脙隆rsak, mert ma Magyarorsz脙隆gon egy 脙露n脙漏rdek脜卤, a hatalm脙隆t a megf脙漏leml脙颅t脙漏sre 脙漏s hazugs脙隆gra 脙漏p脙颅t脜聭 korm脙隆ny uralkodik. A sokszor a j脙鲁 erk脙露lcsnek is ellentmond脙鲁 脙漏s visszamen脜聭leg is hat脙隆lyos jogszab脙隆lyok gyakran az 脙漏j leple alatt, t脙隆rsadalmi vita n脙漏lk脙录l sz脙录letnek. 脙聧gy a korm脙隆ny minden int脙漏zked脙漏se jogszer脜卤, de val脙鲁j脙隆ban k脙漏pmutat脙鲁 脙漏s autokratikus. Egyetlen igazi c脙漏ljuk az itt, a Parlamentben is vel脙录nk 脙录l脜聭 脙隆llamp脙隆rt k脙漏pvisel脜聭inek hatalma, er脜聭s脙颅t脙漏se.

A jelenlegi magyar korm脙隆ny el脙隆rulja a magyarok j脙露v脜聭j脙漏t, de mi ebbe nem t脙露r脜聭d脙录nk bele. A Tisza a jog脙隆llamis脙隆g alapj脙隆n fog korm脙隆nyozni. Megsz脙录ntetj脙录k a politikai befoly脙隆st az igazs脙隆gszolg脙隆ltat脙隆sban, az oktat脙隆sban, az egyh脙隆zakkal 脙漏s a civil szervezetekkel val脙鲁 kapcsolatban. Helyre脙隆ll脙颅tjuk a sajt脙鲁 脙漏s a gy脙录lekez脙漏s szabads脙隆g脙隆t, 脙漏s 脙隆tl脙隆that脙鲁v脙隆 tessz脙录k a k脙露lts脙漏gvet脙漏si gazd脙隆lkod脙隆st. Kisz脙隆m脙颅that脙鲁s脙隆got, jogbiztons脙隆got hozunk, amivel t脙隆mogatjuk a csal脙隆dok 脙漏let脙漏t 脙漏s a kkv-k napi m脜卤k脙露d脙漏s脙漏t. 芒聙聻Magyarorsz脙隆g 脙潞jra sz脙漏p lesz, m脙漏lt脙鲁 r脙漏gi nagy h脙颅r脙漏hez芒聙聺.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandro Zan (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signor Commissario McGrath, grazie alla relatrice Ana Catarina Mendes per l'ottimo lavoro. In Europa le libert脙聽 e lo Stato di diritto sono sotto attacco. Un attacco frontale, senza vergogna, con un obiettivo preciso: soffocare la democrazia, discriminare, colpire le libert脙聽 delle persone, distruggere le basi stesse dell'Unione.

Non parliamo solo di Ungheria, ma anche dell'Italia, dove il governo Meloni restringe gli spazi democratici, criminalizza il dissenso con la legge sicurezza, si spiano giornalisti e attivisti e attacca costantemente i diritti. Nella guerra culturale permanente della destra, le donne e le persone LGBTQ+ sono i bersagli preferiti.

Il voto di domani 脙篓 uno spartiacque: o stiamo dalla parte dei cittadini e dello Stato di diritto o ci schieriamo con gli illiberali, i sovranisti, chi vuole un'Europa pi脙鹿 buia, pi脙鹿 debole e meno libera, come Meloni, Orb脙隆n e Fico. Il Parlamento europeo deve rimanere la voce della libert脙聽, dei diritti e dell'uguaglianza. Dobbiamo essere la voce intransigente dei diritti, senza paura e senza compromessi.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marieke Ehlers (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, de rechtsstaat is het fundament van onze democratie脙芦n. Maar wat betekent de term脗聽eigenlijk? Rule of law, Rechtsstaat, l芒聙聶脙漏tat de droit 芒聙聰 elk land vult dit anders in en dat is prima. United in diversity, weet u nog?

Toch gebruikt de Commissie haar eigen opvatting van wat de rechtsstaat moet inhouden. En ze gebruikt deze opvatting ook als stok om mee te slaan. Selectief, maar vooral politiek. Neem Polen als voorbeeld: jarenlang bekritiseerd vanwege vermeende schendingen van de rechtsstaat. En nu, sinds de machtswisseling, zouden die zorgen plotseling verdwenen zijn. Het lijkt wel magie.

Over de rechtsstaat mag je van alles zeggen, behalve als het over de EU zelf gaat. En dat is nou juist waar het wel over zou moeten gaan. Neem het Timmermans芒聙聭schandaal. Miljarden euro芒聙聶s gingen naar groene ngo芒聙聶s om bedrijven aan te klagen en parlementsleden onder druk te zetten. Een flagrante schending van de machtenscheiding, maar daar wordt vrijwel niets over gezegd. We moeten ons afvragen: is de Commissie de rechtsstaat aan het beschermen of juist aan het ondermijnen? Who guards the guardians?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicolas Bay (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Monsieur le Commissaire, avez-vous conscience que ce rapport annuel sur l'脙漏tat de droit est en r脙漏alit脙漏 une plaisanterie qui discr脙漏dite totalement les institutions europ脙漏ennes?

Votre rapport nous dit, je cite: 脗芦Des m脙漏dias libres, ind脙漏pendants et pluralistes constituent un 脙漏l脙漏ment essentiel de l'脙漏tat de droit脗禄. Pourtant, la Commission reste silencieuse quand le gouvernement de Tusk ferme des m脙漏dias d'opposition et fait expulser des journalistes de leur lieu de travail. Le rapport nous dit: 脗芦Le pouvoir judiciaire doit 脙陋tre prot脙漏g脙漏 de toute ing脙漏rence politique脗禄. Mais o脙鹿 est donc la Commission quand la justice fran脙搂aise essaie d'emp脙陋cher la candidate qui est en t脙陋te dans les sondages de se pr脙漏senter 脙聽 la prochaine pr脙漏sidentielle? Ou est Ursula von der Leyen quand le gouvernement polonais met 脙聽 pied des juges et annule autoritairement les d脙漏cisions de justice?脗聽Enfin, quoi de plus important dans un 脙漏tat de droit que de garantir des 脙漏lections libres dans nos pays? Mais o脙鹿 脙漏tait Bruxelles quand, en Roumanie, la Cour constitutionnelle a purement et simplement annul脙漏 l'脙漏lection pr脙漏sidentielle au motif que le vainqueur lui d脙漏plaisait?

Dans tous ces cas flagrants, la Commission ne dit rien. Elle d脙漏tourne le regard, elle acquiesce, dans un deux poids, deux mesures qui consiste dans le m脙陋me temps 脙聽 s'acharner contre la Pologne hier, contre la Hongrie, aujourd'hui. Pour vous, l'脙漏tat de droit n'est pas une exigence d脙漏mocratique, encore moins une exigence morale. C'est simplement un outil que vous utilisez contre les gouvernements patriotes et conservateurs.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sven Simon (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, colleagues, the European Commission's rule of law report is more than just a technical assessment, it is a political compass. It reminds us that the rule of law is not an abstract principle, but the very backbone of our Union.

For the European Union, law constitutes the very basis of its existence. But let us be clear: credibility demands consistency. Double standards undermine trust within the Union and beyond. Pointing fingers without self-reflection weakens rather than strengthens our position. The European Parliament's report should be a call to dialogue, not a tool for division.

We are facing rule of law problems in numerous Member States: unresolved murders of journalists, corruption, surveillance scandals and interference in press freedom. The Commission's rule of law report does identify such issues. However, in public debate and political rhetoric they are often addressed in an unbalanced manner 芒聙聯 we focus on one or two problems.

And yes, colleagues, deficits include us as well. If we expect others to uphold the rule of law, we must apply the same standards to ourselves, including our own rules of procedure, which are handled quite flexibly from time to time. We must lead by example, with respect for our own rules. Only then will our voice in the world remain strong and credible when we speak about the rule of law.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Angel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner, dear rapporteur Ana Catarina Mendes, dear colleagues, the Commission's rule of law reports have become a vital tool for democratic oversight. And year after year, they highlight persistent structural deficiencies, attacks on judicial independence, pressure on media and the weakening of civil society. These warning signs are systematically repeated in each edition, yet real sanctions remain elusive. Some governments in Member States continue to test how far they can go, crossing red lines without facing meaningful consequences. What begins as an isolated breach spreads from one Member State to another, to two, then three or more.

Among the most alarming trends are the attempts to silence civil society. The systematic attacks on women's rights and on the rights of minorities, particularly LGBTI+ citizens whose dignity and freedoms are increasingly undermined by hostile rhetoric and discriminatory laws. Let us not forget that hate speech too often ends in hate crime. When the Union tolerates the transgressions of some governments, it betrays its founding values. It is time to act 芒聙聯 the Commission must move from observation and monitoring to enforcement.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I know that there is a blue card, but I am sorry 芒聙聯 we are a little bit under pressure, and since you (addressing Arkadiusz Mularczyk) have already spoken, I will continue with the list of speakers.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gilles Pennelle (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, la Commission europ脙漏enne donne des notes, des bonnes notes, des mauvaises notes. Ses membres s'脙漏rigent en juges, en jur脙漏s et pourquoi pas en bourreaux?

Mais l'Union europ脙漏enne ne viole-t-elle pas l'脙漏tat de droit quand elle 脙漏touffe les scandales du Pfizergate, du Qatargate qui touchent ses plus hautes instances? L'Union europ脙漏enne ne viole-t-elle pas l'脙漏tat de droit lorsqu'elle intervient ill脙漏galement dans les processus 脙漏lectoraux de Roumanie et de Pologne? L'Union europ脙漏enne ne viole-t-elle pas l'脙漏tat de droit lorsqu'elle finance des ONG pour corrompre le vote des d脙漏put脙漏s? L'Union europ脙漏enne ne viole-t-elle pas l'脙漏tat de droit lorsqu'elle 脙漏carte le troisi脙篓me groupe de cette assembl脙漏e des postes de responsabilit脙漏 auxquels ils ont droit?

L'Union europ脙漏enne cite dans son rapport 32 fois la Hongrie, mais rien, pas une seule fois, les violations de l'脙漏tat de droit des socialistes espagnols du socialiste Sanchez. Alors oui, il faut respecter l'脙漏tat de droit partout. Mais la Commission europ脙漏enne devrait commencer 脙聽 balayer devant sa porte avec un tr脙篓s gros balai.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dainius 脜陆alimas (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, the European Union is more than a market. It is founded on the rule of law, inseparable from pluralistic democracy and fundamental rights. These values are not optional. They do not have different meaning. They define who we are.

Now, across the European Union, we are witnessing a dangerous trend. The abusive rule by law, replacing the rule of law. We see it when courts are politicised, when civil society is silenced, media freedom is attacked, the rulings of the Court of Justice are ignored.

Hungary stands as a stark example. It has experienced a decade of democratic backsliding. How did we respond? We talk too much. We don't do enough. This must be changed. We need to act at the first signs of democracy erosion. The Parliament has already demonstrated its leadership. However, the Commission and the Council must stop turning away.

In my own country, Lithuania, we can notice signs of concern. The attempts to control the national public broadcaster, to legitimise corruption, and not to comply with the ruling of the constitutional court defending family rights of same-sex couples. And the Commission seems to step back, insisting that family rights belong to national rather than EU domain. Altogether脗聽...

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paulo Cunha (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, somos herdeiros de gera脙搂脙碌es que lutaram contra a ditadura, contra o medo e contra o abuso de poder.

O Estado de direito n脙拢o 脙漏 uma formalidade. 脙聣 o que impede que o mais forte esmague o mais fraco, que o poder se torne opress脙拢o, que a liberdade se transforme em privil脙漏gio. Mas essa base n脙拢o 脙漏 um dado adquirido.

Continuamos a assistir a tentativas de condicionar os tribunais, a persegui脙搂脙碌es sobre jornalistas e professores e a reformas legislativas que enfraquecem a liberdade.

A Hungria 脙漏 hoje um exemplo de como, dentro da pr脙鲁pria Uni脙拢o, um governo pode capturar a justi脙搂a, silenciar a imprensa e corroer a democracia. Tudo sob o pretexto da legalidade. E estas amea脙搂as n脙拢o s脙拢o exclusivas do Leste. Surgem tamb脙漏m onde menos se espera, at脙漏 em democracias consolidadas da Europa Ocidental, como acontece na vizinha Espanha.

A defesa do Estado de direito n脙拢o 脙漏 uma quest脙拢o ideol脙鲁gica, 脙漏 um dever de todos. Porque quando o Estado de direito falha, falha a Europa, falhamos todos n脙鲁s.

(O orador aceita responder a uma pergunta 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Deputado Paulo Cunha, a cumplicidade da Uni脙拢o Europeia com o genoc脙颅dio do povo palestiniano 脙聽s m脙拢os de Israel 脙漏 incompat脙颅vel com as proclama脙搂脙碌es de defesa do Estado de direito.

E a pergunta que fazemos 脙漏: onde est脙隆 o respeito pela dignidade humana, pela democracia, pela liberdade, pelos direitos humanos, quando se 脙漏 c脙潞mplice com o genoc脙颅dio que Israel leva por diante contra o povo palestiniano?

Como 脙漏 poss脙颅vel a Uni脙拢o Europeia proclamar a defesa do Estado de direito e, simultaneamente, manter intocadas as rela脙搂脙碌es pol脙颅ticas, econ脙鲁micas e financeiras?

脙聣 preciso que a Uni脙拢o Europeia olhe para si pr脙鲁pria e para essa cumplicidade que p脙碌e em causa, de facto, as proclama脙搂脙碌es da defesa do Estado de direito.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paulo Cunha (PPE), Resposta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 A Uni脙拢o Europeia, as suas Institui脙搂脙碌es e, particularmente, o Parlamento Europeu, t脙陋m sido exemplares, de forma permanente, na defesa do Estado de direito, na defesa da liberdade, na defesa e na prote脙搂脙拢o dos direitos fundamentais.

S脙拢o e abundam os exemplos que concretizam esta dimens脙拢o. A Uni脙拢o Europeia 脙漏 respons脙隆vel pelas suas a脙搂脙碌es e tem sido, repito, exemplar na forma como tem concretizado essas mesmas obriga脙搂脙碌es. E este hemiciclo onde n脙鲁s estamos, este plen脙隆rio que aqui hoje se realiza, tem dado suficientes evid脙陋ncias de que estamos ao lado das pessoas, ao lado dos direitos humanos, ao lado da sua prote脙搂脙拢o.

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec (S&D). 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica. Poro脛聧ilo o vladavini prava je zadnje leto dele脜戮no vse ve脛聧je pozornosti. 脜陆al ne toliko zaradi napredka na podro脛聧ju vladavine prava, temve脛聧 ker do kr脜隆itev 脜隆e naprej prihaja v 脜隆tevilnih dr脜戮avah 脛聧lanicah na eni strani in ker na drugi strani Unija sama ne spo脜隆tuje lastnih pravil in evropske zakonodaje, denimo v primeru nadzorov na schengenskih mejah ali pa zaradi svojega dvoli脛聧nega odnosa do konfliktov.

Do tega pa 脜戮al prihaja tudi zaradi neodlo脛聧nosti, apati脛聧nosti in mnogokdaj dvoli脛聧nosti Evropske unije 芒聙聯 na 脛聧elu脗聽seveda s predsednico komisije Ursulo von der Leyen 芒聙聯, predvsem ko ugotovljenim kr脜隆itvam vladavine prava in 脛聧lovekovih pravic ne sledijo ustrezne sankcije. Pri tem vpra脜隆anju pa se pojavi 脜隆e svojevrsten paradoks. Vladavina prava je dolo脛聧enim politi脛聧nim opcijam samo orodje za njihovega trojanskega konja. V tem se ne skrivajo iskreni nameni, temve脛聧 teptanje, denimo neodvisnosti sodstva, svobode medijev in neodvisnih institucij. Zato, spo脜隆tovane kolegice in kolegi, 脛聧as je, da preidemo od besed k dejanjem. Seveda za za脜隆脛聧ito vladavine prava tu, doma 芒聙聯 pri nas v Evropski uniji.

脗聽脗聽David Casa (PPE). 芒聙聯 Sinjura President, g脛搂addejjin minn perjodu ta' instabbilt脙聽 globali imma l-akbar theddida g脛搂a脛聥-脛聥ittadini tag脛搂na mhix 脛隆ejja minn barra xtutna, i脜录da minn 脛隆ewwa.

Qed naraw sfidi kbar fil-governanza t-tajba u f'diversi Stati Membri. Skandli ta' korruzzjoni, ta' serq mi脛聥-脛聥ittadini Ewropej u sforz biex il-脛搂allelin jibqg脛搂u fil-poter akkost ta' kollox. Din hija l-akbar theddida g脛搂a脛聥-脛聥ittadini tag脛搂na, pere脜录empju, korruzzjoni bla ra脜录an fi Spanja, drittijiet tal-minoritajiet mittiefsa fl-Ungerija. U f'Malta qatlu l-inkjesta ma脛隆isterjali li tibda fuq talba ta脛聥-脛聥ittadin. L-a脛搂脛搂ar g脛搂odda li kien fadal biex il-Maltin jiddefendu l-korruzzjoni.

Kollegi, i脛聥-脛聥ittadini tag脛搂na jridu jaraw Ewropa aktar b'sa脛搂脛搂itha. Tajjeb li l-bejg脛搂 tal-passaporti se jitwaqqaf, pass importanti imma pass 脜录g脛搂ir meta tara l-脛搂akma fuq l-istituzzjonijiet. G脛搂alhekk insejja脛搂 g脛搂al aktar azzjoni, tal-Kummissjoni u tag脛搂na lkoll biex f'Malta u fi Spanja tirrenja s-saltna tad-dritt u ji脛隆u protetti 脛聥-脛聥ittadini onesti u mhux il-politi脛聥i korrotti.

脗聽脗聽Katarina Barley (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Demokratie lebt davon, dass Menschen wissen, was vor sich geht脗聽芒聙聯 in ihrem Land und auch international. Heute kam die Nachricht: 71脗聽% der Deutschen meiden aktiv Nachrichten. Das verstehe ich durchaus als Reflex: Es gibt jeden Tag schlechte Schlagzeilen, man m脙露chte sich manchmal die Decke 脙录ber den Kopf ziehen.

Aber was passiert dann? Abschalten, das bedeutet oft, auf Social Media unterwegs zu sein. Dann warten da viele unterhaltsame Schnipsel, von denen man gar nicht merkt, dass dadurch auch Informationen transportiert werden und transportiert werden sollen, nur eben oft keine faktenbasierten, sondern Stimmungsmache, oft genug total losgel脙露st von jeder Realit脙陇t. Der ehemalige Trump芒聙聭Berater Steve Bannon nannte das 芒聙聻Flood the zone with shit芒聙聹. Und was dann passiert, welche Auswirkungen das haben kann, kann man sehen in den USA, wenn Sie mal gucken, wie viele Menschen in den USA inzwischen glauben, dass die Erde eine Scheibe ist.

Das wirkt. Deswegen ist es so wichtig, dass die Kommission in ihrem Rechtsstaatsbericht auch ganz genau hinschaut, wo es um den Bereich der Medien geht, vor allem im Bereich Desinformation und Hasskampagnen, aber nicht nur dort.

(Die Rednerin ist damit einverstanden, auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽脗聽Patryk Jaki (ECR), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 During the previous government in Poland, you called for freezing funds in Poland every year in this report because, as it was written in resolution, laws breaching the rule of law in the judiciary were passed. Now you don't call for it. Do you know if any law in Poland, in the judiciary, has changed?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katarina Barley (S&D), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Now, the thing was that the PiS government changed a lot of organisational procedure to elect only judges close to PiS to the Constitutional Court. Now, the Constitutional Court is 100脗聽% loyal to PiS. The current government is trying to change that, but the president can come in with a veto 芒聙聯 so there is no possibility for the current government to change this. Now, do you want to punish a government that wants to change it? It is not my party 芒聙聯 my party is a very small part of this government. But this government is not trying to make it 100脗聽% a loyal Constitutional Court, as your government did.

脗聽脗聽Katrin Langensiepen (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Hurra, wir haben ein Papier, aber wir haben keine Antidiskriminierungsrichtlinie mehr. Hey, immerhin ein Vorteil. Ich beziehe mich hier auf meinen Kollegen Marc Angel. Menschen mit Behinderungen sind 100脗聽Millionen Menschen in der Europ脙陇ischen Union. Und wenn es um Rechtsstaatlichkeit geht脗聽芒聙聯 und wir haben letzte Woche mit den Aussch脙录ssen LIBE, EMPL und PETI die UNO besucht, da z脙陇hlt man auf uns, auf die EU, auf die Menschenrechte, auf die Rechtsstaatlichkeit.

Rechtsstaatlichkeit sind Menschenrechte, und das fehlt mir hier. Und wenn wir 脙录ber Menschenrechte reden, dann reden wir 脙录ber Frauenrechte, dann reden wir 脙录ber LGBTIQ, und da reden wir, auch wenn es schwierig ist auszudr脙录cken, auch 脙录ber Romani.

Daher brauchen wir in der n脙陇chsten EU脗聽芒聙聯 ich rufe Sie auf, Herr Kommissar脗聽芒聙聯 endlich eine Alternative zur Antidiskriminierungsrichtlinie.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael McGrath, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, thank you and thank you to all the honourable Members for their contributions to this debate, and I will once again thank the rapporteur, MEP Mendes, all of the shadow rapporteurs, and indeed all of those who contributed to the report and all of the teams that supported you as well.

The rule of law report is designed to support Member States in addressing challenges or weaknesses in their systems, and no Member State is perfect, but at the same time, in critical situations, the Commission is ready to use all the tools available in the rule of law toolbox, including infringement proceedings and action under the conditionality mechanism where the EU budget is concerned to ensure that the rule of law is upheld across our Union.

I would encourage all of the Member States, as I do in my bilateral discussions with them, to see the rule of law report as an opportunity to avail of the expertise of the European Commission.脗聽The rule of law report represents the objective assessment of the Commission on a country-by-country basis. I want to reassure Members of my personal commitment to ensuring that the rule of law is upheld across our Union.

To conclude this debate, I would like to emphasise once again that the report is not an end, but a starting point for contributing to the necessary changes and improving mutual understanding. The report serves as the EU's compass, guiding action, accountability and solidarity across Member States. By doing so, the report reinforces the foundations of democracy, safeguards our fundamental rights, and ensures that the rule of law remains the cornerstone of our Union's values.

As soon as I took up office, I participated in discussions with脗聽Parliament here, both on horizontal issues and on the situation in Member States, and I will continue to do so. The Commission is open to discuss further ideas for interinstitutional dialogue that could also be supported by the Council. Finally, to assure the House that the preparations for the 2025 rule of law report are well underway. We plan to adopt it in the first half of July, and I stand ready to present the findings of the 2025 rule of law report in Parliament.

Dialogue with Member States comes first, but we stand ready to act where the rule of law, which is, after all, a binding Treaty obligation on all Member States, is not respected.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Catarina Mendes, Relatora. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, em primeiro lugar, queria agradecer a todos os relatores-sombra que participaram neste relat脙鲁rio e na constru脙搂脙拢o deste relat脙鲁rio, em particular, 脙聽queles que se inscrevem no campo democr脙隆tico.

Senhoras e Senhores Deputados, eu pe脙搂o desculpa, mas o Estado de direito n脙拢o 脙漏 um conceito vago, nem 脙漏 um conceito ideol脙鲁gico. 脙聣 mesmo a base fundacional da nossa Uni脙拢o Europeia. 脙聣 o respeito pelos direitos, liberdades e garantias, 脙漏 o respeito pela separa脙搂脙拢o de poderes, 脙漏 o respeito pela democracia.

Aquilo a que n脙鲁s estamos aqui a assistir 脙漏, mais uma vez, o n脙拢o olhar para este relat脙鲁rio como um instrumento que avalia a sa脙潞de democr脙隆tica dos nossos Estados芒聙聭Membros, que exige coopera脙搂脙拢o, solidariedade e melhorias entre todos n脙鲁s para termos uma Europa mais forte. Aqueles que s脙拢o legitimamente eleitos para este hemiciclo, a casa mais democr脙隆tica da Uni脙拢o Europeia, s脙拢o quem quer atacar os fundamentos da Uni脙拢o Europeia, os fundamentos da nossa democracia e a raz脙拢o pela qual existe hoje uma Uni脙拢o Europeia.

Por isso, a esses eu continuo a dizer: a desinforma脙搂脙拢o n脙拢o pode passar, porque perante a desinforma脙搂脙拢o, a manipula脙搂脙拢o e a mentira n脙鲁s temos de responder com factos, com verdade e com aquilo que 脙漏 essencial. Temos de responder todos os dias 脙聽queles que nos elegeram para resolver os seus problemas. Senhoras e Senhores Deputados, ouvi aqui hoje dizer, para grande espanto meu, que a democracia 脙漏 um perigo.

Termino, pois, dizendo: perigosos s脙拢o aqueles que atacam todos os dias os alicerces democr脙隆ticos, porque n脙拢o h脙隆 sistema melhor do que aquele em que n脙鲁s vivemos, ou seja, viver em liberdade e em democracia. Muito obrigada, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, conte com este Parlamento para continuarmos a coopera脙搂脙拢o institucional que temos de continuar para darmos respostas aos cidad脙拢os. 脙聣 isso que de n脙鲁s esperam; n脙拢o 脙漏 脙鲁dio, n脙拢o 脙漏 viol脙陋ncia, ma sim solidariedade e coopera脙搂脙拢o.

脗聽脗聽Joachim Stanis脜聜aw Brudzi脜聞ski (ECR), na pi脜聸mie. 芒聙聯 Dlaczego Komisja Europejska wypowiadaj脛聟c si脛聶 na temat stanu praworz脛聟dno脜聸ci w Unii Europejskiej milczy o tym, co wyprawia w Polsce Donald Tusk i jego rz脛聟d? Gdy przej脛聟脜聜 w脜聜adz脛聶 w Polsce, podobno natychmiast wr脙鲁ci脜聜a do Polski praworz脛聟dno脜聸脛聡.

Tymczasem si脜聜owo przej脛聟脜聜 media publiczne, nielegalnie przej脛聟脜聜 prokuratur脛聶, pos脜聜owie opozycyjni trafiaj脛聟 do wi脛聶zie脜聞 na podstawie fikcyjnych zarzut脙鲁w 芒聙聯 to tylko pocz脛聟tek d脜聜ugiej listy praworz脛聟dnych dzia脜聜a脜聞 premiera Donalda Tuska i jego wsp脙鲁脜聜pracownik脙鲁w. Skandalicznym by脜聜o wi脛聶zienie bez postawienia zarzut脙鲁w dw脙鲁ch urz脛聶dniczek Ministerstwa Sprawiedliwo脜聸ci, podczas kt脙鲁rego naruszano wielokrotnie ich podstawowe prawa, gdy mog脜聜y korzysta脛聡 z prysznica czy z toalety tylko w obecno脜聸ci m脛聶脜录czyzn 芒聙聯 stra脜录nik脙鲁w. Wszystko po to, by wymusi脛聡 na nich zeznania. Ponad p脙鲁脜聜 roku podobnie przetrzymywany by脜聜 w areszcie ks. Micha脜聜 Olszewski, traktowany by脜聜 jak gro脜潞ny przest脛聶pca 芒聙聯 r脙鲁wnie脜录 po to, by wymusi脛聡 na nim jakie脜聸 potrzebne re脜录imowi Tuska zeznania.

Premier Tusk zadeklarowa脜聜: rz脛聟d b脛聶dzie dzia脜聜a脜聜 芒聙聻zgodnie z prawem 芒聙聯 tak jak my je rozumiemy芒聙聺. Mamy dzisiaj potrzeb脛聶 dzia脜聜ania w kategoriach demokracji walcz脛聟cej. Pope脜聜nimy czyny, kt脙鲁re b脛聶d脛聟 niezgodne z zapisami prawa.

I co? I nic. Gdy ulubieniec Brukseli 脜聜amie prawo, to nie budzi to jakichkolwiek podejrze脜聞 ze strony europejskich str脙鲁脜录脙鲁w tego prawa. My si脛聶 na to nigdy nie zgodzimy!

脗聽脗聽Jos脙漏 Cepeda (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 La Delegaci脙鲁n Socialista Espa脙卤ola ha votado a favor en la votaci脙鲁n final del Informe como prueba de nuestro firme compromiso con el Estado de Derecho. Se trata de un Informe clave para mejorar la calidad democr脙隆tica y que ha servido como herramienta preventiva ante casos contrarios al Estado de Derecho promovidos por el Gobierno de Hungr脙颅a y, anteriormente, en Polonia.

Mostramos con nuestro voto el compromiso con los elementos positivos del Informe: entre otras medidas positivas, pedimos la inclusi脙鲁n del discurso y los cr脙颅menes de odio como eurodelitos, el reconocimiento del matrimonio igualitario en todos los Estados miembros, y la inclusi脙鲁n del derecho al aborto legal y seguro en la Carta.

El PPE incluye un p脙隆rrafo que alude a las amnist脙颅as e indultos impulsados por intereses pol脙颅ticos. Este no es el caso de la Ley de Amnist脙颅a en Espa脙卤a. La amnist脙颅a es una herramienta para favorecer la normalidad institucional en Catalu脙卤a despu脙漏s del desgarro del proc脙漏s y la superaci脙鲁n de todos sus da脙卤os gracias al esfuerzo pol脙颅tico realizado.

El Tribunal Constitucional ha presentado una ponencia favorable a la constitucionalidad de la amnist脙颅a, como es el caso en nueve ordenamientos jur脙颅dicos de nuestro entorno y en otros cuatro que 芒聙聰aunque no recogen la amnist脙颅a expresamente芒聙聰 la han aplicado.

脗聽脗聽Kl脙隆ra Dobrev (S&D), 脙颅r脙隆sban. 芒聙聯 A Mendes-jelent脙漏s t脙隆mogat脙隆s脙隆val meger脜聭s脙颅tett脙录k: az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁 nem n脙漏zheti t脙漏tlen脙录l, ha Magyarorsz脙隆gon lerombolj脙隆k a jog脙隆llamot. Az 脙潞js脙隆g脙颅r脙鲁k 脙漏s a civil szervezetek elleni lej脙隆rat脙鲁 kamp脙隆nyok pedig s脙潞lyosan fenyegetik a v脙漏lem脙漏nynyilv脙隆n脙颅t脙隆s szabads脙隆g脙隆t.

Az EP - k脙漏tharmados t脙露bbs脙漏ggel elfogadott - hat脙隆rozata k脙录l脙露n figyelmet szentel a korrupci脙鲁 elleni fell脙漏p脙漏snek. Magyarorsz脙隆gon az uni脙鲁s p脙漏nzek d脙露nt脜聭 r脙漏sze korm脙隆nyk脙露zeli zsebekben landol. Elfogadhatatlan, hogy a k脙露z脙露s k脙露lts脙漏gvet脙漏s forr脙隆sait Orb脙隆n Viktor saj脙隆t hatalm脙隆nak 脙漏p脙颅t脙漏s脙漏re haszn脙隆lja fel.

A jelent脙漏s el脜聭k脙漏sz脙颅t脙漏s脙漏ben magam is r脙漏szt vettem. 脙聣v elej脙漏n egyeztettem a jog脙隆llam 脙漏s az alapvet脜聭 jogok 脙录gy脙漏ben m脙漏rt脙漏kad脙鲁 magyar civil szervezetekkel. Ezut脙隆n Moln脙隆r Csaba k脙漏pvisel脜聭t脙隆rsammal k脙露zel harminc m脙鲁dos脙颅t脙鲁 ind脙颅tv脙隆nyt adtunk be, amelyeket a szoci脙隆ldemokrata frakci脙鲁 is t脙隆mogatott.

A Parlament 脙隆ltal megszavazott javaslataink k脙露z脙露tt szerepelt az is, hogy am脙颅g a magyar korm脙隆ny megs脙漏rti az uni脙鲁s alap脙漏rt脙漏keket, az EU t脙隆mogat脙隆sokat k脙露zvetlen脙录l a Bizotts脙隆g a rezsimt脜聭l f脙录ggetlen 脙露nkorm脙隆nyzatoknak, civil szervezeteknek, kisv脙隆llalkoz脙隆soknak juttass脙隆k el. Az Orb脙隆n-korm脙隆ny pedig mindaddig ne kapjon szerepet az EU-forr脙隆sok felhaszn脙隆l脙隆s脙隆ban, am脙颅g a jogs脙漏rt脙漏seket meg nem sz脙录nteti.

脗聽脗聽Erik Kali脜聢脙隆k (NI), p脙颅somne. 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, spr脙隆va Komisie o pr脙隆vnom 脜隆t脙隆te bola pri svojom zrode prezentovan脙隆 ako dokument, ktor脙陆 bude objekt脙颅vne, nestranne a na z脙隆klade d脙麓kazov hodnoti脜楼 stav a nap脛潞脜聢anie princ脙颅pov pr脙隆vneho 脜隆t脙隆tu v 脛聧lensk脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆toch.

脜陆ia脛戮 dnes, s odstupom nieko脛戮k脙陆ch rokov mus脙颅m poveda脜楼, 脜戮e deklarovan脙漏 amb脙颅cie sa nenaplnili. Spr脙隆va opakovane trp脙颅 rovnak脙陆mi nedostatkami.

Sme na脛聫alej svedkami toho, 脜戮e spr脙隆va m脙隆 probl脙漏m s politickou neutralitou, ke脛聫脜戮e pr脙颅snej脜隆ie pristupuje k niektor脙陆m 脛聧lensk脙陆m 脜隆t脙隆tom, k脙陆m nedostatky in脙陆ch prehliada.

Opomen脙潞脜楼 nemo脜戮no ani nedostato脛聧n脙漏 od脙麓vodnenie tvrden脙颅, 脛聧o sa prejavuje v tom, 脜戮e v spr脙隆ve Komisia neraz neuv脙隆dza d脙麓kazy, ktor脙漏 by podporili jej tvrdenia, 脛聧i 脜隆ir脜隆脙颅 kontext svojich tvrden脙颅.

Napokon, spr脙隆va sa nevyh脙陆ba ani tomu, 脜戮e Komisia vyd脙隆va svoje 芒聙聻prianie芒聙聹 za realitu a 脜隆t脙隆t neraz kritizuje za hypotetick脙潞, re脙隆lne neexistuj脙潞cu hrozbu.

脗聽脗聽Csaba Moln脙隆r (S&D), 脙颅r脙隆sban. 芒聙聯 T脙隆mogattam a Mendes-jelent脙漏st, mert vil脙隆gos 脙录zenetet k脙录ld: a jog脙隆llamis脙隆g 脙漏s az alapvet脜聭 jogok biztos脙颅t脙隆sa nem alku t脙隆rgya.

Az Orb脙隆n-rezsim Magyarorsz脙隆gon az ut脙鲁bbi 脙漏vekben folyamatosan rombolja a demokr脙隆ci脙隆t, 脙漏p脙颅ti le a jog脙隆llamot, politikai nyom脙隆st gyakorol a b脙颅r脙鲁s脙隆gokra 脙漏s a b脙颅r脙隆kra, t脙隆madja a sajt脙鲁 szabads脙隆g脙隆t. Mindenekel脜聭tt pedig rendszerszint脜卤v脙漏 tette a korrupci脙鲁t, az eur脙鲁pai uni脙鲁s t脙隆mogat脙隆sok elrabl脙隆s脙隆t.

A jelent脙漏s hangs脙潞lyozza, hogy az uni脙鲁s alap脙漏rt脙漏kek megs脙漏rt脙漏se semmik脙漏ppen sem bel脙录gy 芒聙聯 hanem minden eur脙鲁pai int脙漏zm脙漏ny 脙漏s tag脙隆llami korm脙隆ny felel脜聭ss脙漏ge. Az Uni脙鲁nak k脙露teless脙漏ge, hogy hat脙隆rozottan fell脙漏pjen a magyar polg脙隆rok jogai 脙漏rdek脙漏ben, az Orb脙隆n-korm脙隆ny ellen脙漏ben is. K脙录l脙露n脙露sen fontos, hogy a jelent脙漏s s脙录rgeti az Eur脙鲁pai Bizotts脙隆got 脙漏s a Tan脙隆csot: ne csak megfigyel脜聭je legyen a magyar demokr脙隆cia lebont脙隆s脙隆nak, hanem cselekedj脙漏k, kihaszn脙隆lva minden eszk脙露z脙漏t.

Nem maradhat k脙露vetkezm脙漏nyek n脙漏lk脙录l az sem, hogy az Orb脙隆n-korm脙隆ny rendszeresen nem hajtja v脙漏gre az EU B脙颅r脙鲁s脙隆g脙隆nak 脙颅t脙漏leteit 芒聙聯 mert ez al脙隆脙隆ssa az Uni脙鲁 ir脙隆nti bizalmat, 脙漏s a bels脜聭 piac m脜卤k脙露d脙漏s脙漏t.

Kulcsfontoss脙隆g脙潞, hogy a jelent脙漏s ki脙隆ll amellett, hogy Magyarorsz脙隆g is csatlakozzon az Eur脙鲁pai 脙聹gy脙漏szs脙漏ghez. Tov脙隆bb脙隆 amellett, hogy ha a korm脙隆ny lebontja a jog脙隆llamot, az EU-forr脙隆sokat k脙露zvetlen脙录l a helyi k脙露z脙露ss脙漏gekhez, civil szervezetekhez 脙漏s kisv脙隆llalkoz脙隆sokhoz kell eljuttatni 芒聙聯 ami a DK r脙漏gi k脙露vetel脙漏se. A magyar embereknek j脙隆r az uni脙鲁s t脙隆mogat脙隆s 芒聙聯 f脙录ggetlen脙录l att脙鲁l, mit tesz a korm脙隆ny.

脗聽脗聽Lor脙隆nt Vincze (PPE), 脙颅r脙隆sban. 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Biztos 脙職r!

A sokadik jog脙隆llamis脙隆gi vit脙隆ban ism脙漏telten meg kell 脙隆llap脙颅tanom, hogy a Parlament 脙漏s a Bizotts脙隆g gyakran elbesz脙漏l egym脙隆s mellett sz脙隆mos fontos k脙漏rd脙漏sben. Ez k脙录l脙露n脙露sen igaz az 脜聭shonos nemzeti 脙漏s nyelvi kisebbs脙漏gek eset脙漏ben.

A Parlament 脙漏vek 脙鲁ta szorgalmazza, hogy a Bizotts脙隆g terjessze ki a jog脙隆llamis脙隆gi jelent脙漏st minden uni脙鲁s alap脙漏rt脙漏kre, amelyek az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁r脙鲁l sz脙鲁l脙鲁 szerz脜聭d脙漏s 2. cikk脙漏ben szerepelnek 芒聙聯 bele脙漏rtve a kisebbs脙漏gek v脙漏delm脙漏t is. En脙漏lk脙录l nem alkothatunk teljes k脙漏pet arr脙鲁l, hogy a tag脙隆llamok mennyiben tartj脙隆k be nemzetk脙露zi k脙露telezetts脙漏geiket 脙漏s v脙隆llal脙隆saikat.

Kir脙颅v脙鲁nak tartom azt is, hogy a Bizotts脙隆g elt脙漏r脜聭 m脙鲁don k脙露zel脙颅t k脙录l脙露nb脙露z脜聭 kisebbs脙漏gi csoportokhoz. Az int脙漏zm脙漏nyi k脙露z脙露ny a nemzeti 脙漏s nyelvi kisebbs脙漏gek 脙录gyeivel szemben megnyitja az utat a diszkrimin脙隆ci脙鲁 脙漏s a gy脜卤l脙露let-b脜卤ncselekm脙漏nyek elterjed脙漏se el脜聭tt.

B脙隆r a parlamenti jelent脙漏s t脙露bb t脙潞lz脙隆s脙隆val 芒聙聯 p脙漏ld脙隆ul az abortusz jogk脙漏nt val脙鲁 felv脙漏tel脙漏vel az Alapjogi Chart脙隆ba 芒聙聯 nem 脙漏rtek egyet, m脙漏gis elv脙隆rom, sok k脙漏pvisel脜聭t脙隆rsammal egyetemben, hogy a Bizotts脙隆g legyen k脙露vetkezetes, amikor az emberi jogok 脙漏s a kisebbs脙漏gv脙漏delem k脙漏rd脙漏se ker脙录l napirendre. A nemzeti kisebbs脙漏gek elleni megk脙录l脙露nb脙露ztet脙漏s tilalma ugyanis szerepel az Alapjogi Chart脙隆ban, a koppenh脙隆gai krit脙漏riumokban, 脙漏s az Eur脙鲁pa Tan脙隆cs is egyezm脙漏nyekkel v脙漏di jogaikat.

A nemzeti kisebbs脙漏gek v脙漏delm脙漏nek term脙漏szetesnek kellene lennie az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁 sz脙隆m脙隆ra is 芒聙聯 hiszen nem 脙露rd脙露gt脜聭l val脙鲁 k脙漏rd脙漏sekr脜聭l, hanem csaknem 50 milli脙鲁 eur脙鲁pai polg脙隆r alapvet脜聭 jogair脙鲁l van sz脙鲁.

12. 2023 and 2024 reports on Montenegro (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the report by Marjan脗聽脜聽arec, on behalf of the Committee on Foreign Affairs, on the 2023 and 2024 Commission reports on Montenegro (2025/2020(INI)) (A10-0093/2025).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marjan 脜聽arec, Poro脛聧evalec. 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica, spo脜隆tovana komisarka Marta Kos, spo脜隆tovane kolegice in kolegi, pred vami je poro脛聧ilo o napredku 脛聦rne gore v obdobju 2023芒聙聯2024. Poro脛聧ilo ugotavlja, da 脛聦rna gora ostaja vodilna kandidatka v procesu 脜隆iritve in je uspela ohraniti visoko podporo prebivalstva za vstop v Evropsko unijo, ki mestoma dosega celo 80 odstotkov. Prav tako je pomembno, da so pri sprejemanju potrebne zakonodaje sodelovale tako stranke koalicije kakor tudi opozicije. To ka脜戮e na visoko stopnjo zavedanja, da je za 脛聦rno goro edino evropska pot prava pot in druge alternative ni.

Pozdravljamo vse dosedanje napore pri sprejemanju zakonodaje za zapiranje poglavij. Kljub za脛聧asnemu zadr脜戮anju soglasja s strani Hrva脜隆ke pri poglavju 31 je pomembno, da bilateralni pogovori med dr脜戮avama potekajo. Hkrati pa 脜戮elim poudariti, da bilateralna vpra脜隆anja ne smejo zaustavljati procesa, kakor ga niso v primeru zadnje 脜隆iritve, 脛聧eprav nekatera vpra脜隆anja 脜隆e danes 脛聧akajo na dokon脛聧no re脜隆itev. Dosedanji uspehi 脛聦rne gore dajejo dobro podlago za re脜隆evanje prihodnjih izzivov, ki pa jih ni malo niti ne bodo lahki. Boj z organiziranim kriminalom in korupcijo, reforma pravosodja, omogo脛聧anje svobode medijev, prepre脛聧evanje vpliva tretjih dr脜戮av so izjemnega pomena za doseganje demokrati脛聧nih standardov.

脛聦rna gora opredeljuje varovanje okolja kot ustavno kategorijo, kar je pohvalno, a po drugi strani zahteva konkretna dejanja. Med prednostnimi nalogami pa mora biti tudi reforma volilne zakonodaje, saj pogoste volitve v razli脛聧nih ob脛聧inah hromijo delo tako lokalnih skupnosti kakor tudi dr脜戮ave kot celote. Lokalne volitve morajo biti izvedene po celi dr脜戮avi na isti dan, lokalna oblast pa mora biti konstituirana takoj po volitvah. 脛聦rna gora je majhna dr脜戮ava in to ima za posledico, da volilna kampanja v posamezni ob脛聧ini postane nacionalna tema. To pa povzro脛聧i, da se politika ukvarja sama s seboj namesto s pomembnimi temami.

Na razvoj 脛聦rne gore bo pozitivno vplivalo tudi izpla脛聧ilo avansa s strani Evropske komisije iz na脛聧rta za rast in razvoj v vi脜隆ini ve脛聧 kot 26 milijonov evrov skupaj z izdatnimi sredstvi ve脛聧 sto milijonov iz programa IPA 3, kar bo omogo脛聧ilo izvajanje novih projektov.

Izpostavil sem nekaj podro脛聧ij, ki jih obravnava poro脛聧ilo, v katerem sem sku脜隆al biti kar najbolj objektiven. Dovolite mi, da se iskreno zahvalim vsem poro脛聧evalcem politi脛聧nih skupin za konstruktivno sodelovanje pri usklajevanju amandmajev, zaposlenim na sekretariatu Odbora za zunanjo politiko za odli脛聧no tehni脛聧no pripravo, nenazadnje pa tudi vsem akterjem v 脛聦rni gori, tako s strani politike, lokalnih skupnosti kakor tudi civilne dru脜戮be za tvorno sodelovanje. Proces integracije je skupno delo in terja trdno zavezanost vseh dele脜戮nikov. Vsem 脜戮elim uspe脜隆no nadaljevanje procesa. Spo脜隆tovane poslanke in poslanci, vas pa prosim za va脜隆o podporo pri glasovanju in se 脜戮e vnaprej zahvaljujem za konstruktivno razpravo.
