脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear honourable Members of the European Parliament, it was good to hear the report on Montenegro in my mother tongue. And thank you to the rapporteur, Marjan 脜聽arec, for your excellent work on the report, and I expressed my agreement with its findings.

Let me begin by reaffirming a simple yet profound truth: enlargement remains the most successful external EU policy, but in the current geopolitical context, it has also become an important security imperative. It is about shaping the future of our continent, strengthening our security and reinforcing our shared prosperity. It is about the unification of Europe under common values and principles. Europe today stands at a crossroads.

The challenges we face 芒聙聯 geopolitical tensions, hybrid threats, energy security and economic resilience 芒聙聯 demand unity and strength. I firmly believe that EU enlargement is a project of unification that we must pursue with even greater determination in a divided world. As President von der Leyen has said, history is now calling on us to complete our Union.

With every enlargement, those who feared it would weaken us were proven wrong. Enlargement has, in fact, made us stronger. It brought stability and I am confident that continued enlargement will have the same positive impact. I am delighted to continue this work with all of you hand in hand, as it is clearly in our shared interest.

Montenegro remains the frontrunner in the EU accession process, with all 33 screening chapters open for negotiations and 6 of them provisionally closed. The Government of Montenegro is fully committed to making progress on the EU path, and the European Commission is equally committed to supporting Montenegro's ambitious plan to provisionally close all negotiating chapters by the end of 2026. In fact, last year Montenegro met the interim benchmarks for rule of law chapters 23 and 24. This enabled the setting of closing benchmarks for these chapters and opened the way to provisionally close others. We hope to close chapter 5 on public procurement later this month.

Still, Montenegro will need to meet all closing benchmarks under the rule of law chapters to complete the accession negotiations. While our current focus is on the technical chapters, the fundamentals continue to determine the pace of progress.

While we encourage Montenegro to move forward, we also recognise certain difficulties on the path ahead, many of which are noted in your report. I will not repeat them all, but allow me to highlight a few issues that have drawn particular attention recently.

First, I hope for a swift resolution of outstanding bilateral issues that would enable the provisional closing of chapter 31 as soon as possible. Montenegro's alignment with EU visa policy is both a commitment under a growth plan and a closing benchmark under chapter 24. Recent decisions to add more countries are concerning and suggest backsliding.

Montenegro should quickly adopt corrective measures to ensure increased alignment with EU visa policy. The agreements with the United Arab Emirates have dominated political discourse in Montenegro. I have clearly outlined our position in my letter to President Milatovi脛聡. In simple terms, the agreement, prima facie, does not contain detailed provisions that as such would contradict EU law. However, the manner in which the agreement is implemented will need to be carefully managed. Transparency, respect for public procurement rules, State aid, investor selection and other requirements from the acquis will be of utmost importance.

On a positive note, I wish to recall that Montenegro is a reliable CFSP partner, and has maintained 100脗聽% alignment with EU positions and sanctions.

I will conclude my intervention by reiterating my appreciation for the high quality of your report, which reflects our shared commitment to Montenegro's European path. At this critical juncture, let us continue working together with clarity, consistency and conviction to support Montenegro in turning aspiration into achievement and enlargement into enduring unity.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, I want to underline what the Commissioner and also our rapporteur, 脜聽arec, said. Yes, Montenegro is a frontrunner towards EU accession with a fully committed, pro-European government. There is also strong support from society: more than 75脗聽% of the people back EU integration, and European legislation is supported by the governmental parties, but also by the opposition. The country has been using the euro since 2002, and Montenegro has been a reliable partner in NATO since 2017.

I was in Montenegro last December, at the time when there was controversy regarding the retirement of a Constitutional Court judge. The Prime Minister took on board the advice of EU diplomats and also politicians, and he agreed to involve the Venice Commission. After their proposal, they found an acceptable and effective solution.

So, what I want to say is that the report shows this progress, but it also includes negative points we have to work on, like corruption or a better electoral and judicial system.

So finally, it recognises the significant progress. This is the most important thing: Montenegro is on its path for reforms and strong commitment to EU integration.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jaroslav B脜戮och, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, Montenegro's progress on its European path is encouraging and deserves due recognition. The reforms undertaken so far demonstrate a clear commitment to meeting the standards required for EU accession.

The European Union must remain an open and credible community, one that is ready to welcome new members who express genuine interest and meet the necessary criteria. Openness, however, must go hand in hand with the firm insistence on respect for our shared rules and values.

As the Member States, we are well positioned to define realistic expectations for candidate countries, having ourselves experienced the challenges of the accession process. It is essential that we offer our support and guidance while also ensuring that we do not place demands on others that exceed what we have achieved or what we are prepared to achieve ourselves.

While certain adjustments to the enlargement process may be worth considering, the principle of unanimity must remain untouched. It is a cornerstone of mutual trust among Member States and a safeguard of national sovereignty.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脜聻erban Dimitrie Sturdza, 脙庐n numele grupului ECR. 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, stima脠聸i colegi, 脙庐ntr-un moment 脙庐n care marile puteri globale se 脙庐ntrec 脙庐n extinderea influen脠聸ei lor, Uniunea European脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 recunoasc脛聝 faptul c脛聝 extinderea nu este doar o oportunitate, ci o necesitate strategic脛聝.

Avem nevoie s脛聝 脙庐ncorpor脛聝m Balcanii de Vest 脙庐n proiectul european pentru stabilitatea, prosperitatea 脠聶i securitatea continentului nostru. Muntenegru este cel mai avansat stat candidat care se bucur脛聝 de un sprijin public ferm pentru aderarea la Uniunea European脛聝 din partea poporului muntenegrean 脠聶i care a 脙庐nregistrat progrese substan脠聸iale 脙庐n reformele privind statul de drept.

Totodat脛聝, reformele privind justi脠聸ia, profesionalizarea administra脠聸iei, libertatea presei 脠聶i combaterea corup脠聸iei trebuie s脛聝 r脛聝m脙垄n脛聝 priorit脛聝脠聸i, dar aceste eforturi trebuie 脙庐nso脠聸ite de un mesaj clar din partea noastr脛聝.

Dac脛聝 partenerii no脠聶tri 脙庐ndeplinesc criteriile, Uniunea European脛聝 trebuie s脛聝-脠聶i respecte angajamentele. Pentru ca procesul de extindere s脛聝-脠聶i p脛聝streze credibilitatea, integrarea Muntenegrului 脙庐n familia Uniunii Europene trebuie s脛聝 aib脛聝 loc c脙垄t mai cur脙垄nd posibil.

脗聽脗聽Vladimir Prebili脛聧, v imenu skupine Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Gospod predsednik, spo脜隆tovana komisarka, cenjene kolegice in kolegi. 脛聦rna gora je v zadnjih letih, odkar se je znebila re脜戮ima pod vodstvom DPS, naredila nekaj impresivnih korakov naprej proti Evropski uniji. Kljub temu se 脛聦rna gora 脜隆e vedno soo脛聧a z dvema klju脛聧nima izzivoma, polarizacijo in korupcijo.

Pri napovedanih poslih z Zdru脜戮enimi arabskimi emirati v Ulcinju imamo me脜隆ane ob脛聧utke, saj se govori o oddaji poslov brez javnih naro脛聧il in spo脜隆tovanja postopkov iz naslova transparentnosti. Ljudem in oblastem 脛聦rne gore 脜戮elim poslati jasno sporo脛聧ilo: 脛聧im prej vas 脜戮elimo v Evropski uniji. Toda da bi se to zgodilo, moramo videti rezultate: reforme in upo脜隆tevanje pravne dr脜戮ave, predvsem pa konkretne premike v boju proti korupciji. DPS in vsi tisti, ki so zlorabili dr脜戮avne funkcije in javna sredstva ter 脛聦rno goro spremenili v ugrabljeno dr脜戮avo, pa morajo za tako ravnanje odgovarjati. Zato je sedaj 脛聧as za u脛聧inkovito in neodvisno delovanje sodstva.

脗聽脗聽Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, ovo izvje脜隆脛聡e je potpora suverenoj i proeuropskoj Crnoj Gori.

Na脜隆a ambicija da Crna Gora u脛聭e u Europsku uniju, a za to je potrebno ispuniti sve reforme i otkloniti snage koje Crnu Goru vuku u tzv. srpski svijet sa svim problemima koje taj antieuropski, antizapadni koncept predstavlja. Dio vlasti u Crnoj Gori i dalje ide u smjeru tzv. srpskog svijeta, 脜隆to je vrlo precizno identificirano u ovom izvje脜隆脛聡u, i dok je tako poglavlje 31. ostat 脛聡e otvoreno jer ne 脜戮elimo trojanskog konja u Europskoj uniji, ali je va脜戮no isto tako re脛聡i da dio vlasti, dio oporbe i veliki dio gra脛聭ana 脜戮eli Crnu Goru u Europskoj uniji i zato 脛聡emo mi podr脜戮ati da na slijede脛聡oj me脛聭uvladinoj konferenciji se zatvori poglavlje 5.

Crna Gora mo脜戮e uspjeti, mi 脜戮elimo da uspije, da Crna Gora uspije ne smije biti pod utjecajem Beograda, nego doista biti suverena neovisna europska Crna Gora.

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec (S&D). 芒聙聯 Gospod predsednik, spo脜隆tovana komisarka, gospa Marta Kos. V poplavi slabih in zaskrbljujo脛聧ih novic iz sveta je dana脜隆nja debata o napredku 脛聦rne gore na poti k Evropski uniji svetla izjema in pozitivna zgodba. Ostaja vodilna dr脜戮ava na poti evropske integracije med dr脜戮avami Zahodnega Balkana, z usklajeno zunanjo politiko z Unijo, z zavezanostjo mednarodnemu pravu in ob脛聧utnim napredkom na podro脛聧ju medijske svobode, volilne zakonodaje ter boja proti korupciji in organiziranemu kriminalu.

To ob脛聧utijo tudi dr脜戮avljanke in dr脜戮avljani 脛聦rne gore, ki so v veliki meri naklonjeni 脛聧lanstvu v Evropski uniji in kjer privr脜戮encem antievropskega sentimenta ni uspelo zarezati 脜隆ir脜隆e v javno mnenje.

In ravno to, spo脜隆tovana komisarka, kolegice in kolegi, je tisto, za kar se in se moramo boriti v Evropskem parlamentu. Za prihodnost miru, stabilnosti in sodelovanja in ne razdvajanja in izklju脛聧evanja. Zato je 脜隆iritev Evropske unije na Zahodni Balkan na脜隆a edina skupna mo脜戮nost in prihodnost.

Pot k 脛聧lanstvu ni le politi脛聧na ambicija, temve脛聧 zaveza 脜隆ir脜隆i dru脜戮bi, ki temelji na zaupanju v evropske vrednote ter standarde. 脛聦rni gori 脜戮elim uspe脜隆no nadaljevanje v reformnem procesu.

脗聽脗聽Matthieu Valet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, mes chers coll脙篓gues, Mesdames et Messieurs les membres de la Commission, pour moi, ce rapport pr脙漏conisant l'adh脙漏sion du Mont脙漏n脙漏gro 脙聽 l'Union europ脙漏enne, c'est non. Tout d'abord, l'Union europ脙漏enne n'est pas une grande braderie o脙鹿 on va accueillir la Turquie, l'Ukraine, le Kosovo, tous ces pays qui ne sont pas pr脙陋ts et o脙鹿 nous avons a d脙漏j脙聽 du mal 脙聽 fonctionner 脙聽 27 dans cette colocation difficile. Et je vais m'en expliquer.

Sur le plan de la s脙漏curit脙漏, le Mont脙漏n脙漏gro demeure le terrain des r脙漏seaux criminels puissants, trafic de drogue, contrebande et blanchiment d'argent. Ses institutions judiciaires et polici脙篓res restent largement expos脙漏es aux pressions politiques, 脙聽 la corruption et aux ing脙漏rences 脙漏trang脙篓res, notamment russes et chinoises. Le trafic d'armes et la possession ill脙漏gale d'armes par habitant constituent une menace grave que nous n'arriverons pas 脙聽 contenir dans notre continent, qui est d脙漏j脙聽 fort expos脙漏, comme dans mon pays, au trafic d'armes qui fait des morts, notamment par le trafic de stup脙漏fiants.

Sur le plan 脙漏conomique, la situation est tout aussi pr脙漏occupante. Le Mont脙漏n脙漏gro d脙漏pend massivement d'investissements ext脙漏rieurs non europ脙漏ens, en particulier chinois, et sa dette publique d脙漏passe 70脗聽% du PIB. Son tissu productif est fragile et sa forte d脙漏pendance au tourisme le rend vuln脙漏rable aux chocs externes. Dans ces conditions, int脙漏grer ce pays au march脙漏 unique ne ferait qu'accentuer les distorsions 脙漏conomiques et mettre en danger nos entreprises. Et cela ne rendrait service 脙聽 nous ni 脙聽 l'Union europ脙漏enne. Il faut rester s脙漏rieux.

脗聽脗聽Carlo Ciccioli (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il Montenegro 脙篓 nazione strategica per l'adesione all'Unione europea degli interi Balcani occidentali, che rappresentano il completamento del lato orientale dell'area europea. 脙聢 il paese pi脙鹿 avanzato nel suo cammino verso l'adesione, con 33 capitoli negoziali aperti e sei gi脙聽 chiusi e quindi dimostra un forte impegno a favore delle riforme.

脙聢 uno Stato chiave della strategia EUSAIR per la macroregione adriatico-ionica, dove abito anch'io. I suoi porti, in particolare Bar, rappresentano uno snodo cruciale per l'integrazione economica e per la sicurezza energetica dell'intera area. Il traffico merci 脙篓 previsto in crescita del 30脗聽% entro il 2030.

Dal 2014 il paese ha gi脙聽 beneficiato di oltre 200 milioni di euro di fondi europei, con ricadute dirette su turismo, trasporti marittimi, transizione energetica. Il turismo incide per circa il 25脗聽% del PIL nazionale. 脙聢 necessario proseguire con determinazione nelle riforme, ovviamente: giustizia, libert脙聽 dei media, protezione delle minoranze e lotta alla corruzione.

Non dobbiamo avere paura. La sua adesione non solo rafforzer脙聽 il paese, ma far脙聽 da battistrada alla sicurezza, alla prosperit脙聽 e alla coesione dell'intera regione balcanica.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thomas Waitz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Commissioner, yes, Montenegro is still the frontrunner and has a realistic chance to join the European Union until the year 2028. Yes, the government is delivering EU acquis legislation at a very high pace. And yes, Montenegro is a state on the Balkans that has kept good neighbourly relations very much in the past, and I very much recommend to invest time and effort to also do this in our present time.

But there are also concerning things happening. The investment deal with the United Arab Emirates was already mentioned, and unfortunately it looks as if it might allow circumvention of EU procurement rules. Montenegro has to be aware, even if the chapter 5 on procurement may be closed 芒聙聯 let us see what the Council says 芒聙聯 this is just provisionally closed and once circumventing the rules, it can immediately be reopened.

Montenegro is also a country of fantastic natural heritage 芒聙聯 a lot of jewels. If you have not been there, go there and have a look. But protecting these jewels like the Komarnica River, Salina Ulcinj or the protected dunes of Velika Pla脜戮a will be key to closing chapter 27 on environment. Let us work on it and let us do whatever we can to welcome Montenegro as the 28th Member of the European Union.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol (PPE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽Waitz, as the head of the delegation of the European Parliament in Montenegro, you are very aware of the situation of the political landscape in this country.

So my question is, what do you think about the malignant foreign influence of pro-Serbian and pro-Russian political forces, which are part of the ruling coalition? And what is your opinion about the political role of the Serbian Orthodox Church, which has directly influenced recent local elections in Montenegro? And do you think that you should do something about it? And do you think that these issues that I mentioned represent an obstacle in the European path of Montenegro?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thomas Waitz (Verts/ALE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Thank you colleague Sokol for this question. And yes, the Montenegrin voters have elected their representatives and it's not my role to comment on this.

And what I see up to now is that the government, with its majority that also includes Serbian representatives, is delivering legislation on a daily basis. But we also see that sometimes there are some obstacles coming in the way, and they might also come from that side.

For me, the question is not whether somebody is Serbian or Montenegrin or Bosnian. For me, the question is, are people pro-democratic and do they support the pro-European course of the country? And this is what I'm forming my assessments on, and as long as the representatives do so, it's not my role to comment on whatever their background is.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katar脙颅na Roth Neve脛聫alov脙隆 (NI). 芒聙聯 P脙隆n Predseda, kolegovia, vstup akejko脛戮vek krajiny do Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie mus脙颅 by脜楼 spravodliv脙陆 a musia by脜楼 rovnak脙漏 krit脙漏ri脙隆. V s脙潞vislosti s Montenegrom, 脛聦iernou Horou, treba poveda脜楼, 脜戮e ve脛戮mi pekne 脛聫akujem pani komis脙隆rke za jej spr脙隆vu, ktor脙潞 predlo脜戮ila. Je to naozaj krajina, ktor脙隆 je najviac pripraven脙隆 na vstup do Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie. Treba oceni脜楼 ur脛聧ite ten pokrok, ktor脙陆 urobili, hlavne 脛聧o sa t脙陆ka technick脙陆ch kapitol, a ver脙颅m, 脜戮e bud脙潞 脛聫alej pokra脛聧ova脜楼. A mysl脙颅m si, 脜戮e Eur脙鲁pska 脙潞nia by mala mo脜戮no zv脙隆脜戮i脜楼 aj vy脜隆脜隆ie financovanie a podporu v脙陇脛聧脜隆iu pre expertov, aby naozaj 脛聦ierna Hora sa mohla sta脜楼 脛聧o najsk脙麓r 脛聧lensk脙陆m 脜隆t脙隆tom Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie. Rovnak脙陆 pr脙颅stup je ur脛聧ite ve脛戮mi k脛戮脙潞脛聧ov脙陆m. 脛聦ierna Hora si zasl脙潞脜戮i aj z tohto miesta, aby zaznelo, 脜戮e maj脙潞 jasn脙潞 perspekt脙颅vu 脛聧lenstva v Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nii, a ver脙颅m, 脜戮e sa tak stane e脜隆te v tomto obdob脙颅 a 脜戮e budeme mo脜戮no aj my ako poslanci Eur脙鲁pskeho parlamentu m脙麓c脜楼 hlasova脜楼 o vstupe 脛聦iernej Hory do Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie. Preto脜戮e mysl脙颅m si, 脜戮e naozaj Eur脙鲁pska 脙潞nia by sa mala roz脜隆irova脜楼 pr脙隆ve o krajiny z脙隆padn脙漏ho Balk脙隆nu alebo Balk脙隆nu ako tak脙漏ho, preto脜戮e tie s脙潞 najlep脜隆ie pripraven脙漏. A uzatvorenie t脙陆chto kapitol aj v 脛聦iernej Hore je ur脛聧ite d脙麓le脜戮it脙漏 pre pokrok cel脙漏ho regi脙鲁nu.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽脜陆eljana Zovko (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Madam脗聽Commissioner, European values encompass the respect of the rule of law, good neighbourhood relations as well as, in the case of Montenegro, the protection of the cultural, material and immaterial heritage of the Croatian minority.

In order to advance towards EU accession, Montenegro must also ensure equal representation of national minorities in media, judiciary, law enforcement and public institutions. It must also intensify efforts for the non-discriminatory use of minority languages in education, in dedicated media space, in the state and in local outlets.

The rhetoric of Montenegrin politicians must align with that of Brussels circles and work towards reconciliation and solutions to past disputes. As I stressed in my amendments to the report on Montenegro, the country must intensify its efforts in punishing war crimes and must work on clarifying the fate of missing persons. These are essential conditions to become an EU Member State adhering to the core European values.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tonino Picula (S&D). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i,脗聽gospo脛聭o povjerenice, prije svega 脛聧estitke kolegi 脜聽arecu na izradi dobrog dokumenta o kojem raspravljamo danas.

Crna Gora predvodi eurointegracijski proces ve脛聡 godinama s ambicijom da 脜隆to prije pristupi Europskoj uniji. Na tom su putu za Crnu Goru 脛聧esto bili izazovniji unutarnji problemi nego pregovara脛聧ka poglavlja. Podsje脛聡am na neujedna脛聧en ritam demokratske tranzicije, promjene vlada i politika uz sna脜戮nu polarizaciju dru脜隆tva, 脜隆to je nepotrebno kompliciralo i odnose sa susjedima. Polarizaciji i dvojbama doprinose i investicijski projekti kao 脜隆to je npr. nedavni sporazum s Ujedinjenim Arapskim Emiratima. Pritom se lista problema vremenom treba skra脛聡ivati, a ne dodatno produljivati.

Crnoj Gori 脜戮elim da bude slijede脛聡a 脛聧lanica Europske unije, ali se na tom putu mora uspje脜隆no oduprijeti cap Lingus s konceptom takozvanog srpskog svijeta koji ne mo脜戮e biti konkurentan procesu europskih integracija jer je njihova potpuna negacija.

脗聽脗聽Stephen Nikola Bartulica (ECR). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, kolegice i kolege, u hrvatskom interesu, kao i u europskom je da Crna Gora bude umjerena proeuropska zemlja no ona, na脜戮alost, u zadnje vrijeme nazaduje.

脗聽脗聽Carla Tavares (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, gostaria de testemunhar aqui hoje o compromisso do Montenegro com a ades脙拢o 脙聽 Uni脙拢o Europeia, que encontr脙隆mos na miss脙拢o da Comiss脙拢o dos Or脙搂amentos, no final de maio, quando nos desloc脙隆mos ao Montenegro, para um conjunto de prof脙颅cuas reuni脙碌es.

脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, povjerenice, kolegice i kolege od svih dr脜戮ava kandidatkinja Crna Gora je najvi脜隆e napredovala na svojem europskom putu, ali ipak, jo脜隆 je puno prepreka i problema.

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Menschen Europas! 脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, Montenegro's continued alignment and reform efforts, particularly in sensitive areas such as the rule of law, will be decisive in shaping its path forward.

With your support and that of the Council, we can send a clear message: genuine commitment to reform will be met with tangible progress on the EU path. When Montenegro delivers, we must be ready to respond decisively and collectively by recognising its achievements and advancing the accession process. Montenegro is and must remain a frontrunner in enlargement. It is my task, it is the task of the European Commission to help our candidate country. And we do this with all the candidate countries.

We are now speeding up the process, but we really stick to the merit-based principle. That's why 芒聙聯 it was mentioned 芒聙聯 1脗聽000 people or even more than 1脗聽000脗聽of my co-workers are working on one enlargement country and this is for everybody.

Only if we do this together with this support, is how we send a strong signal of encouragement to Montenegro to stay the course and intensify its efforts to move ever closer to the European Union and one day become a member of our European Union.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marjan 脜聽arec, Poro脛聧evalec. 芒聙聯 Gospod predsednik, spo脜隆tovani kolegi, zahvaljujem se vam za va脜隆e besede. Iz teh besed je razvidno, da si vsi prizadevamo za to, da bi 脛聦rna gora脗聽postala 脛聧lanica Evropske unije.

Spo脜隆tovana komisarka, draga Marta, kot ste sedaj na koncu povedali: na脜隆a skupna naloga je pomagati 脛聦rni gori, da bo dosegla vse kriterije in da bo zrela za vstop v Evropsko unijo in vse dr脜戮ave, ki so vstopale 芒聙聯 recimo Slovenija, na脜隆a dr脜戮ava leta 2004 芒聙聯 so se oziroma smo se soo脛聧ale s te脜戮kimi zahtevami. Tudi bilo je treba marsikaj izpolniti, ampak smo uspeli, ker smo delali s skupnimi mo脛聧mi. In to bi tokrat polo脜戮il na srce tudi tako politiki kot civilni dru脜戮bi v 脛聦rni gori. Treba je delovati skupaj. Treba je imeti en skupni cilj in to je vstop v Evropsko unijo, kajti za 脛聦rno goro druge alternative ni.

In za nas kot predstavnike Evropske unije je tudi to naloga, da seveda opozarjamo na tisto, kar ni dobro, da smo tudi neizprosni, kadar je to potrebno. Ampak seveda, da se zavedamo v prvi vrsti, da smo tukaj pravzaprav, da pomagamo in da smo vsi skupaj ena ekipa. Hvala vam za va脜隆e razprave. Se veselim prihodnjega poro脛聧ila in prihodnjega dela.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pre脜聼edinte. 芒聙聯 Dezbaterea a fost 脙庐nchis脛聝. Votul va avea loc m脙垄ine.

Declara脠聸ii scrise (articolul 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR), in writing. 芒聙聯 Montenegro has shown strong alignment with the EU's common foreign and security policy. Its active cooperation in sanctions and diplomatic efforts reflect Montenegro's dedication to European security.

However, we must address an important concern: Montenegro's visa-free travel agreements still include Russia and Belarus, countries whose actions contradict, in the most severe way, EU principles and security interests. For full integration into the European framework, Montenegro must change these visa policies and align them with the EU's stance on these terrorist states. This step is crucial for safeguarding our borders and maintaining a consistent, united foreign policy approach across Europe.

It芒聙聶s a step that a candidate country should be ready to take.

脗聽

13. 2023 and 2024 reports on Moldova (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pre脜聼edinte. 芒聙聯 Urm脛聝torul punct de pe ordinea de zi este dezbaterea referitoare la raportul lui Sven Mikser, 脙庐n numele Comisiei pentru afaceri externe, referitor la Rapoartele Comisiei privind Moldova din 2023 脠聶i 2024 [2025/2025(INI)] (A10-0096/2025).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sven Mikser, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr President, colleagues, Commissioner Kos, today, Moldova is a candidate country to the European Union. And how successful it is in its aspirations is of course, of crucial importance for the Moldovan people's future welfare and freedom.

However, Moldova's success or failure also matters for the future of our Union and indeed our continent. Moldova has embarked on an ambitious and challenging path of reforms. The report we are discussing today highlights significant strides the country has made in the past few years. Under the leadership of its pro-European governments and president, and driven by the will of Moldovan people, it has demonstrated commitment to democratic principles even in the face of immense external pressures. Its dedication to strengthening the rule of law, combating corruption and reforming its justice system, though still requiring a sustained effort, demonstrates a clear trajectory towards aligning with European standards and values.

Our unwavering support for Moldova's sovereignty, independence and territorial integrity is crucial. The geopolitical landscape surrounding Moldova, marked by Russia's aggressive and destabilising actions, demands our vigilance and solidarity. During the constitutional referendum and presidential elections last year, we witnessed Russian interference and malign information operations on an unprecedented scale, and there is little doubt that we will see a repetition of those hostile operations during the run up to this year's parliamentary vote.

Therefore, the report underscores the need for continued financial assistance, technical expertise and political backing to help Moldova navigate complex reforms and bolster its resilience against Moscow's hybrid warfare, disinformation campaigns and economic coercion. We must ensure that our support translates into tangible improvements for the Moldovan people, strengthening their trust in the European path.

But beyond delivering immediate, tangible benefits for the people, the reforms also have a strategic objective of transforming the country into a vibrant, sustainable and competitive economy. The report recognises efforts to foster a more stable and attractive business environment, essential for economic growth, and for integrating Moldova into the EU single market. Progress in areas such as energy security is particularly commendable, reducing reliance on single sources and enhancing the nation's strategic autonomy.

However, the report also points to the ongoing need for structural reforms to improve competitiveness, attract foreign investment and create sustainable jobs. We must advocate for policies that facilitate this economic transformation, ensuring that Moldovan businesses and citizens can fully benefit from closer ties with the European Union.

Furthermore, the report emphasises the importance of civil society engagement and media freedom. A vibrant civil society and independent media are indispensable pillars of any functioning democracy. The role in holding institutions accountable, advocating for reforms and informing the public cannot be overstated. We must continue to support these vital actors, providing them with the necessary tools and protection to operate freely and effectively.

Inevitably, the report also acknowledges areas where further action is required. These include full implementation of judicial reforms, consistent application of anti-corruption measures, and ensuring the protection of human rights and fundamental freedoms. The EU's role in these areas has become even more crucial following the substantial rolling back of the US international commitments.

Also, the situation in the Transnistria region remains challenging, and the report reaffirms the EU's commitment to a peaceful and comprehensive settlement based on Moldova's sovereignty and territorial integrity.

In conclusion, we must remain steadfast partners for Moldova, providing our support, guidance and encouragement. By investing in Moldova's future, we are also investing in the future of our continent and our Union.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear Sven, thank you for your report and recommendations and many thanks to the whole Parliament for the interest and support you have extended to Moldova.

Moldova may be a small country, but is strategically located at Ukraine's southern border and has access to the Black Sea. The unprecedented interference of Russia shows that the Kremlin looks at it that way. Moldova matters for our security. As we have heard right now. It is a test case for a stronger and more geopolitically minded European Union. And this is why, for the first time ever, we will organise a EU Moldova summit on 4 July in Chisinau.

I have often said Moldova is best in a class, and we are responding to its political commitment and strong efforts by advancing the accession process at record speed. The Commission has already sent three screening reports to EU Member States for cluster one 芒聙聭 Fundamentals, cluster two 芒聙聭 Internal Market, and cluster six 芒聙聭 External Relations. The Council now has all the elements at hand to come to a decision on the first cluster on fundamentals, we want to see the opening of the first cluster as soon as possible.

In parallel, preparations continue on all our other clusters. We are on track to finalise all bilateral screening meetings in September. Our ambitious goal of opening all clusters this year is technically possible. On supporting reforms, we have also further stepped up, for example, on supporting the vetting commission for prosecutors, which was at risk to be stopped due to the freeze of US financial assistance. We are supporting the justice reform so it does not suffer setbacks following the freeze.

Moldova has set an ambitious goal: EU membership by 2030. This is possible, but to achieve it, they need to stay the course. The incoming government will need to further accelerate reforms and of course we will support them.

But reforms are no longer enough. We also need to fight back against those trying to derail the European path. Last winter we got a glimpse of the Russian playbook. They cut gas supplies to the Transnistria region to destabilise the government of Moldova and discredit its European path. In our response, we showed very clearly what European solidarity with the geopolitical compass means. The EU agreed on a comprehensive strategy for energy independence and resilience to protect Moldovan consumers from steep price rises, and to help the country become energy independent by 2027. And this kind of bold and decisive action must be our standard blueprint if we are to take responsibility for ourselves in Europe.

With the Reform and Growth Plan, we are now building the next pillar for a stronger, more prosperous Moldova. Thanks again for your excellent cooperation during the adoption of the Growth Plan in the record time. It is a historic support package of EUR 1.9 billion that will set Moldova on a path to double its economy in the next ten years. The growth plan will help the government build roads, invest in schools, modernise the hospitals and protect national heritage. If spent wisely, it can double Moldova's economy and create up to 100脗聽000 new jobs in Moldova so young Moldovans can build a future at home.

It will also bring the country closer to EU membership by further accelerating reforms, especially in public administration, the judiciary and anti-corruption. These structural reforms are essential to create a business environment that attracts investments and promotes sustainable economic growth. Last month, while I was in Moldova, I travelled the whole country to meet the people who will implement the growth plan and benefit from it. I met more than 150 mayors and business leaders, farmers and young people. Also travelling with me were distinguished Members of this Parliament, Mr Mure脠聶an and Mr Barna. All of us, we have sent a strong signal of a whole European support to our candidate country. Thank you for this.

We travelled all together specifically to areas where EU scepticism runs highest, because we also need to engage in battle against interference and disinformation. Moldova continues to face unprecedented levels of interference from Russia and its local proxies, just as they did during the last presidential elections and the referendum on EU integration. Every day, anti-EU narratives are planted, narratives like 'You will lose your identity' or 'EU aid will indebt your country for years'. It is very much important to fight back and set the record straight and we can do that and we are doing this. We have good stories to tell. Even in Gagauzia, I was there, a region where 95脗聽% voted against EU accession in the referendum. One of the mayors told me, and I quote, 'My village would not exist anymore without the EU. You helped us build electricity and sewage system. Moldovans who work in the West often want to come back, but they would no longer find their village if it wasn't for the EU'. And then I asked, what has Russia invested in the region? And I got the answer, 'Nothing'. The only investment Russia is doing there is in corrupting politicians and vote buying schemes, money to stop the free choice of people of Moldova.

In September, and this is important, the people of Moldova will again cast their vote. It will be another vote on their future, because only with the majority that wants to step up. The pace of reform is the current goal of membership of 2030 realistic. We will support Moldova in ensuring that those are fair elections. For the first time, the EU, we have deployed our hybrid reaction team. It helps to protect their cyberspace and provide tools to fight back against Russian propaganda. At the same time, together with partners such as the European Endowment for Democracy, we want to strengthen support to independent media and journalists. And I'm working with my colleague commissioners to travel to the country, also to towns and villages, and we have real results to show. We need to talk about them clearly, confidently and loudly, to explain and to take away fears.脗聽Also, in the places in Gagauzia or Taraclia, where many of minorities are living and I visited them.

And I would also like to encourage you, Members of the European Parliament, to go. And this is how I see a European team in action, with one shared goal a Moldova at home inside the European Union: secure, independent and prosperous, and contributing to stability at Ukraine's southern border and contributing to the stability and security of Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 I want to tell you that your visit was much appreciated. I had the same impression, and I wish to convey the thanks of President Maia Sandu for your very big effort, together with the Members of the European Parliament. It was much appreciated.

It is an honour to open today's debate, and we open it with Andrzej Halicki, on behalf of the EPP Group.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andrzej Halicki, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, dear Commissioner, thank you for all the information. This report is the European Parliament's reaction to the 2023 and 2024 Commission reports on Moldova. I would also like to congratulate the Moldovan authorities because the determination and consequences of the pro-European parts of the implementation of all the reforms is really visible, and we have noticed it.

We had the mission to Chi脠聶in脛聝u in February, and I would also like to thank Sven Mikser for the very good cooperation. During this mission, we announced the most important package for Moldova: the Reform and Growth Facility, which is EUR脗聽1.9脗聽billion to support Moldovan society.

From this place, I would like to ask for implementation of the 18脗聽% pre-financing rate as soon as possible, because it's really crucial just now for Moldovan society and for the government. The reform has to be supported also by us, by European society.

I would like to emphasise the political decision after our common mission, because the European Parliament took the decision in March to establish a parliamentary office in Chi脠聶in脛聝u. This is very important for having close ties with the Moldovan authorities.

The report is coherent; it is very good. In the name of the EPP, I would like to emphasise that we, of course, support the whole report, but we are against the amendments coming from the radical right and the radical left. They are not needed. We have 15 amendments in all.

But there is one I would like to recommend to support. This is the amendment from the ECR on 'roam like at home'. It is really expected by Moldovan society, so I recommend to support it. All the other political groups 芒聙聯 like us and Renew and the Greens 芒聙聯 didn't table any amendments.

So thank you again for the very good cooperation.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido, em nome do Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, caros colegas, quero come脙搂ar por sublinhar que o compromisso e a resili脙陋ncia demonstrados pelo Governo e pela popula脙搂脙拢o da Mold脙隆via para, num curto espa脙搂o de tempo e de dif脙颅cil contexto geopol脙颅tico, realizarem as reformas necess脙隆rias para cumprir os crit脙漏rios de ades脙拢o 脙聽 Uni脙拢o Europeia e referendarem a consagra脙搂脙拢o constitucional de um rumo pr脙鲁-europeu, devem inspirar-nos a todos.

Apesar das campanhas, de todas as amea脙搂as h脙颅bridas e interfer脙陋ncias externas, a Mold脙隆via tem vindo a realizar progressos reais em 脙隆reas como o Estado de direito, as reformas socioecon脙鲁micas, a independ脙陋ncia energ脙漏tica e a sustentabilidade ambiental. As elei脙搂脙碌es legislativas do pr脙鲁prio do pr脙鲁ximo outono n脙拢o devem desperdi脙搂ar estes resultados.

Mas a responsabilidade n脙拢o 脙漏 s脙鲁 da Mold脙隆via, tamb脙漏m 脙漏 nossa. As Institui脙搂脙碌es europeias precisam de assegurar o apoio que garanta que para todas estas reformas haja uma irreversibilidade e resultados concretos na vida dos cidad脙拢os.

O futuro da Mold脙隆via 脙漏 uma quest脙拢o de m脙潞tuo interesse estrat脙漏gico e pol脙颅tico. Por isso, s脙鲁 podemos defender, veementemente, a acelera脙搂脙拢o da sua integra脙搂脙拢o gradual num processo de ades脙拢o que deve ser exigente, mas tamb脙漏m justo, transparente e livre de bloqueios artificiais.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cristian Terhe脜聼, 脙庐n numele grupului ECR. 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, stima脠聸i colegi, integrarea Republicii Moldova 脙庐n Uniunea European脛聝 reprezint脛聝 o reparare par脠聸ial脛聝 a nedrept脛聝脠聸ii istorice comise de Stalin 脠聶i Hitler prin Pactul Ribbentrop-Molotov, care 脙庐n 1940 a dus la anexarea de c脛聝tre U.R.S.S. a Basarabiei, ce era parte component脛聝 a Rom脙垄niei.

Aceast脛聝 ocupa脠聸ie sovietic脛聝 a adus suferin脠聸e profunde popula脠聸iei rom脙垄ne脠聶ti din Basarabia: epurare etnic脛聝, 脙庐nfometare organizat脛聝, deport脛聝ri, crime 脠聶i persecu脠聸ii sistematice. Printre victimele opresiunii sovietice s-a num脛聝rat 脠聶i Mitropolia Ortodox脛聝 a Basarabiei, aflat脛聝 sub jurisdic脠聸ia Patriarhiei Rom脙垄ne. 脙聨n 1940, aceasta cuprindea peste 1脗聽000 de biserici 脠聶i 30 de m脙垄n脛聝stiri. Multe au fost 脙庐nchise, distruse sau profanate de comuni脠聶tii sovietici. Peste 50 de preo脠聸i ortodoc脠聶i rom脙垄ni care au refuzat s脛聝 treac脛聝 sub jurisdic脠聸ia Patriarhiei Moscovei au fost asasina脠聸i, iar al脠聸ii deporta脠聸i de autorit脛聝脠聸ile comuniste atee.

Chiar 脠聶i dup脛聝 c脛聝derea regimului comunist 脠聶i independen脠聸a Moldovei, Mitropolia Basarabiei a continuat s脛聝 fie discriminat脛聝, fiind recunoscut脛聝 oficial abia 脙庐n 2002, dup脛聝 o decizie a CEDO.

V脛聝 invit s脛聝 sus脠聸ine脠聸i amendamentul pe care l-am adus la acest raport 脠聶i care recunoa脠聶te Mitropolia Basarabiei ca victim脛聝 a opresiunii sovietice.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dan Barna, 脙庐n numele grupului Renew. 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, salut adoptarea Raportului de 脠聸ar脛聝 pentru Republica Moldova 脠聶i munca raportorilor tuturor grupurilor pro-europene, precum 脠聶i efortul raportorului Sven Mikser, a c脛聝rui contribu脠聸ie a fost remarcabil脛聝 脙庐n acest proces.

Forma final脛聝 a textului arat脛聝 clar, f脛聝r脛聝 脙庐ndoial脛聝, sprijinul Parlamentului European pentru Moldova 脠聶i eforturile sale de integrare 脙庐n Uniunea European脛聝. Vedem cum campaniile de dezinformare 脠聶i manipulare au 脙庐nceput deja 脙庐naintea alegerilor parlamentare din septembrie. Rusia 脠聶i minionii s脛聝i din Europa au pierdut alegerile preziden脠聸iale, au pierdut referendumul privind integrarea european脛聝 脠聶i acum duc o b脛聝t脛聝lie furibund脛聝 pentru Parlamentul moldovean.

Trebuie s脛聝 combatem r脛聝zboiul hibrid al Rusiei cu toate mijloacele legale 脠聶i democratice. Este momentul ca 脙庐nfr脙垄ngerea Moscovei s脛聝 aib脛聝 loc pe toate fronturile, pentru ca Europa de Est s脛聝 revin脛聝 la pace 脠聶i prosperitate.

脙聨n acest sens, cer ferm Comisiei Europene s脛聝 includ脛聝 fondurile de preaderare pentru Republica Moldova 脠聶i celelalte state candidate la aderare 脙庐n proiectul viitorului buget multianual. De asemenea, Comisia European脛聝 脠聶i statele membre trebuie s脛聝 asiste activ Chi脠聶in脛聝ul 脙庐n perioada urm脛聝toare la 脙庐ndeplinirea reformelor 脠聶i accesarea fondurilor din facilitatea de cre脠聶tere 脠聶i o felicit pe doamna comisar脛聝 Marta Kos pentru remarcabilul exemplu de prezen脠聸脛聝 a Europei acolo, 脙庐n Moldova, 脙庐n fiecare regiune, nu doar 脙庐n Chi脠聶in脛聝u.

Extinderea Uniunii cu Moldova este o prioritate strategic脛聝 a Europei. Continentul nostru poate 脠聶i trebuie s脛聝 asigure dezvoltarea, prosperitatea 脠聶i securitatea pentru membrii Uniunii, pentru statele candidate 脠聶i pentru vecin脛聝tatea sa estic脛聝.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Virginijus Sinkevi脛聧ius, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, Moldova has clearly shown what determination looks like. In the shadow of war, under constant pressure from Russian disinformation, constant hybrid threats, it made a bold choice for democracy, for reforms, and most importantly, for European future. And today, Moldova launches accession talks with the EU; it's a move forward on justice reform, climate ambition 芒聙聯 not because it was easy, but because it was the right thing to do.

The road ahead will certainly present new challenges: with elections this September, Moldova will be tested again. Moldova has successfully passed through the two previous elections, but it's a bit like a boxing match: you win two rounds, but if you are knocked out in the third one, you lose. Therefore, it's our duty to help Moldova, because Moldova's future is with us in a free, democratic European home. And this is the voice of the Moldovan people.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jonas Sj脙露stedt, f脙露r The Left gruppen. 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Sven Miksers bet脙陇nkande speglar mycket v脙陇l det stora st脙露d som finns f脙露r Moldavien h脙陇r i parlamentet. F脙露r oss i v脙陇nstergruppen 脙陇r det s脙陇rskilt viktigt att den ocks脙楼 lyfter fram fr脙楼gor om social utveckling, f脙露rnybar energi och j脙陇mst脙陇lldhet. Moldavien 脙陇r ett utsatt land. Kriget i Ukraina har satt stor press p脙楼 det moldaviska samh脙陇llet, landet har tagit emot m脙楼nga flyktingar. Det finns ett stort behov av ekonomisk och social utveckling. Moldavien 脙陇r ett av Europas fattigaste l脙陇nder.

Ryssland f脙露rs脙露ker st脙陇ndigt blanda sig i och p脙楼verka utvecklingen i Moldavien och st脙露djer aktivt den s脙楼 kallade utbrytarrepubliken i Transnistrien. Trots det har Moldavien gjort mycket stora framsteg de senaste 脙楼ren, och det 脙陇r v脙楼r uppgift att forts脙陇tta st脙露dja dem i deras demokratisering och omvandling.

Valen som ska h脙楼llas i september m脙楼ste bli verkligt fria, demokratiska val d脙陇r alla moldaviska medborgare fritt kan v脙陇lja landets framtid. Det st脙露rsta hotet mot det 脙陇r rysk inblandning. Moldavien beh脙露ver ekonomisk utveckling. Den kan inte vara ensidig anpassning till EU. EU m脙楼ste ocks脙楼 ta bort kvarvarande handelshinder f脙露r export av moldaviska produkter till EU:s marknad. Anstr脙陇ngningar f脙露r att l脙露sa Transnistrien-fr脙楼gan p脙楼 ett fredligt s脙陇tt, s脙楼 att utbrytarrepubliken blir en verklig del av Moldavien, b脙露r st脙露djas. D脙陇rf脙露r vill jag tacka Sven Mikser f脙露r det h脙陇r bet脙陇nkandet, som jag tycker speglar tron p脙楼 Moldavien och Moldaviens m脙露jligheter f脙露r framtiden.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexander Sell, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Frau von der Leyen hat die st脙陇ndige Erweiterung der EU zu einer strategischen Priorit脙陇t ihrer Amtszeit erkl脙陇rt; die Kommission verspricht sich davon mehr au脙聼enpolitisches Gewicht. Die EU m脙录sse immer gr脙露脙聼er werden und immer mehr Mitglieder aufnehmen, damit Frau von der Leyen auf der Weltb脙录hne eine bedeutendere Rolle spielen k脙露nne. Deshalb soll neben der Ukraine auch Moldawien in die EU aufgenommen werden. Was ist das f脙录r ein Land?

Seit Jahren steckt Moldawien in einer tiefen wirtschaftlichen und sozialen Krise. Die Wirtschaft schrumpft seit drei Quartalen, also noch schlimmer als in Deutschland. Die Inflation ist hoch, bei Lebensmitteln fast 10脗聽%. Moldawien ist auch ein sehr korruptes Land脗聽芒聙聯 30脗聽Jahre Reformpartnerschaft mit der EU haben nichts gebracht.

Ich frage deshalb die Kommission: Was ist der Mehrwert f脙录r unsere B脙录rger? Wieso sollen unsere Steuerzahler 2脗聽Milliarden脗聽EUR an Moldawien 脙录berweisen? Wie wollen Sie sicherstellen, dass dieses Geld nicht in den Taschen korrupter Oligarchen verschwindet?

Wir haben genug Baustellen in Deutschland, Frankreich oder Polen. Wenn Sie immer weitere Mitglieder aufnehmen, dann wird Ihnen der Laden hier um die Ohren fliegen, und genau das wollen wir verhindern. Deshalb: Moldawien darf nicht Mitglied der EU werden.

脗聽脗聽Victor Negrescu (S&D). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, domnule comisar, stima脠聸i colegi, Republica Moldova nu mai este doar o 脠聸ar脛聝 vecin脛聝, este parte din familia noastr脛聝 european脛聝. 脙聨ntr-un context regional extrem de dificil, Republica Moldova a r脛聝mas ferm angajat脛聝 脙庐n direc脠聸ia reformelor 脠聶i a valorilor europene, iar 脙庐n sensul acesta raportul recunoa脠聶te progresele 脙庐nregistrate, care trebuie continuate de o guvernare pro-european脛聝.

Dar pentru cet脛聝脠聸eni, pentru copii, pentru familiile vulnerabile, pentru cei din sate, Europa trebuie s脛聝 脙庐nsemne 脠聶i protec脠聸ie social脛聝, acces la educa脠聸ie, servicii de baz脛聝. Aderarea nu 脙庐nseamn脛聝 doar capitole tehnice, ci schimb脛聝ri concrete 脙庐n via脠聸a de zi cu zi.

De aceea, m脛聝 al脛聝tur solicit脛聝rilor venite din Parlamentul European privind accesul la fondurile de preaderare, dar 脠聶i la programul Roaming ca acas脛聝. Pentru a 脙庐nt脛聝ri toate aceste rela脠聸ii, Parlamentul European va deschide 脙庐n cur脙垄nd, la ini脠聸iativa sus脠聸inut脛聝 脠聶i de mine, dar 脠聶i de alte grupuri politice, un birou de leg脛聝tur脛聝 la Chi脠聶in脛聝u, un demers simbolic, dar 脠聶i practic pentru o prezen脠聸脛聝 european脛聝 real脛聝 脠聶i constant脛聝 pe teren.

Europa nu e un vis 脙庐ndep脛聝rtat, Europa este o certitudine, iar Republica Moldova are locul ei aici, al脛聝turi de noi. 脙聨l felicit pe raportor, domnul Sven Mikser, 脠聶i 脙庐i mul脠聸umim doamnei comisar Kos pentru eforturile sale.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Eugen Tomac (Renew). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, Republica Moldova, de mai bine de trei decenii lupt脛聝 pentru a-脠聶i consolida parcursul european, 脙庐ns脛聝 Rusia nu-i permite acest lucru, pentru c脛聝 din 1992, pe teritoriul Republicii Moldova sta脠聸ioneaz脛聝 ilegal armata Federa脠聸iei Ruse.

R脛聝spunsul nostru trebuie s脛聝 fie mai mult suport, consolidarea rezisten脠聸ei Republicii Moldova 脙庐n tot ceea ce 脠聸ine de programul de propagand脛聝 脠聶i de dezinformare pe care Federa脠聸ia Rus脛聝 脙庐n mod constant 脙庐l aplic脛聝 脙庐n Republica Moldova.

Cet脛聝脠聸enii din Republica Moldova au trecut testul loialit脛聝脠聸ii fa脠聸脛聝 de proiectul european, pentru c脛聝 脙庐mpotriva propagandei agresive a Federa脠聸iei Ruse, anul trecut au votat prin referendum c脛聝 direc脠聸ia pe care doresc s脛聝 o urmeze este integrarea european脛聝.

脠聵i r脛聝spunsul nostru trebuie s脛聝 fie da, integrare european脛聝 脠聶i nu lui Putin, care 脙庐脠聶i dore脠聶te sl脛聝birea Republicii Moldova 脠聶i abandonarea proiectului european. V脛聝 felicit, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, pentru tot ceea ce a脠聸i f脛聝cut pentru ca Republica Moldova s脛聝 avanseze pe calea integr脛聝rii europene.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, Moldova today is Europe's frontline of resilience and determination. Despite relentless hybrid attacks, disinformation campaigns and economic pressure from Russia, the Moldovan people have reaffirmed, through a constitutional referendum and the presidential elections, their firm choice for a European future. This report rightly recognises Moldova's progress in judicial reform, anti-corruption, energy independence, CFSP alignment areas where the EU support, including through the Reform and Growth Facility, is making a tangible difference.

But this path is not without risks. As we approach Moldova's parliamentary elections, the EU must stand ever firmer 芒聙聯 strengthening the EU partnership mission, countering malign interference, deepening Moldova's integration into the single market. Our message must be clear: Moldova belongs in the European family, and its place is not to be decided by Moscow, but by the will of its citizens. Let us honour their courage with unwavering solidarity and concrete support.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marcos Ros Sempere (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, el camino hacia la integraci脙鲁n en la Uni脙鲁n Europea es el camino de la democracia, la justicia y la paz.

Moldavia lo sabe y ha elegido discurrir por ese camino. As脙颅 lo dejaron claro sus ciudadanos, en el mes de octubre pasado, en el refer脙漏ndum para blindar su Constituci脙鲁n hacia la integraci脙鲁n europea. Y no solo sus ciudadanos, su Gobierno est脙隆 avanzando en las reformas necesarias e imprescindibles para lograr la adhesi脙鲁n lo antes posible.

A los europeos ahora nos toca apoyar este proceso, ayudar a fortalecer la administraci脙鲁n y las instituciones p脙潞blicas y, sobre todo, cooperar en el reforzamiento del sistema judicial independiente.

Debemos reconocerlo: todo apoyo es poco cuando est脙隆 en juego la democracia y la resistencia frente al vecino ruso. Solo la senda de la integraci脙鲁n europea de Moldavia 芒聙聰junto a Ucrania芒聙聰 conseguir脙隆 estabilizar la paz que tanto anhelamos para la zona.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Karin Karlsbro (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, colleagues, while Ukraine is fighting for its existence, Moldova is working hard for a European future. Since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, free trade between the EU and Moldova has not only been beneficial, but has served as a hope for the EU candidate, Moldova.

But soon, on 24脗聽July 2025, the temporary trade liberalisation will come to an end, and the Commission has still not presented a solution for what will happen next. Let's be frank: this is a huge mistake. Competition from Moldova's farmers is not the threat to EU farmers; it's Russia's war. It is in our mutual interest to make sure that Moldova stays on its path towards European integration 芒聙聯 not to push them into Putin's claws.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mika Aaltola (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, supporting Moldova is a strategic investment in Europe's security and future. We have to understand that it is a battleground. Right now, Moldova is facing quite a lot of problems. When I went there two weeks ago, I saw that it is torn. The pro-European forces are strong, but the dark side is present, and Moldova is a testing ground for different new methods for Russian destabilisation operations.

The fight is ongoing, and it is a fight that we will see in our future if we don't solidify Moldova's defences against hybrid and destabilisation operations. I strongly support assisting Moldova on its European way.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Krzysztof Brejza (PPE). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Ca脜聜a Europa z podziwem patrzy na Mo脜聜dawi脛聶. Pa脜聞stwo niewielkie, ale pa脜聞stwo waleczne, bo Mo脜聜dawia stoi po stronie warto脜聸ci, po stronie Unii Europejskiej. Spo脜聜ecze脜聞stwo Mo脜聜dawii wygra脜聜o z tym ruskim mirem. Spo脜聜ecze脜聞stwo Mo脜聜dawii wygra脜聜o z akcjami informacyjnymi Putina, z fa脜聜szowaniem wybor脙鲁w, z oligarch脛聟, kt脙鲁ry przekupywa脜聜 wybory, z grupami przest脛聶pczymi, prorosyjskimi, kt脙鲁re by脜聜y tam zaanga脜录owane w niszczenie demokracji.

Ca脜聜a Europa jest z ciebie Mo脜聜dawio dumna, ale my musimy by脛聡 脜聸wiadomi, 脜录e ta wojna informacyjna Putina dotyczy tak Mo脜聜dawii, jak i Gruzji, jak i pa脜聞stw ba脜聜tyckich, jak i Polski, jak i ca脜聜ych spo脜聜ecze脜聞stw Zachodu. Ta wojna informacyjna Putina trwa i ka脜录dy, kto staje po stronie Putina, tacy politycy, jak Orban, Fico, i inni kolaboranci Putina, to s脛聟 ludzie, kt脙鲁rzy os脜聜abiaj脛聟 Europ脛聶 i stoj脛聟 po stronie ruskiego miru. Nie b脛聶dzie ruskiego miru w Europie.

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, m-am 脙庐ntors duminic脛聝 din Republica Moldova, sunt prim-vicepre脠聶edint脛聝 la delega脠聸ia pentru Republica Moldova 脠聶i merg foarte frecvent acolo. Am participat la ac脠聸iuni extraordinare la Academia de 脠聵tiin脠聸e, Asocia脠聸ia Medical脛聝 din Buiucani. Am v脛聝zut oameni inimo脠聶i care doresc acest drum european 脠聶i trebuie s脛聝-i sus脠聸inem. Cu siguran脠聸脛聝, Republica Moldova aici are locul.

M脛聝 脙庐ngrijoreaz脛聝 脙庐ns脛聝 alegerile din septembrie 脠聶i am constatat, discut脙垄nd, av脙垄nd dialog 脠聶i cu societatea civil脛聝, cu asocia脠聸ii profesionale, cu partide mai mici, pro-europene, c脛聝 se organizeaz脛聝 aceste partide pro-ruse 脠聶i trebuie s脛聝 sprijinim acest lucru 脠聶i o s脛聝 avem delega脠聸ie 脙庐n iulie.

Recomand Republicii Moldova, partidului pro-european, doamnei pre脠聶edinte, s脛聝 poarte dialog cu micile partide pro-europene, cum este Partidul Ecologist, Partidul Verzilor, Partidul Social Democrat, pentru a crea o coali脠聸ie puternic脛聝, s脛聝 avem un parlament pro-european 脙庐n septembrie. Doar a脠聶a se pot continua lucrurile.

脠聵i da, vreau 脙庐n urm脛聝torul exerci脠聸iu financiar s脛聝 fie cuprin脠聶i, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, banii de preaderare, au nevoie cet脛聝脠聸enii de acolo de ace脠聶ti bani.

脗聽脗聽Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). 芒聙聯 Gerbiamas pirmininke, Moldova ypatinga tuo, kad tai yra labai ai脜隆kus Europos S脛聟jungos s脛聟jungininkas tiek buvusioje Ryt脜鲁 kaimynyst脛聴s politikoje, tiek dabar, jai stojant 脛炉 Europos S脛聟jung脛聟. Kuo daugiau Europos S脛聟jungos Moldovoj, tuo ma脜戮iau Putino propagandos ten. Ir rudens rinkimuose mums ypatingai reikalingas 脜隆iandien Europos Parlamento balsas, siun脛聧iantis ai脜隆ki脛聟 脜戮ini脛聟, kad Moldovos s脛聴km脛聴 ruden脛炉 yra ir jos greitesn脛聴s integracijos 脛炉 Europos S脛聟jung脛聟 kelias. Mums reikia kuo daugiau pad脛聴ti 芒聙聯 daugiau pilotini脜鲁 projekt脜鲁, daugiau dvi脜隆ali脜鲁 projekt脜鲁, Europos Parlamento atstovyb脛聴s, daugiau investicij脜鲁. Mes neturime padaryti tokios klaidos, koki脛聟 Europos S脛聟junga padar脛聴 d脛聴l Gruzijos. Europos S脛聟jungos silpnas buvimas Gruzijoje 脜隆tai k脛聟 padar脛聴 su Gruzijos ateitimi. Reikia b脜芦tinai paremti Moldovos stojim脛聟.

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Menschen Europas! Der Kollege Sell f脙录rchtet sich vor einem Beitritt Moldaus zur Europ脙陇ischen Union. Er hat Angst vor der strauchelnden Wirtschaft Moldaus. Als w脙录rden wir L脙陇nder in die EU aufnehmen, um kurzfristig und unmittelbar einen Vorteil daraus zu ziehen und nicht langfristig gemeinsam Freiheit, Wohlstand und Rechtsstaatlichkeit aufzubauen. Er hat Angst vor der Korruption in Moldau脗聽芒聙聯 hier kann ich ihm den Blick in die eigene Partei empfehlen. Er fragt, was der Mehrwert ist f脙录r unsere Bev脙露lkerung. Ganz einfach: Dass wir gemeinsam vorangehen auf diesem europ脙陇ischen Kontinent, dass wir gemeinsam mit allen V脙露lkern Europas an der Zukunft dieser Union arbeiten, an einem gemeinsamen Weg, der f脙录r uns alle besser sein wird als das, was wir in der Vergangenheit hatten.

(脙聨ncheierea interven脠聸iilor la cerere)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Kos, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, I am really touched how positively you have spoken about a candidate country which, I have said before, is the best in my class, if I may say so.脗聽Thank you once again for the interest and support you have extended to Moldova and to our new approach.

And congratulations for the decision that you will open the office of the European Parliament in Chi脠聶in脛聝u. This is how we really show that we care about a candidate country who would like to become a member of the EU and we are supporting this.

Like you have said, as the country faces crucial parliamentary elections in autumn, the support from all of us 芒聙聯 from the European Parliament, from the EU Member States, from the European Commission 芒聙聯 is very crucial and it shows to the Moldovan people that the EU truly cares.

This is especially important as Russia and its proxies in Moldova continue to use all tools at their disposal to ensure that Moldova's EU path is disrupted. This is a new phase in the enlargement process. Never before have we had external disruptive forces who wanted to see us fail.脗聽Today, we have this new situation and we have to adapt.

Moldova has ambitious goals, including EU membership by 2030, which we support of course. To achieve this, Moldova needs to continue with its steady progress in aligning with EU legislation and standards across the clusters, including the fundamentals.

Moldova also needs to accelerate its socioeconomic convergence with the EU. Despite challenges, Moldova reduced its dependency on Russian energy sources, thanks in part to the support of the EU.

The need for structural reforms is also clear. Moldova needs to continue reforming its public administration, judiciary and anti芒聙聭corruption frameworks, and to align with the EU laws and regulations. And Madam脗聽Marta Temido, yes, we should take care of this, we should help them and we are doing this.

Thank you to all of you. This is how I see the role of the European Commission. Once we have a candidate country who really wants to become a member of the EU, is committed and takes the responsibility, we also should do whatever we can to support them.

All those reforms are essential for creating a business environment that attracts investments and promotes sustainable economic growth. All those reforms are essential to prepare Moldova so that it will be able to enter the European Union.

We help them and we will do this in the future too. The EU will continue supporting Moldova as it advances on its path to EU because its place is within the EU family.

Dealing with the enlargement country, and now I will be a bit personal, it's not only about the clusters and chapters and benchmarks. It is really much more. With what we do, we give our candidate countries hope. This is something that we need today in Europe. We are giving them hope.

Mr脗聽Vigenin, you have said that Moldova's choice for EU has been choice by values. Yes, and we should protect and support European values. And Mr脗聽Andriukaitis, yes, we are deploying a completely new approach. We haven't helped Georgia, but we really help Moldova because it is the right thing to do.

For the first time in history, we are deploying a hybrid rapid reaction team to fight Russian propaganda. For the first time we are cooperating 芒聙聯 the European Commission, my team and the team of the Government of Moldova 芒聙聯 to really fight those lies. It is not only about propaganda, they are really lies.

And I tell you 芒聙聯 yes, I am personal now 芒聙聯 I tell you, they will not win. They really will not win and Moldova will become a member of the EU.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sven Mikser, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, I would like to use these two minutes first of all to thank all the colleagues for this very constructive debate and all the shadow rapporteurs for the very constructive negotiations 芒聙聯 the same spirit I sensed during a few months back when we debated the growth facility. I think that there is, really, very much a shared sense of purpose among all the pro-European groups in this Parliament. There has also been great cooperation with the Commission, particularly Commissioner Kos, and also with the Council, led by the Polish Presidency. I think that we all sensed the same spirit and unity of purpose during the debate today.

We, the European Union, have demonstrated has demonstrated our true commitment to Moldova's European aspirations. Most recently, when the Commission speedily launched the energy assistance package during the energy crisis, and then when we adopted the Growth Facility in record speed.

The Moldovan people and Moldovan authorities have also demonstrated that commitment in re-electing a pro-European president and in enshrining the European integration into the constitution, and in pushing ahead at full speed with the reforms.

But, obviously, this is not a time to rest on laurels because the Kremlin has not given up their sinister designs. So we must keep on working and also the Moldovans must keep on working well beyond the upcoming parliamentary elections. So we still have a lot of work cut out for us.

Regarding the amendments, I fully concur with colleague Halicki, and I ask for all of your support during the final vote on the report tomorrow.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Thank you, Mr脗聽Mikser. Good luck with the final report.

The debate is closed.

The vote will take place tomorrow.

Declara脠聸ii scrise (articolul 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Urmas Paet (Renew), kirjalikult. 芒聙聯 Moldova teeb Euroopa Liiduga igak脙录lgset koost脙露脙露d ning pingutab k脙碌igiti, et viia oma seadused ELi 脙碌igustikuga koosk脙碌lla. Riik on viinud l脙陇bi m脙陇rkimisv脙陇脙陇rseid reforme, vaatamata Venemaa sekkumiskatsetele ja naaberriigis Ukrainas m脙露llavale s脙碌jale, ning Moldova suhted ELiga on j脙碌udnud uude etappi. Moldova edusammud, nagu ka riigi valmisolek v脙碌tta k脙陇sile j脙陇rgmised tarvilikud reformid, on tunnustust v脙陇脙陇rt. Euroopa Komisjon peaks Moldovat eesm脙陇rkide saavutamisel j脙碌ulisemalt toetama. Lisaks vajab Euroopa Liit kindlat plaani, kuidas tulevikus v脙碌idelda Venemaa ja tema k脙陇silaste ulatusliku h脙录briidkampaaniaga, mis oli suunatud Moldova vastu p脙碌hiseadusliku referendumi ja presidendivalimiste kontekstis.

脗聽

14. Two years since the devastating Tempi rail accident (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Pre脜聼edinte. 芒聙聯 Urm脛聝torul punct pe ordinea de zi este dezbaterea privind Declara脠聸ia Comisiei 芒聙聭 Au trecut doi ani de la accidentul feroviar devastator de la Tempi [2025/2698(RSP)].

A脠聶 dori s脛聝 v脛聝 informez c脛聝 la aceast脛聝 dezbatere va exista doar o singur脛聝 rund脛聝 de interven脠聸ii ale vorbitorilor din partea grupurilor politice. Prin urmare, nu se aplic脛聝 procedura Catch-the-eye 脠聶i nu se accept脛聝 cartona脠聶e albastre.

Commissioner, we will wait for you to set up. If you are ready, you are welcome to start this debate. Thank you so much.

脗聽脗聽Apostolos Tzitzikostas, 脦聹脦颅脦禄脦驴脧聜 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦聲脧聙脦鹿脧聞脧聛脦驴脧聙脦庐脧聜. 芒聙聯 脦職脧聧脧聛脦鹿脦碌 脦聽脧聛脧聦脦碌脦麓脧聛脦碌, 脦卤脦戮脦鹿脧聦脧聞脦鹿脦录脦卤 脦录脦颅脦禄脦路 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦驴脧聧 脦職脦驴脦鹿脦陆脦驴脦虏脦驴脧聟脦禄脦炉脦驴脧聟, 脧聙脦颅脧聛脦卤脧聝脦卤脦陆 脦麓脧聟脧聦脦录脦鹿脧聝脦鹿 脧聡脧聛脧聦脦陆脦鹿脦卤 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脧聛脦卤脦鲁脦录脦卤脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦卢 脧聠脧聛脦鹿脦潞脧聞脦庐 脧聞脧聛脦卤脦鲁脧聣脦麓脦炉脦卤 脧聞脧聣脦陆 脦陇脦碌脦录脧聙脧聨脦陆, 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脦驴脧聙脦驴脦炉脦卤 脧聞脧聦脧聝脦驴 脦卢脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤 57 脦卢脦陆脦赂脧聛脧聣脧聙脦驴脦鹿 脦颅脧聡脦卤脧聝脦卤脦陆 脧聞脦路 脦露脧聣脦庐 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 芒聙聰脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脦禄脦碌脦鹿脦驴脧聢脦路脧聠脦炉脦卤 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦陆脦颅脦驴脦鹿 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦碌脧聙脦颅脧聝脧聞脧聛脦碌脧聠脦卤脦陆 脧聝脧聞脦驴 脧聝脧聙脦炉脧聞脦鹿 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜. 脦聭脦陆 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脦驴 脧聞脧聛脦卤脦鲁脦鹿脦潞脧聦 脦卤脧聟脧聞脧聦 脦麓脧聟脧聝脧聞脧聧脧聡脦路脦录脦卤 脦麓脦碌脦陆 脦颅脦鲁脦鹿脦陆脦碌 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脦碌脧聛脦鹿脧聠脦颅脧聛脦碌脦鹿脦卢 脦录脦驴脧聟, 脧聙脦驴脦禄脦禄脦驴脦炉 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦陆脦颅脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦卤脦陆脦赂脧聛脧聨脧聙脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脧聡脦卢脦赂脦路脦潞脦卤脦陆 脧聙脧聛脦驴脦颅脧聛脧聡脦驴脦陆脧聞脦卤脦陆 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脧聦脦禄脦路 脦录脦驴脧聟, 脧聞脦路 脦聵脦碌脧聝脧聝脦卤脦禄脦驴脦陆脦炉脦潞脦路, 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦碌脦潞脦碌脦炉脦陆脦驴 脧聞脦驴 脦虏脧聛脦卢脦麓脧聟 脦碌脧聙脦颅脧聝脧聞脧聛脦碌脧聠脦卤脦陆 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦鹿脧聜 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脦潞脦驴脧聙脦颅脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜. 脦聽脧聛脦驴脧聝脧聣脧聙脦鹿脦潞脦卢, 脦鲁脦陆脧聣脧聛脦炉脦露脧聣 脧聞脧聛脦碌脦鹿脧聜 脦驴脦鹿脦潞脦驴脦鲁脦颅脦陆脦碌脦鹿脦碌脧聜 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦颅脧聡脦卤脧聝脦卤脦陆 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦卤脦陆脦赂脧聛脧聨脧聙脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦碌脦潞脦碌脦炉脦陆脦驴 脧聞脦驴 脦虏脧聛脦卢脦麓脧聟 芒聙聰脦录脦碌脧聞脦卤脦戮脧聧 脧聞脧聣脦陆 脦驴脧聙脦驴脦炉脧聣脦陆 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦录脦鹿脦卤脧聜 脧聝脧聞脦碌脦陆脦庐脧聜 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦碌脧聛脦鲁脦卢脧聞脦鹿脦麓脧聦脧聜 脦录脦驴脧聟 脧聦脧聞脦卤脦陆 脦庐脦录脦驴脧聟脦陆 脧聙脦碌脧聛脦鹿脧聠脦碌脧聛脦碌脦鹿脦卢脧聛脧聡脦路脧聜芒聙聰 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦颅脦露脦路脧聝脦卤 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脦潞脦驴脦陆脧聞脦卢 脦卤脧聟脧聞脦庐 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聞脧聛脦卤脦鲁脧聣脦麓脦炉脦卤. 脦聲脧聙脦鹿脧聞脧聛脦颅脧聢脧聞脦碌 脦录脦驴脧聟, 脦禄脦驴脦鹿脧聙脧聦脦陆, 脦戮脦碌脦潞脦鹿脦陆脧聨脦陆脧聞脦卤脧聜 脦陆脦卤 脦碌脦潞脧聠脧聛脦卢脧聝脧聣 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聙脦卢脦禄脦鹿 脧聞脦卤 脦碌脦鹿脦禄脦鹿脦潞脧聛脦鹿脦陆脦庐 脦录脦驴脧聟 脧聝脧聟脦禄脦禄脧聟脧聙脦路脧聞脦庐脧聛脦鹿脦卤 脧聝脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聝脧聟脦鲁脦鲁脦碌脦陆脦碌脦炉脧聜 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦颅脧聡脦卤脧聝脦卤脦陆 脧聙脦卤脦鹿脦麓脦鹿脦卢, 脦鲁脦驴脦陆脦碌脦炉脧聜, 脦卤脦麓脦颅脧聛脧聠脦鹿脦卤, 脦碌脦鲁脦鲁脧聦脦陆脦鹿脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聠脦炉脦禄脦驴脧聟脧聜.

脦聴 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤脧聝脦炉脦卤 脦碌脧聙脦炉 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦卤脦虏脦卢脧聝脦碌脦鹿 脦卤脧聠脦驴脧聛脦卢 脦潞脧聟脧聛脦炉脧聣脧聜 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脦禄脦路脦录脦录脦碌脦禄脦庐 脦碌脦陆脧聝脧聣脦录脦卢脧聞脧聣脧聝脦路 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦碌脧聠脦卤脧聛脦录脦驴脦鲁脦庐 脧聞脧聣脦陆 脦卤脧聙脦卤脦鹿脧聞脦庐脧聝脦碌脧聣脦陆 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦碌脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦庐脧聜 脦陆脦驴脦录脦驴脦赂脦碌脧聝脦炉脦卤脧聜 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脧聞脦路 脧聝脦鹿脦麓脦路脧聛脦驴脦麓脧聛脦驴脦录脦鹿脦潞脦庐 脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卢脦禄脦碌脦鹿脦卤, 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聝脧聟脦鲁脦潞脦碌脦潞脧聛脦鹿脦录脦颅脦陆脦卤 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦驴脦麓脦路脦鲁脦炉脦卤脧聜 (脦聲脦聲) 2016/798 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦庐脧聜 脦聢脦陆脧聣脧聝脦路脧聜 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卢脦禄脦碌脦鹿脦卤 脧聞脧聣脦陆 脧聝脦鹿脦麓脦路脧聛脦驴脦麓脧聛脧聦脦录脧聣脦陆. 脦拢脧聞脦驴 脧聙脦禄脦卤脦炉脧聝脦鹿脦驴 脦卤脧聟脧聞脦庐脧聜 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤脧聝脦炉脦卤脧聜, 脧聟脧聙脦卢脧聛脧聡脦碌脦鹿 脧聛脦路脧聞脦庐 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦戮脦碌脦潞脦卢脦赂脦卤脧聛脦路 脦卤脦陆脦卤脧聠脦驴脧聛脦卢 脧聝脧聞脦鹿脧聜 脧聝脦路脦录脦卤脦陆脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦颅脧聜 脧聝脧聟脧聝脧聞脦路脦录脦鹿脦潞脦颅脧聜 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脧聡脧聛脦驴脦陆脦鹿脦潞脦颅脧聜 脦碌脦禄脦禄脦碌脦炉脧聢脦碌脦鹿脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脧聝脦鹿脦麓脦路脧聛脦驴脦麓脧聛脧聦脦录脦驴脧聟, 脧聞脦鹿脧聜 脦驴脧聙脦驴脦炉脦碌脧聜 脦录脦卢脦禄脦鹿脧聝脧聞脦卤 脧聝脧聟脦露脦庐脧聞脦路脧聝脦卤 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脦卤脦潞脧聦脦录脦路 脦录脦炉脦卤 脧聠脦驴脧聛脦卢 脦碌脦潞脧聞脦碌脦陆脧聨脧聜 脧聝脧聞脦路 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦卢脦陆脧聞脦路脧聝脦庐 脦录脦驴脧聟 脧聙脧聛脦鹿脦陆 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脦录脦炉脦卤 脦碌脦虏脦麓脦驴脦录脦卢脦麓脦卤 脦录脦碌 脧聞脦驴脦陆 脦卤脧聛脦录脧聦脦麓脦鹿脦驴 脦聢脦禄脦禄脦路脦陆脦卤 脧聟脧聙脦驴脧聟脧聛脦鲁脧聦 脦聹脦碌脧聞脦卤脧聠脦驴脧聛脧聨脦陆.

脦聦脧聙脧聣脧聜 脦鲁脦陆脧聣脧聛脦炉脦露脦碌脧聞脦碌, 脦路 脦聲脦禄脦禄脦卢脦麓脦卤 脦颅脧聡脦碌脦鹿 脦潞脦卤脧聞脦卤脧聛脧聞脦炉脧聝脦碌脦鹿 脦颅脦陆脦卤 脧聝脧聡脦颅脦麓脦鹿脦驴 脦麓脧聛脦卢脧聝脦路脧聜 脦录脦碌 脧聝脦潞脦驴脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦卤脦陆脧聞脦鹿脦录脦碌脧聞脧聨脧聙脦鹿脧聝脦路 脧聦脦禄脧聣脦陆 脧聞脧聣脦陆 脦露脦路脧聞脦路脦录脦卢脧聞脧聣脦陆 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦卤脧聙脦卤脦鹿脧聞脦碌脦炉脧聞脦卤脦鹿 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脦陆脦卤 脦鲁脦炉脦陆脦碌脦鹿 脧聞脦驴 脧聝脧聟脦陆脧聞脦驴脦录脧聦脧聞脦碌脧聛脦驴 脦麓脧聟脦陆脦卤脧聞脧聦 脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卤脦禄脦庐脧聜 脦驴 脦碌脦禄脦禄脦路脦陆脦鹿脦潞脧聦脧聜 脧聝脦鹿脦麓脦路脧聛脧聦脦麓脧聛脦驴脦录脦驴脧聜. 脦聭脧聟脧聞脧聦, 脦卢脦禄脦禄脧聣脧聝脧聞脦碌, 脦驴脧聛脦炉脦露脦碌脦鹿 脦路 脦碌脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦庐 脦陆脦驴脦录脦驴脦赂脦碌脧聝脦炉脦卤: 脦路 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卢脦禄脦鹿脧聝脦路 脧聞脦路脧聜 脧聝脦鹿脦麓脦路脧聛脦驴脦麓脧聛脦驴脦录脦鹿脦潞脦庐脧聜 脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卢脦禄脦碌脦鹿脦卤脧聜 脦碌脦炉脦陆脦卤脦鹿 脦碌脧聟脦赂脧聧脦陆脦路 脧聞脧聣脦陆 脦潞脧聛脦卤脧聞脧聨脦陆 脦录脦碌脦禄脧聨脦陆 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脧聣脦陆 脦碌脦赂脦陆脦鹿脦潞脧聨脦陆 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦卤脧聛脧聡脧聨脦陆. 脦聴 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦庐 脦聲脧聙脦鹿脧聞脧聛脦驴脧聙脦庐 脦麓脦碌脦陆 脧聟脧聙脦驴脦潞脦卤脦赂脦鹿脧聝脧聞脦卢 脧聞脦鹿脧聜 脦碌脦赂脦陆脦鹿脦潞脦颅脧聜 脦卤脧聛脧聡脦颅脧聜 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聠脦驴脧聛脦碌脦炉脧聜, 脦驴脧聧脧聞脦碌 脦碌脦潞脧聞脦碌脦禄脦碌脦炉 脧聞脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦赂脦庐脦潞脦驴脦陆脧聞脦卢 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜, 脦驴脧聧脧聞脦碌 脧聠脧聟脧聝脦鹿脦潞脦卢 脦颅脧聡脦碌脦鹿 脦卤脦陆脦卤脦潞脧聛脦鹿脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦颅脧聜, 脦碌脧聛脦碌脧聟脦陆脦路脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦颅脧聜 脦庐 脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤脧聝脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦颅脧聜 脦卤脧聛脦录脦驴脦麓脦鹿脧聦脧聞脦路脧聞脦碌脧聜.

脦聯脦陆脧聣脧聛脦炉脦露脦碌脧聞脦碌, 脦碌脧聙脦炉脧聝脦路脧聜, 脧聦脧聞脦鹿 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聟脦禄脦驴脧聙脦驴脦炉脦路脧聝脦路 脦卤脧聟脧聞脦驴脧聧 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脧聝脧聡脦碌脦麓脦炉脦驴脧聟 脦麓脧聛脦卢脧聝脦路脧聜 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦录脦卤脧聜 脧聟脧聙脦颅脦虏脦卤脦禄脦碌 脦路 脦聲脦禄脦禄脦卢脦麓脦卤 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦卤脦潞脦驴脦禄脦驴脧聟脦赂脦驴脧聧脦录脦碌 脧聝脧聞脦碌脦陆脦卢 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦卤脦戮脦鹿脦驴脦禄脦驴脦鲁脦驴脧聧脦录脦碌 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脧聛脦潞脧聨脧聜. 脦聠脦禄脦禄脧聣脧聝脧聞脦碌, 脦卤脧聟脧聞脦驴脧聧脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聞脦碌脦禄脦碌脧聟脧聞脦卤脦炉脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦录脦庐脦陆脦碌脧聜 脦颅脧聡脧聣 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦驴脧聟脧聝脦鹿脦卤脧聝脧聞脦碌脦炉 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脦卤脧聛脦录脧聦脦麓脦鹿脦卤 脦碌脧聙脦鹿脧聞脧聛脦驴脧聙脦庐 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦驴脧聧 脦職脦驴脦鹿脦陆脦驴脦虏脦驴脧聟脦禄脦炉脦驴脧聟 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦颅脧聡脧聣 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦驴脧聟脧聝脦鹿脦卢脧聝脦碌脦鹿 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦碌脦戮脦颅脦禄脦鹿脦戮脦路 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤脧聝脦炉脦卤脧聜. 脦職脦卤脦鹿, 脦虏脦颅脦虏脦卤脦鹿脦卤, 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦卤脦潞脦驴脦禄脦驴脧聟脦赂脦驴脧聧脦录脦碌 脧聝脧聞脦碌脦陆脦卢 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脦陆脦卤 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卤脦禄脦炉脧聝脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦碌脧聠脦卤脧聛脦录脦驴脦鲁脦庐 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦碌脦陆脧聞脧聦脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脧聝脧聟脦录脧聠脧聣脦陆脦路脦录脦颅脦陆脦驴脧聟 脧聡脧聛脦驴脦陆脦驴脦麓脦鹿脦卤脦鲁脧聛脦卢脦录脦录脦卤脧聞脦驴脧聜.

脦聹脦碌 脦虏脦卢脧聝脦路 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聞脦碌脦禄脦碌脧聟脧聞脦卤脦炉脦卤 脦颅脦潞脦赂脦碌脧聝脦路 脧聙脧聛脦驴脧聦脦麓脦驴脧聟 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦颅脧聝脧聞脦碌脦鹿脦禄脦碌 脧聞脦驴 脧聟脧聙脦驴脧聟脧聛脦鲁脦碌脦炉脦驴 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦庐 脦聲脧聙脦鹿脧聞脧聛脦驴脧聙脦庐, 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脧聙脦鹿脧聝脧聞脧聨脧聝脦卤脦录脦碌 脧聦脧聞脦鹿 脦鲁脦碌脦陆脦鹿脦潞脦卢 脧聟脧聙脦卢脧聛脧聡脦碌脦鹿 脦潞脦卤脦禄脦庐 脧聙脧聛脧聦脦驴脦麓脦驴脧聜. 脦聭脧聟脧聞脧聦, 脧聦脦录脧聣脧聜, 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦碌脧聙脦碌脧聝脦庐脦录脦卤脦陆脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦露脦庐脧聞脦路脧聝脦卤 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦驴脦陆 脦聢脦禄脦禄脦路脦陆脦卤 脧聟脧聙脦驴脧聟脧聛脦鲁脧聦 脦碌脦炉脦陆脦卤脦鹿 脦路 脦碌脧聙脦鹿脧聞脦卢脧聡脧聟脦陆脧聝脦路 脧聞脧聣脦陆 脦卤脦陆脦卤脦鲁脦潞脦卤脦炉脧聣脦陆 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦碌脦录脦虏脦卢脧聝脦碌脧聣脦陆 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聞脦卤脧聡脧聧脧聞脦碌脧聛脦路 脧聟脦禄脦驴脧聙脦驴脦炉脦路脧聝脦庐 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜. 脦聴 脦聲脦禄脦禄脦卢脦麓脦卤 脦潞脦卤脧聞脦卤脦虏脦卢脦禄脦禄脦碌脦鹿 脦录脦鹿脦卤 脧聝脦驴脦虏脦卤脧聛脦庐 脧聙脧聛脦驴脧聝脧聙脦卢脦赂脦碌脦鹿脦卤 脧聙脧聛脦驴脦潞脦碌脦鹿脦录脦颅脦陆脦驴脧聟 脦陆脦卤 脦麓脦鹿脦驴脧聛脦赂脧聨脧聝脦碌脦鹿 脧聞脦卤 脦录脦碌脦鲁脦卢脦禄脦卤 脧聝脧聟脧聝脧聞脦路脦录脦鹿脦潞脦卢 脧聙脧聛脦驴脦虏脦禄脦庐脦录脦卤脧聞脦卤 脦麓脦碌脦潞脦卤脦碌脧聞脦鹿脧聨脦陆 脧聝脧聞脦驴脦陆 脦碌脦禄脦禄脦路脦陆脦鹿脦潞脧聦 脧聝脦鹿脦麓脦路脧聛脧聦脦麓脧聛脦驴脦录脦驴.

脦聦脦录脧聣脧聜, 脦驴 脧聡脧聛脧聦脦陆脦驴脧聜 脧聙脦鹿脦颅脦露脦碌脦鹿 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦卤脧聟脧聞脧聦 脧聝脦路脦录脦卤脦炉脦陆脦碌脦鹿 脧聦脧聞脦鹿 脧聙脧聛脦颅脧聙脦碌脦鹿 脦陆脦卤 脦鲁脦炉脦陆脦驴脧聟脦陆 脦卢脦禄脦录脦卤脧聞脦卤 脧聝脦碌 脧聦脦禄脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聞脦驴脦录脦碌脦炉脧聜. 脦聭脧聟脧聞脧聦 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脧聞脦驴 脦驴脧聙脦驴脦炉脦驴 脦赂脦颅脦禄脧聣 脦陆脦卤 脧聝脦卤脧聜 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脦虏脦碌脦虏脦卤脦鹿脧聨脧聝脧聣 脦碌脦炉脦陆脦卤脦鹿 脧聦脧聞脦鹿 脦碌脦卢脦陆 脦驴脧聙脦驴脦鹿脦卤脦麓脦庐脧聙脦驴脧聞脦碌 脧聝脧聞脦鹿脦鲁脦录脦庐 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脧聙脦鹿脧聝脧聞脧聨脧聝脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦卤脦录脦鹿脦潞脧聛脦庐 脦驴脦禄脦鹿脦鲁脧聣脧聛脦炉脦卤 脦庐 脦潞脦卤脦赂脧聟脧聝脧聞脦颅脧聛脦路脧聝脦路 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聟脦禄脦驴脧聙脦驴脦炉脦路脧聝脦路 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脧聝脧聡脦碌脦麓脦炉脦驴脧聟 脦麓脧聛脦卢脧聝脦路脧聜 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦聲脦禄脦禄脦卢脦麓脦卤脧聜, 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦炉脦麓脦鹿脦卤 脧聝脧聞脦鹿脦鲁脦录脦庐 脦赂脦卤 脧聙脧聛脦驴脧聡脧聣脧聛脦庐脧聝脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聞脦路 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤脧聝脦炉脦卤 脦碌脧聙脦炉 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦卤脦虏脦卢脧聝脦碌脦鹿 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦碌脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦庐脧聜 脦陆脦驴脦录脦驴脦赂脦碌脧聝脦炉脦卤脧聜 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦聲脦禄脦禄脦卢脦麓脦卤 脧聝脧聞脦鹿脧聜 脦碌脧聙脧聦脦录脦碌脦陆脦碌脧聜 脧聠脦卢脧聝脦碌脦鹿脧聜.

脦聭脦戮脦鹿脧聦脧聞脦鹿脦录脦卤 脦录脦颅脦禄脦路 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦驴脧聧 脦職脦驴脦鹿脦陆脦驴脦虏脦驴脧聟脦禄脦炉脦驴脧聟, 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦卤脦潞脦驴脦禄脦驴脧聟脦赂脦驴脧聧脦录脦碌 芒聙聰脧聦脧聙脧聣脧聜 脧聝脦卤脧聜 脦碌脦炉脧聙脦卤芒聙聰 脧聝脧聞脦碌脦陆脦卢 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脦驴脧聛脦碌脦炉脦卤 脧聙脧聛脦驴脧聦脦麓脦驴脧聟 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脧聝脧聡脦碌脦麓脦炉脦驴脧聟 脦卤脧聟脧聞脦驴脧聧. 脦聽脦颅脧聛脦卤, 脧聦脦录脧聣脧聜, 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦驴脦陆 脦颅脦禄脦碌脦鲁脧聡脦驴 脧聞脦驴脦陆 脦驴脧聙脦驴脦炉脦驴脦陆 脦卤脧聝脦潞脦驴脧聧脦录脦碌 脦录脦碌 脦卤脧聙脧聦脦禄脧聟脧聞脦路 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦颅脧聙脦碌脦鹿脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦卤脧聟脧聝脧聞脦路脧聛脧聦脧聞脦路脧聞脦卤, 脦碌脦炉脦录脦卤脧聝脧聞脦碌 脦碌脦麓脧聨 脦颅脧聞脦驴脦鹿脦录脦驴脦鹿 脦陆脦卤 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦颅脧聡脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脦潞脦卢脦赂脦碌 脦麓脧聟脦陆脦卤脧聞脦庐 脧聞脦碌脧聡脦陆脦鹿脦潞脦庐 脧聟脧聙脦驴脧聝脧聞脦庐脧聛脦鹿脦戮脦路 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脦聲脦禄脦禄脦卢脦麓脦卤 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脦陆脦卤 脦鲁脦炉脦陆脦碌脦鹿 脦驴 脦碌脦禄脦禄脦路脦陆脦鹿脦潞脧聦脧聜 脧聝脦鹿脦麓脦路脧聛脧聦脦麓脧聛脦驴脦录脦驴脧聜 脧聝脧聧脦鲁脧聡脧聛脦驴脦陆脦驴脧聜, 脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卤脦禄脦庐脧聜 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦卤脦戮脦鹿脧聦脧聙脦鹿脧聝脧聞脦驴脧聜, 脧聦脧聙脧聣脧聜 脦卤脧聛脦录脧聦脦露脦碌脦鹿 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聦脧聙脧聣脧聜 脦卤脦戮脦炉脦露脦碌脦鹿 脧聝脦碌 脧聦脦禄脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦聢脦禄脦禄脦路脦陆脦碌脧聜 脧聙脦驴脦禄脦炉脧聞脦碌脧聜. 脦陇脦驴 脦炉脦麓脦鹿脦驴, 脧聠脧聟脧聝脦鹿脦潞脦卢, 脦卤脦戮脦炉脦露脦碌脦鹿 脧聝脦碌 脧聦脦禄脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脦炉脦驴脧聟脧聜 脧聙脦驴脦禄脦炉脧聞脦碌脧聜 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脧聞脦驴 脦炉脦麓脦鹿脦驴 脦潞脦卢脦陆脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聝脦碌 脦驴脧聙脦驴脦鹿脦卤脦麓脦庐脧聙脦驴脧聞脦碌 脧聡脧聨脧聛脦卤 脧聡脧聛脦碌脦鹿脦卤脧聝脧聞脦碌脦炉 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聞脦碌脧聡脦陆脦鹿脦潞脦庐 脦录脦卤脧聜 脦虏脦驴脦庐脦赂脦碌脦鹿脦卤.

脦聯脦鹿脦卤 脦卤脧聟脧聞脧聦 脦赂脦卤 脦庐脦赂脦碌脦禄脦卤, 脦潞脦禄脦碌脦炉脦陆脦驴脦陆脧聞脦卤脧聜, 脦陆脦卤 脦卤脦陆脦卤脧聠脦碌脧聛脦赂脧聨 脧聝脦碌 脦潞脦卢脧聞脦鹿 脦卤脦潞脧聦脦录脦卤: 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脦卤脧聙脧聦脧聠脦卤脧聝脦庐 脦录脦卤脧聜 脦陆脦卤 脧聙脧聛脦驴脧聡脧聣脧聛脦庐脧聝脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脦卤脦陆脦卤脦赂脦碌脧聨脧聛脦路脧聝脦路 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦潞脦卤脦陆脦驴脦陆脦鹿脧聝脦录脦驴脧聧 (脦聲脦聲) 2016/796 脧聞脦驴脧聟 ERA, 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦卤脧聙脦驴脦潞脦碌脦陆脧聞脧聛脧聣脦录脦颅脦陆脦驴脧聟 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦驴脧聧 脦聼脧聛脦鲁脦卤脦陆脦鹿脧聝脦录脦驴脧聧 脦拢脦鹿脦麓脦路脧聛脦驴脦麓脧聛脧聦脦录脧聣脦陆. 脦拢脧聞脧聦脧聡脦驴脧聜 脦录脦卤脧聜 脦碌脦炉脦陆脦卤脦鹿 脦路 脦碌脦陆脦炉脧聝脧聡脧聟脧聝脦路 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脧聛脧聦脦禄脦驴脧聟 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦卤脦潞脦驴脦禄脦驴脧聧脦赂脦路脧聝脦路 脧聞脦路脧聜 脧聙脧聛脦卤脦潞脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦庐脧聜 脦碌脧聠脦卤脧聛脦录脦驴脦鲁脦庐脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦碌脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦驴脧聧 脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤脦炉脦驴脧聟 脧聝脧聞脦卤 脦潞脧聛脦卢脧聞脦路 脦录脦颅脦禄脦路, 脧聨脧聝脧聞脦碌 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脦潞脦卢脦赂脦碌 脧聙脧聛脧聦脦虏脦禄脦路脦录脦卤 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦卤脦陆脧聞脦鹿脦录脦碌脧聞脧聣脧聙脦炉脦露脦碌脧聞脦卤脦鹿 脦陆脦卤 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卤脦禄脦炉脧聝脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聦脧聞脦鹿 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦颅脧聡脦碌脧聞脦卤脦鹿 脦卤脦录脦颅脧聝脧聣脧聜 脦路 脦潞脦卤脧聞脦卢脦禄脦禄脦路脦禄脦路 脧聞脦碌脧聡脦陆脦鹿脦潞脦庐 脧聟脧聙脦驴脧聝脧聞脦庐脧聛脦鹿脦戮脦路. 脦陇脦鹿 脦赂脦颅脦禄脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脦陆脦卤 脦潞脦卢脦陆脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌; 脦聵脦颅脦禄脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脦陆脦卤 脦鲁脦炉脦陆脦碌脦鹿 脦驴 ERA 脧聙脦鹿脦驴 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦碌脦录脦虏脦卤脧聞脦鹿脦潞脧聦脧聜 脧聝脧聞脦卤 脦潞脧聛脦卢脧聞脦路 脦录脦颅脦禄脦路. 脦聣脦麓脦路 脦麓脦驴脧聟脦禄脦碌脧聧脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聙脦卢脦陆脧聣 脧聝脦碌 脦卤脧聟脧聞脦庐脦陆 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦卤脦陆脦卤脦赂脦碌脧聨脧聛脦路脧聝脦路, 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦驴脧聙脦驴脦炉脦卤 脦赂脦卤 脧聙脦卤脧聛脦驴脧聟脧聝脦鹿脦卢脧聝脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聝脦碌 脦录脦碌脧聛脦鹿脦潞脦驴脧聧脧聜 脦录脦庐脦陆脦碌脧聜, 脦录脦颅脧聡脧聛脦鹿 脧聞脦鹿脧聜 脦卤脧聛脧聡脦颅脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟 2026.

脦拢脦卤脧聜 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脦虏脦碌脦虏脦卤脦鹿脧聨脦陆脧聣, 脦禄脦驴脦鹿脧聙脧聦脦陆, 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脦卤脦潞脧聦脦录脦路 脦录脦炉脦卤 脧聠脦驴脧聛脦卢 脧聦脧聞脦鹿 脦鹿脧聝脧聡脧聧脦碌脦鹿 脧聝脧聞脦驴 脦卤脦潞脦颅脧聛脦卤脦鹿脦驴 脦卤脧聟脧聞脧聦 脧聙脦驴脧聟 脦颅脧聡脧聣 脧聙脦碌脦鹿 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脧聛脧聨脧聞脦路 脧聝脧聞脦鹿脦鲁脦录脦庐: 脦路 脦卤脧聝脧聠脦卢脦禄脦碌脦鹿脦卤 脦碌脦炉脦陆脦卤脦鹿 脦路 脦卤脧聙脧聦脦禄脧聟脧聞脦路 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦卤脦麓脦鹿脦卤脧聙脧聛脦卤脦鲁脦录脦卢脧聞脦碌脧聟脧聞脦路 脧聙脧聛脦驴脧聞脦碌脧聛脦卤脦鹿脧聦脧聞脦路脧聞脦卢 脦录脦驴脧聟. 脦職脦卤脦鹿 脦卤脧聟脧聞脧聦 脦潞脦卢脦陆脦驴脧聟脦录脦碌 脧聙脧聛脦卢脦戮脦路.

脗聽脗聽脦聲脦禄脦鹿脧聝脧聝脦卢脦虏脦碌脧聞 脦聮脧聦脦露脦碌脦录脧聙脦碌脧聛脦鲁脦潞-脦聮脧聛脧聟脧聣脦陆脦炉脦麓脦路, 脦碌脦戮 脦驴脦陆脧聦脦录脦卤脧聞脦驴脧聜 脧聞脦路脧聜 脦驴脦录脦卢脦麓脦卤脧聜 PPE. 芒聙聯 脦職脧聧脧聛脦鹿脦碌 脦聽脧聛脧聦脦碌脦麓脧聛脦碌, 脦聶脧聞脦卤脦禄脦炉脦卤, 2016, 脧聝脧聧脦鲁脦潞脧聛脦驴脧聟脧聝脦路 脧聞脧聛脦颅脦陆脧聣脦陆, 23 脦陆脦碌脦潞脧聛脦驴脦炉脦聡 脦聯脦碌脧聛脦录脦卤脦陆脦炉脦卤, 2016, 脧聝脧聧脦鲁脦潞脧聛脦驴脧聟脧聝脦路 脧聞脧聛脦颅脦陆脧聣脦陆, 12 脦陆脦碌脦潞脧聛脦驴脦炉, 85 脧聞脧聛脦卤脧聟脦录脦卤脧聞脦炉脦碌脧聜脦聡 脦聶脧聝脧聙脦卤脦陆脦炉脦卤, 2013, 脦碌脦潞脧聞脧聛脦驴脧聡脦鹿脦卤脧聝脦录脧聦脧聜 脧聞脧聛脦颅脦陆脦驴脧聟, 80 脦陆脦碌脦潞脧聛脦驴脦炉, 40 脧聞脧聛脦卤脧聟脦录脦卤脧聞脦炉脦碌脧聜. 脦聰脦碌脦陆 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦碌脧聡脦炉脦露脧聣, 脧聝脧聟脦陆脦卢脦麓脦碌脦禄脧聠脦驴脦鹿. 脦聯脦鹿脦卤脧聞脦炉, 脦卢脧聛脦卤脦鲁脦碌, 脦卤脦陆脦卢脦禄脦驴脦鲁脦碌脧聜 脧聟脧聙脦驴脦赂脦颅脧聝脦碌脦鹿脧聜 脦麓脦碌脦陆 脧聝脧聟脦露脦路脧聞脦庐脦赂脦路脦潞脦卤脦陆 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脦聼脦禄脦驴脦录脦颅脦禄脦碌脦鹿脦卤; 脦聯脦鹿脦卤脧聞脦炉 脧聝脧聟脦露脦路脧聞脦碌脦炉脧聞脦卤脦鹿 脦录脧聦脦陆脦驴 脦路 脧聞脧聛脦卤脦鲁脧聣脦麓脦炉脦卤 脧聞脧聣脦陆 脦陇脦碌脦录脧聙脧聨脦陆, 脦麓脧聧脦驴 脧聡脧聛脧聦脦陆脦鹿脦卤 脦录脦碌脧聞脦卢 脧聞脦驴 脦麓脧聟脧聝脧聞脧聧脧聡脦路脦录脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦碌脦陆脧聨 脦颅脧聡脦碌脦鹿 脧聙脧聛脦驴脦路脦鲁脦路脦赂脦碌脦炉 脦麓脦庐脦禄脧聣脧聝脦路 脦卤脧聙脧聦 脧聞脦路脦陆 脦聽脧聛脧聦脦碌脦麓脧聛脦驴 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦聲脧聟脧聛脧聣脧聙脦卤脧聤脦潞脦驴脧聧 脦職脦驴脦鹿脦陆脦驴脦虏脦驴脧聟脦禄脦炉脦驴脧聟 脧聞脦驴脦陆 脦聹脦卢脧聛脧聞脦鹿脦驴 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脧聞脧聛脦颅脧聡脦驴脦陆脧聞脦驴脧聜 脦颅脧聞脦驴脧聟脧聜; 脦聰脦鹿脧聦脧聞脦鹿 脦路 脦卤脦陆脧聞脦鹿脧聙脦驴脦禄脦炉脧聞脦碌脧聟脧聝脦路 脧聝脧聞脦路脦陆 脦聲脦禄脦禄脦卢脦麓脦卤 芒聙聰脧聙脦驴脧聟 脧聙脧聛脦驴脦潞脦卢脦禄脦碌脧聝脦碌 脧聞脦路 脧聝脦路脦录脦碌脧聛脦鹿脦陆脦庐 脧聝脧聟脦露脦庐脧聞脦路脧聝脦路芒聙聰 脦赂脦碌脧聣脧聛脦碌脦炉 脧聦脧聞脦鹿 脦录脧聙脦驴脧聛脦碌脦炉 脦陆脦卤 脧聙脦颅脧聝脦碌脦鹿 脦路 脦陆脧聦脦录脦鹿脦录脦路 脦潞脧聟脦虏脦颅脧聛脦陆脦路脧聝脦路, 脦麓脦鹿脦卤脧聝脧聧脧聛脦驴脦陆脧聞脦卢脧聜 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聙脦卤脦陆脧聞脦驴脧聧 脧聣脧聜 脦卤脧聙脦驴脦潞脦禄脦碌脦鹿脧聝脧聞脦鹿脦潞脦卢 脧聟脧聙脦卤脦炉脧聞脦鹿脦卤 脦鲁脦鹿脦卤 脧聞脦路脦陆 脧聞脧聛脦卤脦鲁脧聣脦麓脦炉脦卤 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦露脦路脧聞脧聨脦陆脧聞脦卤脧聜 脦录脦碌 脦碌脧聞脦碌脧聛脧聦脦潞脦禄脦路脧聞脦碌脧聜 脧聝脧聟脦录脦录脦卤脧聡脦炉脦碌脧聜 脦陆脦卤 脦潞脦卤脧聞脦卤脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤脧聝脧聞脦驴脧聧脦陆 脧聣脧聜 脦碌脦鲁脦潞脦禄脦路脦录脦卤脧聞脦炉脦碌脧聜 脧聞脦驴脧聟 脦潞脦驴脦鹿脦陆脦驴脧聧 脧聙脦驴脦鹿脦陆脦鹿脦潞脦驴脧聧 脦麓脦鹿脦潞脦卤脦炉脦驴脧聟 脧聙脦禄脦庐脦赂脦驴脧聜 脧聟脧聙脦驴脧聟脧聛脦鲁脧聨脦陆 脦潞脦卤脦鹿 脦驴 脦炉脦麓脦鹿脦驴脧聜 脦驴 脧聙脧聛脧聣脦赂脧聟脧聙脦驴脧聟脧聛脦鲁脧聦脧聜 脧聞脦路脧聜 脧聡脧聨脧聛脦卤脧聜.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandro Gozi, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, Tempi was not a simple accident. It was foreseeable. The warnings were there, the resources too. But the state failed to act. And 57 people lost their lives.

Today, a second injustice adds to the first. The Greek Government is trying to reduce this to a single mistake to avoid political responsibility. This is not justice. The families haven't forgotten. The fight for truth and justice is our fight, because Tempe is more than a national tragedy. It exposes deeper failures. A rule of law crisis, political pressure, violation of EU law.

The European Commission has already opened a case against Greece for failing to guarantee basic railway safety. The Commissioner has just recorded. So it is a European problem, it's not only a national problem. And even before the tragedy, the system was known to be failing because no one acted. And that goes beyond transport. Tempi is a symptom of something bigger, a situation where the rule of law is eroded from within.

Ms Vozemberg, you ask why we didn't discuss the train accident in Italy, for example? Because in that case, justice was done. And in this case, according to our information, the Greek judges association has openly and officially criticised the way the inquiry is conducted. Did or did not, Ms Vozemberg, the government cement the crime scene without any any approval by the prosecutor? Did it happen or it didn't happen? This is the reality. This is why the European Commission must act.

Dear Commissioner, you said safety is not negotiable. Truth is not negotiable either. Justice cannot wait. Silence is no longer an option.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Virginijus Sinkevi脛聧ius, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President,脗聽dear colleagues, let me first express my deepest condolences to the families of the victims, and remember those who lost their lives.

This disaster is a clear reminder that safety on our railways must always come first. And we need to be transparent about what really went wrong and open about what is being done to fix it, to prevent it from happening in future. People deserve to know how EU funds for transport are used and how safety is being improved.

The EU has given significant funds for transport and infrastructure. And when public money does not lead to public safety, then political responsibility must follow. So let us make sure that strong, clear rules are in place and followed across every Member State. Because the goal is simple: safer journeys and renewed trust.

We call on the European Commission to be fully transparent, showing what was done before the disaster and what is being done now to protect lives, improve railway safety and ensure EU funds truly benefit the public.

脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Siegbert Frank Droese, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, verehrte Kollegen! Die Bahnkatastrophe von Tempi vor zwei Jahren ist immer noch nicht aufgearbeitet, das haben massive Proteste von Millionen von Griechen noch einmal eindrucksvoll unter Beweis gestellt. Es bleiben offene Fragen, die gekl脙陇rt werden m脙录ssen. Wieso gab es eine so schlampige Beweisaufnahme durch die Polizei? Wie kann es sein, dass der damals verantwortliche Verkehrsminister Karamanlis zwar zur脙录ckgetreten ist, aber wenig sp脙陇ter wieder zur脙录ck in der politischen Arena war? Wann wird endlich das teilweise marode Streckennetz auf einer wichtigen Strecke zwischen Athen und Saloniki modernisiert? Auf der anderen Seite geben wir Milliarden f脙录r Ausr脙录stung aus, w脙陇hrend unsere Infrastruktur zerf脙陇llt.

Bemerkenswert ist auch, dass die Europ脙陇ische Staatsanwaltschaft ermittelt hat, und dennoch ist bis heute kaum etwas aufgekl脙陇rt, obwohl die Trag脙露die schon 脙录ber zwei Jahre zur脙录ckliegt. Es ist eine europ脙陇ische Schande. Die ESN芒聙聭Fraktion verlangt eine l脙录ckenlose Aufkl脙陇rung des Unfalls und eine Verurteilung der Verantwortlichen脗聽芒聙聯 das sind wir den 57脗聽Toten schuldig. Niemand will mehr Ausreden h脙露ren 脙录ber die Ursachen, dass nur ein kleiner Bahnw脙陇rter schuld sei oder dass die Wirtschaftskrise eben schuld ist. Wir werden den Politkommissar Tzitzikostas bei der Aufkl脙陇rung und Verbesserung der Situation kritisch begleiten.
