脗聽脗聽S脙漏rgio Humberto (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, caros colegas, falo-vos enquanto cidad脙拢o europeu, mas, sobretudo, enquanto deputado respons脙隆vel desta Casa, de um tema t脙拢o significativo que afeta a vida de milh脙碌es e milh脙碌es de pessoas.

Devemos ter em conta v脙隆rios pontos, mas vou falar-vos de tr脙陋s.

Primeiro: a interconectividade 脙漏 importante, sobretudo nas regi脙碌es ultraperif脙漏ricas.

Segundo: o automatismo das indemniza脙搂脙碌es 脙漏 conveniente, pois poder脙隆 aumentar a competitividade das companhias a脙漏reas.

E terceiro: o limite m脙颅nimo para a compensa脙搂脙拢o por atraso de um voo deve ser de tr脙陋s horas e n脙拢o de quatro, para n脙拢o corrermos o risco de enviar a mensagem de que estamos a regredir, em vez de estarmos a proteger os cidad脙拢os europeus.

Como 脙漏 que podemos garantir que estamos ao lado dos passageiros, que os defendemos, quando aos olhos dos cidad脙拢os lhes apresentamos aquilo que se assemelha mais a um retrocesso do que a um avan脙搂o?

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, 脗驴cu脙隆ntas veces nos han llegado a la Comisi脙鲁n de Peticiones de este Parlamento quejas de los pasajeros y las pasajeras por retrasos, por cobros de maletas y por retraso en las compensaciones?

Como dice mi compa脙卤ero Vicent Marz脙聽, ahora que tenemos la oportunidad de acelerar las mejoras de los derechos, el Consejo mete el freno al Parlamento Europeo. Cuatro horas, esperar cuatro horas para que haya una compensaci脙鲁n por retraso y para cobrarla... que pasen muchos meses. 脗驴Y qu脙漏 decir del equipaje de mano? Hace ya diez a脙卤os que el Tribunal de Justicia de la Uni脙鲁n Europea dictamin脙鲁 que cobrar por la maleta de mano es ilegal.

Estos temas tocan a miles de personas en Europa, a los derechos de las pasajeras y los pasajeros cada d脙颅a. Y es que las compa脙卤脙颅as a脙漏reas act脙潞an con impunidad. No solo enga脙卤an a la gente, sino que nos quitan vuelos y, como pasa por ejemplo en Galicia, la gente va a pagar, compra billetes y luego tiene que pagar m脙隆s cuando llegan al aeropuerto. No puede ser que dominen las decisiones europeas los grupos de presi脙鲁n, las grandes aerol脙颅neas y sus consejos directivos, y que no se tenga en cuenta la voz de tantas pasajeras y pasajeros. Aterricen y escuchen a la gente.

脗聽脗聽Magdalena Adamowicz (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Ju脜录 dawno na tej sali nie by脜聜o tylu takich samych g脜聜os脙鲁w.

Ponad miliard pasa脜录er脙鲁w europejskich linii lotniczych w ci脛聟gu roku, prawie 100 milion脙鲁w miesi脛聶cznie. Kim wobec tej skali przewoz脙鲁w jest pojedynczy pasa脜录er, kt脙鲁ry czeka trzy godziny na sp脙鲁脜潞niony lot albo kt脙鲁remu odwo脜聜ano lot czy zgubiono baga脜录? Dla niego to mo脜录e by脛聡 脜录yciowa katastrofa. Dla linii lotniczych jest jednym z miliarda pasa脜录er脙鲁w.

Dlatego im wi脛聶cej latamy, tym bardziej potrzebujemy ochrony naszych praw. Linie lotnicze s脛聟 wystarczaj脛聟co rentowne, aby za swoje b脜聜脛聶dy p脜聜aci脛聡 pasa脜录erom.

Automatyzm odszkodowa脜聞 to bardzo korzystna dla pasa脜录er脙鲁w zmiana, ale je脜录eli mamy poprawi脛聡 ochron脛聶 pasa脜录er脙鲁w, to nie powinni脜聸my stosowa脛聡 metody 芒聙聻co脜聸 za co脜聸芒聙聺, czyli wprowadzamy automatyzm odszkodowa脜聞, ale jednocze脜聸nie wyd脜聜u脜录amy czas dozwolonego op脙鲁脜潞nienia.

Ludzie kupuj脛聟 bilety lotnicze, by dotrze脛聡 do celu...

(Przewodnicz脛聟ca odebra脜聜a m脙鲁wczyni g脜聜os)

(Ende der spontanen Wortmeldungen)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Apostolos Tzitzikostas, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, thank you for your very interesting comments and in particular for your commitment to protecting European passengers, which is also my priority.脗聽I take note of your concerns, which you will certainly have the opportunity to raise with the Council.

I want you to know that my objective and the objective of the proposal of the Commission is to ensure, on the one hand, the effective protection of passenger rights and on the other hand, to provide sufficient operational and financial flexibility to air carriers.

Of course, and I want to underline this, the Commission encourages both institutions to engage in good faith, to continue the spirit of excellent cooperation that you have often demonstrated, and to pursue negotiations in a way that ensures that we have the best possible chance of finding a balanced, fair and reasonable compromise.

Now, I understand that most, if not all, of you want even more rights for passengers and I agree with you. So, I pledge today to you that during the trilogues, we will have the negotiations and the Commission's position will be to make an effort to reach the best possible balance that will allow the passengers to have even more rights.

Ladies and gentlemen, Members of the European Parliament, I really believe that through this democratic process that is starting now, after having managed 芒聙聯 and I congratulate the Council for that 芒聙聯 to unblock this file, we will have the chance to do what is right for the passengers, what is right for the European airlines and for the efforts that we all do to make our European industry and our European aviation sustainable on the one hand, but also competitive on the other.

So, Minister, thank you again very much for your efforts and we look forward during this Presidency to move on with the trilogues and find an amicable agreement.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adam Sz脜聜apka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Mr Commissioner, honourable Members, thank you very much for this frank and fruitful discussion.

Air passenger rights have been and will remain a European landmark for EU citizens. We have together the responsibility to adopt legislation that will address effectively the needs of our citizens and I am confident that we will reach an agreement on this major piece of legislation. Thank you very much for this.

Madam President, since this is my last statement during the Polish Presidency, I would like to thank you very much, Madam President, and all the honourable Members of the European Parliament and the Commission for our common work.

When we were setting the agenda and the priorities of the Polish Presidency, we were thinking about responsibility and security, and I think that these six months were very good. There was good cooperation between the Council and the European Parliament. I am very glad for this House, for this cooperation, and I am sure that we did a very big step to make Europe more secure and safer.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

Schriftliche Erkl脙陇rungen (Artikel 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Angel (S&D), in writing. 芒聙聯 Delays, cancellations or missed connections can lead to major inconveniences for air travellers. It is therefore paramount to ensure that passengers benefit from a strong set of rights in case things go awry. We welcome the fact that discussions on the revision of the Air Passenger Rights Regulation from 2004 are finally advancing.

We are, however, concerned by some of the proposals put forward by the Council. As S&D, we are notably taken aback by the proposed weakening of the compensation rules, which are at the heart of our air passenger rights framework and which have inspired other countries to follow suit. Considering that most delays range between two and four hours, the new proposed threshold looks like a deliberate attempt to exclude a majority of people from compensation, while disincentivising airlines to be more punctual.

We find it baffling that we would undermine one of our flagship achievements in consumer policy, meaning that consumers who pay good money for flights would have to put up with longer delays. The revision of the Air Passenger Rights Regulation should be an occasion to reinforce consumer protection in view of new developments in the sector, not to hand out gifts to airlines.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mihai Tudose (S&D), 脙庐n scris. 芒聙聯 Dup脛聝 12 ani de blocaj nejustificat 脠聶i p脛聝gubos pentru cet脛聝脠聸enii UE, taxa脠聸i abuziv 脙庐n acest timp de companiile aeriene, exist脛聝 speran脠聸a unui acord interinstitu脠聸ional pentru crearea unui cadru adecvat de asigurare a drepturilor pasagerilor aerieni. Reforma 脙庐n domeniu e cu at脙垄t mai necesar脛聝 cu c脙垄t normele privind compensa脠聸iile 脙庐n caz de 脙庐nt脙垄rziere sau anulare a zborurilor dateaz脛聝 din 2004.

Dezbaterea din plen are loc dup脛聝 ce Consiliul UE 脠聶i-a definit pozi脠聸ia 脠聶i sper c脛聝 negocierea Parlament-Consiliu va duce rapid 脠聶i eficient la stabilirea de norme clare 脠聶i rezonabile 脙庐n materie de desp脛聝gubire 脠聶i rambursare, precum 脠聶i 脙庐n ceea ce prive脠聶te taxarea bagajelor de m脙垄n脛聝.

脙聨nc脛聝 din 2014, Curtea de Justi脠聸ie a Uniunii Europene a tran脠聶at c脛聝 bagajul de m脙垄n脛聝 este un element necesar al transportului aerian de pasageri, prin urmare trebuie s脛聝 fie gratuit dac脛聝 are dimensiuni 脠聶i greutate rezonabile. Propunerea Parlamentului pentru un bagaj gratuit p脙垄n脛聝 la 7 kg, pe l脙垄ng脛聝 geanta de m脙垄n脛聝 sau rucsacul deja permise, vine s脛聝 transpun脛聝 脙庐n practic脛聝 decizia Cur脠聸ii. Sus脠聸in, totodat脛聝, ideea a脠聶ez脛聝rii 脙庐n mod automat a copiilor p脙垄n脛聝 la 12 ani l脙垄ng脛聝 adultul 脙庐nso脠聸itor, f脛聝r脛聝 tax脛聝 suplimentar脛聝 de loc.

脗聽

21. Situation in the Middle East (joint debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die gemeinsame Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rung der Vizepr脙陇sidentin der Kommission / Hohen Vertreterin der Union f脙录r Au脙聼en- und Sicherheitspolitik zu dem Risiko einer zunehmenden Destabilisierung im Nahen Osten infolge der milit脙陇rischen Eskalation zwischen Israel und Iran (2025/2770(RSP)) und 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rung der Vizepr脙陇sidentin der Kommission / Hohen Vertreterin der Union f脙录r Au脙聼en- und Sicherheitspolitik zu der 脙聹berpr脙录fung des Assoziierungsabkommens zwischen der EU und Israel und der anhaltenden humanit脙陇ren Krise im Gazastreifen (2025/2747(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kaja Kallas, Vice-President of the Commission / High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, Israel and Iran have exchanged attacks for five days now. The escalation we see is extremely dangerous for many reasons, including civilian casualties and the risk of radioactive spillage.

The impact of the crisis on global energy markets is also a risk. The conflict has already triggered the largest oil price surge in a single day in three years. As President脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen has said, the EU stands ready to coordinate with partners to protect market stability.

This morning, I chaired an extraordinary Foreign Affairs Council to discuss the situation in the Middle East, including Iran. Ministers called on all sides to abide by international law, exercise restraint and avoid actions that could spiral out of control.

Iran is still the main source of regional instability. It is one of the most repressive regimes of the world. The EU has always maintained that Iran must never be allowed to acquire a nuclear weapon. Last week, and for the first time in 20脗聽years, the International Atomic Energy Agency declared Iran to be in breach of its non芒聙聭proliferation obligations.

Israel's attacks, while they may have set Iran's nuclear programme back, cannot so easily erase Iran's know芒聙聭how, technology and intentions. Diplomacy will always be the best way to address Iran's nuclear programme in the long run and Europe stands ready to play its part in brokering a deal.

With regard to the immediate fallout of the conflict, we discussed what the EU can offer in terms of practical support now. We have activated civil protection mechanisms and are working closely with the Member States to assist their citizens who wish to leave the region.

We have offered to deploy civil protection experts to assist with potential water decontamination efforts, and our naval operation, Aspides, continues to protect merchant ships from Houthi attacks while providing valuable situational awareness.

Dear脗聽President, honourable Members, what was also clear from today's meeting is that we will not let the new crisis overshadow what is happening in Gaza. We are now 20脗聽months into a conflict triggered by the horrific attacks of Hamas. Israel has the right to defend itself and Hamas should immediately release the remaining hostages.

But Israel's use of force in Gaza, the continuing deaths of civilians and the targeting of civilian infrastructure go beyond self芒聙聭defence. Israel's actions cannot be justified under humanitarian and international law.

At the last Foreign Affairs Council in May, it was clear that the majority of the Member States were in favour of opening a review of Israel's compliance with Article脗聽2 of the Association Agreement. Article脗聽2 imposes binding obligations on the parties to observe human rights. This review is under way and I will present the outcome to the foreign ministers already on Monday.

At the same time, the EU continues to work on all fronts to provide support to the Palestinian people. We are still the largest international donor. Our humanitarian funding for this year alone has been EUR脗聽170脗聽million, and since October 2023 the EU has helped transport close to 5脗聽000 tonnes of humanitarian cargo into Gaza.

I've repeatedly spoken to the Israeli Foreign Minister with a plea to allow the humanitarian aid into Gaza without interruption and to resume the ceasefire. The answer to aid being weaponised is not to block it, but to flood the region with so much humanitarian aid that it cannot be misused. And there is more than enough to do this.

But the humanitarian situation is still deteriorating. Three warehouses were recently opened in Gaza, but only one is now accepting new deliveries. The world is desperate to help. The amount of aid is not the problem; the access is.

Honourable Members, the Middle East is burning, military aggression is unrelenting and the anger seems to be unstoppable. The short芒聙聭term consequences of fire between Israel and Iran could delay Iran's nuclear efforts, but there is no certainty that Israel will get what it seeks in this way.

Likewise, in Gaza, it is perfectly clear for all to see that military means will not provide the solace that the Israeli people are looking for. It has not resulted in the liberation of hostages and it has now endangered the entire population of Gaza, causing unjustified human suffering.

The European Union must do everything we can to push for diplomacy, meet humanitarian needs where we can and stand up to the values we were built on.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Gahler, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, colleagues, on Iran, I think that the regime has been almost at the point enabling it to produce a nuclear bomb. That is the last thing the rest of the world wants to see. Part of the truth is, however, that Iran has come that far because the US stepped out of the nuclear deal in 2018, a deal that provided comprehensive control and transparency on Iran's nuclear programme. Thereafter, the regime did not feel bound to this treaty any longer.

Moreover, I think that the Israeli Government was unhappy that the US unilaterally negotiated a deal with Iran, which, on substance, would even not have provided the detailed provisions of the previous agreement.

Since 1979, the Mullah regime has proclaimed the destruction of the State of Israel as one of their political objectives. In this regard, I believe them. As a consequence, I am not limiting myself to a purely legal assessment, whether this action can be qualified as an act of pre芒聙聭emptive self芒聙聭defence. Politically, I can accept this strike. Toda raba.

On Gaza, the right of self芒聙聭defence as a consequence of the Hamas terror attack has to be applied in accordance with international humanitarian law. The population in a war zone has to be protected. Military action concentrated on combatants and to block food supplies for almost three months for more than two million people to push Hamas to release the remaining hostages is not justifiable.

Moreover, to announce the creation of an additional 22脗聽settlements in the occupied West Bank is as such illegal and also not a contribution to a two芒聙聭state solution that this Parliament, all Member States and the international community demand.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Iratxe Garc脙颅a P脙漏rez, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, mientras preparaba esta intervenci脙鲁n esta tarde, he recibido una alerta en mi m脙鲁vil: al menos cincuenta y nueve脗聽civiles palestinos asesinados en un punto de reparto de alimentos. 脗驴Alguien puede acostumbrarse a que esta sea la noticia que recibimos a diario en las 脙潞ltimas semanas? Podemos determinar toda la ayuda humanitaria que queramos desde la Comisi脙鲁n Europea: mientras, miles de camiones est脙隆n esperando en Rafah para poder acceder a Gaza. Se ha decidido encerrar a m脙隆s de dos脗聽millones de gazat脙颅es en menos de cuatrocientos脗聽kil脙鲁metros脗聽cuadrados, matarlos de hambre y, de vez en cuando, abrir puntos de reparto de alimentaci脙鲁n para aprovechar y asesinarlos a balazos.

Esto es lo que est脙隆 ocurriendo y esto es de lo que tenemos que hablar, porque en Gaza la vida ha sido arrasada. Hay cad脙隆veres de ni脙卤os bajo los escombros, hay cuerpos envueltos en mantas, hay escuelas devastadas, madres que amamantan a sus hijos entre ruinas. M脙隆s de 55脗聽000 civiles han muerto. El 94脗聽% de los hospitales est脙隆n destruidos. La poblaci脙鲁n vive hacinada, sin agua, sin medicinas, sin alimentos. 脗驴Qu脙漏 m脙隆s tiene que pasar para que reconozcamos que el Gobierno de Netanyahu viola de forma sistem脙隆tica el art脙颅culo脗聽2脗聽del Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n UE芒聙聭Israel? 脗驴Cu脙隆l es el umbral del horror que debe superarse para que la Comisi脙鲁n y el Consejo act脙潞en con la dignidad y la firmeza que Europa debe representar?

La revisi脙鲁n del Acuerdo no basta: la suspensi脙鲁n inmediata de todo acuerdo pol脙颅tico y comercial es una cuesti脙鲁n de coherencia con nuestros propios valores. 脗驴Hasta cu脙隆ndo seguir脙隆 la presidenta Von脗聽der脗聽Leyen mirando hacia otro lado ante un Gobierno que ha convertido la violaci脙鲁n del Derecho internacional en doctrina?

Se脙卤ora脗聽Kallas, la Uni脙鲁n Europea no puede seguir en par脙隆lisis ni con un doble rasero. Es necesario prohibir el suministro de armas a Israel, congelar los activos de los responsables que sostienen la ocupaci脙鲁n ilegal y cooperar con la Corte Penal Internacional. Si Netanyahu pisa suelo europeo, los Estados miembros tienen que cumplir con la legalidad internacional. No se trata de hostilidad, sino de principios. Se trata de humanidad.

Y, precisamente por humanidad, tambi脙漏n debe quedar claro que el pueblo de Israel no es el Gobierno genocida de Netanyahu. Mientras miles de ciudadanos claman por la liberaci脙鲁n de los rehenes y por el fin del exterminio, Netanyahu sigue secuestrando la pol脙颅tica nacional. 脗驴Qu脙漏 mayor traici脙鲁n puede haber al pueblo de Israel que haber financiado con treinta millones脗聽de脗聽d脙鲁lares mensuales a Ham脙隆s desde 2018?

Se脙卤or脙颅as, reconocer el Estado palestino no es una concesi脙鲁n, es afirmar que la paz se construye con justicia, y sin dos Estados que vivan en paz, la guerra no terminar脙隆.

Ante la peligrosa escalada entre Israel e Ir脙隆n, la Uni脙鲁n Europea no puede limitarse a observar desde la barrera. Nuestra diplomacia debe actuar con urgencia para frenar la espiral de ataques. Se脙卤or脙颅as, nos corresponde estar del lado de quienes luchan por la paz, por la libertad y por la justicia. Recordemos las palabras del escritor israel脙颅 David Grossman: 脗芦merece la pena luchar por Israel. Para nuestros amigos palestinos, deseo una vida independiente, y deseo que, dentro de setenta脗聽a脙卤os, nuestros nietos y bisnietos palestinos e israel脙颅es est脙漏n aqu脙颅 y canten sus himnos nacionales, y en hebreo y en 脙隆rabe canten juntos el mismo verso: "ser una naci脙鲁n libre en nuestra tierra"脗禄.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastiaan St脙露teler, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, the title of this so-called joint debate is a Christmas tree that perfectly demonstrates this Parliament's cluelessness when it comes to what's going on in the Middle East. Israel attacking nuclear facilities and military targets in Iran isn't causing instability. On the contrary, by removing the offensive and world-destructive nuclear capabilities of the repressive Islamist and terror regime of the IRGC, Israel is stabilising the region and safeguarding the world. You'd better believe the ayatollahs when they chant 'Death to Israel!', 'Death to America!' and 'Death to the West!', that it is their goal, their sole purpose. The world should be on their knees thanking Israel for its heroic acts.

Eastern European cities could easily be reached by the ballistic missiles of the IRGC. Europe should stand with Tel Aviv, the city that is now facing the enormous missile storm of North Korean technology. Europe and Ukraine should be thanking Israel now that the drone and missile exports from Iran to Russia will likely come to a stop, but instead, the European Commission is investigating Israel's compliance with certain parts of the Association Agreement. For what purpose 芒聙聯 cancelling the agreement or changing the agreements? You cannot do either of those, so what is your aim?

The EU has the same association agreements with Algeria, Morocco, Egypt and even an interim agreement with the Palestinian Authority, whose human rights standards are nowhere near those of Israel. Israel is a democratic country that upholds the rule of law and provides us Europeans with life-saving intelligence, health products, food and technology.

Commissioner, High Representative/Vice President, criticising Israel or the Israeli Government isn't necessarily antisemitism, but holding the only Jewish State to different standards than other countries is. Colleagues, don't forget the hostages, bring them home now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen, namens de ECR-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, beste collega's, de waarschuwing van het Internationaal Atoomenergieagentschap vorige week was glashelder: Iran, dat Isra脙芦l van de kaart wil vegen, zou binnen enkele weken een kernbom klaar kunnen hebben. En dat ondanks alle sancties en diplomatie van de afgelopen jaren. En ja, dan komt Isra脙芦l op een punt dat militaire precisieaanvallen onvermijdelijk worden. Zoals Isra脙芦l wel moest reageren op de slachtpartij van 7脗聽oktober. Een democratische bondgenoot die strijdt tegen het kwaad, verdient onze steun. Helaas lijkt Europa vooral bezig te zijn met het boycotten van Isra脙芦l. Universiteiten verbreken banden. De associatieovereenkomst staat ter discussie en zelfs het nieuwe distributiesysteem voor humanitaire hulp voor Gaza krijgt geen steun. Mijn oproep vandaag is: stop met deze boycotacties. Je mag bondgenoten best op fouten wijzen, maar niet in de steek laten. Zeker niet in het heetst van de strijd.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bart Groothuis, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, High Representative, Israel is not attacking Iran. It is striking the brutal regime of Khamenei and his Revolutionary Guard. And Israel did what it had to do to show how to deal with murderous dictatorships that only understand force.

The ayatollahs have thus far remained in power because of the support of Russia and China. This alliance fuelled a sustained and growing campaign of violence and terror against the West, against Israel, against us and against the poor people of Iran. But the alliance isn't doing well. Moscow is already faltering today. After Syria, Russia is once again abandoning an ally. And the Russia-Iran part of the Russia-Iran-China axis is already beginning to collapse. And now the Iranian people are watching us and they ask us the question here in Europe, do we have your support?

Dear High Representative, Europe must seize the moment. We must reassert ourselves by reintroducing snapback sanctions immediately, put pressure on the regime, immediately place the IRGC on the EU terrorist list. The alternative is far graver: allowing totalitarian regimes or radical Islamist groups to rally behind Tehran. This is a historic moment demanding clarity and courage, and we cannot afford to appear weak or indecisive to Beijing, Moscow or Tehran. That would only embolden them.

To conclude, I urge you, all colleagues, to recognise the gravity of the current situation today and tonight, and stand firm with the brave Iranian people. This is not a call for escalation, on the contrary, but for principled, resolute actions to safeguard peace and secure the world.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hannah Neumann, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, in Israel, families sleep in bunkers. In Iran, people are trapped between bombs and a regime that dragged them into a war they never chose. They feel relief because the IRGC was hit, because the nuclear programme was set back, but they are also afraid of Israeli escalation. Where should 10 million Tehranis evacuate to in just a few hours?

This crisis didn't just start last week. It's the result of years of failure. Trump walked away from the nuclear deal, the Islamic regime advanced its programme, and Borrell and Mora were played again and again by Tehran. This isn't your fault, HR/VP, but you are in charge now, and Europe must finally lead.

The window for a free Iran is wider than it has been in years. We must prevent further escalation, we must ensure that this regime never gets a nuclear bomb, and we must support all those inside Iran that are risking everything for freedom.

While the situation between Iran and Israel is complex, the one in Gaza is not. What the Israeli Government is doing is brutal. It is unlawful. It does not free the hostages, it serves only the cynical agenda of a few clinging to power while children starve beneath rubble in Gaza. If the review of the EU-Israel Association Agreement is honest, it must lead to suspension, and at the same time, our support for Israeli civil society must grow because they too are under attack. We need all hands on diplomacy, all eyes on those struggling in Gaza, in Israel and in Iran. The region has suffered far too long under men who mistake violence for strength.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rima Hassan, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, des accords Sykes-Picot 脙聽 nos jours, le peuple palestinien subit un v脙漏ritable nettoyage ethnique. La passation coloniale entre l'empire britannique et le mouvement sioniste poursuit le m脙陋me objectif: d脙漏poss脙漏der les Palestiniens de la Palestine. Depuis pr脙篓s de deux ans, cette d脙漏possession est devenue une v脙漏ritable entreprise d'extermination.

J'ai deux minutes de temps de parole aujourd'hui. Sachez qu'il m'en faudrait plus de 3脗聽000, ne serait-ce que pour citer les noms des Palestiniens assassin脙漏s par Isra脙芦l depuis octobre 2023. Gaza, 100脗聽000 tonnes de bombes largu脙漏es sur 2 millions de civils soumis 脙聽 des d脙漏placements forc脙漏s, plus de 55脗聽000 morts, dont 70脗聽% de femmes et d'enfants, 130脗聽000 bless脙漏s, plus de 200 journalistes et 300 humanitaires assassin脙漏s, 90脗聽% des 脙漏coles et 100脗聽% des universit脙漏s ras脙漏es. Cisjordanie, plus de 1脗聽000 morts, dont 200 enfants, 800脗聽000 colons arm脙漏s soutenus par le r脙漏gime isra脙漏lien, plus de 10脗聽000 Palestiniens et Palestiniennes qui croupissent dans les ge脙麓les coloniales isra脙漏liennes.

L'histoire se souviendra de ceux qui se sont lev脙漏s et de ceux qui, comme vous, Madame Kallas, ont laiss脙漏 faire. D脙篓s octobre 2024, un rapport interne vous a pourtant 脙漏t脙漏 remis, concluant 脙聽 des crimes de guerre, des crimes contre l'humanit脙漏 et des actes de g脙漏nocide commis par Isra脙芦l. Qu'en avez-vous fait? Je vous le r脙漏p脙篓te ici, vous 脙陋tes ce que vous faites, vous 脙陋tes ce que vous cautionnez.

Les gens qui nous 脙漏coutent doivent savoir trois choses. Premi脙篓rement, via le programme Horizon Europe, l'Union europ脙漏enne finance directement l'industrie militaire isra脙漏lienne. Deuxi脙篓mement, les pays de l'Union europ脙漏enne repr脙漏sentent pr脙篓s de la moiti脙漏 des exportations d'armes vers Isra脙芦l. Troisi脙篓mement, avec l'accord d'association qui n'est toujours pas suspendu, l'Union europ脙漏enne demeure le premier partenaire commercial d'Isra脙芦l.

Les citoyens europ脙漏ens, par millions, vous attendent au tournant. L'impunit脙漏 d'Isra脙芦l doit cesser. La complicit脙漏 avec l'脙聣tat colonial et criminel isra脙漏lien ne se fera pas en notre nom, Madame Kallas, pour prot脙漏ger vos petits int脙漏r脙陋ts. Rien, absolument rien n'脙漏touffera le cri qui r脙漏clame justice et dignit脙漏 pour le peuple palestinien et, 脙聽 travers lui, celui des peuples que l'ordre capitaliste et n脙漏ocolonial pi脙漏tine et entra脙庐ne vers l'ab脙庐me. Vive la Palestine libre!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonio L脙鲁pez-Ist脙潞riz White (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, between democracy and dictatorship, we choose democracy. Between peace and war, we choose peace. But one thing must be clear for us Europeans: if Israel fails, Europe fails. We are the next target of the theocratic regime of Tehran.

Are we clear about these priorities? After listening to the interventions of some colleagues from the left, I sometimes have my doubts. Some of them prefer tension, polarisation, which I know many other colleagues from the left 芒聙聯 as well as myself 芒聙聯 reject.

Israel is a democracy. But does this mean that I agree with everything done by Israel's democratically elected government? No. I support the defence of the State of Israel. I support the protection of the Jewish population in Europe against rising antisemitism. But I do not agree with how the humanitarian situation in Gaza is being handled by this government. Fortunately, because it's a democracy, I know that the people and the justice in Israel will have a say about these decisions in the future.

I cannot say the same about the Palestinian people or the Iranian people. We must support the liberation of the Palestinian people from the terrorist organisations backed by the Iranian regime. And we must help Iranian society, which is seeking freedom and democracy. We Europeans must support Israel's efforts to end the Iranian regime's nuclear threats.

We have to make sure that our money and humanitarian aid reach the Palestinian and Lebanese people, not Hamas or Hezbollah. We have to move forward with the two-state solution and support cooperation with countries like Lebanon or Jordan and nations in the region by providing the possibility for all to prosper and live with dignity.

脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elena Donazzan (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, Israele ha il coraggio di fare quello che l'Europa non ha il coraggio di fare. Israele ha il coraggio di difendersi quando 脙篓 sotto attacco. Non possiamo dimenticare che il 7 ottobre 1脗聽400 vittime erano civili, bambini, anziani nelle loro case, ad un concerto, e 250 rapiti, di cui 30 bambini.

Israele ha il coraggio di reagire e di difendersi. 脙聢 sotto attacco ogni giorno, ma i giornali europei, i giornali del mondo, non ne parlano. Israele ha il coraggio di dire chi 脙篓 colpevole, di sapere che dietro quel terrorismo di Hamas c'脙篓 l'Iran. Ha il coraggio di chiamare le cose con il loro nome. Ha il coraggio di dire che questo terrorismo 脙篓 quello che commuove l'Europa e le anime fragili quando vedono dei bambini. Ma quei bambini sono i figli di quei terroristi che vengono usati da loro come scudi umani. Inaccettabile usare i propri figli come scudi umani, inaccettabile usare gli ospedali come centri di comando del terrorismo.

脗聽脗聽

PR脙聣SIDENCE: YOUNOUS OMARJEE
Vice-Pr脙漏sident

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bernard Guetta (Renew). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, 脙聽 Gaza, Isra脙芦l est condamnable, mais l'est-il 脙聽 T脙漏h脙漏ran? 脙聙 Gaza, nous devons condamner des bombardements toujours plus insens脙漏s qui ne visent, depuis longtemps, plus 脙聽 d脙漏manteler le Hamas, mais 脙聽 d脙漏truire une large partie de ce qui doit devenir la Palestine. Devant ces crimes de guerre, nous devons exprimer notre opprobre et en tirer les cons脙漏quences. Mais les frappes isra脙漏liennes contre la th脙漏ocratie iranienne, son programme nucl脙漏aire, ses cadres et ses infrastructures rel脙篓vent en revanche d'une riposte pleinement justifiable.

Il y a 40 ans que ce r脙漏gime voue Isra脙芦l 脙聽 l'an脙漏antissement. Il y a 40 ans qu'il organise, arme et finance pour cela ses sous-traitants r脙漏gionaux. Et au moment o脙鹿 cette th脙漏ocratie si totalement d脙漏testable 脙漏tait si proche de se doter de la bombe, Isra脙芦l n'aurait rien d脙禄 faire? Poser la question, c'est y r脙漏pondre.

Autant Isra脙芦l est criminel 脙聽 Gaza, autant il est dans son droit 脙聽 T脙漏h脙漏ran. Mais quand viendra la fin de cette guerre, son premier ministre aura un choix 脙聽 faire. Il devra soit ouvrir un nouveau si脙篓cle d'affrontements en optant pour la chim脙篓re du grand Isra脙芦l, soit d脙漏finir avec les Palestiniens les conditions d'une coexistence entre les 脙聣tats auxquels ces deux peuples ont droit. C'est de ce choix que d脙漏pendra le destin d'Isra脙芦l.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mounir Satouri (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, ce d脙漏bat aurait d脙禄 porter sur une seule question, l'accord d'association entre l'Union europ脙漏enne et Isra脙芦l. Ce n'est pas une affaire de mots, c'est une affaire de courage. Pour une fois, l'Europe peut agir et elle doit agir. Mais au lieu d'agir, vous parlez, vous tergiversez, vous r脙漏脙漏valuez. Alors, je le demande 脙聽 Mme脗聽Kallas: que reste-t-il 脙聽 r脙漏脙漏valuer en mati脙篓re de droits humains?

D脙漏placements forc脙漏s des populations: fait; massacres de civils, de femmes, d'enfants: fait; h脙麓pitaux d脙漏truits, personnels humanitaires d脙漏lib脙漏r脙漏ment tu脙漏s: fait; journalistes cibl脙漏s: fait; destruction des infrastructures: fait; privation de l'acc脙篓s 脙聽 l'eau: fait; utilisation de la famine comme arme de guerre: fait. M脙陋me la case g脙漏nocide est coch脙漏e. Mais 脙搂a, Madame脗聽Kallas, et la Commission, vous devez le savoir, puisqu'un rapport secret de l'UE datant de脗聽2024 documente toutes les violations dont Isra脙芦l est responsable.

Qu'est-ce qui justifie qu'on continue encore d'脙漏valuer cet accord d'association? Alors dites-moi, sur quoi repose ce 脗芦rien脗禄, sur quel vide moral, sur quel renoncement europ脙漏en et sur quel sentiment d'impunit脙漏 aussi? Sans l'Europe, Isra脙芦l n'aurait pas tous ces moyens financiers et militaires. Si vous ne suspendez pas cet accord, Madame脗聽Kallas, vous vous rendrez responsable d'une contribution volontaire 脙聽 la poursuite de ce g脙漏nocide. Alors, s'il vous pla脙庐t, agissez!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame脗聽Kallas, Yaran脗聽Ghasemian, un b脙漏b脙漏 de deux mois qui vient d'脙陋tre tu脙漏 par un bombardement isra脙漏lien sur l'Iran. Parnia脗聽Abbasi, jeune po脙篓te, 23脗聽ans, son petit fr脙篓re Parham, 脙聽 peine 16脗聽ans. Une vie devant eux, des r脙陋ves d'espoir, mais Isra脙芦l a d脙漏cid脙漏 de les tuer, en Iran, 脙聽 T脙漏h脙漏ran. L脙聽 o脙鹿 leurs bombes touchent aussi maintenant journalistes et h脙麓pitaux. Vous appelez 脙搂a des bombardements cibl脙漏s? Moi, j'appelle 脙搂a des crimes de guerre.

Isra脙芦l veut faire 脙聽 l'Iran ce qu'ils font 脙聽 Gaza. Et l'Europe? Tandis que vous 脙漏tiez encore un peu mal 脙聽 l'aise par rapport 脙聽 votre soutien aux crimes et au g脙漏nocide isra脙漏liens 脙聽 Gaza, l脙聽, vous assumez. Le chancelier allemand Merz qui fait: 脗芦Ah, ils font le sale boulot pour nous!脗禄

Quoi, tuer des gosses, c'est 脙搂a le sale boulot que vous voulez qu'Isra脙芦l fasse pour nous? Mais c'est quoi cette Europe qui assume la criminalit脙漏 de son imp脙漏rialisme? C'est une honte! Aujourd'hui, rompez avec Isra脙芦l ou vous aussi vous serez condamn脙漏s par la justice internationale.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hildegard Bentele (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, High Representative, dear colleagues, let us speak plainly about who is truly escalating and destabilising the Middle East. Israel, a democratic state, has been fighting for its very existence since 1948. Its core policy objective has remained consistent 芒聙聯 to survive, to defend its citizens fleeing from persecution globally, and to live in peace with its neighbours. Peace treaties with Jordan and Egypt, and more recently the Abraham Accords, are proof.

On 7 October 2023, Israel was brutally attacked by Hamas and simultaneously assaulted by Hezbollah and the Houthis, terrorist organisations and shadow armies of Iran with one goal 芒聙聯 the destruction of Israel and the extermination of its people. Let us be clear, instability in the region, first and foremost, stems from the Iranian regime proclaiming openly 'Death to America!' and 'Death to Israel!'.

Unfortunately, the shifting posture of the United States under President Trump is another source of insecurity that we cannot ignore. The US solely has the attributes of a global stabiliser, and we Europeans have often seconded upholding the rules-based international order. But today, our coordination and cooperation is clearly insufficient, whether on Afghanistan, Ukraine, China or Iran. This lack of unity ...脗聽

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Van Brempt (S&D). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, mevrouw de hoge vertegenwoordiger, collega's, de escalatie van het conflict in het Midden-Oosten is desastreus voor de stabiliteit in de regio en de rest van de wereld. Maar zoals altijd zijn het gewone burgers die de prijs betalen voor dit brute geweld. Tegelijkertijd gaat het gruwelijke en genocidaire offensief tegen de Palestijnen in Gaza en de Westelijke Jordaanoever gewoon verder.

De vraag hier is: aan welke kant staat Europa? Blijkbaar niet aan die van de slachtoffers. Blijkbaar niet aan de kant van het internationaal recht en de mensenrechten. En blijkbaar niet aan de kant van de 250.000 Europeanen die dit weekend in Belgi脙芦 en Nederland de straat op trokken. Ik weet niet of u dat gezien hebt, mevrouw de hoge vertegenwoordiger. Zij krijgen vandaag van de Commissie stilzwijgen, terwijl Netanyahu van de voorzitter van de Commissie schouderklopjes krijgt. Dat is niet enkel schandalig. Het maakt Europa medeplichtig. Europese leiders verstoppen zich vandaag achter elkaar en de Commissie verstopt zich achter de unanimiteit in de Raad. Maar u hebt niet altijd unanimiteit nodig om te ageren. U kunt initiatief nemen, bijvoorbeeld om delen van het associatieverdrag op te zeggen. Ik denk aan culturele samenwerking, onderzoek en ontwikkeling, samenwerking met universiteiten en samenwerking op het vlak van gezondheid. De Commissie kan dat initiatief nemen. It's time to act.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rihards Kols (ECR). 芒聙聯 Dear colleagues, as we debate, yet again Iran has launched another barrage of missiles towards Israel. And Iran's constitutional doctrine calls for Israel's destruction. This is not rhetoric. It's a state policy.

Tehran arms Hamas, Hezbollah, the Houthis, and supplies and co-produces missiles and drones with Russia to aid its expansionist war on Ukraine. Its nuclear programme adds a new layer of existential threat not only to Israel, but to global security.

The UN Atomic Agency confirms Iran has stockpiled highly enriched uranium far beyond a civilian purpose. This is a nuclear weapons ambition. Nothing else. Israel is now dismantling Iran's missile, drone and nuclear infrastructure. All targets are military. Every destroyed facility reduces Iran's capability to threaten Israel, Ukraine and potentially Europe. Every eliminated operative weakens the entire authoritarian axis.

The humanitarian tragedy in Gaza is undeniable. But the root cause is Hamas, armed, financed and directed by Tehran. Remember this: authoritarian axis feeds on fear.

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barry Andrews (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, High Representative, colleagues, we really live in a strange world. The EU isn't sure whether Israel's murderous attacks on Gaza meet the threshold for a review of the human rights article of the Association Agreement.

But many leaders in the EU are sure that Iran's behaviour meets the threshold for a pre芒聙聭emptive strike, even while negotiations on non芒聙聭proliferation are going on.

Unfortunately, what has happened is that Israel's security needs have created a parallel set of international humanitarian law rules. These rules are considered superior to and separate from existing international humanitarian law.

We know that Iran and its mullahs have very little regard for human rights, but Israel doesn't get to decide unilaterally when standards of governance have fallen below a threshold, justifying a strike.

So, I encourage ministers on 23脗聽June脗聽to take a leaf out of the book of the UK, of Norway, of Canada, of Australia and New Zealand and to take action finally.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Villy S脙赂vndal (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Hr. formand! Med alles 脙赂jne rettet mod Israel og Iran, m脙楼 vi ikke glemme, at bomberne stadig regner ned over Gazas udsultede befolkning. F脙赂rst fastholdt i et jerngreb af Hamas siden 2007 og derefter s脙赂nderbombet af Israel. Som Jordans konge sagde i g脙楼r her i Parlamentet: En skamfuld version af vores menneskelighed udfolder sig for vores 脙赂jne, lige nu, og vores globale v脙娄rdier bliver optr脙娄vlet med chokerende hastighed med 脙赂del脙娄ggende konsekvenser. Ingen steder er de tydeligere end i Gaza. Citat slut. Israel bomber fortsat teltlejre, hospitaler, skoler, familier og b脙赂rn. Mennesker, der er flygtet igen og igen og igen. Der er n脙赂dhj脙娄lp nok, men Israel blokerer stadig for mad, for vand, for medicin, intet kommer ind. Heller ikke udenlandske journalister, der kan dokumentere uhyrlighederne. P脙楼 Vestbredden godkender Netanyahus h脙赂jreekstreme regering nye bos脙娄ttelser, senest 22. Voldelige bos脙娄tterbander har frit spil, og pal脙娄stinensernes liv forv脙娄rres af afsp脙娄rringer, arrestationer, 脙赂del脙娄ggelser og 脙赂konomisk ruin. Nok er forl脙娄ngst nok. EU's associeringsaftale med Israel m脙楼 og skal suspenderes.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kathleen Funchion (The Left). 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, when future generations look back on the live-streamed genocide in Gaza, they will not look kindly on the EU institutions. They will not look kindly on the President of the European Commission, who willingly takes phone calls from war criminals. They will not look kindly on this institution, as after nearly two years of war crimes being broadcast onto our television screens and our phones, there has been a failure to impose even the most basic of sanctions against Israel.

How much suffering must the people of Gaza endure before action is taken? How many more children must be slaughtered before the President and the Commission ensure that Netanyahu's regime is held to account? This regime is now extending their war to Iran. If the EU is serious about de-escalation and a ceasefire, it needs to send a clear message to the world that there can be no trade deals for war criminals. This is why the EU-Israel Association Agreement must be suspended with immediate effect. This institution must support the people of Palestine ...脗聽

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ruth Firmenich (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Halten wir fest: Israel f脙录hrt einen v脙露lkerrechtswidrigen Angriffskrieg gegen den Iran. Israel besitzt Atomwaffen, hat den Atomwaffensperrvertrag nicht unterzeichnet und l脙陇sst keine internationalen Inspektionen zu. Iran besitzt keine Atomwaffen, hat den Atomwaffensperrvertrag unterzeichnet, l脙陇sst internationale Inspektionen zu und hat mit den USA 脙录ber sein Atomprogramm verhandelt. Und was macht die EU, die bereits beim Gazakrieg nur herumeiert?

Frau Kallas, die Sie sonst gerne vom V脙露lkerrecht reden, Sie schaffen es nicht einmal, den Angriffskrieg Israels gegen den Iran zu verurteilen. Wo bleibt Ihr Protest, wenn Israel mit Angriffen auf Atomanlagen und iranische Journalisten das V脙露lkerrecht mit F脙录脙聼en tritt? Wenn US芒聙聭Pr脙陇sident Trump davon twittert, den Anf脙录hrer des Irans zu t脙露ten? Warum werden weiter Waffen aus der EU an Israel geliefert, und warum ist das Assoziierungsabkommen noch nicht ausgesetzt? Wie kann es sein, dass Sie die Aggressionspolitik der rechtsextremen Regierung Netanjahu immer weiter belohnen? Die Doppelmoral ist entlarvend und erschreckend.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, we must always have in our mind that the big danger in the Middle East stems unmistakably from the mullah regime of Iran and its proxies, Hamas in Gaza, Hezbollah in Lebanon and the Houthis in Yemen. It is not Israel which is the aggressor. These groups 芒聙聯 enriched, equipped and directed by the mullah regime 芒聙聯 present a unified front of existential threat to Israel and of destabilisation of the whole region. Israel is under immense pressure. Its pre-emptive strikes aim to disrupt the nuclear ambitions of Iran and degrade its operational capacity. This is an act of self-defence. We should respect it and Europe should support Israel.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Catarina Mendes (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, caros colegas, daqui a uns anos todos diremos que fizemos tudo o que pod脙颅amos para evitar o genoc脙颅dio que est脙隆 a acontecer na Palestina.

As nossas escolhas de hoje dir脙拢o se era verdade ou n脙拢o. Por isso, aproveito este minuto para apelar 脙聽 Comiss脙拢o para que conclua rapidamente o relat脙鲁rio sobre os valores consagrados no artigo 2.脗潞 e avalie se est脙拢o ou n脙拢o a ser violados. Parece evidente que sim.

Apelo para que olhe para o que se est脙隆 a passar em Gaza, nos territ脙鲁rios palestinianos ocupados. Apelo para que olhe, veja e aja.

Tendo em conta as decis脙碌es do Tribunal Internacional de Justi脙搂a, o trabalho incans脙隆vel da relatora especial das Na脙搂脙碌es Unidas para os territ脙鲁rios palestinianos ocupados e as imagens que todos os dias nos chegam a casa, torna-se dif脙颅cil defender que n脙拢o estamos perante grosseir脙颅ssimas viola脙搂脙碌es dos direitos humanos.

A Uni脙拢o Europeia n脙拢o pode continuar calada, n脙拢o pode continuar imp脙隆vida, tem de agir e tem de suspender total ou parcialmente o Acordo de Associa脙搂脙拢o com Israel. J脙隆 o disse aqui h脙隆 uns meses.

Daqui a uns anos, volto a dizer, todos diremos que fizemos tudo. A hist脙鲁ria c脙隆 estar脙隆 para nos julgar. Hoje 脙漏 dia de agir.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alexandr Vondra (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr President, the debate about cutting ties with Israel is, quite frankly, absurd. Until now, the European Union has had just a little influence on events in the Middle East, if I do not count the money to Hamas misused for terrorist attacks. If we proceed with reviewing the EU-Israel Association Agreement, we risk losing the last remnant of respect we hold with our Israeli partner.

Let's not forget the real threat in the region 芒聙聯 the Iranian nuclear ambitions. According to the IAEA, Tehran has stockpiled over 400脗聽kg of highly-enriched uranium. Is there anybody in this Chamber who really believes that it's for peaceful purposes? The EU's diplomatic efforts have led nowhere. Israel acted reasonably and the German Chancellor could not have said it better today 芒聙聯 that he's doing a dirty job on our behalf.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Irena Joveva (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, the Middle East is gripped by conflicts, genocide, suffering, injustice, driven not by ancient rivalries, but by ongoing impunity, military aggression and systematic denial of human rights. Israel's ongoing assault on the people of Gaza, the legacy of its attacks on Lebanon, and its aggressive posture across the region are not isolated incidents of self-defence. They form a broader pattern seriously threatening regional security.

This time it's Iran with an important addition: Washington's or rather Trump's footprints all over it, furthering escalations with ominous messages. Yes, Iran is an important Russian airline, but violence is never a solution, is it? Mahatma Gandhi said it best: 'Even when violence appears to do good, the good is only temporary. The evil it does is permanent'. And while some still speak about self-defence, it is so obvious who poses the greatest threat to the stability of the region.

So if the EU really wants a peaceful diplomatic solution, then it's time to finally draw the line against this aggressor too, starting with the suspension of the agreement with Israel and with respecting the ICC ruling now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Vieira (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, the EU-Israel Association Agreement requires respect for human rights from a government that targets aid workers, doctors and journalists; that is blocking humanitarian aid using famine as a weapon of war; that is committing apartheid in the West Bank; that is committing a genocide in Gaza. So, Commission, Council, colleagues in this House, my question to you is this: how long will you ignore them?脗聽The UN experts and special rapporteurs, the international courts, the human rights organisations, the students who are on hunger strike outside the doors of this Parliament, the volunteers that try to walk or sail to Gaza with aid, the tens of thousands of people marching in the streets of Europe. For how long will they be ignored?

The suspension of this agreement is long overdue. It's a legal necessity. We are Israel's largest trading partner. We have leverage. We have a responsibility. If we mean 'never again', we must act today. Our complicity must end today.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Martins (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Kallas, s脙鲁 tenho um minuto e acho que, ainda assim, num minuto 脙漏 poss脙颅vel avaliar o Acordo de associa脙搂脙拢o com Israel e decidir termin脙隆-lo.脗聽Acompanhe-me.

O segundo artigo do acordo diz: 脗芦As rela脙搂脙碌es entre as partes ser脙拢o baseadas no respeito pelos direitos humanos脗禄. E o que est脙隆 a fazer Israel? Massacra indiscriminadamente civis, incumpre o direito humanit脙隆rio internacional. Nega ajuda humanit脙隆ria, tamb脙漏m. Matar crian脙搂as aos milhares, destruir hospitais, arrasar as casas e condenar 脙聽 fome s脙拢o uma viola脙搂脙拢o dos direitos humanos. O genoc脙颅dio 脙漏 uma viola脙搂脙拢o dos direitos humanos.

Senhora Vice-Presidente da Comiss脙拢o, a脙颅 tem os fundamentos para suspender o Acordo em menos de um minuto.

Acho que ningu脙漏m vai conseguir explicar em tempo nenhum como 脙漏 que este Acordo se mant脙漏m ativo. Os l脙颅deres europeus s脙拢o j脙隆 c脙潞mplices do genoc脙颅dio e tamb脙漏m v脙拢o ter de responder pelos seus crimes.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Erik Kali脜聢脙隆k (NI). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙陆 p脙隆n predsedaj脙潞ci, 脛聫akujem pekne. Prizn脙隆m sa, 脜戮e na tento prejav som si ni脛聧 neprichystal, preto脜戮e ma ve脛戮mi zauj脙颅malo, 脛聧o sa tu dozviem v tejto s脙隆le. A mus脙颅m poveda脜楼, 脜戮e som ostal v nemom 脙潞脜戮ase. Poviem v脙隆m tak脙漏 tajomstvo, ktor脙漏 si mo脜戮no niektor脙颅 z mojich star脜隆脙颅ch kolegov neuvedomuj脙潞, ale u脜戮 nie je rok 2003. Va脜隆e rozpr脙隆vky u脜戮 znova nezafunguj脙潞. Viete, 脙隆no, ako autority dok脙隆脜戮ete presved脛聧i脜楼 voli脛聧ov, 脜戮e to, 脛聧o sa deje napr脙颅klad na Ukrajine, je absol脙潞tna katastrofa. 脜陆e je neospravedlnite脛戮n脙漏 to, 脛聧o urobilo Rusko, 脜戮e prv脙漏 za脙潞to脛聧ilo, 脜戮e to sa nem脙麓脜戮e robi脜楼. A 脙隆no, ako autority dok脙隆脜戮ete urobi脜楼 aj presn脙陆 opak a tvrdi脜楼, 脜戮e 脙潞tok, ktor脙陆 spustil Izrael, je spr脙隆vny, 脜戮e genoc脙颅da, ktor脙潞 sp脙隆chala a p脙隆cha na 脛戮u脛聫och v Gaze, je norm脙隆lna a spr脙隆vna a potrebn脙隆. Ale nedok脙隆脜戮ete to robi脜楼 naraz. A vy sa pr脙隆ve teraz usilujete, mnoh脙颅 z v脙隆s, to robi脜楼 naraz. Ale u脜戮 m脙隆me internet. Ml脙隆de脜戮 je na soci脙隆lnych sie脜楼ach doslova zavesen脙隆 a sleduje tieto va脜隆e v脙陆roky a smeje sa v脙隆m. U脜戮 v脙隆m to nevyjde? Takto to 脛聫alej nem脙麓脜戮ete robi脜楼.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wouter Beke (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, hoge vertegenwoordiger, collega's, wat Isra脙芦l vandaag in Gaza aanricht, is onaanvaardbaar: tienduizenden doden, beschietingen op voedselposten, hongersnood als oorlogswapen. Natuurlijk mocht Isra脙芦l zich na 7脗聽oktober verdedigen. Maar wat het nu doet, is disproportioneel, is illegaal en zijn oorlogsmisdaden. Dat kunnen wij niet langer tolereren. Wie zegt dat het internationaal recht moet worden verdedigd, moet ook de moed tonen, mevrouw de hoge vertegenwoordiger, om het te handhaven. Schort de associatieovereenkomst met Isra脙芦l onmiddellijk op. Artikel twee daarvan is glashelder. Respect voor de mensenrechten is geen optie maar een basisvoorwaarde. Zo staat het er. Rode lijn na rode lijn wordt overschreden, ook al probeert premier Netanyahu nu de aandacht te verleggen naar Iran.

Mevrouw Kallas, Europa moet kiezen voor zijn principes. Europa moet kiezen voor het internationaal recht, zonder uitzondering.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Leire Paj脙颅n (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, una vez m脙隆s traemos al Pleno el genocidio continuo y diario de la poblaci脙鲁n de Gaza. Pero hoy basta ya de palabras. Es hora de hablar de la inacci脙鲁n, de la vergonzosa hipocres脙颅a y de nuestra indiferencia.

Se脙卤ora alta representante, 脗驴de verdad vamos a volverles a decir a nuestros ciudadanos que no vamos a hacer nada? 脗驴Vamos a seguir tolerando el asesinato de ni脙卤os y el bloqueo de ayuda humanitaria? 脗驴Con qu脙漏 cara vamos a decirle a los periodistas que se juegan la vida para contarnos la verdad, a los actores humanitarios, que no vamos a hacer nada?

Se脙卤ora Kallas, tiene usted instrumentos para presionar al Gobierno de Israel. Le exigimos de una vez por todas que suspenda el Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n, que aplique sanciones individualizadas, que restrinja el comercio con Israel, que impida la venta y la utilizaci脙鲁n de las armas y que exija de una vez por todas la entrada de ayuda humanitaria de las Naciones Unidas. 脗驴Lo va a hacer esta vez? Porque todo lo dem脙隆s ya es complicidad.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alberico Gambino (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, gentilissima Commissaria, la crisi in Medio Oriente impone all'Europa equilibrio e responsabilit脙聽. Condanniamo con fermezza il terrorismo di Hamas e ribadiamo il diritto di Israele a difendersi. Al tempo stesso, chiediamo la protezione dei civili palestinesi e l'accesso immediato agli aiuti umanitari.

L'Italia non sostiene la revisione dell'accordo Unione europea-Israele. Sarebbe un atto ideologico non inderogabile alla credibilit脙聽 europea e ostacolerebbe ogni sforzo diplomatico. Grave preoccupazione desta anche l'escalation con l'Iran, regime che nega il diritto di Israele a esistere e alimenta il terrorismo nella regione.

Fratelli d'Italia 脙篓 chiara: mai all'arma nucleare iraniana, s脙卢 alla soluzione politica che eviti il conflitto regionale. Il Presidente Meloni lo ha ribadito al G7: serve una descalation, una tregua che apre uno spiraglio alla pace. Come ha detto Papa Leone XIV: "la pace si costruisce con giustizia, dialogo rispettoso e sicurezza per tutti". L'Italia 脙篓 pronta a fare la sua parte.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Abir Al-Sahlani (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, earlier today, 51 Palestinians were killed by Israeli tanks just because they were standing in line for food. They were starving. They were not armed terrorists. The Israeli芒聙聭backed humanitarian fund is nothing else but a war strategy of Netanyahu.

In Stockholm, in The Hague, in Brussels, hundreds of thousands of people have taken to the streets showing a red line, marching for a ceasefire, for ending this war on the people in Gaza, showing more leadership than many of those who are European leaders today. Thank you to all of you who are demonstrating or taking to the streets, showing us that Europe is not only its leadership.

Next week, the leaders of the EU have the opportunity to actually take action. And you, Commissioner, you have the chance to be one of those who will define history. You have the chance to be one of those who will rewrite our complicity with the silence that we have.

Europe should not reward war criminals and acts of war crimes with trade deals. It's time to end the Association Agreement with Israel.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Saskia Bricmont (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, des milliers de citoyens ont pris la rue, entrent en gr脙篓ve de la faim, en r脙漏sistance, marchent sur Gaza. Des dockers se mobilisent pour refuser de charger les bateaux. Des actes de r脙漏sistance face 脙聽 l'inaction complice des gouvernements et des institutions europ脙漏ennes, devant l'horreur et devant le g脙漏nocide du peuple palestinien.

L'Union europ脙漏enne est le premier partenaire commercial. Mais est-ce que l'annonce du r脙漏examen de l'accord d'association a provoqu脙漏 un quelconque mouvement de la part de Netanyahou? Rien. Rien parce que la pr脙漏sidente de la Commission europ脙漏enne elle-m脙陋me soutient personnellement un criminel de guerre sous le coup d'un mandat d'arr脙陋t de la Cour p脙漏nale internationale.

Vous allez pr脙漏senter des propositions au Conseil lundi, Madame Kallas. Nous sommes en d脙漏bat aujourd'hui au Parlement europ脙漏en. Nous voulons savoir. Allez-vous proposer la suspension de l'accord d'association UE-Isra脙芦l? Allez-vous proposer des sanctions? Allez-vous proposer d'interdire l'importation des produits et des services issus des colonies ill脙漏gales? Un embargo sur l'exportation d'armes? Allez-vous demander des comptes, en tant que premier donateur d'aide humanitaire 脙聽 la Palestine, allez-vous enfin demander des comptes 脙聽 Netanyahou? C'est cela qu'il faudra demander. Et nous exigeons la m脙陋me chose de la part de la pr脙漏sidente de la Commission europ脙漏enne.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Kaja Kallas, quem tenha na consci脙陋ncia uma r脙漏stia de humanismo vive revoltado com o que se passa em Gaza h脙隆 620 dias.

As imagens dos assassinatos de crian脙搂as nos chamados postos de distribui脙搂脙拢o de alimentos, que Israel instalou em articula脙搂脙拢o com os Estados Unidos, s脙拢o apenas um 脙潞ltimo exemplo da barb脙隆rie que Israel eleva a patamares que pens脙隆vamos nunca mais voltar a ver.

Contando com a cumplicidade dos Estados Unidos e das pot脙陋ncias da NATO e da Uni脙拢o Europeia, Israel massacra, humilha, desumaniza, impondo impunemente as mais indescrit脙颅veis atrocidades ao povo palestiniano. E, agora, bombardeia tamb脙漏m o Ir脙拢o, elevando a escalada de guerra no M脙漏dio Oriente a um patamar mais grave e de consequ脙陋ncias imprevis脙颅veis.

N脙拢o h脙隆 na Uni脙拢o Europeia um pingo de vergonha que vos demova da c脙潞mplice indiferen脙搂a perante o genoc脙颅dio, que ponha fim 脙聽 vossa hipocrisia e 脙聽 conviv脙陋ncia com a criminosa pol脙颅tica de Israel.

Pela nossa parte, 脙漏 脙聽 solidariedade com o povo palestiniano e 脙聽 sua leg脙颅tima causa nacional que nos associamos. Suspendam o acordo com Israel. Parem de financiar os crimes de Israel. Imponham o boicote de armas a Israel. Reconhe脙搂am o Estado da Palestina como determinam as resolu脙搂脙碌es da ONU. Cumpram o direito internacional.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Pascual de la Parte (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora alta representante, 脗驴suspender el Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n UE芒聙聭Israel脗聽va a avanzar un cent脙颅metro la causa palestina? 脗驴Suspender el Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n UE芒聙聭Israel va a ayudar a la situaci脙鲁n humanitaria lastimosa de los palestinos en Gaza? 脗驴Suspender el Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n UE芒聙聭Israel va a suponer facilitar la v脙颅a de negociaci脙鲁n entre las partes?

Si la respuesta a estas preguntas es negativa, tendr脙颅amos que pensar para qu脙漏 sirve entonces suspender el Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n UE芒聙聭Israel. Yo creo que lo importante es reconocer que el Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n no es un privilegio ni una gracia: es el marco de nuestras relaciones con un aliado, que nos permite tener influencia pol脙颅tica, tener un canal de comunicaci脙鲁n abierto y tener la posibilidad de utilizar este instrumento para influir en Israel, en sus pol脙颅ticas y en su sociedad.

Por tanto, seamos realistas. Somos partidarios de mantener el Acuerdo como instrumento, como herramienta de influencia y como canal de comunicaci脙鲁n y, al mismo tiempo, exigir 芒聙聰evidentemente芒聙聰 la liberaci脙鲁n inmediata de los rehenes y el acceso a la ayuda humanitaria para los palestinos.

(El orador acepta responder a una pregunta formulada con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE), pregunta de 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 脗驴Usted sabe que la Uni脙鲁n Europea es uno de los firmantes de la Convenci脙鲁n para la Prevenci脙鲁n y la Sanci脙鲁n del Delito de Genocidio y, por tanto, deber脙颅a de cumplirla? Usted lo sabe porque ha sido embajador. Me sorprende la ausencia del Partido Popular espa脙卤ol hoy aqu脙颅: solo est脙隆 usted.

Mire para el otro lado: estamos de muchos partidos. Me sorprende la ausencia de la derecha. Me sorprende que ustedes, que siempre est脙隆n diciendo que cumplen las leyes, no apelen a la Convenci脙鲁n sobre el Genocidio. 脗驴Cu脙隆l es su opini脙鲁n sobre ese tema?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Pascual de la Parte (PPE), respuesta de 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Yo estoy encantado de estar hoy aqu脙颅. Lo fundamental, cuando yo pienso actualmente en la crisis palestina, es c脙鲁mo ayudar en t脙漏rminos pr脙隆cticos a los palestinos. Aparte de hacer desahogos ret脙鲁ricos, pol脙颅ticos, 脗驴c脙鲁mo ayudarles en t脙漏rminos pr脙隆cticos? Y la mejor forma de ayudar en t脙漏rminos pr脙隆cticos es tener influencia con los palestinos y con los israel脙颅es para tratar de llevarlos a la mesa de negociaci脙鲁n y evitar estas masacres in脙潞tiles de civiles.

Podemos aqu脙颅 hacer grandes declaraciones, pero eso no ayuda para nada a los palestinos si no somos realistas, no somos pragm脙隆ticos y no tenemos en cuenta los instrumentos que tenemos a disposici脙鲁n para tener influencia sobre el Gobierno de Israel y sobre la sociedad de Israel en lugar de borrarnos totalmente de la ecuaci脙鲁n y apartarnos.

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Alta Representante, a escalada militar entre o Ir脙拢o e Israel 脙漏, infelizmente, uma cortina de fumo sobre a imensid脙拢o da trag脙漏dia humanit脙隆ria que prossegue em Gaza, sobre o abandono pol脙颅tico dos ref脙漏ns do Hamas, sobre os esfor脙搂os diplom脙隆ticos para regressar ao cessar-fogo, 脙聽 discuss脙拢o sobre a situa脙搂脙拢o dos dois Estados e ao respeito pela ordem e institui脙搂脙碌es internacionais, sobre a pr脙鲁pria natureza dos regimes pol脙颅ticos do Ir脙拢o e de Israel.

Foi, portanto, com uma tristeza imensa que ouvi as palavras da senhora presidente da Comiss脙拢o na abertura da Cimeira do G7: Israel tem o direito de se defender; o Ir脙拢o 脙漏 a principal fonte de instabilidade regional. Sobre Gaza, apenas sil脙陋ncio.

As transfer脙陋ncias populacionais for脙搂adas, a limpeza 脙漏tnica, a fome e a sede como armas de guerra, a destrui脙搂脙拢o de servi脙搂os essenciais, a viola脙搂脙拢o do direito internacional com a distribui脙搂脙拢o de ajuda humanit脙隆ria por um proxy de Israel.

De que mais precisam os nossos Estados芒聙聭Membros, os nossos pa脙颅ses, para obterem a unanimidade necess脙隆ria para suspender o Acordo de Associa脙搂脙拢o com Israel? As nossas institui脙搂脙碌es podem querer desviar o olhar, mas os europeus...

(o Presidente retira a palavra 脙聽 oradora)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Leoluca Orlando (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il 23 giugno il Consiglio europeo degli affari esteri dovr脙聽 finalmente sanzionare l'ormai incontestabile violazione dell'articolo 2 dell'accordo di associazione con Israele, responsabile di genocidio, crimini di guerra, pulizia etnica, apartheid e occupazione di territori palestinesi, con 55脗聽104 uccisi, dei quali 16脗聽854 bambini, 127脗聽394 feriti e rimasti mutilati, e inoltre migliaia di anonimi palestinesi che muoiono per carestia e mancanza di cure per il criminale blocco degli aiuti umanitari.

Signora Kallas, di quanti altri crimini, di quanti altri palestinesi uccisi e mutilati e lasciati morire vorr脙聽 che l'Unione europea sia chiamata responsabile dalla storia e dalla giustizia internazionale prima di applicare l'articolo 2, prima di interrompere l'accordo di associazione con Israele, prima di applicare sanzioni e sospendere armamenti e risorse?

Un appello, infine, al popolo ebraico, che ha subito la terribile tragedia della Shoah provocata dal nazifascismo. Ai tedeschi 脙篓 stato chiesto di prendere le distanze e di opporsi ad Adolf Hitler, agli italiani a Benito Mussolini, ai musulmani a Osama Bin Laden. Noi chiediamo agli ebrei tutto il mondo di prendere le distanze dal governo Netanyahu.

(Il Presidente toglie la parola all'oratore)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Luke Ming Flanagan (The Left). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner Kallas, what ever happened to the peace project known as the European Union? We are, as my colleague said, watching a live-streamed genocide in Gaza. And rather than taking concrete action to stop the genocide, the so-called 'peace project' is aiding and abetting the aggressor.

The EU's largest state, Germany, supplies one third of the instruments of death to Israel. The EU refuses to call a halt to the trade agreement with Israel 芒聙聯 instead talking about a possible review. What's to review? We are trading with a psychopathic state, hell-bent on wiping out a nation.

Many MEPs here had undermined UNRWA with insinuations that they were infiltrated by Hamas. You got your way with your corrosive lies. Now, in the absence of UNRWA, the people of Gaza are subject to a horror whereby if they wish to receive aid, they must run the gauntlet of being shot!

You do not speak for Ireland when it comes to foreign policy. You need to end the trade deal, end all weapons sales, push for UNRWA to be given back its mandate and recognise Palestine ...

(The President cut off the speaker)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fidias Panayiotou (NI). 芒聙聯 Mr President, High Representative/Vice-President, the speech I will make right now will not help any Palestinian in Gaza. This is because in the European Union, we love to speak about Gaza, but we don't take actions to actually help the genocide that is taking place right now. Instead, the only actions that we take are to help Netanyahu on his mission to kill more and more Palestinians. Germany is the second-biggest provider of weapons for Israel after our biggest ally, the United States.

Yes, my friends, about 30% of the weapons bought by Israel come from Germany, weapons that are being used for the killing of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza. We cannot allow this any longer. The EU must stop the trade in weapons with Israel right now. Whatever we say in our speeches is useless if our actions will continue to provide the genocidal state of Israel with weapons. Please, friends, Europe, history is watching us.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Walsh (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, colleagues, High Representative, earlier this month, a report from within an EU institution was published online which clearly spelled out Israel's unequivocal and brutal war crimes in Gaza. A report from inside our own European Union documented Israel's deliberate use of starvation as a weapon of warfare. This is a report that was compiled in November 2024, and since then, while thousands more innocent people have been killed, Israel has continued to enjoy the privileges of free trade from the European Union.

How many times have MEPs stood in this very Chamber and demanded a review of the EU-Israel agreement, pleaded for its suspension, begged for action that actually means something? Let me be very clear: the EU's current inaction does not reflect my views, nor does it reflect the views of the many of my constituents I represent, or my country. We demand peace. We demand an immediate end to the killing, and we demand that the EU take serious action.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thijs Reuten (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear High Representative, dear colleagues, I have no sympathy whatsoever for the Iranian autocratic, extremist regime. Its human rights record and threats against Israel are appalling. And Iran must never acquire nuclear weapons. But Israel's recent strike on Iran was not a defence against an imminent threat. This was about derailing diplomacy, about using escalation to distract from its annihilation of Palestinians and from internal problems. And these problems are big because Israeli citizens do see that the release of the hostages, the rule of law and upholding international law was never a priority.

Now the European Union: failing for a long time already to propose immediate suspension of the Association Agreement continues to constitute a failure to act under the Treaties. As the guardian of the Treaties, I expect you next Monday to suspend the agreement and to uphold Europe's credibility that rests on equal application of international law. No exceptions, no double standards.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cynthia N脙颅 Mhurch脙潞 (Renew). 芒聙聯 A Chathaoirligh, a Ardionada脙颅, t脙隆im an-bhuartha faoi ionsaithe m脙颅leata Iosrael ar an Iar脙隆in agus frith-ionsaithe na hIar脙隆ine ar Iosrael. T脙隆im buartha go pr脙颅omha do na daoine soineanta, na sibhialtaigh. Is s脙隆r脙潞 ar dhl脙颅 daonn脙潞il idirn脙隆isi脙潞nta 脙漏 sibhialtaigh n脙鲁 bonneagar sibhialtach a ionsa脙颅 d'aon ghn脙鲁. T脙隆 d脙颅-gh脙漏aruithe ar an gcoimhlint agus measarthacht riachtanach. M脙隆 thagann g脙漏ar脙潞, t脙隆 baol d脙隆ir脙颅re ann go leathn脙鲁idh s脙颅 amach sa r脙漏igi脙潞n. Caithfimid lean脙潞int ag caitheamh solais ar Gaza, 脙隆it a bhfuil radharcanna uaf脙隆sacha f脙鲁s le feice脙隆il. T脙隆 feachtas m脙颅leata Netanyahu agus Rialtas Iosrael, chun Gaza a thabhairt faoi smacht ioml脙隆n, imithe as cuimse. T脙隆 s脙漏 glan in aghaidh an dl脙颅 idirn脙隆isi脙潞nta. N脙隆 ligimis do ch脙潞rsa脙颅 san Iar脙隆in 脙隆r n-aird a bhaint den chinedh脙颅oth脙潞. Cinedh脙颅oth脙潞 at脙隆 ar si脙潞l in Gaza ag Netanyahu. T脙隆 p脙隆ist脙颅 is pobal na Palaist脙颅ne ag f脙隆il bh脙隆is ina sc脙鲁rtha ag iarraidh teacht ar uisce agus bia agus f脙鲁s, fanann an tAontas Eorpach ar leataobh gan faic a dh脙漏anamh. Caithfidh an tAontas tr脙隆d脙隆il le hIosrael a chur ar fionra脙颅 go dt脙颅 go ligfeadh Iosrael c脙潞namh daonna isteach. Go raibh maith agaibh.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alice Kuhnke (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Det g脙楼r inte att bomba fram fred, det g脙楼r inte att sv脙陇lta fram trygghet, det g脙楼r inte att f脙露rtrycka m脙陇nniskor till underkastelse.脗聽V脙楼ld f脙露der v脙楼ld, hat f脙露der hat.

Bomber, m脙陇nniskor som tvingas fly, f脙露r脙陇ldrar som ser sina barn sv脙陇lta ihj脙陇l, grannar som tvingas ner i skyddsrum och begravda, leml脙陇stade syskon leder inte till mer trygghet. Inte f脙露r n脙楼gon. Det 脙陇r alltid civila som betalar priset f脙露r maktfullkomliga m脙陇nniskors galenskap, som betalar priset f脙露r v脙楼r likgiltighet och of脙露rm脙楼ga att g脙露ra det enda som 脙陇r det r脙陇tta och l脙楼ngsiktigt b脙陇sta.

Det stora hoppet i allt m脙露rker 脙陇r m脙陇nniskor runt om i v脙陇rlden som trots allt v脙陇grar l脙楼ta sig tystas, som entr脙陇get s脙陇ger ifr脙楼n, som samlas, som protesterar.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Danilo Della Valle (The Left). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, ancora una volta dobbiamo constatare l'inadeguatezza dei rappresentanti politici dell'Unione europea. Avete annunciato il 18脗潞 pacchetto di sanzioni alla Russia mentre guardate in silenzio Israele fare a pezzi tutto ci脙虏 che resta del diritto internazionale.

Le agenzie umanitarie stimano oltre 120脗聽000 morti a Gaza, la pi脙鹿 grande operazione di pulizia etnica dalla fine della seconda guerra mondiale ad oggi. E noi siamo qui a discutere se sospendere o rivedere l'accordo di associazione tra Unione europea e Israele.

Cosa sarebbe successo se uno dei paesi considerati nemici dell'Occidente avesse messo in campo un quarto dei crimini commessi da Israele o avesse rapito un europarlamentare, come 脙篓 accaduto con Rima Hassan? Dove sono l'aggressore e l'aggredito quando Israele attacca un paese sovrano come l'Iran? Dov'脙篓 il diritto alla difesa sbandierato in tutti questi anni? Dove sono la democrazia e l'umanit脙聽 quando l'esercito di occupazione sionista utilizza la fame come arma da guerra?

Sospendere, altro che rivedere, l'accordo con Israele sarebbe forse l'ultimo sussulto di dignit脙聽 di un Parlamento che ha perso ogni credibilit脙聽, schiacciato da ipocriti doppi standard. Qualsiasi cosa farete, sappiate che la storia non vi assolver脙聽 mai.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽David Casa (PPE). 芒聙聯 Sur President, iva, l-attakk tas-7 ta' Ottubru kien atro脛聥i. U iva, ner脛隆g脛搂u nsostnu li l-osta脛隆脛隆i I脜录raeljani g脛搂andhom ji脛隆u me脛搂lusa immedjatament.

Imma b脛搂alissa g脛搂andna sitwazzjoni ag脛搂ar f'Ga脜录a: qeg脛搂din naraw qtil ta' persuni inno脛聥enti, ta' tfal, ta' nisa u ta' r脛隆iel li qed imutu bil-脛隆u脛搂 jew sa脛搂ansitra ji脛隆u maqtula waqt li jkunu qeg脛搂din jistennew l-ikel. Il-krudelt脙聽 u s-sofferenza, li qed in脛搂allu g脛搂addej b脛搂alissa, se tkun tebg脛搂a permanenti fil-kuxjenza internazzjonali. Enough is enough u na脛搂seb l-Ewropa g脛搂andha tag脛搂mel il-parti tag脛搂ha biex twaqqaf dan il-脛隆eno脛聥idju.

Hemm b脜录onn, iva, li nissospendu b'mod immedjat l-Association Agreement, g脛搂andna nirrikonoxxu l-Palestina, g脛搂andna nag脛搂mlu sanzjonijiet kontra l-politi脛聥i li huma responsabbli g脛搂al delitti tal-gwerra bla ra脜录an, u g脛搂andna naraw li dawk responsabbli j脛搂allsu ta' g脛搂emilhom quddiem qorti kriminali internazzjonali.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Chlo脙漏 Ridel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, nous d脙漏battons enfin de la suspension de l'accord d'association entre l'Union europ脙漏enne et Isra脙芦l, qui est cens脙漏 脙陋tre conditionn脙漏 au respect des droits humains. J'ai du mal 脙聽 comprendre de quoi il faudrait encore d脙漏battre puisque la violation de tous les droits humains par Isra脙芦l est flagrante, mais allons-y.

Madame la Haute Repr脙漏sentante, la presse nous apprend que vous aviez sur votre bureau, depuis octobre脗聽2024, un rapport qui documentait de fa脙搂on pr脙漏cise les crimes de guerre et les crimes contre l'humanit脙漏 脙聽 Gaza. Mais aucune r脙漏vision de l'accord n'a alors 脙漏t脙漏 demand脙漏e. Depuis un an et huit mois, 55脗聽000脗聽Palestiniens ont 脙漏t脙漏 tu脙漏s, dont 16脗聽000脗聽enfants. Toutes les infrastructures ont 脙漏t脙漏 d脙漏truites. 脙聙 Gaza, c'est un g脙漏nocide et nous sommes encore 脙聽 nous demander si la clause des droits humains de l'accord d'association est respect脙漏e. La Cour internationale de justice avait d脙漏nonc脙漏 le risque g脙漏nocidaire d脙篓s avril脗聽2024 et nous en sommes encore l脙聽.

L'inaction europ脙漏enne est dict脙漏e par la droite et l'extr脙陋me droite, dont l'histoire n'oubliera pas la complicit脙漏. Nous entendions encore tout 脙聽 l'heure une d脙漏put脙漏e europ脙漏enne italienne de Georgia脗聽Meloni nous expliquer que les enfants de Gaza 脙漏taient des enfants de terroristes et ne m脙漏ritaient pas notre compassion. On peut encore entendre des propos g脙漏nocidaires dans cet h脙漏micycle!

Alors, Madame la Haute repr脙漏sentante, l'Union europ脙漏enne doit agir imm脙漏diatement si elle veut encore un jour pouvoir se revendiquer du droit international. Il faut imm脙漏diatement suspendre l'accord d'association. Agissez!

脗聽脗聽Gerben-Jan Gerbrandy (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, High Representative, the people of Gaza are on the brink of starvation. Families are fleeing continuously to safe zones where they know they will not be safe. Starving families, desperate for food, are camping out near distribution points, hoping to get a food parcel. But instead of getting aid, they are facing gunfire from Israeli forces.

The EU must finally recognise this situation and, in the very least, suspend the association agreement we have with Israel. Netanyahu has very effectively shifted the world's attention away from Gaza to Iran. Please do not fall into this trap. Please do not let Mr Netanyahu dictate the terms of our debate.

The public opinion in Europe is shifting, and this is the moment for Council to step up and support in more than just words the people of Gaza. Please take action against these horrific, dehumanising crimes. And let us remember, colleagues, the words expressed by King Abdullah this morning: 'When the global response is silence, we normalise the suffering of the innocent'.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Majdouline Sbai (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, l'Union europ脙漏enne dispose de la comp脙漏tence exclusive en mati脙篓re de commerce international et elle ne cesse de r脙漏p脙漏ter que c'est un instrument pour promouvoir nos valeurs. L'article脗聽2 de l'accord d'association avec Isra脙芦l pr脙漏cise que ses relations avec l'Union europ脙漏enne sont fond脙漏es sur le respect des droits humains. Alors, comment peut-il 脙陋tre maintenu aujourd'hui? Comment peut-on m脙陋me encore se poser la question de son maintien?

Quand Isra脙芦l appelle 脙聽 un d脙漏placement forc脙漏 de 2脗聽millions de Palestiniens, bloque l'aide humanitaire, vise les h脙麓pitaux, quand les Nations unies parlent de g脙漏nocide, que 200脗聽ONG europ脙漏ennes nous demandent sa suspension, quand nous savons que le libre-脙漏change dans l'industrie et l'agriculture constitue un soutien majeur 脙聽 l'脙漏conomie isra脙漏lienne脗聽芒聙聯 24脗聽% des exportations du pays se font vers les 27脗聽脙聣tats membres芒聙娄

Alors jusqu'脙聽 quand allons-nous bafouer le droit international, notre engagement et nos textes? L'Europe a un poids capital dans les 脙漏quilibres mondiaux. Il ne doit pas servir 脙聽 alimenter les situations de guerre et de terreur. Je vous le dis avec gravit脙漏, Madame la Commissaire, c'est un enjeu de civilisation pour notre continent.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽C脙漏line Imart (PPE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Haute repr脙漏sentante, je me r脙漏jouis que le Parlement europ脙漏en organise un d脙漏bat sur la situation au Proche-Orient, car ce sujet m脙漏rite mieux que les gesticulations de quelques influenceurs en mal de 脗芦likes脗禄 jouant 脙聽 se faire peur sur un voilier.

Le脗聽7脗聽octobre脗聽2023, le monde a bascul脙漏 dans l'horreur. Les terroristes du Hamas, soutenus par l'Iran et par certains agents de l'UNRWA, ont perp脙漏tr脙漏 une attaque barbare, exterminant des civils, des femmes et des enfants. Ce massacre n'est pas un d脙漏tail de l'histoire. Il est le point de d脙漏part de toute la souffrance humaine au Proche-Orient, qui est un v脙漏ritable cr脙篓ve-c脜聯ur.

Pendant ce temps, l'Iran arme, finance, alimente le chaos, depuis Gaza jusqu'au Liban, depuis le Y脙漏men jusqu'脙聽 la Syrie. Ce r脙漏gime th脙漏ocratique, obs脙漏d脙漏 par la destruction d'Isra脙芦l, est le pyromane r脙漏gional qui menace l'脙漏quilibre mondial en faisant de la guerre par procuration une doctrine d'脙聣tat, avec le Hamas comme bras arm脙漏.

Alors oui, il faut le rappeler, Isra脙芦l est notre alli脙漏 et notre partenaire 脙漏conomique, et l'un de nos remparts face 脙聽 l'islamisme. Et il doit le rester. Entre le terrorisme et la d脙漏mocratie, mon choix est fait. La France et l'Europe seraient bien inspir脙漏es de faire芒聙娄

(Le Pr脙漏sident retire la parole 脙聽 l'oratrice)

脗聽脗聽
