脗聽脗聽Vasile D脙庐ncu (S&D). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, revizuirea Acordului de asociere este o decizie grea, 脙庐ns脛聝 greutatea acestei decizii nu trebuie s脛聝 ne fac脛聝 s脛聝 ne pierdem echilibrul. Da, pare o confruntare de percep脠聸ii, una 脙庐ntre David 脠聶i Goliat, dar 脙庐n aceast脛聝 lupt脛聝 de percep脠聸ie s脛聝 nu uit脛聝m c脛聝 脙庐n 7 octombrie a fost un atac brutal, cu peste 100 de mor脠聸i civili, cu sute de ostatici.

A sanc脠聸iona Israelul f脛聝r脛聝 nuan脠聸e 脙庐n acest context, risc脛聝 s脛聝 fie perceput ca un cec 脙庐n alb pentru terorismul organizat. 脙聨n acela脠聶i timp, nu putem ignora realitatea din Gaza, cum a脠聸i spus, obliga脠聸iile asumate 脙庐n cadrul Acordului de asociere, deci, decizia noastr脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 fie una de echilibru.

Peste 60脗聽% din locuitorii kibu脠聸urilor au p脛聝r脛聝sit casele lor, zeci de familii a脠聶teapt脛聝 ostaticii s脛聝 revin脛聝. Guvernul trimite ajutoare umanitare 脙庐n Gaza, dar nu face publicitate ca s脛聝 nu-脠聶i piard脛聝 partenerii de coali脠聸ie.

Deci, revizuirea acordului trebuie s脛聝 fie un instrument de dialog, nu de ruptur脛聝, pozi脠聸ia noastr脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 fie clar脛聝: dou脛聝 state 脙庐n pace 脠聶i securitate. Dar p脙垄n脛聝 acolo trebuie s脛聝 r脛聝m脙垄nem drep脠聸i, nu partizani, nu radicali, ci europeni fideli valorilor noastre - umanitate, justi脠聸ie 脠聶i echilibru.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael McNamara (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Madam Kallas, every statement of the European Commission on Israel begins with its right to defend itself and the right of every state to defend itself. And of course, every state does have that right. But Israel is no longer involved in a defensive war against Hamas in Gaza; it is engaged in war crimes in Gaza.脗聽It is also seeking to emasculate the Palestinian people in the West Bank, while people's eyes are diverted to the horrors of what it is doing in Gaza.

Israel's attack on Iran was not a defensive strike. It was an unlawful and unprovoked strike designed to subvert the negotiations that were taking place between Iran and the Trump Government.

Madam脗聽Kallas, you rightly call for the respect of international law in Ukraine by Russia, but those calls are fatally undermined by your failure to do the same in Israel. The European Union must suspend its trade association agreement now. And Germany 芒聙聯 which, because it fuelled a genocide against Jews, now feels obliged to fuel another one 芒聙聯 must end its arms shipments to Israel.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anna Strolenberg (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, more bombs, more dead, more children starving, burned and buried. The suffering we once called 'unbelievable' is now streamed, live, into our phones, into our minds. And while the war in Iran gets headlines, peace in Gaza remains as far away as ever.

How can we pressure the Israeli Government if we give them preferential access to our markets? How can we condemn their attacks on Palestinians while we provide them with weapons? How is it possible that Netanyahu is still not facing any consequences although investigation after investigation shows that we are watching a genocide unfold.

Last Sunday, I marched for peace. I joined hundreds of thousands of people 芒聙聯 people demanding an end to the suffering. Europeans are calling for peace, but Europe can only be heard if we speak with one voice. We must end the Association Agreement. We must enforce a weapons embargo, and we need to sanction those accused of war crimes.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Szczerba (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Madam Vice-President, the escalating situation between Israel and Iran is a serious threat that demands quick action. We must pursue a clear and firm path towards a negotiated resolution to this conflict. The EU and its Member States must stand united in their message: Iran must not be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons, and Israel has a right to protect the citizens.

The EU has a crucial role to play in mediating this crisis, and we should actively offer our support. Collaboration with the US is essential. It is imperative that all parties abide by international law. A lasting solution must be grounded in legal principles, and the EU has a critical responsibility to uphold an international order based on legitimate claims and just solutions.

We cannot lose sight of a dire situation in Gaza and the West Bank. We demand that humanitarian aid can flow freely. Terrorists must release all hostages. Hamas has no place in any future governance structure in Gaza.

Finally, protecting the security of European citizens is non-negotiable and should be our top priority.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Aodh脙隆n 脙聯 R脙颅ord脙隆in (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, High Representative, why does the Commission say they care about the rights of children, unless they are Palestinian? Why do they say they care about hunger, unless it is in Gaza? Why do they say to defend international law, until it is violated by Israel?

High Representative, Europeans have had enough of this hypocrisy. Europe's leaders only offer silence and shame instead of condemnation. Ursula von der Leyen picked up the phone to Netanyahu over the weekend. You visited Israel in March when the death toll was already 50脗聽000. And now we hear that Israeli representatives are to be welcomed to Brussels next week for the EU-Southern Neighbourhood ministerial meeting. This should be unconscionable.

And let me tell you how, High Representative, citizens will be there to protest. And people often ask us, do we feel this way because we are Irish? It's not because we are Irish. It is because we are human. With eyes, with spines, with hearts. We ask you to open your eyes, to grow a spine, to grow a heart, and to end the association agreement and to sanction Israel.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, the war between Israel and Iran is unfolding before our eyes, with civilians on both sides bearing the devastating consequences, not Khamenei nor Netanyahu.

We must stand firmly against war, against oppression, terrorism and violation of international law. We must oppose the use of weapons of mass destruction, genocide and occupation.

No person or entity, be it Netanyahu, Khamenei or Hamas, stand above international law.

The actions by Israel in Gaza is claimed to be self芒聙聭defence. There is no self芒聙聭defence in killing over 60脗聽000 civilians, many more injured and on the brink of starvation.脗聽These actions violate international law.

Those who fail to see that Israel is breaching Article 2 of the EU-Israel Association Agreement are either blind or complicit. It is time to suspend this agreement.

The maniac Trump's possible actions right now are dangerous. The EU, however, must play a crucial role in de芒聙聭escalating the conflict and preventing the heinous Tehran regime from accessing nuclear weapons.

Our collective responsibility is to promote human rights, rule of law, democracy, peace and uphold international law.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Regina Doherty (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, conflict in the Middle East has been a feature of our lives and on the news screens for many years now, for far too long. Many Europeans are so desensitised to the daily tragedies that they've kind of switched off, or they genuinely don't believe that we have a solution. But I don't think we should forget what instability in the Middle East really means, because the escalating conflict between Israel and Iran will have social, economic and security consequences for the entire world.

Let me be very clear, what's happening in Gaza today, and for the last number of months, is an infamy. It's indiscriminate slaughter. What is horrifying is the notion that it is a tactic for political survival by the President of Israel, or a game by the President of the United States. Human rights, be they freedom, sovereignty, statehood, the protection of lives, should never be optional, but right now in Gaza, they don't even exist.

With every passing hour, children in Gaza are starving to death, communities are being wiped out and lives are being brutally ended. Yet there are people in the world with the power to end this slaughter today, and many of them are in the European Union. So I make the call again 芒聙聯 stop the killing, let the aid in, release the hostages and end this brutal war now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Vytenis Povilas Andriukaitis (S&D). 芒聙聯 Pirmininke, gerbiama komisare Kallas, kaip buv脛聶s komisaras 脜隆脛炉 kart脛聟 jau脛聧iuosi labai prastai. Pirm脛聟 kart脛聟 man nejauku u脜戮 Komisijos pozicij脛聟. J脜芦s laik脛聴t脛聴s gerai Putino at脜戮vilgiu 芒聙聯 puiku. Trumpo at脜戮vilgiu ne kart脛聟 paslydot. Netanyahu at脜戮vilgiu j脜芦s suklupot. Moralin脛聴 mirtis yra baisus dalykas. Gaila, kad 脜隆iandien negird脛聴jote Jordanijos karaliaus kalbos. Jinai tur脛聴jo jus 脛炉kv脛聴pti. Ir akivaizdu, kad 脛聧ia kai kurie krik脜隆脛聧ionys u脜戮mir脜隆ta, kad akis u脜戮 ak脛炉, dantis u脜戮 dant脛炉 yra Senojo Testamento taisykl脛聴. Kristus j脛聟 nuneigia. 脛聦ia krik脜隆脛聧ionims skiriu. O 脜隆iaip, laikas nutraukti Asociacijos sutart脛炉, remiantis antru straipsniu, sankcionuoti Netanyahu ir jo komand脛聟.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr President, the world cannot afford a nuclear Iran. A regime that openly sponsors terrorism, destabilises its neighbours, and calls for the destruction of Western democracies cannot be trusted with nuclear capabilities.脗聽Only the idea of such a regime with nuclear material 芒聙聯 or worse, such a regime giving it to terrorist proxies like Hamas 芒聙聯 sounds horrific.

Israel's pre-emptive strikes against Iranian nuclear facilities were not only justified, but also necessary. These actions were not acts of aggression, they were acts of global defence for which we should all be grateful.脗聽We saw again an example of how Israel strikes military targets, including nuclear sites, while Iran's Islamist regime deliberately targets civilians. Preventing Tehran from crossing the nuclear threshold is not just in Israel's interest, but in the interest of every nation that values peace and stability.

脗聽脗聽Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora Kallas, m脙颅renos a los ojos. Mire a los ojos de las personas que se mueven en las calles europeas, que han denunciado este genocidio. Yo la miro a los ojos, porque conf脙颅o en que la Uni脙鲁n Europea, al final, cambie la posici脙鲁n, porque, si no, ser脙隆 una verg脙录enza. Si no lo hacen, pasar脙隆n a la historia, pasar脙隆n a la historia por la hipocres脙颅a, pasar脙隆n a la historia por la mentira, pasar脙隆n a la historia por el silencio y pasar脙隆n a la historia por la complicidad.

El 23 de junio hay una oportunidad. Es la oportunidad de dar un paso honorable, un paso decente, un paso por la paz. Tenemos una oportunidad, porque ustedes han llegado tarde. Prefieren cogerle el tel脙漏fono a un criminal de guerra que estar con las calles que demandan la paz, con las calles que demandan sanciones, que demandan el cese al fuego, que demandan acabar con el sufrimiento. M脙颅relos a los ojos a los ni脙卤os de Gaza, se脙卤ora Kallas.

脗聽脗聽Jaume Asens Llodr脙聽 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, yo siento verg脙录enza por ser europeo, y m脙隆s cuando escucho a la se脙卤ora脗聽Kallas: 脗驴usted puede dormir tranquila cuando las armas y el dinero europeo sirven para exterminar un pueblo entero en Gaza, cuando mantenemos un acuerdo comercial con quien practica el genocidio, con quien tiene una orden de detenci脙鲁n internacional? 脗驴No tiene un atisbo de humanidad, de duda, de inquietud en su conciencia?

Hannah Arendt dec脙颅a que la banalidad del mal no nace de los monstruos, nace de gente corriente que convive con el horror como si fuera algo normal y que no ve el sufrimiento humano, solo n脙潞meros, negocios, alianzas. Eso es lo que usted y esta Europa representan.

脗驴Por qu脙漏 creen que Sud脙隆frica termin脙鲁 con el脗聽apartheid? Porque se la aisl脙鲁 internacionalmente, porque hubo un boicot. Israel continuar脙隆 con el genocidio hasta que nosotros dejemos de ser c脙鲁mplices de ese genocidio.

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, I am speechless, and you all know this almost never happens.

I am speechless that so many seem eager to pick sides between a corrupt prime minister wanted by the International Criminal Court of Justice, and an Islamist ayatollah who kills young women for not wearing a hijab. I am speechless that some deliver weapons to an army guilty of countless war crimes, while others tolerate a terrorist group that murders anyone daring to challenge their rule over rubble.

I am speechless that, faced with this unbearable human tragedy, the leaders of the world have nothing more to offer than the same tired talking points I heard as a teenager.

I am speechless. And that, dear colleagues, tells you everything.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katar脙颅na Roth Neve脛聫alov脙隆 (NI). 芒聙聯 脛聨akujem, p脙隆n predseda. Kolegovia, tento 脙潞tok, ktor脙陆 sp脙麓sobil Izrael na Ir脙隆n a takisto v脜隆etko, 脛聧o moment脙隆lne Izrael rob脙颅 v regi脙鲁ne, treba jednozna脛聧ne ods脙潞di脜楼. A je mi skuto脛聧ne zle z kolegov, ktor脙颅 tu dokonca hovoria, 脜戮e sa m脙隆me za tak脙陆to 脙潞tok po脛聫akova脜楼 a m脙隆me by脜楼 za脜聢 v脛聫a脛聧n脙颅. Ako m脙麓脜戮e niekto schva脛戮ova脜楼 脙潞tok na nukle脙隆rne zariadenia? To sme naozaj tak脙颅 脙潞plne hl脙潞pi a nevieme si predstavi脜楼, 脛聧o m脙麓脜戮e znamena脜楼, ak by toto nukle脙隆rne zariadenie naozaj vybuchlo? Ako m脙麓脜戮eme schva脛戮ova脜楼 脙潞toky na civiln脙漏 obyvate脛戮stvo v Ir脙隆ne? Ako m脙麓脜戮eme hovori脜楼, 脜戮e t脙颅 vedci, ktor脙陆ch tam zabili, ktor脙颅 boli s脙潞脛聧as脜楼ou nukle脙隆rneho programu, je spr脙隆vne, 脜戮e sme, 脜戮e ich Izrael zabil? Ako m脙麓脜戮eme poveda脜楼, 脜戮e teraz t脙颅 nepriate脛戮sk脙颅 vedci m脙麓脜戮u by脜楼 zabit脙颅, preto脜戮e to je to spr脙隆vne, 脛聧o sa m脙隆 robi脜楼. Ve脛戮mi by ma zauj脙颅malo, kde sa chce Izrael zastavi脜楼 a ko脛戮ko sme mu ochotn脙颅 tolerova脜楼. Rok a pol tu nevieme ods脙潞di脜楼 genoc脙颅du v Gaze, kde zomreli tis脙颅ce civilistov, tis脙颅ce 脛戮ud脙颅, ktor脙颅 tam u脜戮 zomreli a 脛聫al脜隆ie desiatky tis脙颅c, ktor脙漏 脛聧akaj脙潞 na pomoc a umieraj脙潞 hladom. Ve脛戮mi by ma zauj脙颅malo, pre脛聧o Eur脙鲁pska komisia nem脙麓脜戮e da脜楼 jasn脙漏 stanovisko a nem脙麓脜戮e jednozna脛聧ne poveda脜楼, 脜戮e to odsudzujeme. A takisto ma zauj脙颅ma, ak脙漏 sankcie a konkr脙漏tne ak脙漏 sankcie pl脙隆nujete navrhn脙潞脜楼 na zasadnut脙颅 premi脙漏rov a na zasadnut脙颅 ministrov zahrani脛聧n脙陆ch vec脙颅 proti Izraelu, preto脜戮e toto sa nem脙麓脜戮e jednoducho tolerova脜楼.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 Madame la Vice-pr脙漏sidente脗聽Kallas, votre pr脙漏sence parmi nous ce soir 脙漏tait tr脙篓s attendue. De nombreuses questions vous ont 脙漏t脙漏 pos脙漏es et vos r脙漏ponses sont tr脙篓s attendues 脙漏galement. Je vous donne la parole.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kaja Kallas, Vice-President of the Commission / High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy. 芒聙聯 Mr President, do you think that it is not painful for me to see the suffering of Palestinian people? Really, do you really think that it's true?

That is why I'm talking almost every day to the Minister of Israel that the humanitarian aid would get in, that the human suffering will stop and that they would not weaponise humanitarian aid, because the suffering of people is happening. And I'm asking all the time, all the time, have we done enough to stop this? And clearly we haven't, because this is still going on. And I have equally the same frustration as you do.

What I don't agree is with is that we have been silent. We have not been silent. The EU has never been silent in the face of the war and this tragedy. We have consistently passed very strong messages. And I can assure you that I will continue to do this, also in the future.

I've had exactly the same positions on humanitarian law and international law, regarding Gaza, regarding Ukraine. I also addressed these issues in my speech today. You were not in the plenary, but I addressed this. I have exactly the same positions, so this is equally frustrating for us.

The decision to carry out the review of Israel's compliance with the human rights obligations sends a clear message to Israel that the EU's commitment to human rights and international law are not just words.

Now, to the Association Agreement, as I said, the review is being conducted. And then, of course, it's the question, what do we do about this?

Of course, I mean, listening to you, in the Council, we have unanimity. We have to have 27 countries on board. And that's why we know you know, we know here, listening to different speeches, that you have very different views in different Member States. And yes, there are some measures that are QMV, but that needs to be proposed by the College and especially, I would say to the former College member, I mean, you were a member of the College and you know that in College you also have anonymity. So this has been going on for quite some time when you were also a member of the College. So also the question, why didn't you do anything if it is so easy? But it is not easy. That is the problem.

On the suspension of the agreement, would it stop the killing? If we don't have the agreement? You know the answer to this question, it will not stop the killing, but it will close the channels to talk to Israel and press on the humanitarian aid reaching the people. And of course, I can propose measures that I know will not go through, not in College, not in Council. So I would certainly feel much better saying that I've done enough, because I propose this, but I know it will not go through and I'm sure you expressing your anger at me here, you also feel better that you have done something, but it's not enough, and that is the frustration, equal frustration that I have, that you have too.

So I'm trying to do my job, but I'm not you know, we are not a European Union where one person makes decisions. We are not an institution like this. We have 27 in the College, and we have also in the Commission, 27 that need to be on board. So that is the situation right now. I mean, we are trying to do everything to really help the Palestinian people. We are the biggest donor of help and humanitarian aid. The issue is the access, and that's why we are raising this issue all the time with our Israeli counterparts to let the humanitarian aid in to help the people. Because the amount is not the problem. The access is.

So we are trying to do our best, and of course, we need also all of your support to do that, but it's very hard, as you can hear also here in the in the plenary. There are very different views from different political groups, but also from different countries, and this is equally represented in the College and in the Council.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 Le d脙漏bat est clos.

D脙漏clarations 脙漏crites (article 178)

脗聽脗聽Lina G脙隆lvez (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 Hemos solicitado de forma sistem脙隆tica a la Comisi脙鲁n Europea la revisi脙鲁n del Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n UE-Israel, conforme al art脙颅culo 2, que condiciona el texto con respecto a los derechos humanos. Tras un a脙卤o y medio desde que el Gobierno de Espa脙卤a pidiera esta medida, diecisiete Estados miembros apoyan la revisi脙鲁n en un contexto en el que Israel ha provocado 55脗聽000 muertos, 123脗聽000 heridos y 632脗聽000 desplazados en Gaza. Adem脙隆s, Israel contin脙潞a con su ofensiva con la ocupaci脙鲁n de tierras en Cisjordania y Jerusal脙漏n.

Condenamos las vulneraciones de Derecho internacional y Derecho internacional humanitario cometidas por Israel. Rechazamos el sistema de distribuci脙鲁n de ayuda humanitaria implementado por la Fundaci脙鲁n Humanitaria de Gaza sin la ONU. Solicitamos a la Comisi脙鲁n que tome acci脙鲁n e incorpore las medidas sugeridas por la opini脙鲁n de julio de 2024 de la Corte Internacional de Justicia con respecto a los territorios ocupados, como son la revisi脙鲁n de la participaci脙鲁n de Israel en Horizon Europe, poner fin al acceso sin visado para los colonos israel脙颅es y establecer sanciones a colonos y miembros del Gobierno.

Apoyamos la resoluci脙鲁n diplom脙隆tica y el respeto sin fisuras del Derecho internacional en el conflicto entre Ir脙隆n e Israel.

脗聽脗聽Nacho S脙隆nchez Amor (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 Hemos solicitado de forma sistem脙隆tica a la Comisi脙鲁n Europea la revisi脙鲁n del Acuerdo de Asociaci脙鲁n UE-Israel, conforme al art脙颅culo 2, que condiciona el texto con respecto a los derechos humanos. 脗聽

22. Assassination attempt on Senator Miguel Uribe and the threat to the democratic process and peace in Colombia (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 L'ordre du jour appelle 脙聽 pr脙漏sent la d脙漏claration de la vice-pr脙漏sidente/haute repr脙漏sentante de l'Union pour les affaires 脙漏trang脙篓res et la politique de s脙漏curit脙漏 sur la tentative d'assassinat du s脙漏nateur Miguel脗聽Uribe et la menace pour le processus d脙漏mocratique et la paix en Colombie [2025/2749(RSP)].

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kaja Kallas, Vice-President of the Commission / High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, thank you for the opportunity to intervene on the situation in Colombia following the tragic events of recent days.

Let me first reiterate the full solidarity of the European Union with Senator Uribe Turbay and with the people of Colombia. Last week, I conveyed this message also personally to the Foreign Minister of Colombia, Laura Sarabia, who was in Brussels for an important bilateral visit. We welcome the commitment of Colombia to bring to justice all those involved in the recent attacks. We deeply appreciated the presence of the Minister in Brussels at such a delicate time for Colombia. Her visit was an opportunity to launch negotiations between the European Union and Colombia on a partnership and cooperation agreement, following the mandate received from the Council in 2023.

We also agreed to hold the EU-CELAC ministerial meeting in the margins of the UN General Assembly in September, and this will prepare the summit taking place in Colombia in November. I discussed with Minister Sarabia how to deepen our bilateral cooperation in several areas, including the fight against organised crime and the protection of democracy against disinformation and hate speech. The recent tragic events only strengthen our resolve to intensify efforts in these areas. As part of these efforts, if the conditions are met and if we are invited by the relevant authorities, the European Union is also ready to consider the deployment of an electoral observation mission to support Colombia in its upcoming general elections.

Minister Sarabia's visit was also an opportunity to reaffirm the European Union's continued political and financial commitment to the Colombian peace process, including during her meeting with the EU's special envoy. The implementation of the 2016 peace agreement remains key for Colombia as a prosperous, peaceful and democratic society. The persistent challenges faced by the country should not darken the historic success achieved with the end of the conflict with the FARC. Current challenges should rather intensify efforts to fully implement the 2016 Agreement, to ensure transitional justice and bring socioeconomic development to the communities and territories most affected by violence.

Honourable Members, in addition to our solidarity with a close and like-minded partner, our support for and intense cooperation with Colombia are also extremely important for our own security and interests. The EU needs a strong and democratic Colombia as a reliable and like-minded partner on the international stage, including in its current capacity as the pro-tempore presidency of CELAC and member of the UN Security Council.

We also need a strong and democratic Colombia to address the many common challenges that we face, from migration to climate change to drugs and organised crime. We cannot ignore, for example, that what happens in the most remote areas of the Colombian Amazon has a direct impact on both the security of our cities and the safety of our environment.

Honourable Members, the recent attacks brought back painful memories from Colombia's most difficult periods. These times must not be allowed to return. The European Union will continue to support Colombia in the pursuit of peace, the fight against organised crime and the protection of democracy against extreme polarisation and political violence.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Davor Ivo Stier, en nombre del Grupo PPE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora alta representante, el atentado contra el precandidato presidencial, el senador Miguel Uribe Turbay, no fue solo un vil ataque a su vida, sino tambi脙漏n un atentado contra la democracia en Colombia. Fue un acto de violencia con el que se quiere forzar al pa脙颅s a retornar d脙漏cadas atr脙隆s: al terror de los ochenta, de los noventa, de Pablo Escobar, del narcotr脙隆fico, de la guerrilla antidemocr脙隆tica inspirada en la dictadura castrista.

Hoy, los violentos nuevamente levantan cabeza en un contexto de fuerte polarizaci脙鲁n pol脙颅tica en el pa脙颅s y estos violentos tambi脙漏n est脙隆n fortalecidos por los v脙颅nculos con el r脙漏gimen dictatorial de Nicol脙隆s Maduro en la vecina Venezuela.

Ante esta amenaza, la respuesta debe ser fortalecer el Estado de Derecho y el respeto a las instituciones democr脙隆ticas. Sobre todo, las m脙隆ximas autoridades del pa脙颅s tienen la responsabilidad de asegurar la seguridad y evitar los discursos divisivos y polarizantes, apostando en cambio por la moderaci脙鲁n y la concordia.

Desde este Parlamento seguiremos apoyando la democracia colombiana, entre otras cosas con una misi脙鲁n observadora en las pr脙鲁ximas elecciones, pero tambi脙漏n exigiendo a las autoridades de la Comisi脙鲁n y del Consejo una posici脙鲁n firme con aquellos que socavan el orden constitucional, promueven la violencia y se financian con el narcotr脙隆fico.

Finalmente elevamos nuestras oraciones y expresamos nuestra solidaridad con el senador, pero tambi脙漏n con los miles de colombianos que se manifestaron de manera pac脙颅fica, reclamando justicia, rechazando la violencia, defendiendo la paz, la convivencia y la democracia en Colombia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Leire Paj脙颅n, en nombre del Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, quiero condenar con toda la firmeza la violencia pol脙颅tica en Colombia y, por supuesto, el atentado al senador脗聽Uribe y los atentados a todas las v脙颅ctimas que le precedieron. Tambi脙漏n condeno la escalada de violencia que hace unas semanas sufr脙颅an en Cali y en el Cauca.

Pero quiero tambi脙漏n aprovechar para condenar el asesinato de nuestra compa脙卤era dem脙鲁crata y de su marido hace unos d脙颅as en los Estados脗聽Unidos, porque no hay mayor lacra para la democracia que la violencia pol脙颅tica, verbal y f脙颅sica. Y debemos reaccionar. La polarizaci脙鲁n irresponsable, el acoso permanente al adversario 芒聙聰especialmente a las mujeres芒聙聰 y la destrucci脙鲁n del contrario como forma de hacer pol脙颅tica son el caldo de cultivo de esa violencia. Y debemos decir basta, en Colombia y tambi脙漏n aqu脙颅.

Colombia merece alcanzar una paz total, duradera, por la v脙颅a democr脙隆tica. Debemos reforzar nuestro apoyo pol脙颅tico al Gobierno y a la sociedad. Debemos incluir a la sociedad civil y a los Gobiernos locales en su implementaci脙鲁n, y nuestra sociedad merece un debate p脙潞blico, sereno, centrado en resolver los problemas y que no siembre m脙隆s odio.

Como dec脙颅a nuestra compa脙卤era Jo Cox, asesinada hace ahora nueve脗聽a脙卤os, precisamente por el odio: 脗芦los dem脙鲁cratas tenemos m脙隆s cosas que nos unen que cosas que nos separan脗禄.

Desterremos de una vez por todas los extremismos de la democracia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Kruis, en nombre del Grupo PfE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, dos semanas despu脙漏s de mi nacimiento en Medell脙颅n, el candidato presidencial Luis Carlos Gal脙隆n fue asesinado en Colombia. Era el a脙卤o 1989, un per脙颅odo especialmente dif脙颅cil para mi pa脙颅s.

En medio de aquella crisis, los Pa脙颅ses Bajos me ofrecieron algo que Colombia no pod脙颅a: un futuro.

Hoy, treinta y cinco a脙卤os despu脙漏s, la esperanza democr脙隆tica de Colombia vuelve a estar en peligro. El reciente intento de asesinato del senador Miguel Uribe es una se脙卤al alarmante. Desde aqu脙颅 expreso mi m脙隆s sincero apoyo a 脙漏l y a su familia.

Por ello, llamo al Gobierno colombiano a garantizar elecciones libres, seguras y justas, porque, al final, la fuerza de una naci脙鲁n nace de la esperanza de su gente.

Los colombianos estamos unidos bajo los colores amarillo, azul y rojo. 脗隆Viva la democracia! 脗隆Viva la libertad y viva Colombia!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carlo Fidanza, a nome del gruppo ECR. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Alta rappresentante, l'attentato che il 7 giugno ha ridotto in fin di vita il senatore Miguel Uribe, uno dei candidati pi脙鹿 accreditati per le prossime elezioni presidenziali, ci ha colpito profondamente. In queste ore critiche per le sue condizioni di salute rinnoviamo le nostre preghiere per lui, come nei giorni scorsi hanno fatto tantissimi cittadini colombiani che hanno invaso le strade del paese in silenzio, con grande compostezza. Esprimiamo la nostra vicinanza alla sua famiglia, gi脙聽 provata in passato da vicende simili, e agli amici del suo partito, il Centro democratico.

Ma esprimere tristezza e solidariet脙聽 non 脙篓 pi脙鹿 sufficiente. L'Unione europea deve pretendere che il Presidente Petro, che in questi anni ha lasciato campo libero ai cartelli del narcotraffico, che hanno ripreso a colpire impunemente, si assuma la responsabilit脙聽 politica di questo attacco contro la democrazia in Colombia. Non possiamo pi脙鹿 fare finta di nulla. In una nazione in cui la violenza politico-mafiosa cresce, dove il presidente va avanti con decreti d'urgenza e forzature costituzionali, l'Europa ha il dovere di parlare chiaro.

Il governo del Presidente Petro 脙篓 un pericolo e una minaccia per la democrazia, perch脙漏 deve la sua sopravvivenza ad anni di ambiguit脙聽 e collusioni. Servono osservatori internazionali per le prossime elezioni e un impegno costante per difendere la libert脙聽, sopra ogni cosa. Vale nel Venezuela del tiranno sadico Maduro, vale nella Cuba del persecutore D脙颅az-Canel, vale nel Nicaragua del regime sanguinario Ortega Murillo e vale anche nella Colombia di Petro. Viva la libert脙聽!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cristina Guarda, a nome del gruppo Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, quando un candidato 脙篓 costretto a sottrarsi ai colpi di un sicario, 脙篓 chiaro l'allarme per tutta la democrazia di un paese. Il tentato omicidio di Miguel Uribe Turbay ci ricorda come la violenza politica in Colombia, purtroppo, non 脙篓 ancora un capitolo chiuso.

Per svolgere la nostra funzione come Europa 脙篓 fondamentale rifiutare fin d'ora, fin da qua, la violenza e non inasprire pi脙鹿 i fronti e i conflitti politici. Perch脙漏 se il dibattito politico scivola nel linguaggio dell'intimidazione, allora vince chi vuole violenza, e a pagarne le spese sono le persone pi脙鹿 fragili.

Ecco perch脙漏 fin da questa istituzione dobbiamo costruire rapporti di dialogo e non aizzare il conflitto, gi脙聽 estremamente polarizzato, perch脙漏 cos脙卢 si mettono a rischio le comunit脙聽 che stanno lottando per difendere i propri diritti, a partire da quello della propria terra, e che in Colombia 脙篓 una lotta estremamente complessa e rischiosa, in cui le persone perdono anche la propria vita.

Quindi tutto questo, se non ci comportiamo responsabilmente, andr脙聽 a vanificare il lavoro svolto per difendere il primo accordo di pace con le FARC e tutti gli sforzi portati avanti dal governo, in particolare di Petro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anthony Smith, au nom du groupe The Left. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, la Colombie s'appr脙陋te 脙聽 entrer dans une s脙漏quence de haute importance pour l'avenir du pays, notamment en ce qui concerne le maintien de la paix et la continuation de la r脙漏volution citoyenne mise en 脜聯uvre depuis 2022.

La tentative d'assassinat de Miguel Uribe, potentiel candidat 脙聽 l'脙漏lection pr脙漏sidentielle, est un acte odieux qu'il faut d脙漏noncer. En d脙漏mocratie, seul le peuple souverain doit pouvoir choisir ses repr脙漏sentants. Le recours aux tueurs 脙聽 gages est une l脙垄chet脙漏 sans nom. La vie politique colombienne est marqu脙漏e depuis des d脙漏cennies par ces assassinats politiques qui concernent 脙漏galement les d脙漏fenseurs des droits humains et de l'environnement.

Mais cette tentative d'assassinat ne doit pas 脙陋tre instrumentalis脙漏e par certains pour brider l'ambition des r脙漏formes sociales, dont celle du droit du travail souhait脙漏e par le pacte historique. Je tiens 脙聽 saluer l'action du pr脙漏sident Petro qui a fermement condamn脙漏 l'attaque, renforc脙漏 la s脙漏curit脙漏 des candidats 脙聽 l'脙漏lection pr脙漏sidentielle et encourag脙漏 l'enqu脙陋te diligent脙漏e par la procureure g脙漏n脙漏rale pour assurer la s脙漏curit脙漏 et la bonne tenue du processus 脙漏lectoral.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francisco Jos脙漏 Mill脙隆n Mon (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora alta representante, yo me uno a la condena del intento de asesinato del senador y precandidato colombiano Miguel Uribe. Es imprescindible una investigaci脙鲁n exhaustiva y que todos los responsables sean llevados ante la justicia.

Colombia atraviesa un momento delicado. Este terrible atentado deber脙颅a ser un punto de inflexi脙鲁n para todos los actores pol脙颅ticos y, en especial, para las autoridades. En primer lugar, el Gobierno debe garantizar la seguridad de los candidatos cara a las elecciones presidenciales del a脙卤o que viene. Adem脙隆s, es imprescindible que no aumente la gran polarizaci脙鲁n pol脙颅tica existente. Me parece necesario evitar discursos de odio, lenguaje de confrontaci脙鲁n y propuestas divisivas. En este sentido, celebro la iniciativa del arzobispo de Bogot脙隆, que reuni脙鲁 ayer mismo en una mesa al presidente Petro, a los presidentes de las c脙隆maras legislativas, al fiscal general y a otras autoridades.

A m脙颅 me parece que en estos momentos es prioritario que los pol脙颅ticos colombianos se centren en la b脙潞squeda de amplios consensos y tambi脙漏n en reforzar y fortalecer la estabilidad institucional y la legalidad.

La Uni脙鲁n Europea debe seguir ayudando a Colombia, pa脙颅s relevante y que preside ahora adem脙隆s la CELAC. No podemos permitir que, tras el proceso de paz, regrese la pesadilla del pasado, la violencia extrema.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Javi L脙鲁pez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora alta representante, en primer lugar, como han hecho mis compa脙卤eros hoy aqu脙颅, expreso nuestra condena m脙隆s en脙漏rgica al intento de asesinato de Miguel Uribe Turbay y nuestra m脙隆xima solidaridad, no solo con 脙漏l en estos momentos cr脙颅ticos, sino tambi脙漏n con su familia, con el pa脙颅s y con las autoridades del pa脙颅s.

La violencia pol脙颅tica lo destruye todo: la democracia, las instituciones, la convivencia, la coexistencia. Y en Colombia este intento de asesinato es diferente de otros lugares que acabamos de ver 芒聙聰por ejemplo, en Estados Unidos, que se ha mencionado antes芒聙聰 porque reabre heridas y cicatrices en un pa脙颅s que, durante d脙漏cadas, ha vivido atravesado por la violencia pol脙颅tica.脗聽Por eso, es tan importante y tan grave el momento que se vive.

Es un momento para ayudar al Gobierno, en un momento grave para el pa脙颅s. No es un momento para instrumentalizar un intento de asesinato para golpear al Gobierno, sino ayudar al Gobierno porque necesita ayuda. Es un momento de amplios consensos, de rebajar las tensiones y la polarizaci脙鲁n. Es el momento de la sociedad civil, del di脙隆logo y del pluralismo. Y, sobre todo, tambi脙漏n es el momento de la institucionalidad, el respeto al Estado de Derecho y la v脙颅a electoral y la misi脙鲁n de observaci脙鲁n electoral que puede desplegar la Uni脙鲁n Europea seguro que ayuda en este camino.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jorge Mart脙颅n Fr脙颅as (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora Kallas, se脙卤or脙颅as, quiero expresar nuestra solidaridad con el senador Miguel Uribe y, asimismo, trasladar nuestras condolencias a los familiares de los siete colombianos asesinados por las FARC.

El atentado contra Miguel Uribe no es un hecho aislado, como tampoco lo son los sesenta y cuatro atentados de las FARC cometidos en los 脙潞ltimos dos meses. Petro es el m脙隆ximo responsable. Socio de los peores liberticidas y protagonista del pasado m脙隆s sangriento del pa脙颅s como militante del grupo terrorista M-19, Petro ha devuelto a Colombia a sus a脙卤os m脙隆s oscuros.

El asesinato se ha convertido nuevamente en instrumento de poder de la izquierda, como hemos visto en Ecuador, en Brasil y en Estados Unidos. Por ello, la Comisi脙鲁n Europea debe romper todo v脙颅nculo, inmediatamente, con los reg脙颅menes que amparan, promueven o justifican estas pr脙隆cticas: Cuba, Venezuela y Nicaragua.

Y, por ello, tambi脙漏n debe condenar firmemente y sin ning脙潞n tipo de ambig脙录edad, se脙卤ora alta representante, los asesinatos pol脙颅ticos de la izquierda y las pr脙隆cticas de mat脙鲁n de Gustavo Petro, el mismo que fue aplaudido en el Congreso de los Diputados de Espa脙卤a por populares y socialistas para verg脙录enza de todos los espa脙卤oles.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Reinhold Lopatka (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, the Inter-Parliamentary Union, with 181 member parliaments out of 193 countries in the world, expresses each year more and more concern over the growing number of human rights violations against parliamentarians. The number of cases brought before the IPU has more than tripled within one decade, from 311 in 2014 to 956 in 2024. The assassination attempt against Colombian Senator Miguel Uribe is one of these grave attacks on democracy, the rule of law and the fundamental rights of political participation and free expression. Therefore, we have to be in solidarity with Senator Uribe, and the EU must defend human rights and stand clearly against all forms of political intimidation.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Julien Sanchez (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, je ne partage pas vos id脙漏es, mais je me battrai pour que vous puissiez toujours les exprimer. En tant qu'脙漏lu, je suis profond脙漏ment d脙漏mocrate, respectueux de la libre expression de chacun, et je voudrais dire ici ma vive 脙漏motion face au drame qui a touch脙漏 la Colombie. Voir un candidat 脙陋tre victime d'une tentative d'assassinat quasiment 脙聽 bout portant est extr脙陋mement choquant et ces images restent grav脙漏es dans nos m脙漏moires.

Ces attaques violentes, ces tentatives de meurtre, comme ont pu par exemple en conna脙庐tre Donald脗聽Trump avant son 脙漏lection, ou Jair脗聽Bolsonaro qui avait re脙搂u un coup de couteau, nous ne devons pas nous y habituer. Elles sont parfois le fruit d'un climat de violence, d'abord verbale, puis physique, dont certains mouvements sont coutumiers. Ici, c'est la violence du cordon sanitaire et de la discrimination. En France, les mouvements d'extr脙陋me gauche qui emp脙陋chent des r脙漏unions politiques adverses de se tenir dans certaines r脙漏gions ou les attaquent, s'en prennent aux forces de l'ordre ou saccagent des commerces lors de manifestations, incarnant la violence.

J'exprime ici notre solidarit脙漏 la plus sinc脙篓re envers Miguel脗聽Uribe, s脙漏nateur et candidat 脙聽 la pr脙漏sidence de la R脙漏publique de Colombie, gri脙篓vement bless脙漏 lors d'un attentat abject qui a boulevers脙漏 tout un pays et qui lutte actuellement pour sa vie. Nos pens脙漏es vont 脙聽 lui, 脙聽 sa famille et 脙聽 tous ceux qui, en Colombie, croient encore en la force de l'engagement politique face 脙聽 la violence. L'attentat contre M.脗聽Uribe n'est pas un fait isol脙漏, pas plus que ne le sont les 64脗聽attaques perp脙漏tr脙漏es par les FARC au cours des deux derniers mois. J'adresse aussi mes condol脙漏ances aux familles des Colombiens assassin脙漏s par les FARC.

Car oui, ce drame survient dans un contexte particuli脙篓rement inqui脙漏tant, une mont脙漏e brutale de l'ins脙漏curit脙漏 et des attaques r脙漏p脙漏t脙漏es contre les policiers et les militaires et d脙漏sormais contre les opposants politiques. Dimanche dernier, des milliers de citoyens se sont rassembl脙漏s dans plus de vingt villes lors de la marche du silence pour dire non 脙聽 la terreur, non 脙聽 l'intimidation, et pour exiger un sursaut national. Ils l'ont fait dans la dignit脙漏, avec courage, au nom de la paix et de la d脙漏mocratie.

La Colombie vit aujourd'hui des heures bien sombres. Vous n'脙陋tes pas seul. Votre combat est aussi celui de millions de Colombiens qui refusent de c脙漏der face 脙聽 la peur. Nous saluons votre engagement et esp脙漏rons de tout c脜聯ur votre r脙漏tablissement.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rody Tolassy (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Commissaire, chers coll脙篓gues, ce qui s'est pass脙漏 en Colombie n'est pas seulement une attaque contre Miguel Uribe, c'est une atteinte brutale au sein de la d脙漏mocratie. Une balle tir脙漏e contre la paix, une tentative d'assassiner l'espoir.

Et ne nous y trompons pas, cette violence n'a pas de fronti脙篓re. Elle r脙麓de d脙漏j脙聽 chez nous, en silence, parfois avec la complicit脙漏 et l'aveuglement volontaire. Rappelez-vous, en 2022, Marine Le Pen, candidate 脙聽 la pr脙漏sidence, a 脙漏t脙漏 violemment agress脙漏e. Pas de toll脙漏, pas d'indignation m脙漏diatique. Rien. Le silence assourdissant des bien-pensants.

Mais ces attaques, qu'elles soient ici ou ailleurs, sont les sympt脙麓mes d'un m脙陋me poison. Le terrorisme politique, celui qui vise 脙聽 faire taire, 脙聽 faire peur, 脙聽 soumettre. Laisser faire, c'est trahir, c'est allumer un incendie qui demain consumera m脙陋me ceux qui se croient 脙聽 l'abri. La d脙漏mocratie, c'est un combat. Elle appartient au peuple tout entier. Et ce peuple a le devoir sacr脙漏 de condamner avec force tous les actes de violence, d'o脙鹿 qu'ils viennent, quelle que soit leur origine.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Interventions 脙聽 la demande

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, democracy and safety from political violence are not self芒聙聭evident.脗聽They are the results of hard work, blood, sweat and tears.

Still, the work is far from over.脗聽The recent assassination attempt of senator and presidential candidate Miguel Uribe in Bogota paints a horrifying picture of the democratic state of Colombia.

In Colombia, the criminal networks have grown so large that they have created parallel societies who use severe violence in order to further advance their own goals.

Unfortunately, the same phenomenon is also becoming ever more apparent in today's Europe.

This nightmarish bloodshed must be stopped. Violence cannot continue to cast a shadow on the democratic processes of the world.

I wholeheartedly wish Mr脗聽Uribe a swift and full recovery.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Fin des interventions 脙聽 la demande)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kaja Kallas, Vice-President of the Commission / High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy. 芒聙聯 Mr President, honourable Members, thank you for this debate. Today's debate was a further demonstration of the importance of Colombia as a close and like-minded partner of the European Union. The EU-Colombia partnership and cooperation agreement is under negotiation and it is the best way to enhance this partnership.

Of course, today's debate also highlighted the worries that we have: the importance of supporting our Colombian partners in the pursuit of peace and the fight against organised crime, as well as in the protection of democracy against political violence and extreme polarisation.

The recent attacks should not lead us to forget or minimise the historic results achieved by Colombia since the signature of the agreement in 2016. They must instead serve as a warning about the persistent challenges faced by the country and remind us of the continued need for international support and solidarity.

As made clear in today's debate, the European Union has heard the warning and will continue to do its part as a strong and reliable partner to Colombia and its people.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 Le d脙漏bat est clos.

脗聽脗聽Cristian Terhe脜聼 (ECR). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, Raportul privind propunerea de directiv脛聝 pentru combaterea abuzului sexual asupra copiilor abordeaz脛聝 o problem脛聝 extrem de grav脛聝 脠聶i urgent脛聝. Din p脛聝cate 脙庐ns脛聝, textul a fost compromis de infiltrarea unor elemente ideologice precum ideologia de gen, care nu au nicio leg脛聝tur脛聝 cu scopul juridic al acestui demers.

Abuzul sexual, exploatarea minorilor 脠聶i producerea de materiale cu pornografie infantil脛聝 sunt realit脛聝脠聸i cutremur脛聝toare, care cer o reac脠聸ie ferm脛聝, clar脛聝 脠聶i unitar脛聝 din partea Uniunii Europene. Sus脠聸in f脛聝r脛聝 rezerve m脛聝suri eficiente, concrete 脠聶i juridic bine fundamentate pentru combaterea acestor crime. Combaterea infrac脠聸iunilor, 脙庐ns脛聝, se face prin instrumente juridice care necesit脛聝 claritate 脠聶i precizie 脙庐n exprimare, nu ambiguit脛聝脠聸i ideologice.

Astfel, introducerea 脙庐n raport a unor formul脛聝ri ideologice ambigue 脠聶i imprecise, precum referirea la copii LGBTQ+ dilueaz脛聝 grav obiectivul central - protejarea tuturor copiilor, indiferent de context. Copiii trebuie ap脛聝ra脠聸i 脙庐n mod egal, nu 脙庐mp脛聝r脠聸i脠聸i pe criterii ideologice absurde. Interven脠聸iile hormonale sau conceptele fluide de identitate nu au nicio relevan脠聸脛聝 脙庐n contextul prevenirii abuzurilor sau combaterea infrac脠聸iunilor. Ele creeaz脛聝 confuzie 脠聶i sl脛聝besc mesajul directivei. Am ales de aceea s脛聝 m脛聝 ab脠聸in la acest vot.

23.2. Strengthening rural areas in the EU through cohesion policy (A10-0092/2025 - Denis Nesci)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cristian Terhe脜聼 (ECR). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, sus脠聸in cu t脛聝rie Raportul privind consolidarea zonelor rurale din Uniunea European脛聝 prin politica de coeziune, deoarece aceste regiuni merit脛聝 un plan adecvat pentru a se dezvolta durabil 脠聶i echilibrat.

Este esen脠聸ial ca popula脠聸ia 脠聶i agricultura din mediul rural s脛聝 fie sprijinite prin reducerea disparit脛聝脠聸ii regionale, combaterea depopul脛聝rii, asigurarea securit脛聝脠聸ii alimentare, cre脠聶terea capacit脛聝脠聸ii de rezilien脠聸脛聝 脙庐n fa脠聸a dezastrelor naturale 脠聶i promovarea patrimoniului cultural local.

Sprijinul financiar trebuie direc脠聸ionat c脛聝tre micile 脙庐ntreprinderi, fermieri 脠聶i ini脠聸iativele comunitare care pot genera locuri de munc脛聝 脠聶i stabilitate economic脛聝. Accesul la educa脠聸ie, servicii medicale 脠聶i infrastructur脛聝 nu trebuie s脛聝 fie un privilegiu urban, ci un drept egal pentru to脠聸i cet脛聝脠聸enii europeni, indiferent unde locuiesc.

Zonele rurale sunt un microunivers 脠聶i adev脛聝rate repere culturale 脠聶i identitare ale na脠聸iunilor europene. Ele sunt mo脠聶tenirea pre脠聸ioas脛聝 l脛聝sat脛聝 de genera脠聸iile trecute 脠聶i avem datoria moral脛聝 de a o proteja 脠聶i transmite genera脠聸iilor ce urmeaz脛聝.

脗聽
